Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 10th of January, 1721
Italy

NAPLES, 17 December. Yesterday the Viceroy attended the customary Procession of Saint Januarius, which Saint showed His compassion by allowing His Blood to liquefy, whereupon great jubilation and rejoicing was displayed among all the people of this City. According to letters from Leda and from Andrea Alberti, the Baron Mantica acquitted himself well in a duel, though he gave to the Baron (his opponent) a very fine horse with costly trappings in exchange. Don Fabrice Rubio has passed away. The Health magistrates have given orders regarding the march of troops, which are to form a line along the coast to the borders of the Ecclesiastical Territory, with appropriate modifications.

ROME, 21 December. The Pope is beginning to recover more and more from his recent indisposition. Last Sunday morning, the third Sunday of Advent, the Sacred College held its ordinary chapel service in the Quirinal; however, the Pope did not attend: in the afternoon, Cardinals Renducei and Spinola visited him in his rooms, and accompanied him to His Holiness's antechamber: the same day Cardinal Aquaviva departed with a courier from Parma; knights of the Spanish court; and particularly carried special letters from the King himself to the Pope, wherein His Majesty informed him of victories against the Widows and Moors for Corsica; along with the confirmation of the victories of the Believers that had been delivered, were sent to His Holiness; wherefore His Holiness on the first Friday made known this matter, and had it displayed in the Papal Chapel; it was trusted that Cardinal Aquaviva would depart rejoicing on account of this victory.

The following morning His Holiness held a Consistory wherein, upon announcing the aforementioned victories to the Cardinals, he presented: the Archbishopric of Ravenna for Signor Gripi Audieusof of the Rota; the Bishopric of Tiberica for Signor Caraffia; the Bishopric of Caicia for Signor Philippus Poshrami; and upon the nomination of the King of Spain the Archbishopric of Tarragona for Signor Michel van Treverner Rubii; the Bishopric of Barcelona for Signor Andreas van Orbe; and the Bishopric of Tuy in Galicia for Signor Ferdinandus Ignacio Abargo; along with many other bishoprics presented by various Cardinals; and Cardinal Corfini finally resigned from the Title of S[...] via Santa Susanna, and the one of Saint Peter in Chains to his successor: as this nomination was the most numerous that has occurred since the reign of His Holiness Leo [XII] [Editor's note: The text is unclear here and may contain errors in the original transcription], it is said that he discharged his duty well, and from all those who were to take possession of the appointments, sent a very confidential brief to the [?] after the dispensation of its General [?] Eternal [?].

Generally the aforementioned [?] was dispatched by the Pope to inform His Holiness that his General [?] had already encountered some necessity regarding his ships, wherefore the gentlemen who oversee the matter will take care. Thursday morning a public Consistory was held, at which Cardinal Georgius Spinola, the Cardinal [?], was received in the customary manner by the Pope. That afternoon the customary Congregation regarding the Trial of Cardinal Alberoni was held. [Gillietti?] for a Congregation of the Propaganda regarding the commission of Archbishop Michetti, to work on some works for the embellishment of churches in the territory to reach the fort. The Duchess of Saxe-Gotha and the General Governess for Sekkendorf have arrived here from Sicily, and will shortly depart for Vienna. The Ambassador of Portugal has had the examination, and upon the result [?] were sent out of the Ecclesiastical Territory. The courier from Milan was arrested at the admission to the gate of this City, and it is said he came for eight [?] having no century [?] [?] had been cleared; however, when it was noticed that the same were published, [?] was again [?]; after which those in these were laid; Cardinal Althan had a private audience regarding this, wherein he complained to His Holiness strongly about this undertaking, saying that such measures were only a pretext for investigating the aforementioned Conrad as contraband goods which he carried with him, which in such a case would be confiscated, which had not been [?] otherwise.

GENOA, 5 December. According to recent advices, in order to repopulate the city, over 300 marriages were celebrated there in a single day.

MILAN, December. Upon learning that three French deserters from Milan had secretly betaken themselves to Piedmont, our Health magistrate has severed all communication with the said Piedmont.

VENICE, 23 December. After the Extraordinary Ambassador to the Bridge, Charles Ruzzii, had completed his quarantine, he came Tuesday morning with a numerous retinue and in customary ceremonies before the College; that evening the Government observed the solemn Christmas night Mass, which was celebrated in the Church of St. Mark: and for the following day in the morning Mass on Christmas day in the Church of the Preachers. All the attending Ministers offered prayers for the King's feast and the coming year, the compliments of congratulation to the Government being delivered by speakers. Thursday morning the Government visited the body of St. Steven in the Church of St. George Major; and the Doge, according to custom, gave a magnificent meal to all the foreign Ministers and Prelates who were seated at the Doge's table in the city. That same occasion many invited persons attended and in the evening an opera was performed. A very strict proclamation was published against fraud, particularly against adulterated [goods?].

The Netherlands

Vienna and [on the way?] and hard things, for lights, (well heard) much rain has [fallen], and indeed great damage was suffered. The continuous rain has caused the rivers in Terra Ferma to swell greatly, and to such a degree that several have overflowed, by which great damage has occurred.

Germany & the Adjacent Kingdoms

VIENNA, 21 December. Christmas night the Emperor and the Archduchess attended at the Court chapel, where they received communion; and the following morning having attended divine service in the Court Chapel, His Imperial Majesty and the Dowager Empress received the compliments regarding the Christmas feast, and in the afternoon dined in public; the second Christmas day, being the Feast of St. Stephen, they attended divine service the following Sunday; that day the former great cellar-master of the Abbey of St. Blasius departed for the Black Forest, where he has been elected Abbot of the Empire for some time by the convent; and yesterday the first chambermaid of the Archduchess Amalia, along with the imperial tapestry-keeper Quentin with the costly bedstead and cradle, and many other valuable presents for the Archduchess Maria Josepha, departed for Dresden; the said childbed is of red velvet, very richly worked with gold. It is understood that at the coming New Year great changes will occur there, which time will show. Yesterday a courier from Milan arrived with news that the Governor of that duchy, the aforementioned high official of the Imperial chamber, along with the Commandant of Cremona, had been attested to, because they had conducted themselves irregularly in the service of the aforementioned Treasury.

REGENSBURG, 2 January. The Ministers of the Protestant corps received yesterday several days absolute news from Vienna, and [it] lengthy response with the appendix, the aforesaid [?] by the Saxon resident, as a matter of that directorate dependent, was handed over to the Emperor, also examined and entirely investigated by His K. M., was delivered to the Imperial directorate, as it now also passes from one to another in order to report on it; so that one is eager to learn what answer will be given to it by the Emperor; and particularly since the Roman envoys claim that it will not be advantageous, as the Emperor would have taken very badly the behavior of some petitions at the Vienna court; against which the Protestants contend that they proceeded on the path of truth and not with passion; and everything without respect of persons has been brought to light, so that His K. M. through his renowned equity and justice might thereby give satisfaction, and to deprive the injured parties of the means thereto for the future. The news of the arrival of the Hanoverian legation secretary in Mannheim is awaited with great longing, and somewhat regarding the treatment of the governor might be concerned there; the opinions here regarding his admission are very varied, as the Elector has declared that he was equally capable of giving the injured party satisfaction, without that deputation being necessary. Those of Ulm, having sought moderation in their Imperial taxes for 2 or 3 years, have finally obtained it, and the Imperial decree regarding the same has been brought here to the dictatorship.

Switzerland

GENEVA, 30 December. According to the latest letters from Marseilles, the pestilential sickness there has nearly ceased entirely; the city greatly desired rain, both so that the air might become fresher, and so that the streets could be cleaned; nor were any persons from outside allowed to enter before they had completed a suitable quarantine; by which means it was hoped to prevent the pestilential sickness from taking hold in the said city for the third time: since the magistrate had placed a general tax on provisions, these had been obtainable at a reasonable price; and they were very strict against the plague-stricken, who did not comply properly with that order. The latest letters from Aix say that many persons still die there daily from the pestilential sickness; however, since it had rained for a few days, the air had been so purified that daily only 15 or 6 persons died, but outside the city still 30 or 40 persons perish; provisions were very high in price there, and money remained in continuous scarcity. The lieutenant of Provence, having ordered the inhabitants of Riez to provide the necessary provisions for the batteries of the city of Aix (with compensation), the magistrate of the said city fell [to disorder?] but against the intendant the instruments of Riez not only condemned [him] to deliver 150 sacks of grain without payment to those of Aix, and that equally companies of soldiers and the battery to maintain, as long as the pestilential sickness shall continue there; but it is understood moreover from further letters that the principal magistrate persons of that city have been condemned by sentence to be hanged publicly; and others to go to Aix to help bury the bodies of those there who have died of plague, which last article of their sentence [?] they have pleaded before the gallows, and have already been taken there; also other persons, which also took part in the government, and were themselves also taken prisoner at the same time, have been condemned to [?]; the remaining complicit members of the government have [?] soldiers in their house, which live at their expense there.

France

PARIS, 1 January. The meeting of the Bank was held on the 24th ultimo by the Duke Regent with a preliminary discourse regarding the condition of the affairs of the Company, and [the text breaks off]

The Netherlands

DUNKIRK, 5 January. Due to the violent storm from the N.W. on the 31st of last month, in the Vlie at 3 o'clock in the afternoon the water rose 3 feet higher than the normal height at the sluice gates of the Canal at Mardyk; whereby the fortification, where the old harbor flows, was so completely washed away by the sea as if nothing had ever been built there; and the confusion on land was so great as to be almost unbelievable; for people feared that the damage, should the wind not soon diminish, would remain irreparable. In the Yzer and in the surrounding countryside as much as 30 miles around this city people were compelled to flee due to the flood; and since no means could be found to hold back the water, it has now receded.

AMSTERDAM, 5 January. At Port à Port (where ships from Rio de Janeiro were still being seen daily) Aug. Jahn Harma van Hof is garrisoned, and it was reported that on the 9th of last month the Hamburg ship was still before the Bar; the St. Jan de Luz ship, dated 3 January, Corn. Gentile, sailing from the Maas to Baltica; the Stockholm (where the weather was strong for) Hans Florière sailing; the Stockholm (where the weather was strong for) Hans Florère growing, [text unclear—multiple corruptions in original]; 3 at Karalshaven without otherwise seventeen skippers from Stockholm to Lübeck having to [sail]; at Hammerston in Jutland Jacob Kock, sailing from Copenhagen to Drammen; in the south Matthys Jantsen and Jeans Jactpens from Hamburg; Pieter Spafian and Pieter Martens from here; 5 at Copenhagen [driven by storm] Cnut Bellya, from Danzig to Bremen having to [sail]; and at Villa Vittiola Hendrik Jantse Wasmer before the Brouwers hag the ship of Capt. Louis de la Fuente sailing toward [?] wards. From Bordeaux it was reported that the ship of Iswel Imeltte must remain in Hamburg; the kist of St. Anthony without crew having to [arrive]; the ships from Bordeaux have continued their voyage; it being there [?]

GENOA. Cob Hendrik Tymesde and the Black Raven from here and the Maas. The Genoa ship named Bielt having remained, is that in Pirée harbor. from Nantes here the [?] wards coming. The ships of Jotgen Simonie and Jo[?] from the Escaut having to [sail], and near Mardat having [appeared], from the first time alone the crew born. Skipper Fredik Olders; from Gorinchem to Italy having departed, returned up the river due to contrary wind. Skipper Michiel Schade, from Borkelm having to come here, after seeing the hawser of the ship Waterland drifting in the sea; as he the hawser of the ship Waterland had seen drifting in the sea; various ships from the Jut coast having become wrecked [?]; also Sibie Wouteis reports, from Viekkeren in the Vlie within, that 10 to 12 days before her departure, also from there toward here had sailed. Wolkert Janitie, Douve Fredrichs, Jelle Naming, etc. Wybrend Dorway, having to go to foreign parts, along with a hocker with one mast and a frigate, lay in Medemblik. One for shipping from [?]ige to [?]mans having to [sail], ship of Steven van de Linde. I say on by the Zuidwal on 7 feet water sit, were 2 lighters with goods [taken], and it is hoped that the ship, which was still tight, would also come out. The ship of Lichter Hendriks still lies in Hammerrik sailing toward here; and Klaes Rijpkes has arrived here at Hammerrik. The stranded ship on the Koog in Teiell, arrived from Bieburg. The stranded ship on the Koog in Teiell, from Bienue having to keep, is that with Capt. John Hildes. [text unclear] as they from Teiell (where Mattbye Touly up...) toward St. Omer had gone through here, and under which 3 [?] come through) well so shipmasters toward Cadiz, [?] toward Lübeck, is fast, and also [?], [?] merchants toward Cadiz, [?] toward Lübeck, is fast. This city [?], Klaas Sweers toward the Barbiscus, Jaak de Jong and Piete Uomia toward Surinam; the Suelte Gailly toward Rutallan; Jan Roskolle toward the [?], and various others toward Vlanetryx, England, etc.; [?] others toward Oudshoorn; Simion and [text unclear].

The Hague, 9 January

The Lords States of Holland and West-Friesland have hereupon again gathered together. Yesterday morning the Spanish Ambassador de Mongte Betetti Landi has returned to Kamerick. The Count Tiarucca, Ambassador of the King of Portugal, was at that same time with the Hl. [?] ing: meeting until a gathering of the Lords States General concerning the Province of Groningen. The Swedish Minister, the Hl. Frys, was at that same time with certain Lords of the Government. This morning, however, Sa-tecà General de Graeff has [been appointed] Commander of the Citadel of Tournai, in place of the deceased Hr. Col. Vrybergen.

London, 4 January

Today the Commons in grand committee have resolved to continue the imposition of 3 shillings per pound on landed estates for the service of the year 1721. The court of directors of the Z.Z. Company and the general court have received, and [?] to one another [...] the rank of the O.I.C. 150, and that of the Z.Z. 150.

[Marine C.p.] Director of the State Lotteries, makes known by express order to the public, that the completion of the lots from the 4th to the 5th class of this lottery of [...] at Leiden by Johannis van Brande at Joanne van Brakel, at Rotterdam Damme: that with this completion shall be continued until Saturday the first of February 1721 and no longer, and the tickets of the 5th class again infallibly on the 10th of that same month of February shall well be class able to be received.

The Directors and Head Participants of the Commerce and Assurance Company within Gouda, make on one side, which it on the 11 January of this year 1721. navigation the Assurance Company to Edam, serious reflection having taken [consideration] which could bring about, notify that the third installment has been paid out, and on 4 July thereafter following, one per cent shall be required to be paid for the general year; further that with special permission of the [?] is furnishing as 10 per cent of the borrowed; then the [?] per cent and the payment of the loan regents 4 per cent in the year shall be continued all from sand land.

Course of Exchange

The EXCHANGE RATE today on Madrid 88 and 1½ to ¼, Cadiz 114 and ½, Seville 113 and ½, Lisbon 41 and ½, London 41 and ½, Venice 65 and ½ to 79, Livorno 86 to 85 and ½, Genoa 9. Paris [?] 45, Landes 32 sch. s., Leipzig current is ¼, St. Franks fort current 29 and ½, St. Anthony st. Bertlen 12 and ½, St. Kandsberg 12 and 9 [?]ered St. Antwerp st. Breda 12 and ½ Cent. Ghent 21 and ¼, Brussels 2 and ½, Pt [?] 2 and ½ Pt Cent. Ghent 21 and ¼, Brussels 2 and ½, Pt Zeeland 7 agile, Rotterdam 3 agile ½. The Shares of the Bank [are] 50 w. L C. 108 to 10. [Editor's note: This newspaper contains typical 18th-century content: shipping news, exchange rates, real estate notices, advertisements for publications and medical services, and governmental announcements. The text quality degrades significantly toward the end, suggesting either damage to the original document or transcription errors.]

Notices & Advertisements

For sale a dwelling, standing and situated in the Barony of Wassenaar, just below the Devil, with a stone house, barn, and outbuilding of the Generality in the Bockhorsstraat in The Hague.

On Tuesday the 14 January 1721 there shall be held at the courthouse in Alphen, a public auction and sale of a most pleasant dwelling or farm, of about 14 morgen, and provided with two delightful viewing places, both toward the canal of Alphen and the Gouda sluice.

On Saturday the 13 January there shall be held within the city of Leiden at the Lord's Lodging by the Burg, a public sale of a considerable property of which well 32 morgen is occupied with stone and tile land, situated at the eidorp.

At Leiden by Christiaan Vermey is printed and to be obtained, as also in other cities by the booksellers, monasteries and establishments, and also in other cities by the booksellers, as also in other cities by the other booksellers, etc. outline, this progress of the cities, Haarlem, Amsterdam, Amsterdam, etc., and the unmarked Abbey of Egmont, with curious clarifications regarding the old churches and cloisters, famous men, etc. 2 volumes, with figures in 8.

Printed at ROTTERDAM and at LEIDEN by the JANSSOONS VANDER AA; as well as in other cities by the booksellers; the happy fluyt-ship the Slot van Cappellen, as also the adventurous events of [?] persons, who saved themselves in life, in 8. Item weeklies of the principal news, in a certain historical manner containing a brief note of all the principal events of the year 1720, in 4. Item, an ETERNAL LOVE ALMANAC, in octavo, with figures.

Mr. Thomas Angelhome, Surgeon, operator and rupture-master at Amsterdam, on the Spiegelgracht in the White Sun, makes known that through master at Amsterdam, he has been appointed a Philosophical Mercury, which his dwelling through his son so that the same unencumbered of pregnant women and young children may be used; [?] other use there, in a certain and convenient way however old or malignant the condition may be; without anyone being able to keep themselves from his [?] patients [being able] through the aforesaid medicines to help themselves, provided they [?] certain accidents, which my daily oversight and own treatment require, the chambers portly [?] there according to its fashion to lodge, to the end thereof its healing work [?] assured are, do we (the patients always [?], but to complete healing thereof by agreement accept; through the aforesaid, the [?] the surgeon and much [?] here to note: The day after tomorrow: [?] if not helped by me, provided they do not have venereal disease.

It is made known that with more permission of the Ed. Gt. Agtb. Magistrate of this city Leiden, the American [?] are to be seen, in which the raising of their sex can be observed, and even [?] can be paid; [?] 2 princes can [?] on the Broedelzaat around the city around the Vrouweliteerp, in the Coat of Arms of Great Britain, be seen.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 10th of january, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 6.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 13th of January, 1721
Portugal

LISBON, 9 December. We are informed again, as of a settled matter, that a new Company will be established there in January, to commence operations in March; it will consist of 20 Million Crusados; with shares of 1,600 Crusados each, which will also be in Blue [certificates] and may only be sold for cash; the trade in Brazil, Goa and Angola will be expanded thereby; and to this will also be added the Slave Trade, all commerce in Africa, and other Colonies, and all former Conquests will fall to the Company; besides other great advantages that His Majesty will grant to a certain Company, and further from Brazil and Bahia orders are expected here from Madrid to put the finishing touches to the work.

Spain

CADIZ, 17 December. Three mornings hence, Admiral Guera arrived with three [vessels?] bringing news from Vera Cruz, having departed the previous year, came to anchor in this Bay, and two others, together with an Advice [dispatch] from Cartagena, were likewise in the convoy of ships, which were also from another certain port, arriving together in 42 days from Havana, with news that the Fleet of 20 ships sailing in the early part of August departed from here; in the aforementioned voyage the return cargo, which 200 men [?] sailed with, brings much fruit, which the last Fleet had left behind; and the loading thereof, which again in Seville is drawn to 2 Million [units], one Fruit; but because no one has yet come ashore, the further particulars cannot be reported; being only a Servant from the [?] journey to Madrid.

CADIZ, 17 December. The joy here is uncommonly great regarding the happy arrival of the ships from Vera Cruz, as likewise from the former Haven of the Moorish [territories] in Africa, in that they had suffered very desperately. That which on our part also cost several brave Officers and common Soldiers their lives; wherefore the attention to them is not as slight as into each well imagines; and since the Moors have returned to their old Encampment near Ternate, the besieging of that City will remain under consideration; and although this warfare from the Battle has not only received a pure Reinforcement, but even a Rich Land, so as to relieve our [forces] of difficulties, they have been secured, Alliance and Malaga continue with the necessary supply of our Army. In this Bay have arrived the ships Britannia, Capt. John Exeis from London, the Catharina Caly, Capt. Pieter Hofdalia from Amsterdam, the Quadrugelliantie, Philip Strang, the St. Lucia de Rota, Roelof Rolendaal &c. from the Moorish Coast, together with some from Ternate and from Iceland; whereas in turn only departed the Spanish Fleet, Capt. Peter Molena toward Andalusia, and the St. Maria, Capt. Anthonij Colsart toward Ostend.

MADRID, 24 December. The Governor of Cadiz has given us a Letter wherein the article [states] that from which in that Spanish [territory] is ruined; one speaks variously of the cause of this violent Fire; the poor People have been unable to save anything of their Goods; but the rest of this City is still protected by the Citizens against Fire.

MADRID, 24 December. The Governor of Cadiz has informed the court by Express of the arrival without doubt of the West Indian [fleet] in the Bay, and with the same one has received news that the Fleet, which departed Cadiz in early August last, likewise arrived in Vera Cruz. One speaks of further Troops for the reinforcement of the Army under the Direction of the Second in Africa; meanwhile ordinary orders have been given to oppose the Moorish Encroachments before Ceuta with all possible speed.

MADRID, 25 December. The day before yesterday one received here the pleasing news that Admiral Don Balthazaar de Guevara with the following ships from Vera Cruz, but lastly in 42 days from Havana, arrived in the Bay of Cadiz, namely the Gambe, the Trijana, the Finks, the St. Bartholomew, the St. Joseph, the Fidelei, and the St. Carlos; the ships N. Sra. de Bourgonia and N. Sra. del Carmine, which also departed with the Fleet from Havana, but found themselves further away on the journey, are still expected daily. The Loading of these ships, which is quite rich, amounts in the following for the King to One Million 268,396 pieces of Eight and 1,800 Cochineal; 280 casks Indigo, at 200 Elephant Tusks, 400 pieces of Guayacwood, 60[?] Bales of Fine Tobacco, 1,300 Aromatics in Bundles, 500 Rolls Tobacco, 130 Hides, 1,213 Copper Plates; also for Particulars & Military [personnel] 1,750 pieces of Eight, 1,850 Casks or Bales Cochineal, 240 Casks Indigo, 128 ditto Vanilla, 44 Bales Cascarilla, 738 Quintals Chocolate, 156 Kilns Sugar, 175 Kilns or Bales Drugs, 12 Bales or Kilns of more expensive Drugs, 8 Kilns Saffron, 1,634 Elephant Tusks, as Brazilwood, 1,625 Hides, 139 Quintals Cacao &c.; so that this Fleet, for 200 [units] which is registered, is valued at 9 Million in Silver and 4 Million in Goods. The Buenos Aires ships are likewise expected at Cadiz at all moments, indeed one makes haste so that they will arrive in this current Month. To various Garrisons ordinary orders have been sent to detach Troops toward Africa.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 25 December. With the building of the Galleys, progress of 200 [units] is being made here, so that one absolutely intends to carry [them] from here to Wisby, and thus to send the ships from Germany over the Russians. Yesterday an advice from Petersburg came to this Court, but one has not yet been able to learn anything of its contents.

France

ST. MALO, 3 January. On the 31st ultimo this beautiful City caught fire, and through that sad accident 32 Streets were completely laid to ashes, and many Houses, workshops and some Churches [were] ruined.

---

No. 6

Monday's Courier

PARIS, 6 January. One sees two Arrests of the King's Council of State, both dated 31 December; the one suppresses the Mandate, issued by the Bishops of Sorèze, of Montpellier and of Boulogne, together with the attached Advice and further appendages. The other suppresses a Mandate of 13 October issued by the Archbishop of Arles. [Editor's note: These were religious mandates during the Jansenist controversy]

One learns that at the general Meeting of the Company of the Indies, which was held on the [1st?], among other things the principal Shareholders expressed that the 8 Honorary Directors were not sufficiently instructed in the interests of the Company to direct the Principal operations; and that therefore one had nothing but conferences and laying aside of thoughts; in a matter where penetrating knowledge and very prompt expedition are required; but although this objection on the Shareholders' side was judged reasonable, the given plan was not wholly rejected, and the meeting was postponed eight days, after which from the Citizens further Shareholders and those with sound knowledge of the affairs of the Company were to be summoned; these shall thoroughly examine the accounts and the conduct of the previous Directors, and that on their report to the Company the necessary Measures shall be taken, both with respect to the distribution as well as the Regulation for the future administration, and to restore public confidence and commerce, to cause the Shares to rise to greater value, and to cease the discredit of the Bank notes and other public Effects; but at the separation from the meeting it was observed that all this, the bulk of the Shareholders seemed to be instructed [about]; nevertheless most People cannot refrain from the preconceived notion that the Company in itself is fundamentally [sound?] and others fear that the numerous Operations will yet have grave consequences; the Shares of 2,000 [livres], which were reduced to 1,000, this reduction of 17,000 [livres] is accounted for by the Tradition, which loss seems bearable to those who flatter themselves that the mentioned reduction to 1,000 would remain unchanged; but others are against it and incline further to see that the others will be subject to further reduction. For the call of 150 Livres per Share, much Money has been brought to the Bank for some days. The Shares are now somewhat more willing; and if one has abstained from the general [accounts?] and Receipts, as well as the Coins, of the Company, one reckons that the Shares will continue to maintain themselves. The Cardinal of Rohan [Editor's note: Cardinal de Rohan] has not yet undertaken his journey to Rome. The List of Ecclesiastical Benefits, which had been drawn up in order, has been rejected anew by the Duke Regent; and one says that such rejection happened because of the multitude of Minors, but chiefly because of the disagreement of the Cardinal de Noailles thereon. The Bishop of Amiens has issued a Mandate thereabout, wherein he says that, according to the Accommodation and the Constitution purely and uniformly accepted, and that such is the multitude of those who have approved the Corpus of Doctrine, which Mandate however has not yet been published; and according to the opinion of the Cardinal de Noailles, this Mandate will never come to light. The Bishop of Nevers maintains a light Neutrality in these matters; and is thus well in friendship with the Appellants as with the Constitutionalists, entertaining the one as well as the other at his table, except with this express prohibition, by reason of the Constitution, to speak either for or against [it]; on pain thereof being observed; [he required that] during the entire meal nothing but clear Water be drunk; and when recently a Jesuit, in the presence of a large Company, was at his Table, making jests about the Accommodation, said to the Bishop, "there where the Fathers are," whereupon the Bishop replied, "you will not speak thus at my Table, and therefor you are not aware what I will do," upon which he called to a Lackey, who served the mentioned Pater Jesuit, commanding him to give him Wine, and the Pater, imagining that the Bishop was merely jesting, was very surprised to discover that the Lackey, notwithstanding his remonstrances, punctually obeyed his Master's command and served him nothing but Water alone; whereupon he found himself forced likewise to comply; which caused him to be mocked throughout the entire Company.

Germany & the Neighbouring Realms, &C

HAMBURG, 7 January. According to the latest Letters from Pomerania, the Evacuation was so completely regulated, and the Term, which one believed to be the 1st of this month, was made known to the Nobility, the Officials and Cities; and they were also immediately prepared to be released from their Oaths: the same Letters say that the Danish Troops by Land would march toward Holstein, and others believe that further orders regarding this are awaited from Copenhagen.

MUNSTER, 7 January. The day before yesterday, being Sunday, the Baron von Ifenburg, extr. Envoy of the Gentlemen States General of the United Netherlands, had his public Audience with His Serene Highness the Lord Bishop of Munster and Paderborn; toward midday the aforementioned Minister was called from his Lodging by 2 Courtiers of the Court in 1 Coach with 6 Horses, together with 6 Heiducks and 6 Lackeys of the Prince; arriving at the Court, the same was received with all Honor and Ceremony (as is customary for Envoys of Sovereign Powers here) very friendly; his Address took place in the Netherlands Language, and was answered by His Serene Highness, in an obliging manner, in the High German [language], in the formal matters serving therein, answered respectfully.

13 January 1721

---

Great Britain

London, 28 December. The conditions which were recently publicly announced on the door of the Comptoir of the East India Company by the Directors are as follows. The Company is to be charged with millions of the public debt, for which sum the Government shall grant to them an annuity against [?] per cent per year. In return, the Company shall provide 9 million [pounds sterling] for 9 million 2 per cent of the capital stock, at the rate of 5 per cent, both of the interest and [?] millions of pounds sterling to complete the capital of 2 [?]. The division shall be made as follows: [?] per cent capital shall be given in per cent [?]; [?] at per cent paid [?]; the remaining [?] of the aforementioned [?] pounds sterling shall be in the hands of the Directors and the Company for the conduct of trade, and [?] Commissioners shall annually [?] per cent to the East India Company [?] and Semester. And the conditions proposed to the Bank, the Bank and the Company shall [?] millions, for which sum the Government shall pay annuity of [?] per cent from [?] year, [?] with the same capital of [?] years 4 per cent as before, receiving the continuous revenues. [?] And the additions in committee [?] millions for [?] in the capital shall be [?] at per cent and per cent, and subsequently incoming payment for the 190 per cent in the 9 millions, and an addition of [?] per cent in the Bank shall have, and the 90 [?]; and [?] millions shall [?] for the year [?] of the Republic, which [?], [?] so that over shall [?] disposal of the Bank; and that the [?] of the Directors [?]

Amsterdam, [?], according to the immediate letters [?] Rotterdam 142 [?] fund [?] [Adriaan van der [?]] the first [?] subscription. So [?] at 3 per cent Company, also [?] [?] York [?] hand in the [?] this [?] [however in general receiving of the Government [?]

Brussels, the customary [?] in the [?] became of the [?]

Amsterdam, [?], [?] [?], [Klaas Hoo[ft?]], which [?] [?] of [?] Alc[?] ter Boot, which, [?] in [?] and the [?] Eagle Capt. Ric[hard?] [?] Walton [?] Middelburg [?] van d[?] some ships [?] [?] and the [?] [?] toward [?]

The Netherlands

Brussels, 9 January. Since it is customary on the day of the Three Kings to open the Carnival, it has become known in this region that the Prince of la Tour and [Taxis?] held [?] on Sunday, and the following Sunday was celebrated at the Prince of Eitchbüche. The Cardinal Archbishop of Mechelen and the Archbishop [?] this year to go felicitate, in which occasion His Eminence the Marquis with his entire retinue pleasantly [?] have visited at Lyon [?]; His Honour, in anticipation of the third ceremony of the Prince Claude de Ligne his marriage with the Princess of [Zaim?] is to be made public, and that he with his customary [?] in the [?] shall return to [?] city.

Amsterdam, 10 January. At Livorno have arrived 2 ships, namely Adriana Hillebom from Naples, Eduard Wils from Lisbon, Klaas Hooft and Picter Reyete Klopper in 2 weeks from [Archdangel?], which all have [?]. By [?] Dalton, on the height in [?], had seen a Hocquet or a Galliot [?]. the Boot, which, before they advanced through the Algerian corsairs named [?]: at Livorno [?] Suenno disappeared, the Suenno Capt. Robert Mount to [?] word William Godwin toward Gibraltar and Lisbon, the [?] Eagle Capt. Richard Ashton toward the Levant, and the ship the Providence to Lion, Willem Godwin toward London, which on account of the fair weather Middelburg to Fort Mahon had to enter. With the ship out of the [?] East Indies arrived at Middelburg, one has news that Skipper [Baisiaua?] Voge and his brothers in the Levant; out of which report [?] glorious ships [?]. The ships the [?] Andreina Tythees and the Jorge Willem Maiten, Willemtie have departed from Nantes toward [?]. The ship of Picter Roon is from the River of Nantes toward Königsberg going, and that one [?] in the [?] [?] to get, [?] at Copenhagen entered, where [?] [?] [?]; and to the ship of Picter Roon [?], one out of the aforementioned [?] of [?] [?] to [?] the [?], even the hope with the ship also [?] to get. At Copenhagen arrived [?] Ricberth from Buelt to [?]. [?] of Königsberg; the Danzig Michiel Preental from London, and in [?] city Joseph [?] from [?]. According to the letters from London, one sends of another ship; [?] that Cornwall Wyt [?] through the true storms of the 30th and 31st of last [?], [?] [?] ships was [?]. At [?] are about 30 Hoeckers, most without [?] in [?] or Terceira, [?] the [?] come, and [?] other [?]. The greatest part of the Fleet, out of [?] [?] ships [?], has there by [?] come too [?].

The Stadholder's [?]

---

England

Amsterdam, 11 January. At Lillesbou the ship the St. Andrea, Capt. Guillaume Boucau, from Guadaloupe bound toward Rochefort, has entered in damaged condition, holding the [?] John of last [?] Corn. Jasfen, from Droitholm [?]. At Bordeaux has arrived the [?] Mercury, Capt. Jan Mancerd from Ireland; the Frawns Arun [?] from London, and meanwhile the ship of [?] Tede, and on the return of that city Josis [?], Sander Norvig and Rasmus Boes of this city, but Dirk Gerding and Isaac Holstein fell there still [?]. In the [?] come Lieve Pieter of Danzig [?] he had there [?], and the ships [?] [?] made themselves ready to be able to depart. The ship the Roessing David Aert, [?] Boriman, from Hamburg bound toward Port à Port, lay there [?] still in good condition in [?] and the St. Joannes, Jochiem Bok, from Lübeck bound toward Bordeaux, so much [?] broken, as [?] a [?] of the cargo had come to drift; the Skipper, Carpenter and one Mate remained with the boat, but the other folk were recovered.

Groningen, 12 January. The Assembly of the States of Holland and West-Friesland shall continue their session next Tuesday.

Wednesday, 15 January 1721, at 5 o'clock in the evening, in the Dike-path in the [?] and since [?] under the house of the Great [?] and under the house of [?] Henrico Vaas of the [?].

Amsterdam, for sale on Wednesday, 15 January 1721, at 8 o'clock in the evening: new Varinas tobacco, lying in a cellar on the Herengracht, [?] Cornelis Schoon, and mixed loaded with Hamburg pipe clays and copper [?] and [?] is, 200 [?]. And one [?] requests the [?], residing at Amsterdam on the new Waal Island, by the [?] on the water, is printed and published the second part of the wonders of the [?] passed on his island, his second [?] thereof, take [?] and strange meetings, [?] both at sea and on land, from [?]

DE KLOPPER, on the Breedestraat

13 January 1721: With privilege.

---

Hungary

Petersburg, 17 D[?] With such extreme cold [?] skin on the face [?] to say of this place [?] the Count of [?] and [?] [?] in all [?] of the letters from Lisbon [?] age [?], and publication of all [?] [?]; according to the same days [?] [?] [?] troops on height [?]

---

Germany

Vienna, 1 January. At the Imperial court [?] having his [?] [?] [?] has appointed to Colonel [?] Daun, the Lord King [?] [?] [?]. The [?] [?] [?] hunt [?], and which very [?] from here toward Venice [?] will [?] to dwell, and in [?] [?] since [?] in [?] [?] Majesties of the [?] [?] the [?] [?] of the [?] [?] [?]. One reads in his [?] in the policies with the [?] [?] become, but [?] are [?] [?] says that such [?] to the [?] [?] regarding the Bank is [?] of all the aforementioned [?] [?], the Peers [?] years 19 900 Florins [?] on the incomes [?] with [?] so have those estates a hand [?] [?] [?] undertaking, to this [?] is arranged, and [?] [?] sums to [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?], prepared, and [?] societies [?] [?] [?]. Course of Exchange

**The Exchange Rate of the WISSELBANK is on Amsterdam 33 [?],** with respect 32, [?]. The Wisselbank Rotterdam 34. Antwerp 34. [?]. Hamburg 34. [?]. 3. London 34. in [?] 30. Hamburg 34. [?]. 3. London 34. Cadiz 34. Bilbao 31 and [?] ratio. Madrid 4 and 1 half. Cadiz 4. Bilbao 31 and 1 half. Livorno 43 and 1 half a 3e. Genoa 31. Venice 4 and 1 half. Valencia 34 and 1 half. Porto 4 s 1. Dublin 12. Z becomes the Lisbon i 31 and 1 half. Porto 4 s 1. Dublin 12. Z becomes the [?] of the P. at 133 s 60, of the E.I.C. 133 s 60, of the Z.Z. is a 50, the General Subscription, 2d ditto, 3d Subscription, ditto for Cash, 4th Subscription, Subject of the Antiquities. Lottery Annuities, Post Annuities, and ditto at per C. the millions Bank [?] Assurance of Berg Assurance 1 and 1 half, ditto London 9, York Building 18; and although during the Holidays there is no trading in the Gallery of Actions, yet the public [?] these three days overstayed at their [?] remain [?], yet it is firmly arranged for the Armenians that the Z.Z. Actions will still run to 100 by the end of [?]. **The Exchange Rate of the WISSELBANK is on Madrid 33 and 1 fourth. Cadiz 114 and 1 fourth. Seville 113 and 1 half. Bilbao 87 and 3 fourths. Lisbon 41 and 1 half. Venice 72 and 1 half. Livorno 64 and 1 fourth. Genoa 89 and 1 half 4 3 fourth. Geneva 93 and 1 half. Paris 49 4 47. London 32 Sch. 5. [Frankfurt?] current: 29 and 1 fourth Ry [?]. Danzig 292 et [?] et [?] 32 and 3 half-thirds, Hamburg 12 and 1 fourth. Antwerp 2 per cent. Ghent 1 and 1 half. Brussels 1 and 3 fourth. Zeeland 1. Rotterdam 1 half. The Agio of the Bank 5.**
