No. 1721.No. 39.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 31st of March, 1721
Spain

CADIZ, 3rd March. A neat specification is now visible of the regulated silver which has arrived here on the ships from Buenos Aires, totalling 2,732,542 Pesos, including one million 237,000 Pesos in fine silver plate; one million 415,548 Pesos in goods, 21,000 hides, 40 bales of Vicuña wool, and various freight for convents and private parties: the same silver has been melted down by order of the Court and kept in the King's warehouse; and since the dispatches from Madrid remain uncertain, it is feared that this silver, like the other, will likewise have to be sent to the Mint. The captain of the ship Cuba Beuiet, which arrived here, reported that the ship N. S. de los Reyes, which followed the Armada of Barlavento (of which one ship is said to be damaged), had already sailed for Vera Cruz: also, news has been received that there would be 44 French ships in the Zaydalee. In this Bay (where the Dutch ships of Aube Volgers, Gaetit Buys and Gerusit Quys are still lying at anchor) have arrived the Camperwel, Captain Theoroti Gough, and the Tryon, Pieter Tivry from London, the Just. Sarjas, Iybrand Kool, and the Nicolaas, Jacob Buik from Amsterdam, the Hamburg Neuf, and the Maag from Dublin, and the Petel, Captain Rombuus from Dunkirk, which last ship will have to undergo quarantine; meanwhile only Willem Badh departed for St. Lucia.

France

PARIS, 24th March. The Dutch Ambassador, Mr. Hop, arrived here on Friday. Some days ago, Mr. Baudri, Lieutenant of Police, received orders from the Court to continue the interrogations against all those who have spoken regarding the Constitution, or who have lodged appeals against it; and the bishops in question are not yet ready. In the appeal against the Constitution, the bishops continue to adhere on the same foot: Friday last the Parliament issued a decree to publish this Constitution, which was composed of words from the appellants and presented to the King. According to letters from Madrid, the Court is to go to Buen Retiro the following week, and a mission that set out for Amsterdam to prevent the transport of silver returned home without accomplishing their purpose; the same letters report that the place which was excavated was by order of the Court perhaps moved to a certain place in Navarre to be handed over to the Inquisition. The Marquis de Leede [?] eleven battalions and some squadrons for the fortification of the Citadel; it is said that they were stationed differently at Ceuta; it is believed that a certain person living at Ceuta found means to bring a good quantity of money to the Moors; by this being revealed, and after conviction of the fact, was condemned to be buried alive. The Italian lady, Relia Alfaba by name, who has long been favored by the Court, although the Princes and other great ones have lost many appointments with royal portrayals in [?], departed with Dr. Peregrini, who founded the gallery of the Bank, towards Rome, after some delay. At Chail was admitted as member of the Academy of Painters. The murders continue violently every night, and scarce a morning comes when the light and [?] and Syndics, who were appointed some months ago for certain societies in Bastille, are not again discharged and released to freedom.

Great Britain

LONDON, 23rd March. Friday the Lower House, regarding the affair of the deceased Caswell, came to the following resolution; that it appeared to the House that, at the time when the Bill for increasing the capital for the House was pending, 50,000 pounds had been given privately to E. Turner, J. Sawbridge and the Member G. Caswell, without payment or security, and that this was part of 574,000 pounds, fraudulently entered in the account books of that Company, as if the same had been sold for 12,500 pounds; that 200 from the papers of Turner, as the documents for the House show, appeared [?]; that the partnership of the 3 aforementioned gentlemen gained 25,000 pounds on that 50,000 pounds; that the said R. Caswell, Turner and Sawbridge, by this shameful conduct, are guilty of destructive practices, which are dangerous and injurious to the seat and authority of Parliament, and most destructive to the interest of the King and the Government; that R. Caswell should therefore be expelled from the House and taken to the Tower; that they should be made to pay back the above-mentioned 25,000 pounds sterling gained improperly, over and above the 122,000 pounds paid to the said Company; that the gentlemen Brodeuski, Lord Molesworth, Hutchington and Ward should draw up and present a bill regarding this; and Tuesday, being today, the further consideration of the report continues; when the House adjourns to Monday again. The election was held by H. Humphrey Parton as Alderman of this city, in place of the deceased John Green, and the Member Sam. Gerard as President of the Hospital and Bethlem, in place of the deceased Member Will. Withers. There is also rumor that a bill has been brought in by one of the deceased Members and Tyne Sieeswyker to prohibit the Chamber and Tyne Sieeswyker linen-weaving, and thereby favor the Dutch. Which the East India Company shall as an equivalent [?]. The use of Indian cottons has raised many public rejoicings. Saturday [?] Heiberg and with his wife, while in service, R. Caswell [?] his household in the [?]; that morning R. Caswell [?] the money at Two Jews, paid 4,000 pounds East India shares to transfer; perhaps it was refused to him. That evening the corpse of the Duke of Buckingham was buried with great ceremony in Westminster Abbey; the Duke of London was at the burial; the Dukes of Pitteus, Montague, Kingston and Newcastle, each supported by another gentleman, bore the 4 corners of the cloth, and the Bishop of Rochester [?] performed the service; thus the title of the [?] was borne by the coat of arms of the arms [?] those people, who was formerly [?] and very much praised, takes the place of that many which was seen at the death of Queen Anne. One sees his epitaph, [?], which the [?] of Westminster [?] [?]; [?] and further [?], [?] in these words; [?] on his [?] to set.

Left Column

[...] concerning the Republic's commerce, Doctors, was impudent [?]. Uncertainly, his pirates [?] Messour. Courteous [?]. In Deo Confide [?] Offense, and omnipresent Ens Editerra Miserere mi: that is, I wish. [?] over the Canon [?]. For the Republic on the appointed day, put as a given answer. I first [?] [?], nor tenfold I sir Christmas, I [?] at the Honor-raising of the Almighty God. Which as a meal! [?]-turned served the Great thousand the Toising still Bill to the bearer first this [?] Acc., and in great Committee turned being, through-run the Lords the Bill to an end Wishes and in the Bank and East India Company, without that any change was made, and slid the same Listing on tomorrow. Those of the Lower House read a second time the Bill to the regulating of the Day-mark the [?] Court and left it in Committee. The Hl. Fox gave on behalf of the Auditor of the Exchequer to the House over an Account of the arrears, owed on the Annuities, and £100,000 of the Comp. of the day, which was laid on the Table. The Commons have then all the Members, in particular Commission employed, through the Sergeant of the Arms found at their places to fulfill, to hear the Report of the last Committee read; and it was ordered that Knight John Blunt and others should appear before the House tomorrow, to answer the petitions of the day brought in [?]. The Lords read various partial Bills, and concluded on Thursday to work on the Bill; treating the public Credit.

Brussels

BRUSSELS 27 March. The Letters from Camerik of the 25th inform, that one was there busily engaged in furnishing the Apartments, intended for the Peace Congress, with Tapestries and other Furnishings; although the House [?] arrived; also one was busy putting the Opera in state to stand, which is said will be very magnificent. On the [?] minutes was there a great Place allocated to build a Palace where the Engineer of the Ambassadors of Portugal [?], this structure shall be made entirely of Wood, that from Holland [?], and Water shall be shipped to Valencia. One saw daily a tax approaching on Victuals, and people were in procurement of Firewood and Beer already tired, and this the Merchants there hold very much in necessity. From Douai it is reported, that the exporting of Grains from France into the East [?] Netherlands is permitted at [?] Stuivers per Sack; which in the Frontier Places has caused great concern.

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM 29 March. To Martinique has arrived the Ship [?] made at one of the [?], that from Utrecht soon a Chapel on Breda, so that the same evening at Breda, from where the next day Zoom [?], so speedily in all the Quarters of Brabant and Zealand to come, shall continue until Saturday the 4th October; which same on the said hour at the Swan.

Jacob van der Houves, Marinus Juweel and Cornelis Verlinde Brokers, shall at Rotterdam, in the Inn of the Black Lion, sell a Ship's cargo of extra fine tobacco of the harvest of 1719. As well as a party of fine Swiersnedt Tobacco; and that at Boom-lots, in the Warehouses of the Gentlemen Jacob Senfert and Son.

Jacob van der Houves, Marinus Juweel and Cornelis Verlinde Brokers, shall at Rotterdam in the Inn of the Black Lion, sell a Ship's cargo of extra fine Tobacco, of the harvest of 1710, which is now being unloaded, and that at Boom-lots as they [?] the Warehouses of the Gentlemen Jacob Senlcef and Son.

Jacob Magnus Broker, to be sold at Amsterdam on Thursday the 3 April 1721, Cambray in the Old [?] Lodging, a good party of English Serges, new-model [?], and other Norse Stuffs, consisting of striped and yellow [?]. And other Goods, all by the Pack, being on the Sale day in the Gentlemen's Lodging [?] The Sale through Broker Johannes Staats on 21 March 1721 shall occur at Amsterdam in the New [?] Lodging, which called Doors, Weed-Ashes, shall now on Friday the 4 April, afternoon [?] New Gentlemen's Lodging [?] Elias de Mattos and Jan Wilhelm Filcher Brokers, shall at Amsterdam sell [?] in the Kalverstraat in the Emperor's Crown, a party of made Jewels, loose Stones morning at [?] hours in the said Emperor's Crown to see.

Nicolaas Lemmers Broker, shall at Amsterdam sell on Thursday the 17 April [?] in the Doodekrat, and party of made Jewels and some [?] Goods, Rings, made Silverware, consisting of a Coffret, Lamp and Dish, Coffee-combs, [?] a Table-Toilet; Candlesticks, Scissors and Boxes, Arm-Blakers, Saucepans, Shaker [?] bottles, Spoons, Forks, Mills &c. &c.; besides an extra fine Gold Horolog Sale morning at [?] and afternoon at 2 hours to see.

At Amsterdam shall one on Thursday and Friday, being the 17 and 18 April next, sell the [?] Mr. Adrian Buys, in his lifetime Advocate and Notary; consisting of distinguished Books, among which excel the Thesaurus Romanorum and Graecorum of Otto [?] Works; which shall be sold in the Town-house of the Deceased, by [?] the Book-seller Hague, Stolkeman & Co., where the Catalogues, as well as [?] in the Town-house Bisam, Rotterdam Jr. Hofhoen, Hague J. de Hond, Haarlem W. van Kettel, Leiden J. [?] by the principal Book-sellers.

The Heirs and late Burgemeester Joan van Leeuwen and Wife [?] first May 1721, at the house of Andries Lullevink on the Corn-market, [?] so beautiful bottles of Hochheimer and Moelwyck of the Years 1699, 1700, 1704, 1706, 1710, 17 [?] as well as fine Fruit and Foreign Eating Wines, which all there [?] days for testing; anyone desiring further instruction, may address to Nijmegen [?] Leonard Verlpck.

Antonio Brizelaerts, Cornelis de Roos and Johannes van Ingen Brokers at Amsterdam, extra pleasant and pretty Housing, named Buyten-Hof, recommended with fine [?] all such requested Comforts, as well as a fine Couple above the House, [?] Housing, Gardener's Dwelling, Orangery, Coach-house, Stabling for 4 and one [?] and given on the Beverwyck of the Weespeir Plain and the Roeleis Eylandt, [?] of 260 feet, as everything can be seen with the [?].

At Leiden printed by FELIX DE KLOPER over the Town-house, 3 March 1721.

---

Right Column

Aureus Capt. Etienne Gierard from Bordeaux, and in the last [?] the ship the Anna Maria from Cadiz. From Bordeaux have departed [?] Feyes to Smyrna and Pierre Andriefte to [?]. News has been received that Skipper Aard van Dielen, going from here to Bordeaux, [?] that the People and Cash through the Spanish [?] but that the Ship [?] Louise was salvaged. Near Yarmouth remained the Sailing-vessel [?] Capt. Jan Remey, from Norway to Dunkirk going. Women from Bordeaux, Roelof Jansle and Jacob Bekhout from Rochel, [?] from Bordeaux, Roelof Jansle and Jacob Bekhout from Rochel from Bayonne, Robt. Boele from London, Jan Binfton from St. Malo, &c. from Bayonne, Robt. Boele from London, Jan Binfton from St. Malo, &c. Dirk Kreeft, Pierre Jan and Corn. Groot from Nantes, the S. John Baptist.

THE HAGUE 16 March. The day before yesterday the Baron Flohr, Minister of the Hanover of Holstein, was in Conference with the Gentlemen Deputies of the States General. The Gentleman Leonard Saar of Amsterdam took yesterday morning the Oath as Receiver and General Treasurer of the Roads in Brabant. The day before yesterday and yesterday the British Minister Gentleman d'Ayrelles was in conversation with the Gentlemen of the Government. There is an expresse from the North to the British Court arrived. The Gentlemen Commissioners of the West Indian Comp. Chamber of Timur, commence their Conference this week. Before the Council of State have taken the Oath the Gentlemen William Cuttos as Colonel; the Gentlemen William Meyers and Nicolaas Johan van Glinstra as Majors, and the Gentlemen August Burner, Rochelstune, and Robbers Friedrich Herlt as Captains.

VIENNA 18 March. The day before yesterday the notorious Silesian [?] Humaan was transported to Breslau in custody: likewise Doctor Mulier from here to Spielberg in perpetual Imprisonment has been brought; and the Court-chamberlain Schönheim, shall, as soon as he recovers from his Sickness, soon be transported to the Fortress; also the Registrar of the Tyrolean Chancellery has been dismissed from his Office, but a Pension of 600 Gulden yearly has been left to him; which Punishments have come upon them. Because Dr. Mullers as Advocate and Schönheimer as Solicitor, [?] certain Austrian [?] against the Government served in an unlawful matter. The English Colonel Churchill has arrived here incognito, to request in the name of the King of Great Britain the surrender of the [?] Knight. This Court has not yet decided who in place of Baron Hofman, which [?] in London, from the Chamber Congress shall come. The [?] appointed to settle the Religion and other Grievances in Hungary, are at the bad Roads near Pelt not yet arrived.

MAINZ 13 March. Tuesday in the presence of our Elector and that of Trier, the Test was taken to throw Granada over the Rhine, which has had Effect on the opposite side.

FRANKFURT 16 March. An Expresse from England has been dispatched here towards Vienna. At Worms a Ship-bridge over the Rhine is being laid.

[...] although at 6 hours a Tour-Coach shall ride over Gorcom and [...] where the next day a like Tour-Coach at Antwerp and Bergen on Zealand to come; where one shall begin on Monday the 7th April of the said [?] outside the Toll-house Gate before the Hasburg [?] Lace-workers, shall on Wednesday the 2 April, evening at 6 hours, within [?] extra fine tobacco, being the first new [?] Leaves [...] ones as they numbered shall lie in the

Lace-workers, shall on Wednesday the 2 April, evening at 6 hours, within [?] extra fine tobacco, being the first new [...] Leaves [...] ones as they shall be numbered, and lie in the Tree-lots, in

[?]day the 3 April 1721, evening at 5 hours, at the house of George [...] serges, new-model colored Drogeries, Serge for Ladies, Crown-raffia, [...] Cottons, Damasks, half Silk Crepes, Durances, Satinettes &c. Gentlemen's Lodging morning from 8 to afternoon at 4 hours to see. [...] March 1721 shall occur at Amsterdam in the New [...] Lodging, which called [...], Weed-Ashes, shall now on Friday the 4 April, afternoon [...] New Gentlemen's Lodging be sold.

[...] Amsterdam sell on Thursday the 10 April 1721, morning at 9 hours [?] jewels, loose Stones and Pearls; being the day before the Sale of the [...]

Thursday the 17 April 1721, morning at 9 hours, in the Civic Guard Hall, Colors; and afternoon at 2 hours, a large party new-model [...] Coffee-pots, Tea-kettles, Conduits, Cap-boxes, Spirals and further [...] ers, Saucepans, Serving-boards, Oil and Vinegar-Stands, Tobacco-Conduits, [...] fine Gold Horolog; being everything in the above-said Hall the day before the [...]

and 18 April next, shall sell the late considerable Library of [...] ris, consisting of excellent Theology, Miscellanei, Juridici and Historical and Gravius and Gronovius, 25 Volumes, and other distinguished [?] Deceased, by the same deceased Widow on the Rockin by the Bear, through [...] in the Town-house, to be obtained, as well as at Dordrecht by J. van Kellet, Leiden, J. da Vivie and H. Thering, Waterland Vis, and in other Towns [...]

and Wife Geertruuy Beekman to Nijmegen, are of opinion the [...], being on [?] exclusively to sell, a choice Cabinet of about [...] there 3 days before the Sale of a [?] can be seen to [...] to Nijmegen to the Gentlemen and Steppes Cornelis Veelmeer, and the [...]

Brokers at Amsterdam, present to sell or to rent, a [...] provided with fine hangings and other Rooms, Kitchen, elsewhere, and [...] on the [...] having a outside common fine outlook; as well as the [...] Roeleis Eylandt, opening to the [...] between the Garden, to be [...]

the Towns sent the never sufficient to praise Trade, described by Gentlemen [...] [?]

[...] [...]

DE KLOPPER, on the Breedelsstraat 31 March 1721, with Privilege.

Course of Exchange

The Course of EXCHANGE is on Amsterdam 34. 4 & 5. respect 34. [Rotterdam] 34. 7. 6. Antwerp: 31. 10. Hamburg 33. 4. Paris 24 and three-quarters to one-half. Bordeaux: no fixed Price. Madrid 47. Cadiz 48. Bilbao 47 & 6 and one-half. Livorno 11 and one-half to one-quarter. Genoa 13 and one-quarter, Venice 46 and one-half. Lisbon 5 s. Porto 5 2 4. Dublin 15 and one-quarter to 15 and one-half. The Shares of the Bank are 141, of the E.I.C. 134 and one-half, of the S.C. 146, the first Subscription, third Subscription, ditto for Money, [?] Subscription and Subscription of the Annuities no fixed Prices, the Million Bank 15, Lottery Annuities 89, Bank Annuities 92 and one-quarter, ditto 4 per cent 93. Afric. C. 33 and one-half, ditto new 23 and one-half, the Bonds Atlantic 7 and one-quarter, ditto [?] 7 and one-quarter, and York Building 10. The Course of EXCHANGE is today on Madrid 69 and three-quarters to 90. Cadiz 113, Seville 112. Bilbao 50. Lisbon 44. Venice 84. Livorno 91 and one-[?]. Genoa 93 and three-quarters to 94. Geneva 94 and one-half. Paris [?] and one-half. London 33 Sch. 9 to 8. Leipzig current 38 [?] 290 100ths gr. Bremen and one-half St. Frankfurt current 29 Rix-dollars. Danzig 290 100ths gr. Bremen and one-half St. Frankfurt current 29 Rix-dollars. Breslau 16 and one-[?]. Hamburg 12 and 7 seventeenths St. Antwerp 2 and one-quarter [?]. Ghent 2 and one-half. Brussels: 2 and three-quarters. Zealand 1. Rotterdam one-quarter. The Agio of the Bank 3 and one-[?].

Notices & Advertisements

At Leiden by J. du Vivie Book-seller, is now printed and in all Towns sent the [?] F. Richard Meyd, fellow-Member of the Natural and Medical Sciences [?] of the [?]:

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 31st of march, 1721. — With Privilege.
