Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 9th of May, 1721
Italy

NAPLES, 15 April. This afternoon at 2 o'clock, the Viceroy departed for Rome under a salvo from all the cannons of the fortresses. In the interim, the Collateral Council has assumed the government until Prince Borghese, appointed Pro interim by the Emperor as Viceroy, shall arrive here. The Countess Caraffa, daughter of the aforementioned Prince Borghese, has been delivered of a son in childbed. Cardinal Tignarelli, [without archbishopric], is preparing for his departure to Rome.

ROME, 11 April. According to [the chamber], it being Easter Day, Cardinal Borromeo of Milan has taken up residence here, lodging with the Marchioness Gallicano, and went Monday evening to the Consistory. For these three days, the cardinals meeting in the Consistory have been at the infirmary of the Jesuits on account of his [illness], which was so severe that he had a strong fever, and although one administered him much water, which appeared to help somewhat, nevertheless one was not without fear for his safety. This cardinal nevertheless left his conclavists in the Conclave, in hopes of being chosen from there shortly. Wednesday, Cardinal Orsini also arrived here, and took lodging that day and the following night in the Palace of the Duke of Bracciano; on Thursday he entered the Conclave, as did Cardinal Caraccioli, Bishop of Aversa, who had arrived that same day. Yesterday morning Cardinal Orsini was also received here; who betook himself immediately to the Church of St. Peter to the crypt where the deceased Pope lies, and having climbed [there], his Eminence at once spoke aloud from the De Profundis [?], it being 13 May, with the priestly vestments of the Deceased, after which he betook himself at once to the Conclave. Thursday morning, the Secretary of the Imperial Embassy received an Express from Cardinal Cincineos, with notice that the said cardinal wished to speak with him before he entered Rome, whereupon the said Secretary went to meet his Eminence, and after holding a lengthy conference, the secretary arrived in the city the following day, where the cardinal followed him 2 hours thereafter, having been received outside the city by 2 carriages of Cardinal Altieri and a man of Cardinal Bull; so that this cardinal having arrived here, the aforementioned secretary went to compliment him upon his happy arrival at the Palace Altieri, which his Excellency has rented, and by [there] tomorrow to enter the Conclave. That same day, Signor Forcia, Lieutenant of the Swiss Guard of Naples, arrived here, sent by the Royal Collateral Council of Prince Borghese, to inform him that Cardinal [Schrattenbach] (of Naples being on his way to the Conclave) had opened to the said Council a Royal Diploma, wherein his side [?] had been appointed as Viceroy; and this Diploma by the said Count was delivered to the Prince; his Excellency gave notice thereof yesterday to the Cardinal Heads of the Orders; and 8 persons departed for Naples. This Prince, before his departure, declared [his intention] to the Neapolitans to wish to maintain them in a certain way during the short time; his aforementioned [intention] was nothing other than to relieve them; that he pursued this with such earnestness that the court officers were forced to let him escape or both to die, to which last [alternative] they came over. That the inhabitants of Frascati were thereby so inflamed with wrath that they took up arms and besieged the monastery where these court officers had taken refuge, to escape the fury of the raging commune, in that the said court officers had the good fortune to escape themselves through underground passages, which were on the side of the Signory, without which they would certainly have been massacred. This week a guard at the Conclave Palace, a man who wandered about the whole day around the same house, and being marked as a spy, wished to take flight, was beaten with sticks and driven off; and for the same reason a guard was also posted at the Palace of Signor l'Aisteau.

VENICE, 21 April. Last Sunday the government celebrated the Feast of St. Isidorus, second patron of this city. Thursday, the day before St. Mark's, the government heard Vespers in the Church of the Saint, and Friday morning they also attended the solemn Mass, which was sung by various choirs of musicians, but after [the service] the Doge gave a splendid feast to the whole Senate and the present Ambassadors.

Germany & the Neighboring Kingdoms

VIENNA, 26 April. Last Thursday the Emperor attended his art chamber and ordered that all necessary things be prepared. The following day early, his Imperial Majesty betook himself to Luxemburg to amuse himself there with hunting until the Empress with the Archduchess comes to him; and subsequently the Dowager Empress with the Archduchess her daughter have betaken themselves today, and this evening are expected to return thence. Monday the Emperor, the Imperial Chamber of Bohemia and Austria will follow there, to remain there as long as the court stays, but the 2 young Archduchesses will not go there, as it is still too cold, having the preference to remain here.

REGENSBURG, 1 May. Since the indisposition of the Emperor's envoy, it is doubted whether the imperial assembly will proceed tomorrow; although the Protestant envoys would gladly see another put in his place on that occasion, so that their affairs might not be impeded thereby. The Roman [Catholic] ministers consult how to declare themselves upon the sharp points presented to them in the Emperor's name by the Cardinal of Saxe-Zeitz, and whether their answer will be satisfactory; but since it is known that they themselves consult with the said cardinal, it appears clearly that all this is designed to weary the Protestants through troublesome obligations. One expects from the Lord of Reck, who will remain some time in the Palatinate, further information regarding the interests of the Protestants, which will be set forth in more detail with the press. The Lord of Hollen, Minister of Denmark, has made the marriage of the King his master with the Princess of Schleswig very well known. The Lord of Dorensberg has this week given his farewell from the Minister of Sweden, to be able to employ himself in forecoming matters thereof.

France

PARIS, 2 May. Since it has been reported that the Emperor is making a confirmation of his troops, and that the King of Spain will also not increase his, one believes that those of the King's forces will now stand firm; and as troops [?] have gone forth to reform the gendarmery, and all the colonels and inspectors, which are here and in the provinces, have orders to make their state complete. Besides the orders which are to be improved with money this month, [there occurred the following:] The Prince of Conti, [who was] called upon the children of a village near the Wood of Compiègne playing, in a corner of the wood found some golden Louises, which they brought to their parents, who afterwards searching that place found 40,000 livres in gold and brought it to safety; and since one has come to know since then that this money was from a theft committed 6 or 7 years ago by the so-called brigands of the forest [?] and his accomplices. The Signor de Pontchartain and his accomplices, which were hanged at the Place de Grève, and the money came to the banker le Coureux, who claims restitution; but this sum of money, having been found on the ground of the Duke of Bourbon's estate, the prince claimed the same to come to him, to make a present thereof to the Abbess Olier de Franciuil, who had obtained it for him. This Abbess Olier de Franciuil, whom the [?] General has sent, and about 4 months ago went bankrupt, was arrested on the 27th of the aforementioned month and brought to the Châtelet, by virtue of a decree of the Civil Lieutenant against him, whom he had granted as bankrupt; and whereupon this Abbess took refuge under the name of the Abbess de St. Croix with the Fathers of St. Lazare, but the monks discovered this; so she had to depart from there, and went then to the Visitation; but the prioress, who could not save him on the day of his arrest, nevertheless [?]; she was then brought to the Châtelet, from whence the [?] at the Visitation, who on the day of her detention nevertheless could not [?]; he was as a visitor admitted and brought to the custody of the officials [?]; one saw [?]

AMSTERDAM, May 9. A list is seen here of the pirate ships that lay at Algiers three to four months ago, and from which some in this century were among them the Admiral, called de Belk, and also 14 guns mounted; the White Horse with 48 guns commanded by a Portuguese renegade, a Biscayer, a Ham Maria Grazie, Director of the Land, Loteycje, advertises that at present everywhere again is being exchanged for the conversion of the Lottery tickets from the 6th to the 7th Class; that this conversion shall have to take place, running out before the expiration of the coming month of May, on pain of forfeiture of the right of the unconverted tickets, and that the drawing of the 7th Class, as [the notice] says, positively on the 2nd [?] following shall itself begin.

The Representative of the Liberty Oosterwijck in the Meierij of Den Bosch, situated by Tilborgh, are of the mind within the same Liberty on the 19th May 1721, by virtue of approval by the Commissioners of their Excellencies the Honored Councils of States on the Request presented to their Excellencies, recognized on the 12th June 1721, for all month to be sold a large beautiful metal vat, weighing 356 pounds, with the goods therein, may find themselves there on the aforementioned days; the chief [officer] shall go forth from there the next day: the principal of the conditions is, that the buyer shall have to furnish 3 [?] pledges, the judiciary of the Councils Leenhove of Brabant decides, that the buyer per the month and date [?] the bell at that place shall be laid, which also on Saturday shall be heard [?] the merchants shall be called to complete there.

Altuerns Yzendorp, Broker, shall sell at Amsterdam, on Monday the 13th May 1721, in the afternoon at 5 o'clock, in the Old Lord's Lodging, a Bond of 2000 Guilders Fiandde or W. B., and one likewise of 1500 Guilders. In blank of 1600 Guilders in name, and Comptoise Hogedijde; one likewise of 1600 Guilders in blank, on the Generality; and one likewise on the East India Company [?] Guilders in blank, at Amsterdam, in name, amounting to 2000 Guilders Bank money, on which 400 Guilders have been redeemed; further information is in the enclosed Bills and to be heard from the said Broker.

The Brokers Onkelboer, J. and D. Bontekonig, shall sell at Amsterdam, on Monday the 12th May 1721, on the Nieuwendyck in the White Swan, a parcel of timber goods; consisting of 20,000 green Cooperwijck planks, extra blank and fine; 6,000 green Hanscantalien planks, 10, 11 and 12 of 3,000 Fredrikslade, 2,000 Swynmondale, 2,000 Dingenbrohe, and 1,000 Wyburger planks; 6,000 Outrequite, and 1,000 Wyburger planks; a parcel of Coperwijck, Wyburger, Stolk, and black Hamburg beams and masts; a parcel of firs, spars, mate-beams, bark-knots, [?] planks, etc.

Jan Hoof, Jan Riedemaket and Corn. Hoos, Brokers, shall sell at Amsterdam on Tuesday the 13th May 1721, in the evening at five o'clock, in the Diefsijke in the Old Burg, a parcel of extra fine new Varias tobacco; lying in a cellar on the Heerengracht between the Korsjes and Red Mule lanes.

Filips Pietcrszoon, Broker, shall sell at Amsterdam on Wednesday the 14th May 1721, in the evening at 5 o'clock, in the Dykstraat in the Burg, a parcel of extra fine Havana small tobacco; lying on the Spui, at the corner of Handboogstraat.

Jacob Magnus, Broker, shall sell at Amsterdam on Thursday the 15th May 1721, in the morning at 9 o'clock and in the afternoon at 2 o'clock, in the Old High Lodging, a parcel of fashionable red, blue and black woolens, and other shop goods, such as flowered and [?] silk stuffs, circa 150 pieces colored and black crowneys, 150 pieces likewise Serges de Dames, dyed Luyaels, Jayan, white Chalons, Londaite and other Calamancks, Eflamines, colored Sergies, Coullen, silk and wool stuffs, and other goods; all to be seen the day before.

Gommgels van Diuijn, and of hail and Wittig, Brokers, shall sell at Amsterdam on Monday the 19th May 1721, in the afternoon, a great parcel of fine [?], and other sorts of Muscovite [goods] and [?] at 's o'clock, in the New Wide High Lodging, on the Haarlemmerdijk, a great parcel of St. Petersburg hemp, as well as 70 bundles of mirror-band [?].

Jacob van de Houve, Martiuus Juwel and Cornelis Verlinde, Brokers, shall on Tuesday, shall on Tuesday the 20th May 1721, in the evening at 6 o'clock, within Rotterdam, in the Heybergie Black Lion, sell a ship's cargo of fine [?] an extra curious parcel of London sweetened tobacco, which still [?] to be laid; and that by Kavelinga, 200 as [?] lying in the Zoonpoort, in the warehouses of the Lords Jakob Senfeif and Son.

Jan du klueven Pieterr Elders, Brokers, shall sell at Amsterdam on Wednesday the 21st May 1721, in the evening at 6 o'clock, in the Nea [...]

---

LEIDEN, April 15, 1721. ENGLAND. Martin would have what loan from English Creditors daily given. Sees Leiden Jan. Jacobs goes forth, in Legacy toward his Court good new be; the departure of the Emperor; but Pieter Payk the Ship's wharf passed by. And some others departed. Wednesday, Vera Cruz, etc.

MADRID, April 22. Admirals the 16th thereof toward through the Infanta followed toward beautiful windmills, just hygienic windmill, just the great [?], magnificent his Minister connected daily and Mr. Pitched that it is, and that those Cameruste C opened will be. Mentioned Despatch toward his Court bound the public Grades Hr. Robin beurid axed.

PART ELSENEUT, May 3. Its bells of the Oil-[?] happened Ship of He went, is here arrived timely. that the 3rd thereof [?] brand [?] where one found indeed no [?] by fire run [?] the credeurcia [?] that city by [?] which one there has given out, by force the great [?] Voost by which pushed off, the great Lord [?] become; and to the Moliasdie Streets, etc. [?]; [?]: the Damage says with very great troubles set [?] of the demolished end that the justice the both times the City [?] throughout; at the Ships run not [?] are the Streets matched [?], which all the [?] why still in ho to [?] that the [?]

PARIS, May 5. Sublons run is pattern, though they the 'syandereri in two days thereafter through going tread, bought the Zoe ring annoversredie the Re adt the mentioned hwid say one the Enemy hold Wmd told [?] lands, was [?] by the W frdt, that the City Biarnada Diamouh por, the [?] L could, says the French without to buy to kind or the [?] and in, etc.

Notices & Advertisements

At Amsterdam by Hiltonis and Bekómen, Serious & Important Reflections of Robinson Crusoe, Made by—New pious [works] of Mr. the Archbishop of Cambray, In 12mo. Likewise from the same [publisher] there has just come out, the chosen Fables of Mr. [?] tome a in 12mo. By the same [publisher] there has just come out in short, the Observations over some passages of Holy Scripture, chiefly [?] The amusements to delight the Spirit, or Religion and Leveling at Moordrecht, in Quarto.

At Leiden by Cornelis Winio & Company, shall on the 16th May 1721 by public Auction be sold the attached Books of Da—[?] what different ways to be obtained are, at Amsterdam at S. Schouten, Rotterdam at Joh. Hofthoud and Dan. Iclemun, Uytgetr Kroon, Delft Bool, in The Hague Wyck. By the same [publisher] there shall come out within a week or three, Math. Camariora, Orations II, in Picthonem et Pattium P. Mlto V. cel. Joh. Alberti Fabricii, joh. Georgij Neufcherleri Didert, Philol. de Dilis Tutelarius Verses, Heimannus Voget Brementsis [...] on the Lane wing, the fourth house from the Diefsijck, where the hands hang outside the Verfheit, Care of Hermanni Samuelis Reiman, Hamburgens. & praemia Praia, alia Thesaurus Veterum Gentium, where the hands hang outside the Verfheit, [?] a head of Trusses without Iron or Copper; still [?] and in the evening a candle for the glass fire, made [?] [?] days for Men and Women; the children [?] at home, [?] the wise him [?] to fit about the body and [?] can him a bit, for those children and [?] all loins of Venusg-wale, great and [?], how very [?] the expired in shorter time, [?]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 9th of may, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 12th of May, 1721
Spain

CADIZ, 15 April. Since the Consular affidavit regarding the 3 per cent, which they expected the value of new money from the Mints would amount to, 100 [?] May, the entire levy of one million reales in the Commerce College has not yet been collected. Daily the Mint continues to produce new arrangements; particularly that the Catholic [Church] has obtained certain obligations, which were fixed at their departure in May. It was first heard that some ships have been robbed and lost by Algerian pirates; in fact the state ships are still uncertain; but Peter Fryk sails directly to Amsterdam, and English ships to London. Here various merchants have been equipped with long provisions for the journey. The various additions to the legal documents, Vera Crux &c. have not yet arrived in this city.

MADRID, 11 April. Their Majesties have left the Frías of Araujo on the 16th and departed to Aranjuez, and the following day they were joined by the Infantes; all the Grandes and Courtiers, together with foreign Ministers, paid their respects to Their Majesties and the Queen. Official court business. On the 13th the Marquis de Liede as Grande of Spain of the first Class presented himself to His Majesty, having appointed as [?] the Count of Fuenteduena, who had held this position; General [?] continued his conference with the Ministers of this Court. It is reported that his promotion will include all the Troops of the Monarchy, which will be garrisoned toward Aragon, to relieve the Court; the said Marquis is highly regarded by [?] in this office, and they have already supported him at Court. The King has appointed his general [?] the Reverend Pablo Yanez, of the Order of St. Bernardus. Furthermore the Count Fontaine, Governor of Gibraltar, arrived in this City, and since his arrival has held various conferences with the British Ambassador Mr. Stanhope; by means of an opening regarding the[?] and division, it is thought that he will soon depart for France toward London, to still be present at the opening of Parliament there; [confirmed] by Mr. von Collier, Ambassador of the High and Mighty States General of the United Netherlands to this Court, equally as the Ambassadors of Great Britain, France, Portugal, and the three Grandes; especially at a general gathering. The British Minister continued daily his conferences with the Ministers. It is reported that the matter regarding Gibraltar is proceeding and he let the Knight Caproli be properly reinstated in his seat of [?]. The said Minister has sent an Express with important Dispatches to his Court, and it is reported that these have arrived; and it is thought he can soon assume the character of Ambassador. Mr. Robin has kept himself absent from Court for some days.

The Netherlands

UTRECHT, 18 [April]. The ships that lay here have departed toward the East and North Sea; [and] the crew of the broken ship of Hendrik Bandow, sailing from St. Martin to Danzig, have arrived here. [It is reported] that on the 13th previous, at night at Gothenburg a very severe fire broke out, without knowing what caused it; and which destroyed no fewer than [?]; the medicines which the [?] saw through fire, since at least a quarter of that City was consumed by Fire; and there such things happened; it was said, one suspected, as happens in the East and West [?], that the Flame with great force the great Harbor having destroyed, down to the [?] of the great Buildings [?] to the ground burned; and of the Houses in the Walls; Church; King; [?] Molland Streets &c., there remained but [?] ruin; the damage is not yet known; and the [?] of the several great [?] destroyed [?] was unclear as to the exact number of burned Houses; one still [?]; but not with the [?]; however, if the Fire had extended further along the River, the damage would probably have been greater; but whether the damage would truly have been greater, one will [?]; and one also could not know, whether the Fire arose from accident, or whether it was [?], because the [?] from Gothenburg not yet arrived and on the 20th February [?] 200 great nothing would be [?].

France

PARIS, 3 May. When the King had remained some time in the place of Sablone, to see the [?] and Swiss Guards the Review; the [?] the King [?]; but because it was not [?] and [?] many cloudbursts, the King decided to postpone the prepared Review; but still His Majesty stood in the Evening to ride in the Carriage; when the Sun somewhat broke through, which the King observed and the Review began, and brought but even [?] not present His Maj. wanted all other present [?] even the Sun struck Guards, and was accompanied by a reasonably strong rebounding Wind; except the Courtiers, who were there, there was also the Ambassador Effendi, and now they say the Opera goes, that the [?] the Company shows itself; then 18 or 20 [?], that the [?] the Company shows itself; then 18 or 20 [?] His Excl. [?] his. Germain go visit. A Diamond-cutter, who had some Diamonds of Jewels in his keeping, whether to [?], or to Model come, whether to [?], or to Model come, whether to [?], or to Model come, whether to [?], or to Model come, whether to [?], or to Model come, whether to [?], or to Model come to sell; but [?], who did not need them, [?] to the 11; per a very large [?] received the [?].

Great Britain

LONDON, 8 May. The King has granted several audiences this week and has occupied himself with affairs of state. Mr. Van Hoorn remains gravely ill. There is rumour that the Viceroy of Ireland, who is now here, shall receive another position. The Mint continues to produce new gold coins, and the gold held in the collections of the Directors of the East India Company is being sold at the Mint for one-third more than other gold coins fetch. Should goods from the India Fleet arrive here, they will certainly be verified by the Directors of the Company according to the Directorate's regulations. Significant changes have occurred in the meeting of the Directors of the East India Company. [The remainder of this section is too corrupted in the original transcription to render accurately.]

The Netherlands

DORDRECHT, etc. According to advices from this city, ships arrive daily with all manner of sea goods; the portion destined for here, as in other places, is being sold with good success, and thereby trade here flourishes, although with considerable delay, as foreigners are still reluctant to place their goods here. At Gouda there has occurred an unhappy accident involving a boy of about 12 years, who became caught in the millstones when the mill was operating, and was so seriously injured that physicians fear he will lose his limbs. At Haarlem, a pair of thieves have been apprehended, who had stolen some 40 Guldens.

THE HAGUE, etc. [Text too corrupted to translate reliably.]

AUGSBURG. [Text too corrupted to translate reliably.]

HILDESHEIM, etc. [Text too corrupted to translate reliably.]

HILDESHEIM, 6 May. It is reported that all the militia of the Lords of Lüneburg and neighboring [territories] are preparing to take the field at the first opportunity, and that through some of the Protestant lords similar contingents of troops have been called out.

MANNHEIM, 6 May. On Thursday there was a great celebration at Court on the occasion of the Emperor's nameday.

FRANKFURT, 7 May. The square of Voßplatz and the Grabenmarkt are at this fair as large as ever seen; and never have there been so many complaints heard about a sluggish market as at present.

REUIL, 8 May. The Emperor has held a Mass to pray for a favorable outcome of the papal election from Heaven.

---

Amsterdam Auction Notices

Amsterdam. On Tuesday, 13 May, at five o'clock in the evening, the largest and most notable gardens of the Company at the Red Promenade will be offered for sale, with great corals and stones, Venetian and German coral, shells, shellfish and Nuremberg wares, and much more besides, to be viewed as heretofore and by appointment. Will be sold.

In the garden of Jacques Bara, situated outside the White Gate of the city, [displaying] tulips; among them very fruitful varieties, as well as choice flowers; likewise a fine flower garden—such as never before has been seen in such variety—which will be sold at Amsterdam.

[Additional auction notices follow with sales of various goods, bulbs, and plants, with dates 19-21 May 1721, though portions remain corrupted.]

---

Dordrecht, 11 May

Here have arrived the ships Fortune Wood, Toorn, and Cornelia Wilhelm., the Joseph and Henry, Henry Pieter, and Cornelia, the Margriet van Hoorn, the Watton, the Ernest [?] and half, [?].

[The remainder of this shipping news section is too corrupted to translate reliably.]

---

Florence, 12 May

[Text too corrupted to translate reliably.]

---

Gothenburg

[Text too corrupted to translate reliably.]

---

Paris, 9 May

[Text too corrupted to translate reliably.]

---

Course of Exchange

**ELSINORE, 3 May.** All the ships that have departed from here have been sent toward the East and North Sea; and the crew of the broken ship of Hendrik Bandow, sailing from St. Martin to Danzig, has arrived here. Some merchants here [?] have arrived. Some merchants here [?] have arrived. [A certain] Gottenburg a very heavy [?] and strong sign in lighters, and not well [?], thought that he, unless the Ambassador would trust him, would be cared for in the Fort; and His Excellency him the [?] by reason of the Danish [?] order, from which one can clearly see this as the opportunity, and the Ambassador's Jewel by saying is trusted and by the Government [?] and also [?] to properly [?]. A Decree of the Council is published, by which the great Merchants [?] of Denmark are fixed at 18; the half at 10 10, and the hand at 9 Daaler and a half. Mr. d'Argenſon has enjoyed this favor, finds himself in an extreme state regarding his recovery of [?]; and extends all the courtesies to receive him; the King will with his death [?] the second time [?] the [?] Counsellor general, who is his Friend, is selected the 2nd of this [?] named pension of the House of Bergen. The Intendant of Maubunge and Tuin, Son of the said Mr. d'Argenſon, [?] also [?] might come. Mr. Dermaeres is [?] [?] morning [?]. The Madame de Flavavoir Spouse of the Marquis, Governor of [?], is also deceased in the course of this year. The King has granted to the Knight of Conflans a Pension of 1000 Livres on the Bishopric of [?], for which the Abbot of Conflans his brother is appointed. The Duke de Loges has received a Pension of 3000 L., so that [?] Saint Chil [?] remains. The Duke of Cousin Bishop of Metz took leave Wednesday of the King to return to his Bishopric. The Countesses [?] and Sansom have [?] the King's Coming [?] for the [?] Postmail, which His Majesty has done in the Ceremony of the Baptism of the Count de la Masutte, Son of the Prince of Conti; which Postmail is buried on good [?]. The Count of Tavanes has taken leave of the King to depart to Dijon for the [?] of the States of Burgundy in the absence of the Duke of Bourbon, who was [?], came into this City, and is now placed in the Council of Regency; yet to better his situation, [?] to [?]. The third [?] who will have the inspection over the Infantry is somewhat in disgrace over [?] words between him and [?]. It is however that the Congress at Cambrai will soon be opened, but the day is not yet fixed. It is reported that the Duke of Lorraine has renewed his claim for legitimation regarding the [?] very emphatically with the Emperor, Kings and Great-Britain, Spain, and this Court, for the coming Congress at Cambrai. Mr. de la Jarrie, Lt. of the King's Field of the Invalides, being ordered, Mr. Beaujeu, an old officer, is appointed in his place, and has obtained a letter of Survivance for the Government of the King's Hospital, if Mr. Beauvoir, [?], should come to die. The Red Riband, which Mr. de la Jarrie had, is given to Mr. Brillarderie eldest [?], of the Bodyguards. Mr. Tronchoï, Receiver General of Finances at Rochelle, and Officer of [?] is condemned by Sentence of the Council, to pay in hard currency to a Private [?], recently returning from America, the value of 40,000 Piastres, which this Private has sold to the correspondent of Tronchoï, and for which payment a Bill of Exchange was given by the same Tronchoï, which stood at £1,000 in the Bank account in credit; but he did not wish to allow such an account; but the said Sentence being confirmed by Decree of Parliament; they must make payment in Specie. The so-called le Maître, [?] Innkeeper in the House [?], thinking that he [?] him as the Comedian Poittion, with [?] and other sufficient games [?], is condemned in a book. Today the case of the Duke of la Force was heard in Parliament, and that proceeding is expected to [?], but for the reason that it seems [?] that Lemers [?] unable to condemn him. The [?] and diminished such, that the merchants of Lyons will not deliver anything to those of Paris, before their money is reimbursed them, and for this reason many specie [?] are sent through 100 with the Police-Carts, which on the 28th previously by the diminished [?] Chisos on Saone was seized, which brought up the Gold houses, and all the specie [?], without [?] the Persons. The Messenger of Chettes and his servant were murdered by other Highwaymen, but they both [?] that good Gold in it, did not satisfy, because the horse on which it was loaded, escaped. One sees letters from Rome, which report that the Cardinals Paolucci, Colsni, Fanaza and Cosini held many votes at the Papal [?]; that the French Cardinals who had votes; and that Cardinal Pampbili in a first [?] had much criticized the conduct of the deceased Pope, because he regarding the Constitution so [?] took money from the [?] of the court where Card. Albani Nephew of the deceased Pope replied 200 [?], that the words finally ran so high, that the 2 Eminences came in the Conclave, and however much the Red Miters of the Head pleaded them, from which [?], 200 for the present as for the future, nothing good is foreseeable: it is further written that the [?] of Cardinal Albani still in the [?] remained in the Conclave, and that around him the Cardinals are his Party attached; both that Cardinal Consti came into most consideration to be elected Pope. Cardinal de Billi who on the 21st previously left [?] to Verona is expected on the 24th to Rome. The pestilential sickness rages currently at Toulon strongly; at Aix it looks likewise sad; in the surrounding villages in Provence the sickness is breaking out anew and there is great fear for the consequences with the approaching summer season.

Notices & Advertisements

On Wednesday, 14 May 1721, at five o'clock in the evening, in the Dykstraat in the Burgh, at the corner of the Handboogstraat: Bock-linen, Burlap, Linen, Yarn, and other goods.

On Thursday, 15 May 1721, at nine o'clock in the morning and two o'clock in the afternoon: Black Woolens and other wares, including flowered and plain silk serges for ladies, painted linen, white Châlons, English and white stuffs, and other goods; all to be viewed the day before.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 12th of may, 1721. — With Privilege.

No. 1721.No. 58.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 14th of May, 1721
Italy

FLORENCE, 22 April. According to the latest advices from Rome, the affairs of the Roman College have become similarly confused; amongst the Cardinals Albani, Fabroni and Corradini there had been great dispute over the 4 votes that Cardinal Paolucci received in the last Scrutiny, and this same Eminence had broken with his friends because the Emperor petitioned his Exclusive against him. Meanwhile the Italian Cardinals who hold sway in the Conclave have not abandoned the Imperial faction, and seek to maintain themselves in the privilege of electing a Church Prelate, without regard to others. Cardinal Octoboni wished to place himself at the head of the faction in favor of Cardinal Cornaro; but it was thought this was merely a ruse to bring over Cardinal Paolucci to the Emperor by certain special reasons. Cardinal Alberoni gave an oration in the Conclave, through which he obtained very great honor and pleasure, on account of the great qualities and affection [shown], although the low standing [of his origin], which great degradation from a man who played such a remarkable role in Europe, brought all the Cardinals to condemnation; it is seen that everyone remains alone in the Conclave, and that many who had already shown themselves inclined toward him, on the foundation that one must find an object [suitable] to the Pauline [faction] and the Italian [faction], other friends, by these writings would note the loss of his name, have found Albertoni is worthy, or Albertoni in worth.

The Netherlands

GOTHENBURG, 8 May. In the night between the 25th and 26th of last month, a fire broke out in the house of the Widow in the Holland Street, and as the wind from the N.W. and S.W. was very strong, the fire was driven so rapidly that in a very short time a great quantity of houses, of which many were in Holland, caught in full flame, and were consumed to ashes in an instant, in the Wind Yards; further damaged buildings were also devoured by fire, likewise the misfortune made the street-end apparent, and the wind making everything in an instant unquenchable, all the houses which were driven from those rows were razed to the ground; but nonetheless very many goods were preserved, because by the strong wind [which] was foreseeable where the fire began [to spread]. The great wooden church was destroyed by fire; at the Town Hall, the German Church, and Warehouses on the other side of the River, nothing was damaged, as likewise not all the Shops, and the stored goods were preserved there through the Militia's care.

France

PARIS, 9 May. The Prince of Montauban, Son of the Prince of Guingene, on the 7th of this month in the morning had a dispute in words with the Lord le Blanc Minister of War, and the said Prince uttering some harsh words against Mr. le Blanc, this Minister complained thereof to the Regent Council; whereupon he issued a Letter de Cachet to bring the said Prince to the Bastille, where he now finds himself. This act shows that the King favors le Blanc at Court. [Various persons] have departed from this City. Guillaume Law, Brother of the late John Law, has been arrested at Orleans; it is not known whether through this money or [?] has been secured, or he [faces] sentence of Prison or Copy which some Individuals might have observed against him. The Lady of the same Brother, who leaves her conjugal Carriage at the [?] gift de Billy, is reported to be intending shortly to follow her Husband to Italy. The Lord d'Argenlon died on the 11th of this month in the morning, and the day before the Abbot de Memes, Lord Brother of the old President in the country. The benefit of the Tobacco trade was assigned on the 7th of this month to the Lord Daras, for three Million and 15,000 Livres annually. One sees an Article of the King's Order of State of the 12 January and old March, which grants to the States of Languedoc permission to raise the Monies on the basis of 20 per penny, to obtain the necessary Sums to pay the taxes of that Province from the previous Year; as likewise the deliberation of those States of the Province to obtain permission for that Loan. The Lord Gisalin, Director General of the Mints of this Kingdom, showed this day to a Parliament in good standing, respecting the fraud which was committed with counterfeit Gold Louis, of which one now sees many rolling in Commerce; they are of the same weight and Character as the true ones at the Mint, Stamped on the good Christian Royal Vincit et Imperavi, and on the counterfeited ones with Vincit, in place of Vinci. Although some days ago Thieves were caught on the spot, the House-breakings [...] and another place has [?], are the House-breakings [...] Theft is now in [?] as ever before; these last having [?] in the Palace of Luxemburg [goods] of 8 to 10,000 L. in Silverware, and that Secret Machine sent out from there through the hands of the Thieves behind the Windows of the Shops on the other side to force them so that they can creep through. A [?] of this month the Princes of the royal blood, together with the Dukes and Peers were in Parliament, where all the names were assembled. At the same time an Article was given, containing that the Duke of la Force should be newly examined and set against George; which we will through the Maurel, Son of this [?] must appear and with the Witness [?], will a Witness [?], such Lord [?] Man (speaking of the Bishop of la Loire) and principally through his prelacy [?] Duke...

No. 58. Wednesday's Gazette.

And the Procureur Général: is of the following content:

And an Allegation much made much noise in the Cloister of the great Augustinians, from some Merchandise that came from China, has the foresight brought, an accusation of Monopoly in which many persons are involved.

If one is to believe the Accusers, the Duke of la Force is the owner of these Merchandise; which the Knight Landais, his Confidant, by his order had brought hither; and the Lord Bernard, his Secretary, was charged to place them safely; The Lord Dupatt rented a Hall for this purpose in the Cloister of the great Augustinians, where they were laid in secret; and the Lord Orient [as] Broker of the same secret, afterwards profited by buying those in his name through a false Contract: there [is] the condition of all alone and ideas of the word [?] Accusation.

The [?] of the Defendants is on the contrary founded on real Facts and Actions in good form. The Merchandise in question was brought from China with the Ship the Count of Toulouse, of which the Knight Landais was the Owner; they were publicly sold at St. Malo with the usual discount; the Knight Landais was rightfully entitled to them; he sent them to Paris addressed to the Lord Bernard, to whom [he] had entrusted Funds to remit to him, and who paid the costs of transport thereon; the Lord Bernard being charged to provide a Warehouse for these Goods, had one sought through the Lord Dupatt, who having found a Hall in the great Augustinians to rent, thereupon concluded a Rental agreement; and the Knight Landais having come to Paris, sold the said Goods to the Lord Orient, who as a Merchant had the right to buy them: thus simply and faithfully is the Tale of the Defendants told; hereby one sees that the Accusers, who could only prove by Proofs, have none, and that the Accused who could defend themselves to trouble the Court, still have all such [proofs], whereby they place their innocence in the clearest day.

According to the misfortune of an accusation for much common [good], the Lord Dupatt is in particular extremely culpable. He was not obliged to investigate the origin of these Merchandise, nor even to ask what use they were fitted for. The Lord Bernard his Brother, of one Mother, had only charged him to obtain a Place to lay them, and one may well judge that he, having this merely in commission, was not drawn to any further investigation. He nonetheless learned that they belonged to the Knight Landais, and were a return with a Ship at St. Malo delivered; also he knew moreover that the Knight Landais had been admitted to a Society of prominent Persons with a Concession of Louisiana; and that the Lord Bernard [as] Cashier of this Society had charge to remit Funds to him, wherefore it was then very natural to calculate the aforementioned costs from this, and that the Lord Bernard was thereby charged with the reception of the Merchandise and the payment of the duties under examination. These Clarifications had themselves come to the Lord Dupatt, because no secrecy was made of the matter; but he was freed from all investigation, because he did not know the circumstances of the Knight Landais, and his Commission came only from the Lord Bernard, to whom he could not refuse the costs and charges, and somewhere a suitable place to seek to lay the Goods. It has never been found blameworthy that a Brother, a Friend, and even an indifferent or vicinal Person, who had the Commission to seek or rent a room for a Friend who requested such; such services are ordinary in suitable friendship, without one ever having imagined a crime therein.

The Lord Dupatt, because he did it at the request of his Brother, which anyone in his place would have done, finds himself understood to be subjected in a Criminal Accusation of Monopoly, and on this title of accusation, which seems to have little agreement with the nature of the Merchandise which concerns it, he is with a seizure of body through an Arrest of 15 February hastily sequestered, which he has obsequiously followed because his conduct was without reproach. He had at least good reason to hope that after declaring himself in the Interrogation on the Points of which he had knowledge, he would be released to liberty; which hope was founded on the disposition of the Ordinance, which permits no Imprisonment of the Accused, unless they remain exposed to an ablative or infamous Tenacity. The Lord Dupatt did not presume too much in his favor, seeing that he had thought to defend himself against these 2 Tenacities, and nothing could be imputed to him, but only to have rented a place to lay Merchandise which the Knight Landais stood ready to send to the Lord Bernard. He was so little wishing ill of this, that instead of going to seek in remote and named Places what he had need of, he troubled himself with those he found open in his District and in the liveliest quarter of Paris.

What his astonishment was, moreover increased, finding himself treated with seizure of body, and also his hope to be at least after his investigation released, is that the Knight Landais alone was merely summoned to be heard, is not one of the Accused, as the principal Author of the present Monopoly must be marked; for if it were true that he had lent his name to the Duke of la Force, not only to buy Merchandise in Brittany, but much more and by way of return of the deed to Orient, to the end that the regulating [?] would be defrayed, he would be much more guilty, than he who beyond this Militia, would only have followed his orders, as to rent, or in particular of the [?] so much a fixed establishment, as well as such sale, still what [?]

PARIS, 24 April. [Beginning of article about proceedings at a Post-house regarding nobility]

ROME, 26 April. [Article regarding Raccioli, Archbishop, and proceedings with Cardinals]

THE NETHERLANDS

BRUSSELS, 12 May. On Saturday news was received from Ostend that the Vooraae coming from China, and those awaiting it from the previous day, three ships coming from China, all very richly laden; and named the Kaiser, Captain Tobyn, the Prince Eugenius, Captain Moote, on the third ship the Polnieren; these ships bring much silk goods, tea, porcelain; the inhabitants of Ostend have also reported that the voyage of these ships has been very prosperous. During the time of the expedition with his brig, it was repeated on the way, finally, because the courier was taken prisoner, in order to be examined as to whether he had not delayed too long, and the Count finds perhaps well slaughters and he has to bring to Vienna against the Lt. Colonel. On Saturday the Baron van Andau the Excellency as Major Commandant of Dendermond laid down in place of the Mr. Pellefcier, Resident to the High Mighty.

AMSTERDAM, 14 April. As it in the ship moored ship on 10 March departed from Surinam, against there after the wind to the way-den to the ships of Hend. Kok, Hend. Rionk, Kasper Mulder, Corn. kret Verzyn, Jacquet Roos, Corn. Gerritsz, Dick Harm. And yet another; this arrived on the 17th of that month at Salee (where at that time no prizes had yet been brought in) Captain Nic. Thimont, who has been benefitted through the neglect of the forage fort. The Lidoon has been mended, the ship and the cargo out of the Schedegom Barent Janfen of Arendalis also in the Vlie and before the city well 20 ships.

THE HAGUE, 13 May. The Lords States of Holland and West-Friesland will convene tomorrow. The Lord Myndertshuagen Master the Kings of Friesland was yesterday morning with the Baron van Renswoudé presiding at the meeting of the Lords States General regarding the Province of Utrecht. The Lord Ports has been called regarding the Province of Friesland to the meetings of their High Mighty selves, being there entertained by the Lord Vegedin van Claasberg. The Lord van Weede has departed for Utrecht.

PARIS, 9 May. Finally the long-awaited expedition of the Spanish Court, regarding the subsidy, has in all diligence been sent through the city to the British Court. Now one believes that the differences between Great Britain and Spain shall be settled so far that the Congress at Cambrai shall be opened shortly. According to the letters from Rome the appearances grew larger and larger: that the Curelave would last long. The great patience of the Catholics and France has caused the Court to send to Nantes, in order to arrest the Commander of the Castle there, since he insulted the nobility in that entire district so much that the Estates of Parliament in Brittany found themselves obliged to complain to the Court, and this Commander in good motion in a prison of 4 years. The troops which were garrisoned in Languedoc, Guienne, Saintonge and Poitou have received orders to march to Roussillon. The money of the daily retribution of the French, according to the latest letters from 4 to 5 days was reduced to 70, and in the Marseille were very sad: people in a house died, which again caused much alarm: that side truly of that setting may again be named. The price increases daily, a pound of meat now costs about 15 Stuiver. All payments in trade go from bad to worse, and so many people practically in no previous present has been [?], to know that they postpone the payment of their bills of exchange until the time that they shall have money. Besides the counterfeiters, the time and the thousands over the counterfeiting of the gold Louis in Lechtenite named, there are also some here brought to prison, among whom is a Genoese. It is Mademoiselle des Marres from the drama, who leaves the troupe of the Comédie Française; the Lords Baron and Poisson will continue to play there, despite the pension of 1200 Livres that the King gave her some weeks ago as her yearly allowance. A few days ago they dug behind the wall of the Carmelite convent, under a heap of [?].

Marin Copius, Director of the Land Lottery, advertises that currently everywhere the lottery of the second class; that this exchange shall have to occur, at the latest before the completion of the year, and that the drawing of the 7th class this evening at 5 o'clock, being Wednesday the 14 May, shall the Malelaas Filip Pick a party extra fine Havana snuff tobacco; lying on the Spuy, on the corner of the [?]

The sale, which through the broker Cornelis Wacheels this morning at 9 o'clock was to occur, of the linens, silk damasks, woollen satins, Ariston, armogynes continues until Friday the 18 May; all of which can be seen tomorrow in the aforementioned Kaisers Crown

At Leiden with Conraad Wiihor book merchant, shall on 16 May 1721 by public [sale] the Motte J. & Lit. At. Colhon, consisting of juridical, medical, mathematical works of which the catalogues can be obtained at Amsterdam and elsewhere. At the same [place] within 1 week or three shall come out, Math. Cain by S. Schouren, Rotterdam Joh. Gr. Biblioth. Engd. here. much fine extra edition, added with Latin version, care of Hermano V. Cel. Jo. Albert Fabricii, Joh. Georgi Neufchler, Diss. Philol. & Pria Tutelarious

Cornelis van Duist, Jan van Baalen and Jan Wittig brokers, shall at Amsterdam on [?] at 3 o'clock, in the New [?] here lodging, on the Haarlemmedyk, a great St. Petersburg hemp, with goods 70 rounds mirror-brass, as torte.

Johannes Verkoije and Filip Fick brokers, shall at Amsterdam sell, according [to] Tuesday the 23 May 1721, mornings at 9 and afternoons at 2 o'clock, in the New [?] future costly paintings of the foremost masters, as Julio Romano, M[?], Rubens, Weninx &c &c., a party costly busts of Oriental marble, a flute, all viewable daily before the sale.

Jan Nager, skipper, under-buyer at Alphen, presents his service for the youth in the Italian and sharp bookbinding, navigation or for the steersmen, land measurer, anyone disposed to send their children there to board may [?]

These go yet 2 hours, being Wednesday the 14 May, one shall in the garden [?] each first small baguettes, and also various other new forms of Enckeludy the garden extending to be sold, being down to the clay in a [?]

Course of Exchange

The Exchange rate in Brussels on Madrid 82 and 1 half a 88. Cadiz 110 a 109 and 2 fourths. Seville 109, Bilbao 89 and 1 fourth. Lisbon 43 and agitation. Venice 83 a 82 and 2 fourths, Livorno 92. Genoa 94 and 1 half. Paris posted 82 and a vigintie and 1 half. London 33 Sch. 5 and 1 half. Leipzig current 3 St. Danzig 288 Polish gt. Breslau 3 and 7 agitation St. Hamburg 32 and 2 sailing St. Antwerp 2 and 2 fourth per Cent. Ghent 2 and 2 fourth. Zierikzee 2 fourth. Rotterdam 1 fourth. The Agio of the Bank 1 and 1 agitation. The shares of the O.I.C. 750, W.I.C. 104 remainder, Subsc. 1060, the Eng. Bank 133, O.I.C. 140, and Z.Z. 145, all constant. [Editor's note: This newspaper contains typical early 18th-century content including diplomatic news, shipping reports, exchange rates from major European trading centers, auction announcements, and advertisements. The text quality of the original transcription from blackletter print is occasionally unclear, particularly in proper nouns and technical terms.]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 14th of may, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 16th of May, 1721
Italy

AVELS, 24 April. Yesterday, Prince Borghese, acting as regent during the absence of Cardinal Schönemberg, sent an official letter to the Duchess of Parma. [Editor's note: The text here is heavily corrupted in transcription] The Cardinal's departure was accompanied by a great procession of noble carriages, all drawn by six horses, passing through the city in grand fashion. On the same day, the Ecclesiastical Council was convened, and Wednesday it met with the usual formalities. Cardinal Pignatelli, being indisposed that day, has not yet left for the Conclave. Cardinal Caraccioli, Archbishop of [?] and [?], having arrived, the Conclave proceeded Thursday morning with Cardinals Corfani and Davii, and Cardinal Schönemberg in attendance.

Cardinal de Rohan, as Minister of France, likewise presented two letters on behalf of his Sovereign to the Sacred College. The first concerned the recent understanding between France and the Republic of Venice, and explained why the Most Christian King had sent ambassadors to be received by the Republic in order to congratulate the King upon his accession to the Crown. The second letter concerned the death of the Pope, and Cardinal de Rohan sent therewith a missive expressing the precarious state of the Holy See, urging the Cardinals to proceed without delay to the election of a Pope worthy of the Pontificate.

There are believed to be about 23 candidates in consideration for the Papacy, among whom Cardinal Spada is held in particular regard. Many prelates believe that, as he is still unprovided with a benefice, he may be the choice. It is thought that Cardinal Conti has also received many votes, and that Cardinal Paulucci is likewise being seriously considered. Some believe that efforts have been made on behalf of Cardinal [?]. It is said that Cardinal de Rohan has urged the election of Cardinal Paulucci. Cardinals Gozzadini and Buoncampagni are also spoken of as candidates. However, it is believed that the election has not yet taken place, because the Cardinals are divided into five councils, which are named the Imperial, French, [?], and other sides. [?] Cardinal Scott is said to have received many votes in the last ballot. The Ambassador of Venice to the Sacred College at Rome is preparing to go to the Conclave Monday morning, where he will be received by Prince Chigi, Marshal of the same.

---

Germany & Adjacent Realms

VIENNA, 3 May. Today, being the Feast of the Finding of the Holy Cross...

---

France

PARIS, May. The commotion caused by the decree issued by the Doctors of the Sorbonne on the 1st of this month regarding the meeting of that assembly has prevented any resolution concerning the denounced letter of the Bishop of Soissons presented in that assembly. Wherefore 16 commissioners were appointed to examine that letter, which contained propositions contrary to the principles of the Gallican Church, and to report thereon. Among these commissioners were the Abbots d'Asfeld and de la Coste, who together with two other Doctors have each been banished from this city by Lettre de Cachet. This measure has been felt most keenly by the Port-Royal party against the Constitution, as they observe that the Court is now turning against these blind adherents of the Constitution.

When the testimony in the case of the Duke de la Force was confronted with the witnesses, it was found that those whose testimony had been presented used contradictory language, wherefore it is believed with certainty that they have been bribed. Nevertheless, it is hoped that there will be sufficient proofs to bring the Duke's case to complete resolution, and the outcome of this notorious trial is shortly expected.

The Abbe de Brecourt is diligently working on the continuation of Phalaus, namely on the inventory and catalogue of the Palace, which the Devil, departing from it, left to him in possession. A new edition of that poetical work is being prepared, purged of all errors and corrupted passages. Besides the Tower of Babel, which appeared some days ago, a pictorial representation is also being produced showing that edifice ascending to Heaven, wherein all the Prelates who had a part in the Constitution are depicted in various positions of office and dignity, with certain marginal notes on their shamelessness. The great procession of Church heads is following at a certain distance the Appellants of the Constitution, which Cardinal de Noailles has given them...

The Count de Cartada, an nobleman in service to the King, has died, and in his testament he has made a bequest of 30,000 livres to the Priests of the Oratory, which is a cause of considerable wonder, as he had never had any connection with them.

It is reported that the King will expend some 20 millions yearly in reforming the Troops. The Court is continuously occupied with the trial of the former William le Mothe, who is charged with counterfeiting louis d'or, and the circumstances are being examined of those bankers, who claim that, for payment of about 60,000 livres in promissory notes, the amount was paid in such gold coins by a correspondent...

---

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, May 14. The Spanish Ambassador, the Marquis de Villa Major, has departed after remaining only one day. The citizens of Bilbao report that on the 14th [?] turned away in the churches, and among other ships, including three Spanish Gonzalez, [?] to Porto Galego; and from one of them the Victoria and [?] began to concern themselves with goods bound for France. Before I ought to mention anything personal here regarding goods bound for France.

At St. Domingo, the Sienna, Captain Ainsault of Nantes, and the Hope, Willem Valle refugee, at Lieurone (where Captain Kris Harrelt and Jacob Santfoort [sailed to] Smyrna [?]) the ship of Captain Louis Marseille Matron, Georg King, Staet of Rotterdam, John Cany, from Gloning to Calleans Trusis Noak of Rotterdam, Jan Adriaanssen, the Joff. Catharina Leendeu Crooy, Jan Adams, Aulke Brouwer, etc.; at Remels Gert. Corfien and other [ships] much [?] here under from the Meuse. The storms continued so severely in the Bay that no ships could depart from the great harbors. From Eeckelle, one learns that a ship, bound from there to St. Domingo, was driven back by storm and near Boulyin encountered an Algerian sloop [?] cruising there, [was captured?]

From Droenen (where Tydyn Middan and Mathias Bruynbaas came from Leyden) the skippers Seigendaal, Andrea Giaam, Heybert Rinsgie, Breed Forst, and another sloop to Beer Rissele bound for Holland departed, where sailed Maib. Brand.

Martin Cojurus, Director of the State Lottery, announces that currently in the 2nd and 7th Classes, the exchange has already taken place; drawn in May 1721.

The Representatives of Freedom, Oudereyck in the Marquette of the 19 May 1721, reported from military headquarters to the Commissioners of Her Ed. Mo. [Editor's note: Her Edele Mogendheden - "Her Noble Powers," referring to the Dutch provincial government] reported on 29 June 1719, for all men to hope for a great door in [?], can arrange to ride there on [?]; the Highest shall [?] other Councillors, that the Buyer's [?] Burgem shall [?], the judicial [?] one Month after date the Clock shall be delivered at that place, which is also held to be known that by order of the Land-Grave of Heyden, t'Edel, as Voo and Rids-Direct., of His Majesty's Building in 't Emeler-Bad following, in the [?] there are 9 [?] departed to sweat) besides the renowned warm mineral waters, the [?] chambers, and several new houses for prominent persons to lodge and [?]; at which [?] everyone, who uses the said building can arrange themselves very civilly with service, whether with keeping their own household, or otherwise with one [?]

The Royal French Indies Company shall on Monday, the 9 June 1721 at Nantes [sell] the merchandise; to wit, 40,000 pounds Couris, 4,210 fl. white Salempouris, 690 fl. blue Guineas; 600 fl. Denastas, 1,275 fl. Pondichery Neusdecken, 15,000 fluks Zw Port Louis) [?] on [?] of complete [?], [?] on this continent money.

---

Frankfurt

[Fragment] report from His Imperial Majesty has arrived, and [?] towards Sweden forth [?]

---

Paris, May 12

The preceding squadron named Spain given [?] of the Regiment Canal [?] [?] west [?] regiment of [?] a redoubt at [?] the Weaving month June vollail leys Buemet came Fruclion [?] from the jump over an [?] fell, and Bylock thereafter [?] also found [?] hold, where none in the Opera of [?] angen, the Mur ties [?] connected too [?]

---

DE KLOPPER, on the Breadstreet

16 May 1721. *With Privilege.

Notices & Advertisements

On Wednesday morning, the Imperial envoy presented at court a very lengthy letter from the Emperor for Pope Paul, which His Eminence delivered Thursday to the Cardinals. Friday morning, this letter was read in the full assembly of all the Cardinals. Its contents concerned principally Cardinal Clermont, who was offering condolences on the death of the Pope, exhorting the Cardinals to a speedy election of his Emperor's preferred candidate.

On Saturday, the 17 May 1721, in the afternoon at one o'clock, will be sold in the Blood of the Soeterman-ijd, some curious and fine Baguettes s Regolte and English Antique [?], Bygcwaelen, 3 Tulip Tents with their sails, [?] Bollekias with his [?] belongings; all remaining with the gentlemen Fredericus and Huberius Dekkers at Leyden, where the Balesaat P. Onteboorg, t.Cs D. Bontekoning, shall sell at Amsterdam on the White Swan, a party of timber, namely 4000 Copperwick, 2000 Freduksbad themer parts; 2000 Marcouwode ta. Vlekkeife Stolbjalcen, 600 Fire Beams, etc.; furthermore, so also [?] some fine stream-wood masts, of 7 to 10 palms and a [?]. There will be sold to the bidders [?] a party of fine tulips, [various] flowers; those inclined to buy, come on Monday, the 19 May 1721, in the afternoon on the south side of the new Veckade, and continuing in the Tryp on the south-buyt by Jacob [?] Houve. Martiinus Juweel and Kornells Verliade Brokers shall on [?] at Rotterdam, in the Heyberg the Black Lion, sell a ship's cargo [?] and some London sweetened tobacco, which will still lie [?] in the Roompjes, in the warehouses of the gentlemen Jacob Senfsrf and J. T. Zomer Brokers. Shall sell at Amsterdam on Wednesday, the 21 May 1721, Moers, [?] of considerable capital paintings, which among others [?] from recent sea battles between the English and Dutch, by Van de Velde, a cap Gerard Hoot, Liuis, Metzu, Lingelbag, Hondekorter, Wenninx, the Bourgonjon, by Al Brouwer. Frans., Paeeibiis, Sawmills, by Noort, W. Schallilaes, and other great [masters]; all to be seen the day before: the catalogue to be obtained from J. P. Zomer. Jean Brouwer Broker shall at Amsterdam, on Wednesday, the 28 May 1721, in the evening, a coparty of some [?] Barien cette super fine unwashed cotton wool.

At Amsterdam by Andreas van Damme, book seller on 't Rokkin south of the Beuc de Ridgenootr of the Apostle Paul to those of Athens, contained in 7 sermons, in the [?] week, and on special times and occasions adapted by the same Mr. B. Piders in the [?] Daniel Willink, the third printing; are also to be obtained at Leyden by H. van Damme and Jan Meer, schoolmaster at Voorschooten. Alphen, presents his service, to [teach] Italian and [?] Bookkeeping, Navigation or the Art of Navigation [?] whoever is inclined to send their children there for board, can address Mr. Thomas Apollogyn, Surgeon, Operator and Rupture Master at Amsterdam, [?] 209 that the same [?] of heavy women and young children may be used [?] 2 Accident-[?] by its use, in a certain and easy manner [?] patient [?] can through the [?], Medicine case, provided they [?] before that some accidents, [which] my daily inspection and own treatment require, such the [?] heal. [?] made clear that it is in my nature to lodge, so that their healing [?] its healthy working afterwards; certain, will we (the patients beforehand supported, but to complete healing them by accord [?] the fore, [?] Medical [?] Sicknesses and accidents, too many to mention here; overnight [?], can by me [?] be helped, provided they have no Venus sickness, which [?]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 16th of may, 1721. — With Privilege.
