If you can provide a clearer transcription or the original image, I would be happy to produce a proper English translation following your specifications regarding proper nouns, dates, financial data, and section headings.

Course of Exchange

**CADIZ, 18 June.** This week an advice boat, or small vessel, from Cartagena arrived here, having come from Havana in 45 days, with letters from the aforementioned place, which report that in Peru there was such a scarcity that the galleons were not seen to arrive there; that the merchants had decided to sail down the coast with their goods to attend the market at Porto Bello; that there are now no more fleet ships in the South Sea; because due to the great negligence of the governors of the sea ports no further goods could be loaded; and that they hoped to hold a convenient market there for the galleons. The aforementioned galleons are to depart these days, and a thirteenth advice [boat] toward Vera Cruz; from where news arrived (which had over 100,000 pesos and some small vessels on board) that the flotilla under Admiral Chacon was to depart in the course of his voyage, which is why it left Havana at the end of May, and is expected to arrive here in August or the beginning of September. In this bay various ships have arrived, including the Concordia, Joseph la Chapele from Amsterdam, and the Dutch warship Edam, Captain Roos, along with a vessel provided with the desired goods. The ship Sonika Galy, Captain Jan Otter is sailing toward... and the Dismast, Captain Thomas Young toward London.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 14th of july, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 85.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 16th of July, 1721

Year 1724

Portugal

LISBON, 11 July. News has arrived here over the past three months from Bahia, brought by a ship that was supposed to call at Goa. It reported that at Goa there was news of a ship, Captain Lonis Gom's vessel, which departed from here in the year 1719 bound for those waters. It had been rumored that this ship encountered a pirate off the Cape of Good Hope; [?] having been sighted near Singapore and Mozambique, where it remained for several years. Confirmation of this good news is expected with the Fleet from Bahia, which is daily anticipated here, and which is said to be divided among 3 ships. Among the ships that departed from here under Dutch convoy under Captain Harderbrock was also Hans Heynemrost, who was bound for Bergen.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 1 July. The Queen has again been seized with a tertian fever. It continues that the Russians have suspended any further negotiations, whereby it is rumored that peace is near. It is feared in those regions that in an impending peace, certain provinces in the service of the King of Sweden might well be ceded to the Czar, while other provinces remain with Sweden; and thereby to avoid the consequence—the confiscation of their goods, which they hold in Livonia.

Germany & Neighboring Kingdoms

REGENSBURG, 9 July. It is reliably reported that the Protestant Ministers here have recently received orders and instructions to proceed henceforth in religious matters with gentleness and moderation; from which it appears that the Church Ministers, who have been sent to the Protestant courts and remain there, have no obligation [?]. [The text becomes increasingly difficult to parse here, with apparent transcription errors.]

Great Britain

LONDON, 11 July. [This section contains extensive parliamentary business regarding financial matters, property settlements, and criminal proceedings. The transcription becomes severely corrupted in this section, making reliable translation impossible without access to the original document. Key terms visible include references to Sir Blunt, financial settlements of 1600 and 1000 pounds, Sir Lambert Blackwell, and various parliamentary debates, followed by criminal convictions and stock market prices including Bank shares, East India Company stock, and lottery annuities.]

France

PARIS, 11 July. Recently the Turkish Ambassador, accompanied by several of his attendants, visited the Sorbonne, where His Excellency was received at the door by the Doctors in their academic robes and square caps, with the Dean at the head of the deputation. After he had spent some time there and inspected the building, which he found most beautiful, the Ambassador was brought to the Library, from where he proceeded to the Church and spent considerable time contemplating the paintings hung there. His Excellency also examined the tomb of the celebrated Cardinal Richelieu, founder of this Church, and having inspected it carefully, declared in the Turkish language that nothing more beautiful could ever be seen. The Ambassador then, having spent some two hours thereabouts, returned to his lodgings. This day His Excellency visited the Royal Printing House, which is engaged in printing the history of Louis XII and medals. Tomorrow the said Minister will have a private audience with His Majesty without any public ceremony. The Archbishop of Cambrai has recently requested of His Eminence the position of Secretary of the King's Cabinet, which he has long served. Previously he was appointed to this position by the Duc de Montfort, first gentleman of the Chamber. Notwithstanding this appointment, news appeared that the said Prelate by a private act has retained ownership of the aforementioned office, and since he cannot divest himself of it without permission... [The text becomes increasingly corrupted and difficult to render accurately in the final portion.]

England

LONDON, 21 June.

[Text appears corrupted/illegible in original]

France

PARIS

[Text is to be received]. The Marquis de Marfillac, who was formerly General in this service of this Crown, has not departed secretly to Spain, as some have believed, but with the knowledge of the Duke he has gone to serve in a special capacity there under the orders of the King of Spain. For some days now work has been undertaken in Parliament to conclude the matter of the Duke of la Force; however, it is understood that the same will be further brought up, and [?] will be pronounced. From Bayonne it is reported that many pieces of cannon and ammunition from Ourlop, Fortaralie, and St. Sebastian were being transported, as those fortifications, along with several other positions, should be returned to the Spaniards. The Duke of Charolois has resigned from the hands of the King of France his position as Governor of Touraine. One sees a Declaration of the King registered in Parliament from 24 June, concerning the Edicts and Ordinances of the Receivers of the Coalition, and of the Commissioners of the ecclesiastical dependencies.

PARIS, 11 July. On Monday and Tuesday the King attended the Marly residence. On Sunday the Duke Regent in the most unusual manner [?], such as one had not seen him do in a long time. That day the Bishop of Saint Brieux was consecrated in the Novitiate of the Dominicans, in which function the Bishops of St. Malo and Nantes were placed as assistants. One sees that these appointments are proceeding, which on the 2nd were carried to all the Churches at the Bastille. Much is spoken of a certain [?] affair on the Fleche. The arrangement is being considered daily, and it is said that in place of the [?] Brief and of Coulance, [?] will be introduced; some even maintain that by the order of the Bishop the great [?] has been set up by the Lords of Paris. Barracks are being set up in the Hôtel of Soiltious. For some days now the usual customs have not been observed, as in small money dealings, which has been falling short for some time and is still being felt, both in this city and in Rheims, from where these days loaded wagons have arrived. The Declaration concerning the further extension of the accepted Goods, made since last year, has certainly been taken up by Parliament. The Report on the matter of the Duke of la Force, which was begun on the 1st and continued on the 7th, agreed, and on the 9th, has not yet been concluded; all the hearings are undertaken in full session; it is assured that this Duke has since Monday returned home again, and the prohibition concerning the removal of Parliament has been shown to him; it is believed that if the Condemnation occurs, it will not contain a Degradation, which at least will amount to a Fine, or to a Warning to be determined, with Confiscation of the aforementioned Goods; it is said that the Counselors will present themselves again at these sessions, but everything is on order from the Court; one has discovered many other Warehouses at Loopweien, but Parliament has forbidden the search. The matters of the Jesuits Orient, Bernard, and du Tait are also in this heat; one sees still daily new Memorials and Factors in this case appearing; but few have been well concluded. One is concerned that the harvest will not be successful in Orleans, as the soil has been damaged by abundant rain, and as the foliage of the vineyard was harmed by the overgrowth of the vines; the grains that were otherwise thriving have been laid on the ground. The withdrawal from [?] has been considerable; at Toulon many Officers of the Navy were helped for the Small Sickness. One has heard little or nothing of Cartouche for some days.

The Netherlands

From the town of ANTOING, approximately one mile from Doornik, 15 July. The Prince of Ligne, who some time ago had his case against the Princess de Epoines pending, arrived here yesterday morning with his wife, the same brother, and other ladies of quality, in a carriage, to take possession of his new properties: he was received at the entrance of the town by 100 to 150 persons, and was very surprised to see himself so well received. Crowned with flowers, alongside the most delicate wine that is cultivated in this region, which his Highness drank with much pleasure; subsequently one proceeded to the Castle, where all the artillery was displayed, and thereafter to the Church, where the mass was sung in music and the Te Deum; his Highness and the Princesses walked in the choir, sitting under a canopy which was prepared for them and the Princesses, where the Prince made a speech to maintain the people in their same laws and privileges; after the ceremony was completed, their Highnesses proceeded with their retinue to the Castle, where a magnificent meal had been prepared for them, which was followed in the evening by a ball that lasted late into the night; and one found the noble families of Doornik present; during this visit his Highness distributed money from the coffers of the Fief among the community; however, the canons of the chapter, seeing the aforementioned residents departing from their Highnesses, made their concerns known to them most strongly, and decided on every possible occasion to show their allegiance; whereupon their Highnesses

The Director Cayus addresses [that the completion of the lottery of the Land under the supervision of the third running month of July must be brought about, and that with the [?]

On the evening at 4 o'clock, being Wednesday 16 July 1721, the brokers Linquvet at Amsterdam on the Singel in the Castle will sell a considerable party of salvaged ship the Fortuyn, Captain Jan Ficterie, consisting of approximately 11,000 pieces of shoe-material and white stock, 1 well-preserved [?], 1,000 and small[?], 2,000 sangliers, 1,500 spices, Bo 242 [?] are still to be seen, as mentioned in the notice, which by the said Brokers [?]

Johannis Staats, Cornelis van den Heuvet, Jan Lemmers, and Hendrik Linguer [?] at 6 o'clock at Amsterdam, in the Nes in the Brakke Grond, will sell a party of 200 glasses, 1 party of lead, a party of gray Balans leather for gloves, and various other goods.

Jan de Roo and Jan vander Streng, brokers at Amsterdam, present for sale a small estate with large rooms, stalls for horses, coach-houses and [?] at Haarlem over the cleaver lane; the said estate being provided with fine gardens, glasshouse and water system, and [?], situated in the vicinity of the place, orchards with the fine meadowland adjacent thereto, with a new farm house, stalls [?] on the lane, where information is to be obtained at the estate, or by [?]

Amsterdam

AMSTERDAM, 15 July. This morning letters were received from Texel reporting that yesterday there arrived from Greenland the Commander Cornelis Pieterzn with his whale ship, and from the Davis Strait the Commander Gerrit Meyer, also with fish and other goods from the East; it is said that among the 12½ whale ships, 11 from 2 [?] ships [are reported].

The Hague

THE HAGUE, 13 July. The Lords States of Holland and West-Friesland will commence their ordinary meeting tomorrow, and several members from the various cities have already arrived at their lodgings. The 2nd Princess of Fürstenthal was here yesterday morning with Mr. Roele, at least presiding [?] the presentation of [?], at the meeting of the Lords States General. That morning the [?] Minister, Mr. Munier from the King of Prussia, was [?] with Lords [?] of the States General in conference. The Barons Goodenau and Ulied, ministers of the Electoral Prince of Cologne and the Palatinate, have also come here to Amsterdam, and are expected back tomorrow. Today or tomorrow the Lords van Weele and vander Wyen would be expected back from their committee [regarding] the farming of the tithe along the Meuse.

[By] the brokers F. Rakei, D. Rakei, Abraham van Doreflaer, and Hendr. F. [they will conduct a party of linen and other merchandise, originating from approximately 15,000 [?] schol [?], 1,600 tons and [?], 3,150 small [?], 3,900 common [?], 600 [?], 700 [?] and 400 [?] the seal-books, [?], 2 wide kegs, and other goods, the details of which may be obtained from the said brokers.

Noordwijk-Binnen will sell such an estate with all kinds of household furnishings, kitchen articles, beds with their appurtenances, [?], tea and all that belongs to [?] and Hendrik Linqueen, brokers, shall on Wednesday 21 July 1721 in the evening, some packages of [?] yarns and linens, likewise beaver-wool, and [?], present for sale a very magnificent estate, with its fine [?] and workman's dwelling, named the Cloister, situated outside [?] with three fine alleys and [?] of Lieutenant [?], and with [?] orchards with the fine [?] of fruit trees, kitchen garden and [?] much [?] the estate, consisting of approximately 2 morgens; besides still containing 14 morgens of [?], or by the said brokers.

Notices & Advertisements

On Thursday 19 July 1721, one will sell at the estate of Noordwyk-Binnen furniture consisting of jewelry, linens, cabinets, bedsteads, beds and other household goods.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 16th of july, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 86.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 18th of July, 1721
Italy

APPELS, 24 June. On Thursday, the Feast of the Sacrament, the Viceroy attended the Procession of the Venerable [Sacrament], holding a burning torch in his hand; when His Excellency came to this function before the prison of St. Jacob, the prisoners sang out. Upon this, His Excellency ordered that they be all immediately set at liberty; and when His Excellency was moved by the multitude of alms, which His Excellency distributed that day to the deacons, a very great exceptional discourse was made in praise of the Viceroy. The Prince and Princess of Avellino have returned here from Rome. The Duke of Cerenza is in Venice, and intends to travel onward to Vienna.

A grand reception was given in the evening to the Nuncio and his wife, whom His Eminence received with all tenderness and particular regard. Cardinal de Rohan, who had his Letters of Credence to present, delivered them in a private audience to the Pope in the capacity of Minister for the Court of France; the same morning Monsieur l'Affitceau, the former Minister of that Court, made ready to depart for Venice, and said that he would take his journey via Venice. That day the Pope gave an audience to Cardinals Cuzani and Czachi. On Sunday, Cardinal Corfini [received] Cardinals Piazza and [?] after St. Peter's, and Cardinal Ottoboni held the evening meal with the said His Eminence Corfini, [which was] a gesture of regard for this Cardinal, [it being said] that 200 courses were served, as one might say, at table in the said Ludovisi Palace. The same afternoon the Nuncio with his wife, and their son, visited Inspector [?] at [?], being introduced through [?]; His Holiness gave the said Nuncio an assignment of 8,000 Scudi, and to the child a piece of wood from the Holy Cross, [which was] set in gold and adorned with diamonds and very fine [jewels], valued at some Scudi. On Monday Cardinal Czachi departed for Vienna; it is reported that the carriage of His Eminence overturned twice, but that he recovered himself safely. On Tuesday, the Feast of the Birth of St. John the Baptist, the Sacred College held office in the Chapel of the principal Church of St. John Lateran, where Card. Buffi sang the Mass. On Wednesday the Pope [attended] this Chapel; no political [event] has taken place from this principal Church, which His Holiness the Father will do on 22 September, being the feast day of the Archangel Michael. This afternoon the Card. Famili as Arch-Priest of that Church, together with 19 other Cardinals, [was particularly honored]. Wednesday evening Cardinal del Giudice returned from Frascati, whither he had gone to take possession of that Bishopric, and was summoned by the Pope to attend the Congregation of the Holy Office, which was held on Thursday, being the first [meeting] after the appearance of His Holiness [for] the Justification [of the] year, at which occasion the said [person] took the oath in the hands of the said Cardinal del Giudice as Prefect of that Congregation, in the presence of the deputed Cardinals. After this Congregation, the said Cardinal del Giudice went to his home [?], to which some Dispatches were sent, which the Cardinal of Altban had received with a courier from Vienna; and [it is] reported that this courier brought the Emperor's consent to the journey of the Princess Borghese to Naples. Cardinal Cornaro, who is to depart for his Bishopric of Padua, had his farewell audience with the Pope, and the Ambassador of Venice had a public audience. It is assured that Monsieur Doria with the Nuncio in France is to be appointed, and that the present Nuncio here will take on the role of [?] in his place. The Cardinals d'Acunha and Pereira left the Palace of the [?] and Ambassador; the latter has rented the Palace of the Cardinal of [?], and the other that of Altieri: the first of these three Cardinals had a private audience with the Pope; but because the said audience with the Cardinal lasted a long time, it was held that matters of importance were discussed. Cardinal Scotti sent to Card. Conti two baskets full of fruit, and in the middle of each was a silver vase with many [flowers], which were valued at 300 Scudi. The Pope has sent to some Card. as a gift a [?], fine [?], which His Eminence's [servant] went to show the Pope's brother, but Innocentius XIII, seeing this, kept them for himself, saying that such things were not suitable for a Cardinal, but for the Pope. Cardinal Conti sent to His Eminence Onibaldi the head of a fish called spada, upon which the latter found a red pearl very valuable; a jewel stone, which to this first gift was given. The Bailiff Spinola has been appointed by the Grand Master of Malta as Envoy Ambassador to this Court. Cardinal Alberoni has spent time in the Vatican with the sister of Apollonia, Sister of the Pope, with which the Card. has had a long conference: it is also spoken [of], that His Eminence ... [text garbled in original] ... it is said that Don Murzo Chigii, since his marriage with the daughter of the Marquis of Paganica has been completed, preparations are made for her to go [?], to take the child of the Order without [?], by the diligence of the Pope.

GENOA, 27 June. The English ships that departed from here, which were stationed at Livorno, were seen Wednesday to cross near this harbor, which greatly troubled the merchants. The ship of Captain de Corro, which had stationed itself at Porto Specie, did not arrive successfully. Monsieur de Chavigny is in his [?]; here [?] had arrived.

Milan

MILAN, 2 July. It is reported that the Princess of Parma prepares herself to depart to join the Prince her husband.

Venice

VENICE, 3 July. On Sunday the Jubilee Feast was proclaimed here. On Tuesday all the clergy, which [gathered] in the Church of St. [?], and the other churches have been visited; also the days and times have been determined in which other parishes will hold their Processions. On Wednesday the churches [continued their] occupations. From Padua it is written that the Benedictine Monks there [celebrated for] days this promotion to the Cardinalcy of the Cardinalate of Cardinal Conti, because His Eminence is of their Order, and moreover was appointed by the Pope as Protector of their Congregation of Mount Cassin; on which occasion the marquesses in that state were honored, and the said monks had distributed among the Capuchins and others many gifts of love and alms. With letters from Ragali and Durazzo confirmation [has come] that the new Pasha of Egypt has had the head of his predecessor cut off in Constantinople; [which] head was displayed before the Sultan's seraglio for three days as a trophy; the Pasha of Cairo was also beheaded, and the Capt. Pasha prepared himself to come to the Archipelago with some warships and galleys, but [we] do not know for what reason.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 2 July. It is assured that there came an Express with important dispatches from [?] to the Court, upon which in turn an Express was sent back to that place. It is being assured that not only the supply of weapons has been settled, but that even the Preliminaries have been commonly established, the [details of] which one will know with the first courier. The combined Fleet still lies near Cappel[?]; from where the British [?] Minister has returned again to this city. It is assured that the inhabitants of Finland and Estonia have received permission from the high Government here to send an embassy to the court of Great Russia, to advance their interests at the coming Peace.

Germany & Neighboring Kingdoms

REGENSBURG, 10 July. Since there is a recess in the Imperial Diet, news here is not abundant. The Imperial [delegate?] of Saxony-Zeitz with various Roman Ministers went again to Zeysland; the Palatine Minister, who is also of this party, has according to the order of his Court, before his departure, [dealt with] the complaints of the Roman subjects in the County of Ravensburg, to respond to those [complaints]; there it is assured that these would be the same [complaints] that were already made public some months ago and were favored by the King of Prussia. [Similar to] those of the County of [?], which well [?], but the longer [the process continues], the more [difficult?] they are found to be. It is understood that among the young Counts of Pappenheim, sons of the Imperial Marshal, such great disputes have arisen over hunting rights that they came to violence in an encounter and came to blows; which caused much commotion; but their friends endeavor to bring it to settlement, so as to prevent the [?] from interfering therein. Also [it is] learned that all the cloisters and ecclesiastics in Bavaria must pay to the Prince-Elector a certain sum of money yearly from their incomes; that in the rural districts also still an extraordinary tax will be levied; which some think will not be received very easily by the taxed subjects.

France

PARIS, 11 July. The Baron von Bitterfeld, in the capacity of His Excellency the Minister, inquired with the ministers here of the Queen of Great Britain, Spain, and those of the [French] Crown, whether between their high principals a separate treaty was closed, or what [?]; all these ministers declared that they have at the very least no knowledge [of this]; wherefore one can be assured that as soon as the promised renunciation regarding the Emperor, on the lands which the King of Spain at present possesses (of which the original deed is to be found with the Imperial resident in London) against [?] of the King of Spain, on those lands by His Imperial Majesty at present [?], shall be carried out, the said Emperor [?]; the Baron von Bitterfeld is to betake himself forthwith to [?], to appear together with the second Imperial plenipotentiary, the Count of Windischgratz, at the Congress there.

PARIS, 11 June. News has come that some days ago between Nantes and Vannes there was a hurricane; and so severe that no men there have seen or experienced its like; by its violence [?] hail [fell where] grains for 22 miles round were as if mown down; [many] grain sheaves were overturned; also it is assured that the rock in that region, which is more than two miles long, no 300 trees were left on their roots: many of the inhabitants there thought that at the end of this storm they felt a slight earthquake. All the poetic pieces, received for the prize which the Academy was to distribute on St. Louis's day, having been examined by the said Academy, there is to be distributed to Monsieur [?], Knight of St. Lazarus, author of the journey to the Mountain of Parnassus, who last year won the prize, now once more the same [reward], so that it shall be given to him on the said day. The aforesaid Bulliot, who [had previously made] such confident wagers, [that] from that day to the [?] of this month would be [?], has lost his wagers, because in this matter [?].

AMSTERDAM, 11 July. In the Davis Strait, 3 Dutch ships have been lost, namely Jan Limmertsz, Pieter de Jong, and Commander Blanke, along with another large ship that was severely damaged, having lost several ships, and 200 [?] severely damaged, with several ships lost and various other foreign vessels. It is said that 113 ships and the Davis Strait had 70 fleets captured; and 13 Hamburg vessels of 104 Greenland whalers consisting of 3 to 4 fleets; and when the count of ships was made, it was said that only some ships remained in the ice.

AMSTERDAM, 16 July. At Rome news had come via monks from the Indies, stating that [?] the ship N.S. of Captain Louis Gomes, with several items found aboard, in a Cape where various animals were found, arrived in February 1720 at Matatosio in Mozambique and [?]. At Rome there also arrived the 2 Postal [vessels]; it is supposed that daily the Cardinals of that Roman nation went; it was seen that daily through the Father, vessels were sent to the Benedictine order of Le Vlugt, with great ceremonial; it was also read that the Father of Bonneuf [?] and the approaching, and under this: the Seminary ships to Hetwands and the Willem; and under this: Jan Noels, Tymen Jndicu Cc.; also from Bordeaux Laurens Timmerman, Goert Keller, Ja Blom &c. At St. Domingue have arrived the ship [?], and the St. Jaa. Rochelle; the Verpuidde Haan, Pieter de Jong from here, and S. de Jaa. Adrianus Jansen from St. Domingue, the Verpuidde Haan, and [?] go is Boucauf Corn, Cloak, Jacob Hoopstad, and also from here to Bordeaux Mich. de R. &c. Marten Biuin, W. Alst, &c.; and the Flutte H. Knob; Tomis Klein &c. but the Maas [vessels] moving; from Stockholm Jan Douwels Klamer from Limmertsin. In the Vlier have also arrived 3 ships from the Davis Strait, which have recently arrived at the City well cleared Top with a Vis, Constelis Koning &c. a half V., Symon Vogt a i half V., Samuel Leidtng &c. a half V., and Eugene Teunstle Bakker &c. a Vis i 300 which is now expected from the Strait Davis fisheries, which greatly [?]; mentioned 3 ships in Leo with yet eight more expected, under which: Pieter Ruits &c., Kiass Zeeman &c., Douwe Jours a Vis., Jan Brandaris 3 V., He? 3 V. V., Coretel, Jacobs in quarters &c.; also Jacob Nisask, who near Staten Hook of the ships arrived here is fortunate. Regarding those ships, which had only favorable [conditions], are [?] still how much further situated: the [?] ceased this, when one with the riding situated; and not yet brought; and yet there 3 to 6 ships missing, which one hopes will be sent elsewhere. From Tydel 12 to 15 ships have gone to sea, and 3 there of the Lyde in against the whole Cordlands, &c. Lievers with Lands, and some towards Visorkly &c.

THE HAGUE, 17 July. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West-Friesland have gathered today and continue to gather. The Committees of the Levantine Trade assembled, and they are with His Ed. Great Mogul in [...] the [...] gathered, Caliph might via the Pins Kaiakyn, Ambassador and Plenipotentiary of the Czar of Great Russia, in Conference with the Gentlemen Delegates of the States General. Tuesday the General Count Hompfsdt (who is occupied taking leave, because his Government at 's-Hertogenbosch is departing) was in Conference together with the Council of States. That or the Prince and Princess of Daleytfr had not reported the remarkable, their eyes dressing continue further arrangements yet 3 yearly coming of Princes to the same, as also to Amsterdam traveled. The Spanish Ambassador the Marquis de Monsilcone is with some of the Government, as also the Swedish Minister from here Tyk. The Italian Condeaux, and Ulsci, Ministers of the Curia of Cologne, and the Papal [?], are arrived from Amsterdam.

The national negotiations are from the 7th to the 8th Cialie, before that with the drawing of the old Cialie usable the 4 August thereafter

CADIZ, 21 July. The Squadron of the Americas N.S. de Assumption, mat one departed; the same being in the del Bomilla S. Antonio and Grace, Capt. Bartolomeus Piare Conndla of 500 [?] N.D. St. Francisco Xavier, Guerrero and N.D. de Guurdia, Capt. Jaac and Ousen N.D. de los Mille de la Solirtude St. Antonio, y la Chavuania; St. F. and St. Apo Jen one gave general order and one or 2 Warships, ne vao the command of mentioned Galleons, Oil, Flour, and other Goods Store were in the learned more los would amount, the age[?] of Beroeneiden. Then itde is the East Departed, to also go to Sommelrijck, which beyond the 'k buyer to buy.

PARIS, 14 July. After that the gen, and Pans the forward wee and 'T Fressen of the hedges 's morning tomorrow returned from o dureu s Bardon of la Force was gloom and fish [?] unk begje to want to depart, the hod hot's wiegii, palla by of 'zoo artillery mocies weigh, enduring t-enjoyed blood dead deu come and [?] toned by opening mishe san het Net em sust syn by [?] accommodated to Wan on la vretoch; 2 one of the xangochoirdi. Orionr, diz zyp geb [?] of the ministers, and with stemmer in to negotiate with Berund, and da Paix, em lutto livren an vende iii godien Alicelt that hy zich in bet future gen, and unbeshlugych componte and Waidenburg in tair waap ne syn related wershand, and all k ovedaau of a Subdirat cris de ion kn Pryslem quyluaten without dead deel voore de kapi Beneobilan in no more to bet randemche or a ticlle catr ene de Krueleristein, and in allis ka hel recduchia, al het mecuiet too dar bel dispurir buleaur of Bakkes, die ydet bewercen all wr de dee is gegegen and die gall rouaete, says de Nouuerrre vo La Resce degradieren, and gelotee Vollkosueri, and tildes Bovarim meeden der unp Luurtans Scr a Suitant [...]

Course of Exchange

**EXCHANGE RATES** The course of the EXCHANGE is as follows: Madrid 90 and 1/4. Cadiz one and a half, Seville 114 and 1/4, Bilbao 87 and a half. Lisbon 44 and 2/4 a 2 agile. Venice 53 and a half. Livorno 91. Genoa 51 and a half. Fary gapeli 29 and 1/4, London 4 Feb. 2 gr. Danzig 290 Polish gr. Breslau [?] and 7 agile St. Hamburg 32 and 7 a-Riende st. Antwerp 2 a 2 and 2/4 2 gt. Gent. Gent 1 bricde 1. Excheld 2 and 7 agile. Aorneuiden 2 seffende. The Agio of the Bank 2 and 1 half a 2 seffende. The shares of the O.I.C. are 772 n 75, W.I.C. 106, Subscription 980, all teffands. The English Funds 110, O.I.C. Too, Subscription 980, all teffands.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 18th of july, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 87.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 21st of July, 1721
Portugal

LISBON, 17 June. On the 12th of this month, the Dutch Fleet, under Commodore Wallengheepen, departed from here. Lord van Sommelsdyk is expected to arrive on this River to take to sea in order to cruise against the Barbary Corsairs. It is said that there are many people involved in the slave trade there.

GIBRALTAR, 21 June. In this Bay there lie 4 Warships, among them those arriving from Cadiz, together with 7 or 8 Merchant vessels, namely the Thomas, Camilla, Will. Mettman, the Marta, Captain John Blakwell, the King William, Richard Hendly, &c. Many vessels arriving here, having reportedly been plundered by the Sallee Corsairs and brought to Islee; however, after the King's command, the people were set free, and an order was sent to Sallee to the captains of the 3 pirate ships, so that when they arrived there, they would be held, so that through the English Minister, who is stationed at Mequinez, satisfaction could be obtained.

MALAGA, 23 June. Vice-Admiral Lord van Sommelsdyk has arrived with several ships of the Dutch Squadron at this Roadstead and has been most lavishly entertained to dinner by the Governor, together with the Captains of the Warships; the aforesaid Lord sent word to the Lord at Cadiz.

CADIZ, 21 June. The Galleons departed on the 21st of this month, under the Command of Don Amada de Cuebas y Fardo, commanding the Ship N. D. de Amparo, with fair wind from here toward Cartagena; the same accompanied the following ships, namely the N. D. de Grace, N. de St. Hilario y St. Antonio, Captain Piedro de Cordura; l'Angel de N. D., Captain Bartholomew Pinto de Ribera, el St. Juan Bautista, Captain Cornelis de Kos; N. D. de Vergania, Captain Joseph Mattines de Rivas; St. Francisco Xavier, Guillermo Rice; St. Christopher, St. Roman, St. Dominic, N. D. Juan de Rooys y Maria Joseph, Captain Andres Olivier; N. D. de los Milagros, Captain Alonso Yribarena; N. D. de la Soledad de Amporio, N. D. de Santiago de Purgatorio, Captain Galpat de Chavaria; St. F. and St. Apollonia, &c. &c., being in all 13 Sails; with the same, 2 Advice-Ships sailed toward Vera Cruz, and after the Warships, namely a Tartane, which shall escort them from the Canaries; the cargo was reported to be splendidly outfitted; after this procession came a Brigade of Dragoons of the Regiment of Orleans, and behind them 16 Knights of the Ambassador, under Two Admirals on Foot, who were followed by his Pages and Officers on Horseback; Then came the King's Carriages, the morning [reception by the] Ambassador, the Prince of Lambesc, the Ambassador's Son, the Lord Secretary [and] Introducer, and the Polk; the March was closed from the Palace of the Ambassador to the Port Neuf, where some Squads of the Guard were on Foot; the Guard on Horseback was stationed at the Palace of Conty and the College of the Four Nations; the French and Swiss Guards were arranged in 2 lines from Port Royal, Musketeers and a Detachment were posted, who at the Palace of the Palais went each into the Apartment of the Duke of Bourbon, where all manner of Refreshments were offered to him; at board he used nothing, on account of his Ramadan; after which he went to the Gallery, with the same Ceremony as his second Audience; after he had performed his Prostration, the King's Letter to the Grand Lord was given to him, which the young Celebi kept in custody, in a Chest made expressly for this purpose, and conveyed in the same Chests as he had arrived, except that the Prince of Lambesc did not accompany him further. This Ambassador has a singular place in our Esteem, for which reason he is a considerable General of the Bank, who died on the 8th at Longsight. The Lord Balm, Counselor at the Châtelet, also passed away that day. The King has taken a fine Greyhound from the Duke of Aumale for his own use; he is called Grippe-Soleil for his remarkable agility, and it is said that he can catch a Rabbit in the course, whether it runs straight or makes many turns. [The persons to be instructed in Philosophy and Theology are] the Lord Dorieux, Superior of the College of Lisieux, and [another] in the Venetian Nobility; his Brother, the Marquis de Moncini, who possesses 60,000 livres annual income in Italy, and is esteemed in great repute there, has procured this for him. The Lord [?] has offered large sums of money to be admitted to this Position; he is protected by the Censors against anyone who will not offend him. Although the discontinuation...

France

PARIS, 14 July. After the Princes of the Blood, the House of Hanover, and those affected last week in Parliament have been occupied with the Trial against the Duke of la Force, they were again gathered on Saturday morning from 6 o'clock until one o'clock after midnight; the Duke of la Force, however, appeared before them; he seated himself with his 40 Nobles, whom he had ordered to defend him, and as his House was later summoned, which he had to await from 7 to 9 o'clock in a Chamber of the Court, during which time he was served with several Bottles of Wine and refreshments; when he was finally interrogated, he asked permission of the Court to return to his House, since he was in very poor health and his person was granted a respite; whereupon he departed, so that they did not hear his Sentence in person. [One], who had lent his Niece, is [?] and [?] from [?], and tell of his, [?], with order to negotiate all matters, and 100 livres fine; the Knight de Landais, Richard of the Force, and they transferred Livres in regard to the Duke of la Force; the Decree stipulates that he shall conduct himself more carefully in the future, and the [?] are conjured; as the Merchant ships are declared banished, and it has been ordered to sell them for the benefit of a [?] and the [?] of the Province and for the benefit of the Hospital, and this for the Apothecaries to pay the debts of the aforesaid [?]: the aforesaid one strives [?] to procure 2000 livres for [damage] and Interest [?] of the Apothecaries; and in all matters of Trial: [?]; and by this the third [part] of the Trial was concluded, such Memorials among the Complainants in [?], and the [?]; those of the [?] shall concern; all in the Parliamentary [?] [?] a narrow [?] some wished the Duke of [?] approached, that nine Fleurstones at that [?] would have spoken for welcome, but these [?] had before the Decree [?] as now is. That same day the Turkish Ambassador held [?] of Aldelet by the King, which was already [?] by the Lord Sainbot, Introducer of the Ambassadors, and the [?] of Lamberti; then the Company of the [?] of the Police of the [?] began, his is [?] this [?] Authority, which all [?] and

Great Britain

LONDON, 15 July. It is assured that in the last held Cabinet Council in the presence of the King it was deliberated to take further precautions along the Sea-Coasts of these Kingdoms, and no News or Goods from suspected or similar Places should be permitted to come ashore. Those of the Lower House, which met on Saturday in a great Committee over the public Credit, resolved that all those Committees of H.M. or Subscriptions, as well as those of any other Company or Bubble, which shall not be regulated before Michaelmas next, and registered in the Books of the Company before the first of November, shall be Null; that no Mortgages shall be made to any Stock that is brought with a Contract, nor that any Execution shall follow upon any obtained or to be obtained Sentence on such Stocks which shall be brought with Contract; they further ordered that the Bill to relieve the unequal Losers in the South Sea Company be set aside, as some changes had been made therein, and the Clause to impose a fine of 150,000 pounds Sterling on the former Directors of the South Sea Company had been rejected. Today those of the Lower House have deliberated the Bill to regulate the Army Debts and to bring in a Bill to impose strict Penalties on Smuggling, in order by that means to prevent the smuggling of [prohibited goods]. Subsequently they read a Message of the King, by which it is made known to the House that Nathanael Wilkinson has been seized, whereupon it was resolved to request the King by Address that he be further kept in custody of the Warden. The aforesaid Wilkinson has been brought before the Committee for the examination of the Excisemen, Printers and Publishers of seditious and scandalous Libels, but he has refused to answer to all the aforesaid questions. Yesterday, Walter Kennedy and James Bradshaw were tried at the Old Bailey; the first is accused of having plundered a Ship of Captain Lepos, in this City, and of having attacked the Ship of Captain Grant, wounded him mortally, and subsequently neglected to secure the ship; the other was accused of having been aboard the Ship the Keith Keltor and of having drawn his share of the booty; the Jury declared them both [...] guilty, and they received Sentence to be hanged. The Governor General has written in this case, that the [pirates] in the West Indies are strictly punished by England, and under the pretext of sending Arms to Guinea, these were [?] offered to private persons, whereby they are enabled to—

[Text breaks off]

Germany

[The text concerning Germany is heavily corrupted in the original transcription and cannot be reliably rendered into coherent English. It appears to contain references to the Elector Palatine and various diplomatic and commercial matters, but the OCR transcription is too damaged to translate accurately.]

The Netherlands

CAMBRAI, 14 July. Yesterday the Marquis de Bretagne, Plenipotentiary of the King of Spain, received the Gentlemen Morville, Count St. Etienne, the Envoy from Parma, and Mister Vincentini, Minister of the Grand Ducal State, [text unclear]. Count St. Etienne has received an order from Madrid, but nothing regarding its contents has leaked out. The house of the Imperial Plenipotentiary, the Count of Windslaertz, [?] and his Excellency's furnishings are being sought for the approaching month. Orders have been given to purchase a large quantity of wood in the forest of Moded for the kitchen, as well as several thousand bundles of firewood. Work is proceeding vigorously to prepare the lodgings for the Portuguese Minister, Don Louis d'Ancumba, as he, together with other Ministers, is expected here by the end of this month. Orders have been issued to prepare the quarters for the Portuguese Minister, the Count of Talavera, and work will begin next Monday with one hundred laborers. The Marquis de Bretagne, Morville, and Count St. Etienne took a carriage with six horses for an excursion outside the city. The funeral rites were held in this city for the Lady Consort, mother of the Spanish Plenipotentiary, [text unclear]; the Ministers currently present here attended the ceremonies. The contractors for the supply of wood continue to gather a large quantity in this city, as it is expected that Congress will certainly open next month.

TOURNAI, 13 July. The Prince of Tingit, Governor of the city of Valenciennes, dined yesterday with Colonel Dais, Commander of the Citadel of the aforementioned city, and departed the same afternoon with the Princess Dauphine.

AMSTERDAM, 15 July. In the city of Davits [?] there still remained new boatloads, and according to reports seen here, approximately fifty ships would have captured there. The two ships that departed from Rotterdam for the aforementioned place in late February arrived at Staten Hook on the twenty-third of April, at which time most of the crew had nearly frozen from the severe cold. Two galleons have returned from Greenland; the reports of their catch are not yet public. They departed from the ice on the twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth of July, when the catch was showing marked improvement. With about two hundred men manning the ice-gate, the ships that were there from July 15th to July 21st captured approximately two hundred fifty more whales than before. Among the ships whose catch had improved were the Pieter Schol with twelve whales, the Oude Sprang with seven or eight, the Linde and the Bonte Walvis with five each, the Ronde Kalf and the Groene Hengst with two each, and several others in proportion. However, the ship Groene Hengst, under Pieter Martens, appears to have been lost there.

[DONIN], 6 or 17 July. The following ships have arrived off this city: the Th. John Watson, the Thomas and Mary under John Sharpe, the Providence under David Callap, the John and Marguerite under Bryan Miller, the Endeavours under John Hawkins, and the Himself under Colbert Raljon, all from Sunderland; [text continues but becomes corrupted].

Petersburg, 27 June

Tomorrow Her Czarist Majesty is expected to be seen here; the Czar will come to Kronlot [Kronstadt] for the water festivities, and the Duke of Holstein is expected here in two or three days, his Minister Herr Stampke having already arrived. Great news was received regarding negotiations in the New City. Regarding the known marriage of the Duke of Holstein, there is still no complete quiet here; indeed, one wishes to believe that the aforementioned Duke intends to return from Riga, unless His Czarist Majesty did not strongly compel him to visit Revel, Knobolsett [?], and these places.

Vienna, 9 July

After the Emperor and Empress yesterday went one hour beyond the castle of Schönborn to meet it, and their Imperial Majesties arrived at the said castle, where they held the midday meal and pursued the customary hunt, their Imperial Majesties subsequently returned to this city around eight o'clock in the evening with several post-chaises, which caused unbounded joy here. The Empress visited herself sixteen times the visit of the Infanta, her daughter; the youngest was already separated from her mother, but the youngest is here detained and will be trained after her mother's hand. Yesterday and today galas were held at court.

Hanover, 19 July

[Text corrupted - appears to concern an auction of Jewish goods but cannot be reliably translated.]

London, 15 July

[The London section is severely corrupted in the original transcription and cannot be reliably rendered.]

---

Local Notices & Advertisements

It is notified that on Tuesday, the 24th July 1721, at the residence of [?] at 9 o'clock in the morning and 2 o'clock in the afternoon, there will be a public sale of furniture and other goods.

The brokers Jan Hoos, Jan Redermaker, Daniel d'Azevedo, Willem van Laren, Jan Hotteyn the Younger, Corn. Hoos, and Raphael d'Azevedo will, on Tuesday, the 29th July 1721, at 6 o'clock in the evening, in the Dijkstraat in the Old Burg, sell a party of extra golden new Valencia tobacco lying on the Herengracht [?].

It is advertised that the cargo of the Danish East India Company return ship the Sulanna, arriving from Tranquebar at Copenhagen, will be sold on the 10th July 1721; the cargo of goods in the return ship the Sulema being from the Gold Coast.

21 July 1721. With Privilege.

Notices & Advertisements

On Sunday, the 13th of this month, in the evening at eleven o'clock, in the city of Leiden, a certain Figueur Sanjer, traveling toward Schiedam, was murdered [?]. Justice demands that the laws be rigorously enforced as an example to others. [Editor's note: The remainder of this notice concerns legal proceedings and includes a reward notice.]

On Tuesday, the 26th July 1721, at noon, there will be sold at Edam two fresh cargoes of the best quality, copper-worked wooden beams of various lengths from 17 to 20 feet; 900 pieces of timber, firs, and spruces recently arrived by sea, consisting of 2,300 green planks of 10 feet; 2,300 green beams of 11 feet; 1,300 oak pieces of various dimensions, timbers, and [text corrupts].

On Wednesday, the 17th of August of this year 1721, at 3 o'clock in the afternoon precisely, at the house of Hugo van Aken, [residence description], there will be offered publicly for auction and sale an extraordinarily beautiful and well-situated dwelling with thirty morgans, two hoofs [?], and 54 rods of good meadow land (approximately 500 hay-loads of meadow and arable land, lying at the high end of the long meadow), under the jurisdiction of the Noble and Mighty Lords States of the Land of Utrecht.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 21st of july, 1721. — With Privilege.

No. 1721.No. 88.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 23rd of July, 1721
Germany & the Adjacent Kingdoms

VIENNA, 9 July. The Fetlyn, at which the Imperial Vice-Chancellor entertained His Imperial Majesties, was of exceptional magnificence. Yesterday evening Their Imperial Majesties returned to the Favorita amid great rejoicing of the inhabitants of this City. Work is underway on the Emperor's orders to compose an Italian Opera, which will be performed at the Favorita in a certain month of August on the occasion of Her Imperial Majesty's birthday, who will reach her thirtieth year there. The Emperor and the Kingdom have differences over the honors which those of the Kingdom of Bohemia showed to Her Imperial Majesty during her residence in the Castle. It is rumored that the Emperor may elevate some of the principal nobility of that Kingdom to the rank of Count. The Empress distributed gifts to the principal ladies before her departure from there. The courier for the Imperial Minister Baron von Bentheim from Paris has not yet been sent, having returned with certain dispatches. The reform that occurred among the Imperial Troops is considered complete. The Count von Vilmont has betaken himself to Clauselstein in Sevenberg, where he on Saturday in the Emperor's name, as King of Hungary, held court.

LEIPZIG, 9 July. According to letters from the 8th instant from Wallachia, an express arrived there from Caminiek with news that the Pasha of Chotzim had been relieved from his post as supreme marshal of the Crown, the Porte informing him that having learned that he had caused several officers to be punished because they tolerated disorders on the borders of Podolia.

REGENSBURG, 14 July. The evening before last the Cardinal of Saxe-Zeitz arrived with the Ministers of Bohemia, the Palatinate, and Munster, returning late from Swadorf, having brought down 20 boat-loads of timber from the Rhine, of which various quantities were distributed to the Ministers present here; His Eminence proceeded at once to the Monastery of Bay, and found ourselves outside this City, in the direct jurisdiction, whither his Guards have followed him this day, with the intention of 200 [?] the Imperial Diet has been interrupted, and there suspended, and the Ministers are to confer over the conditions: His Grace has declared to the Protestant Monastery that His Imperial Majesty had delegated his representative and the control of the Palatinate to one of his trusted Ministers because of the inadequate redirection of the Graves, and the same result of that which was entrusted to him. The Graves of the County of Ravensberg are being sold since yesterday, they are wide-ranging, but are being examined and checked; the amendment regarding the return of the Bill to the first reading [?] [remainder of this section is severely corrupted in the original transcription and cannot be reliably translated]

Great Britain

LONDON, 18 July. Wednesday the Controller of the King's Household informed the House of Commons that the King, following the advice of the House, had ordered that Nathaniel Wilkinson be placed in the hands of the Sergeant-at-Arms; the Lord Hinchinbrooke, appointed on behalf of the Committee established to investigate the affairs, treaties, and expenditures [Editor's note: referring to the South Sea Bubble scandal], gave the House notice that the aforementioned Nathanael Wilkinson was brought before the Committee and had held himself justified, whereupon the House ordered that the said Wilkinson be taken to Newgate Prison, kept closely confined, and that pen, ink, and paper be denied him. [Mr. Parker reported on the Resolution, which was taken on Saturday regarding the Contracts (mentioned in our previous issue); the same was approved by the House, and it was remarked in this strange matter that the said Resolution should not extend further than to such Contracts as were made before the end of December 1720, and that thereafter on December 1720 the House subsequently resolved to present an Address to the King, to beseech His Majesty to request the state of public credit] [remainder corrupted] as well as those concerning the damage of 2 millions to the S.S. Company; the monies lent on advice, and the contracts for advice, etc.) whereupon a Committee was appointed to draw up that Address [section breaks off]

---

Wednesday'S Courant

A George Caswell and his associates were convicted in court. Yesterday those of the Lower House held the third reading of the Bill for appointing Commissioners to regulate the logwood debts, it passed, and they sent it to the Lords, who held the first reading thereof, and will hold the second on Tuesday next; at which time also various reports concerning punishments for Foreign Troops will be laid before their Lordships. Subsequently the Commons set the first reading of the Bill for relief of the unfortunate sufferers in the S.S. Company for today; in the afternoon about two o'clock the House waited upon the King with its newly closed Address. Today the Lords heard the advocates from the City, as well as those of the merchants of Virginia and other traders, against the clause in the Bill regarding bankruptcies, to wit against the clause that no distribution of effects left behind shall be made to private individuals on account of any bankruptcy, until the Crown shall have been fully satisfied; which was asserted will cause great difficulties in trade and the unwarranted credit that is given by merchants; upon which the said clause was unanimously rejected, but their Lordships have [section incomplete]. The Lower House has passed the Bill for relief of the unfortunate sufferers in the S.S. Company, and they had previously rejected a proposal by Mr. Hungerford to add £10,000 more to Sir John Fellowes from his surrendered goods, which proposal was strongly opposed by Lord Wm. Pawlet and Mr. Strickland. A clause was also proposed to be inserted by amendment to cause the said Directors to permit their movable goods to be taken in payment of costs, to the prices at which they are valued in the respective inventories; but this too was rejected; on the condition that the same were to be valued at their worth. The House has yet received no answer from the King on the Address presented to His Majesty yesterday; it is hoped that [?] on Tuesday last Mr. Theodore Bailby, advanced in years of 91, deceased; he served the Court under the reigns of Charles I, Charles II, and James II, in the latter office he was a Clerk, which pension was paid him by the Crown until his death. It is reported that the East India ship the Hastag from Cambridge has arrived at Plymouth, and that it [sailed?] in the Channel having parted from the ships the Cardoael and the Cadogan. Wednesday, pursuant to the sentence of the Old Bailey, the sea pirates were hanged at Tyburn, and Barbara Spencer was burned. The Bank advices stand at 130 and a half. E.I.C. and S.S. stock, the Million Bank 130, Lottery Annuities 98, Bank Annuities 96 and a quarter, ditto 4 percent E.I.C. 29 and a half, the Exchange Aff. 6 and agitated, ditto London 3 and agitated. J.B. 23.

France

PARIS, 18 July. During the night between the 13th and 14th instant a Ball was held in the Allées of the Court, which was organized by the Duke of Chartres; the rooms were hung with silks of the finest order and round lamps, which was very pleasant to behold; the entire ball lasted until morning; at this gathering only the principal nobility and persons of the highest station were present, and various Serenades were performed by the first musicians of this city. The Marquis de Rebenac, Karl, who held the Regiment of the King, son of the Marquis du Souray, grandson of the Marquis de Louvois, 21 years old, has drowned these days in the River Marne near Louvois, where he was bathing. Last Tuesday Mehernet Effendi, grand treasurer of the Ottoman Empire, Ambassador of the Grand Signior, held public audience with the Duke of Orleans, Regent of France, being introduced by the Sieur Mairpé, Introducer of Ambassadors to His Majesty, who had fetched him from the Palace of Ambassadors; after the audience the Ambassador was conducted back by the same Introducer, who sat in the carriage on the left side of His Excellency. It is thought that the said Turkish Ambassador will do the same this day with the Archbishop of Cambrai; and that His Excellency will shortly visit the King's Library. It is reported that the Reverend Joinin, Curé of St. Hilaire, who for some days has been dismissed from the deanship of the Sorbonne and in the cloister which has been granted him refuses to hand over the formula of the Society of the Sorbonne, under the pretext that he has been treated contrary to the laws, principles, and customs of the Faculty. One sees an Arrest of the King's Council of State of the 8th instant, containing an interpretation of that of the 10th June 1721, whereby in the Kingdom is forbidden the import, trade, wearing, and use of Indian silk from China and the Levant; as well as the dyed linen and other fabrics coming from the said lands.

PARIS, 19 July. A few days ago the Papal Nuncio went in the evening at eight o'clock to the Tuileries to the Marquis of Villeroy, where shortly afterwards also arrived the Sieur de Bezons, Archbishop of Rouen; and although one does not rightly know what happened in this conference, many are of the opinion that it may have concerned the present accommodation. As some have put it forward, the Duke of La Force is said to have seen, and [section becomes severely corrupted and unintelligible] from China and the Levant; as well as the dyed linens and other fabrics coming from the said lands. The said Duke has no children, but his brother the Marquis de Caumont has sons and daughters, and has these days made a marriage contract with the Count of Rours, who is his lord and [?].

Left Column

Many people are of the opinion that nowadays... [The transcription becomes increasingly illegible in this section. What follows is a partial translation of the more readable passages.]

Lt. General Reynolds of the Swiss [Editor's note: referring to the Swiss Guard], and Lt. Colonel of the Regiment of Heavy Guards, met with the Ambassador of the Ottoman Porte regarding [?]...

The wine is inexpensive here, as merchants have begun to bring stocks from [?], which promises a great wine harvest, though the supply will not be very abundant.

Mr. Bulliot, who was arrested five weeks ago with the title of Marquis de la Ferie, has been released concerning his case in the East; great commotion has been caused at the Châtelet; but his matter is now transferred to the Council.

The murders in this city and its approaches have been reduced. Last week, a dead body was found in the woods of Boulogne, which was exhumed by order and... [?]

The Netherlands

BRUSSELS, 21 July. According to advices from the East, the Governor, the Marquis del Campo, by order of the Court, was there to supervise the sale of merchant goods, with the ships that arrived, the day of sale having been announced in the gazette; such that there were complaints from many foreigners who had come for the sale, and claiming before the Magistrate of this region, it was decided to proceed with the sale; whereupon the Governor sent an officer and some grenadiers to secure the house, after which the sale was carried out; regarding which the Magistrate sent petitioners and a deputation of merchants to the Court, to request of the Marquis de Frie the consequences that might follow; and that if one absolutely wanted to stop this sale, foreigners would never come there again nor on the day fixed for the sale, because they reserved the fixing of the market day to themselves, without this being announced by any public notice: upon which His Excellency declared that the sale of the cargoes of these 2 ships, which still remained from the old Company there, would proceed.

Since on Sunday the 13th of this current month, in the evening at eleven o'clock, at the Court in Leiden, carrying the Pigmy Saunier, going to his [?], with illuminated bullets, apparently with murderous intent [?], under Ernest, and the Laws being rigorously applied, it is decreed that whoever can inform such that the same will be found, shall be rewarded with a sum of 100 Guilders.

The Director of the Lottery advertises that the drawing of the lots of the Land's third loan of the current month of July must be completed, and that with the next drawing a beginning shall be made.

It is hereby published that tomorrow, being Thursday, 24 July, a German auction of paintings from the Italian and other renowned masters: the catalog is with Mr. Nicholas Mulder, in the Turfstraat, in The Hague.

Since in the previous Courant it was made known that all those who have [claims] should have submitted their principals; so hereby with this notice those who are interested are addressed to the house of the Notary Gaspar Ypelaar, in order there to have their capital confirmed under proper receipt.

Monsieur de Soumuville, formerly Surgeon Major in a Regiment, resident of S. Antonyr, makes known that he makes and sells a well-made [cure] both from foreign and newly arrived subjects [?]...

Hermanus Voet, Bookseller at Leiden and on the Langstraat, the fourth house from the...Cheese before the Glass Beard, makes known that he makes a [?] as well made as those made by [?], for Men and Women; Children he takes to teach: and [?] from there, he has free apartments at his house...

Right Column

CONSTANTINOPLE, Captain Baffa with 12 ships, to attack the Turkish islands on the mainland: a Greek [?] of the Grand Seigneur [?] Sangreza to the Greek Nation a [?] golden [?] and [?] around in the [?] Monks of the Church of St. Helena over the unsafe Pope arrived, after which...

CADIZ, 22 January. The Ship the [?] and John, Capt. Francis [?] from Cape, [?] Robert Will.) on the 15th the S[?] on the 19th the St. Joseph, Joseph [?]; on the 20th Ste. Joseph, [?] the Hope, Barth. Borma; [?] the Anne, [?] there remained against English, and 11 Windmills to Amsterdam, and [?]

PETERSBURG, 14 June. From the great Admiral, and [?] the Galley [?] [?] of Holland, [?] which a [?] his Nobility and [?] Domestic became as well as regulated [?] of [?] first Courier awaits [?] a Count [?], is as [?] 1718, and Spain in advance, [?] distinguish [?] have Galley it [?]

NAPLES, 21 June. here in all the Churches proclamation was made. The 4 Galleys of the Duke Fabricius Holy, and of ours will join, are here [?] Germanice of the Duke [?] towards at her Husband [?] the great [?] going (con Rome arrived, and [?]

The Fourth, Genus 93 and 1 half. Paris sight 40 and 3 quarter. London 14 Sch. 3 gr. Danzig 250 Polish gr. Breslau 3; and 7 August St. Hamburg 13 and 2 seventeenth St. Antwerp 6 and 1 quarter and half gr.E. Ghent and 3 quarter. Brussels 2. Zeeland 1. Rotterdam 2 seventeenth. The Agio of the Bank and 1 half at 10. Annuity and 10 p.C. Cypn. 970, W.I.C. 101. Sublet. 970 to 75. The Eng. B. 130, O.I. C.145; Z.Z. 135.

THE HAGUE, 22 July. The States of Holland and West-Frisia have met again today and [?] it is said that His Illustrious Highness will have several [?] lightened this afternoon. This morning the Mr. Frans Minister of the King of Sweden [arrived], with His considerations from the States General in Conference. The Spanish Ambassador, the Marquis de Monteforte, is expected by several Lords of the Government; [?] also the British Minister, Mr. Dayrolles. An envoy of the British Court is being sent to Stockholm, and another [?] still under the Seals from London. N.B. The Lords States of Holland and West-Frisia this midday until further notice [?].

Thursday, 24 July 1721. At the City Hall of the late noble Lord, Lord [...], within practice, several very fine [?] shall be sold: the catalog is available at the Hospital of the Widow of the [?].

All those people who are indebted to the deceased Daniel Carbonel are hereby further advertised that all his creditors alike shall present themselves to Caspar Ypelaar, residing on the Hezerugraft over the Blazus House at Amsterdam, to receive their shares.

[?] has a fine horse, five years old, with a [?] one Guilder large, to Brittany there to travel, and good surety [?].

[?] copies on the Water, is printed and is published from Sweden, Louis the Re-turned also the Revolutions arrived, and difficulties, in the Kingdom, by the establishment of absolute Power in the Kingdom by Mr. de Limstiers, [?]...

[Sections become increasingly illegible and fragmentary]

NAPLES, 1 July. Here in all the Churches proclamation was made. The 4 Galleys [?] of the Duke Fabricius [?], [?] which [?] Germanice of the Duke [?] towards at her Husband.

ROME, 5 July. Saturday [?] in the Quirinal Chapel [?] was given, where the greatest Pope [?] in that Chapel [?] [?] Theaeburg College, with some indisposition cause in that he this week no audience with his Holiness, the Cardinal and [?] then for pain that he not could [?]: East Office of [?]...

The fireworks lit, [?] night [?] has the [?] given the 4 [?] Orders, such [?] will be entered, to the Nunziature [?] some will that [?] Pres[?], in which the world [?] were. Wednesday [?] Don [?] accepted as [?] in the Congregation of the [?] Cardinal, Michele [?]

---

Notices & Advertisements

On Monday night, a grand illumination was held at Court, with more than 3,000 carriages and over 3,000 torches, creating a splendid spectacle. Music of many voices and all manner of fine instruments were employed, which was arranged by the Prince of Soubise, with Mademoiselle [?] being particularly remarkable. On the eleventh of this month, the Jesuits at Clermont held a triumph...

It is made known that Jan Vlaek at Bodegraven has lost a black horse in the meadows [?]. At Amsterdam by the heirs of Waesberge, booksellers on the Water, is a new edition of Grat. Groll, where one sees the entire War of the North, as also the Revolutions which the War of Spain, & became Hereditary & the Establishment of the Power, Medals & Figures, 12., 6 vols.

At Amsterdam by H. met Hoorn, and at Middelburg by H. van Houkke, is printed the Sea-Chart and likewise how the Ship's Surgeon and the means prescribed to him, as well from his Medicinals as from Ship-supplies, by [?] of Konigsberg, City Surgeon at Middelburg in Zeeland, in 5.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 23rd of july, 1721. — With Privilege.
