Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 25th of July, 1721
Turkey

CONSTANTINOPLE, 13 May. Several days ago, Captain Raffa departed from here with 12 galleys toward the Archipelago and the islands, apparently to collect the annual tribute that is customarily paid there. A Greek priest, who was convicted of having removed the body of the Countess of Redi, has been hanged by order of the Grand Turk during this year in his priestly garments, notwithstanding that the Greek nation offered a considerable sum of money that he should be spared. People here are not enjoying good health. The monks of the Church of St. Mary of Pera have held funeral services for the murdered Pope [Editor's note: likely referring to ecclesiastical matters in Constantinople]. Comfort has immediately disappeared, from Ragsta [?], hastening to arrange their public interests shortly.

Spain

CADIZ, 23 June. On the first of this month, the ship Th. and John, Captain Th. Heuwlou, departed for St. Lucas; on the 17th, the English chronometer ship of Captain Arthur del Garno for the Levant; the Th. Robbert Will. on the 16th, the Samuel, Captain Newbon, for the Canaries; on the 19th, the St. Joseph, Joseph Durand, and Captain Juan Gazet for Bordeaux; on the 20th, Edmund King, the King George, Lazaro Oxman, and the Hope, Barth. Boreneer and Deems, all for the Canaries; on the 22nd, the Anna Cath., Fredrik Meyer, for Hamburg; [there remain] 200, and here still lie 11 English and 11 French ships, Jan Norman and F. Moerman for Antwerp; and Jeane Collen for Hamburg.

Russia

PETERSBURG, 30 June. The Czar arrived yesterday in the afternoon; the great admiral, the Fruitful Magebet, is returning there; the Czarina is expected tomorrow; and next week the Duke of Holstein, for whom fine lodgings are being prepared; his nobility and court officials have already arrived here, as well as Herr Herpten, his minister. Peace with Sweden is being maintained well; meanwhile, it is reported that the negotiations at Wygartzien are settled, so that the first courier is expected here with confirmation that all will be done and concluded. The young Count Golowitski has departed with [?] toward the court of Warsaw and Spain. The Commandant Gordon has returned to Kronslot with his galleys under his command.

Italy

NAPLES, 1 July. The previous Sunday, the Jubilee was proclaimed in all the churches here, and the general procession will take place tomorrow. The 4 galleys from Malta, under the command of the Knight Don Fabricius Ruffo, which were still securing the coast with our ships, have arrived here and are undergoing quarantine at sea in the harbor. Since it is now known that the Princess Germaniame of the Viceroy has issued permission to journey here to be reunited with her husband, this princess will be received here shortly. The Prime Physician Quoiano, who has arrived in Rome, is also being treated in this city. Sentence of death has been pronounced against some Spanish soldiers who, among the common people marching from Manfredonia toward this city, had risen against their officers and damaged some of their goods, which happened in a penal case. The Royal Counselor Salzedo has passed away, and Don Affitto, General Auditor of the Troops, lies dangerously ill. The Papal Nuncio and the Abbot of Mont Cassino have for some time past set off fireworks over the elevation of Lord Conti to the Purple [cardinalate]. The ban against purchasing goods from suspected pirates is not being as strictly enforced here as it was thought to be at sea.

Friday's Gazette.

All the portraits of the Popes were removed from the House of Conti, and beneath each portrait two Latin verses [appeared]; the gathering there was also considerable; there were 33 cardinals, a great many prelates, and the nobility in festive dress. That same day, Princess Borghese departed from this city with her household for Fiumicino to embark; the galleys that the Princess there awaited to carry her to the Chapel: this Princess was complimented through the Cardinal of Althan and the secretary. The list concerning the governors has been published; according to the same, the gentlemen Carolus is declared Apostolic Commissioner on the shores of the Adriatic Sea, Iultaani governor of Mercerata, Barni governor of Perugia, Ialdi governor of Viterbo, Anquilla governor of Tivone, Aragona governor of Civita Vecchia, Aquaviva governor of Ancona, Mathel governor of Fermo, Ercolaei governor of Spoleto, Ernofcio governor of Fano, Farmiami governor of Orvieto, Cefta governor of S. Severino, Gerineau governor of Sabina, Tempi Vice-Legate of Bologna, Telta Ponani of the Congregation of the Council, and Mr. Petroni Ponani of the Congregation of Good Government. Cardinal [?] took high political measures, and there was great hope; his Eminence gave a like homily upon the feast, in which his Eminence made a like gift to the monks. The Count Kinski awaited the Column in the telling of the Pope for his audience of the [?], which will be the first that he will have with his Holiness. The Cardinal of Schlorembach also regulated all his domestics, some here as in Naples, to depart for his Bridon of Olmidt. The Cardinal Cornaro has also departed to his bishopric of Brixen; many, knowing his merits and great capability, complain about the departure of his Eminence, who has also made known that he would have liked to remain here, for the lodge here is excellent. The Father Capuchin Bonaventura, who is a godly and wise man and has preached with much eloquence throughout Italy, has been appointed by the Pope as Apostolic Preacher. It is not rightly known where Cardinal Alberoni is staying; some say he is near this city, others that he is in a cloister in this city; but although everything concerning this Cardinal is suspect, it is nevertheless known from good authority that he will shortly be declared not only innocent but wholly innocent of everything; which one had never understood: the same cardinal has indeed dismissed his domestics, but one sees them daily walking about with cheerful countenance, without anyone treating them as coming into service. A mandate has been published concerning monetary species, regulating them, and some foreign coins are forbidden, as well as to forbid foreign bakeries.

ROME, 2 July. Saturday, the eve of the Feast of St. Peter, was held in the Quirinal Chapel to sing the first Vespers, where the entire Sacred College appeared, but the Pope did not attend the chapel until his Holiness was overcome by a violent fever, which indisposition caused the visiting missioners during this week to have no service with his Holiness. The entire night between Saturday and the following Sunday the Pope found himself in such pain that we feared [?] but the doctors and the nurses having prescribed the dross of olive oil of various [?], the Holy Father has vomited a very large stone, whereby the pain diminished and the cough much improved; thus his Holiness found himself so eased that on Tuesday he still kept to bed. Sunday, the Feast of St. Peter, the ordinary chapel was held in the Basilica, where all the cardinals appeared; fireworks were set off over the streets, and the entire city was illuminated, [with] the display of fireworks. The entire city was illuminated, [with] the display of fireworks. [?]

Several nights ago, Cardinal Paolosci gave to Mr. Pafoncel the 4 lower orders, with the 3 remaining to follow shortly; the Pope is also very devoted to and by revolution of Seed and living order to provide for the Nuncio in Switzerland; although some wish that this [?] go to the East; in which the Pope firmly believes as apostolic minister at cities. On Tuesday, Don Stephen Conti, the Pope's nephew, received the Society garments as an apostolic notary. On Thursday, the Pope, Bishop of Semerin, administered the oath to the Congregation of the Inquisition according to custom. Yesterday in the afternoon, the Count Conti further went in the Wallpollooe journey east in the Jesuit church of St. Ignatius; to the praise of the redeemed Pope and the worthy celebration of his elevation to the pontificate; this ceremony is a great joy like the original; besides the heartfelt militias, [?] the church was [?] in the [?].

LIVORNO, 4 July. According to some advices, there would be a battle between the confederates and the armies of Tunis and the Bey Jean Coggia, in which the latter mentioned would be the victor in the fight.

GENOA, 7 July. Since the two English ships continue to remain before this harbor, and all those from them load the ships, and with the concert that 2 others from Port Mahon come to join them, the Senate has given occasion to assemble over the matter of the nobleman Justiniani with the English merchants. An English ship from Cadiz arrived, having on board two pieces of eight for our merchants, [asking] above its freight, 4 per cent of the cargo, to favor the English, which they in this city might still have.

FLORENCE, 3 July. His Holiness Lazaro Palliererio, Archbishop of Thebes and Patriarchal Nuncio to this court, having arrived in this city Tuesday evening, the Grand Prince has sent a delegation of gentlemen and prelates to compliment him on his return, and sent him at the same time a gift of 16 fine silk scarves with pins. This Nuncio will make his public entry in the coming week.

PARMA, 3 July. News has been received from Modena that the Bishop, coming from Padua, has been lodged there, and some report that he remained only about an hour at the court, and from there went to Salvolo to provide the courtesy.

BOLOGNA, 3 July. Our Vice-Legate has returned to Ferrara to receive his Uncle, Cardinal Rubio, our new Legate, lodged there. The House Aldrovandi lit fireworks over the elevation of the Lord Conti to the Purple, and distributed much bread and wine to the people.

MILAN, 9 July. Orders have come from Vienna to visit all the prisons in this state, and to review all the regiments, both cavalry and infantry, that are stationed here on land, which troops already amount to more than 20,000 men; without counting the garrisons. It is reported that in Como there is difficulty with the frequent Spanish ships, [attacks] on those of the said republic, partly [due to] the fact that the Senate there has with difficulty compelled the heirs of the nobleman Iulianus to payment of his debts; although he has claimed that the same [?] are his. It is expected that this money will be sent to us in about a month to England.

VENICE, 11 July. Cardinal Csaski, traveling from Warsaw to Vienna, passed through here Sunday with his nephew and a retinue of 20 servants. Several illustrious ladies and countesses have arrived from Terra Ferma here to attend next Monday at the public entry of the Pope's Nuncio is abbreviated. Saturday evening, the Knight [?] Cunean of Bulgaria has been elevated to Count, which dignity shall pass in all their succession. The magistrates of health of this city and Bergamo have postponed the quarantine for this year, and it is declared that the fairs of Senigallia will now also not be held.

---

Sweden

Word has been received that the Prince of Modena has arrived there, during the time when General Saveuren holds the Kingdom, and that he has been received by His Majesty his Father with all signs of goodwill.

STOCKHOLM, 9 July. The Plenipotentiaries of Mr. Campreden have received some dispatches through the Chiqueu [?] of the French Court, and that concerning the Ministers of this Court in Conference [?] with the satisfaction of Ministers still well established. Petersburg or Newsland might do so, yet regarding the preservation of Peace with Muscovy there is no longer doubt. The dismissal of the Czar's troops, which have plundered and burned on the Sea coasts, has been recalled; the Stiltenant of Wabedom is 200 more secured and refused, and one awaits daily a further Council of our Ministers at Newsland. The French Minister is also frequently in Conference with the British Envoy. From The Hague we have no further news, and it is thought that it will soon return. The King remains sufficiently occupied with the Queen's affairs, though His Majesty is still partly committed to the matter.

---

Germany & Adjoining Kingdoms

VIENNA, 12 July. It is assured that Her Imperial Majesty has allocated ten million Guilders for the purpose of a journey to Marienzel. Thursday was the Name Day celebrated of the widowed Empress Amalia, and in the coming weeks the same day [?] the Counsel of Constantinople with letters from the Legation Secretary, which contain that one need not be concerned about the Hortons, the Christians would not wage war. It is assured that Count Kunsky [?] is to undertake the journey to the Czar of Great Russia on the coming Tuesday.

BRUNSWICK, 17 July. The Barons of Jodonie and Zeelli, being Austrian Ministers, have said to the Protestants some days ago that they will shortly answer the recent Memorial of these latter, concerning the Prisoners of Berenthal, where both they to Vienna, and the Government of Luipruk had gone, good three hours the same is long, so as to hope for something good there. The [?] of Waening, Minister of the Duke of Holstyne, who was found by the [?], and some time ago departed to Holsteyn, reports that [?] in Meslier had assigned him a Commission concerning the matters of the Audit Chamber of that Duchy regarding the [?] 100 gulden and shall be able to [?], but that the same Chancellor would not [?], so as to inform him what in the Religious matters has been [?]. The Count Said of the halfheartedness of high Bishops, how more and more experience, has through his Plenipotentiary thus made a [?] of a certain precise Memorial to set forth all the Controversies and [?] through the oversight, and common Bishop, against their examinations, so as to [?] the Protestant Courts and the Clergy [?] of the Treaty of Westphalia, and other Reich Capitulations, which the Roman Catholic Count is held to observe inviolably. The English Minister [?] for his three parishes, if the Men of the Church might wish to return in the event [?], whereby the Roman Catholic [?] himself to His Imperial Majesty, who had hitherto shown himself pleased to disapprove of [?], and wishes to lend to the Protestant Ministers at Vienna, [?] openly to [?] also, as a matter of his which by the Ring of Great Britain, as they assert, would not itself be approved.

FRANKFURT, 20 July. Word has been received that the Prince [?] of Nassau with Prince Maximilian of Hesse Cassel from Holland has arrived at the Cologne Court. Last Thursday the Elector Landgrave of Hesse Cassel attended the first Jubilee Feast of the University here.

COLOGNE, 21 July. Since Mr. Santini has not yet arrived here from Brussels, the Bishop residing here, the Count of Michtem, has undertaken the journey to him the same afternoon. The same day the sub-Dean [?] of the Cathedral, the Count of 22 [?] on.

---

Great Britain

LONDON, 18 July. This morning word is received here that the ships Cambridge, Cardigan, and Graves, 200 from Bengal, Bengala, as the Gulf came, had arrived in Plymouth; and that at the Cape of Good Hope they had taken the Mary of Port St. George, the Dabby of the gulf in the Bay, and the Cardigan of Bengola; the Count of the already mentioned ships (which are uncommonly [?]) will not [?], before those same have come in the Dunes; one knows not yet when these ships have sailed from the Cape, and whether they [?] the expected Holland ships, or had departed before. One awaits here yet the ship.

Also on Sunday the 13th of this running month, in the evening at the clock eleven hours, in the City of Leiden, having with him the Bijcuer Sauniet, going toward his House, near the Scheuzergat with wild shots, in all appearance with a murderous intent. And Justice on [?], another Example, in the Werte rigorously [?] to be observed, so that those who Terror to the aforesaid undertaking in [?], [?] to direct, thus that [?] in hand [?], from this City's Chest shall be expelled with a sum of 500 Guilders, and the Director Cyrua there said, that the exchange of the Lots of the Land by hand [?] expiring of the running month July must be completed, and that with the drawing of the remaining [?] shall be made.

---

The Netherlands - Auction Notices

Amsterdam. F. Denial, n.d. Boutekoning, Brokers in Timber goods, shall sell at Amsterdam Marine Beams of diverse lengths, a great party of Diontheimer and other Parts, a Hamburg Beams, Great-Beams, Fire Beams, Masts, etc.

Jan Wolfels Broker shall sell at Amsterdam on Wednesday the 30 July 1721, a party of English Hoods, Silk and Safette Threads, and Silk Gloves, bought [?] for a party of Indian Cottons, Cornsilk [?], great Colonials, [?] Streets, [?] pen, [?], and very many [?] Goods; all the day before and at Sale to the third time and Brokers, and thereafter Brokers shall at Amsterdam [?] a party 28 Bales Radix Jatappa, 18 Casks Cassia Lignes, and other Drugeries more.

One is ready with great on Wednesday the 13 August this Year 1721, in the afternoon Moses is the Hedick named the Star, Under Outshoorn, publicly to auction and to sell [?], with thirty Mettgo, two Hunt, and 54 Rozden good daily 200 Wei-High-as Welds, and those of the dil-Mog, Hereon States's Land of Wterion. At Amsterdam-by Andries van Damme, Book Seller on the Rokkiu beside the Bea [?] stuffs, out of East-over an Alphabetical Collection, of the like, Historical, [?], and of the Lieifberris combined: are to be obtained at H. van Damme.

---

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 25th of july, 1721. — With Privilege.

No. 1721.No. 90.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 28th of July, 1721

LEYDSE BARBARYEN.

ALGIERS, 18 July. On the 19th, an English pirate ship brought in here an English vessel from Ireland bound for Barbados, laden with all manner of goods. A captain, having no pardon, had the goods freely released. The same day another pirate ship brought in a Dutch flyboat, called the Young Jan Adriaan, with skipper Lauren Donck. This skipper [was bound] from Amsterdam to France, [carrying] a barrel of Napoleon [wine], a barrel of Normandy cider, and bales of cotton. Six cans [of goods?]. The English ship's [captain] had said that a Dutch cargo had arrived at Cadiz, which with Spanish warships would be brought before the Bey or King of the Dutch shippers' reputation and remunerated, to hear from him whether the account of the English captain is true; but it is said that nothing certain [?]; no good news could [be obtained?], and the small [unclear]; and because said warships were stationed at [?] above Larache in that time, all the inhabitants brought their families and goods within the city, and the great fear was, it was not thought would be taken [?]: cost of confusing matters. Also, news has come that another pirate ship with 20 [guns?], without knowing by what necessity for a third, that following is set to sea obtains. This day another pirate [ship?] with people laden, [carrying] wine, brandy, and sun-wine brandy toward England [was brought in], also without pardon of the same; it had already been taken 32 days before, and that Turks are at Cairo, when it was recovered. Also 2 men have been brought in from a Biscayan [vessel?], which still in the sea-going are well. From Salé in the last of the previous month: Rds. [reales] issued of 3600 Rds., 200 of Salé as at Larache at sea are.

TUNIS, 11 July. There is great anxiety here that another pirate ship with 36 [guns?] and 300 Moors was taken by a Maltese warship, and it is noteworthy that the Bey will not proceed against the 3 other captains entered here (being renegades and [?]) to be both executed, since they did not assist the great ship, although all these young warships were Bashaw's, [?].

ENGLAND.

CADIZ, 11 June. Our galleons have made such good progress with a favorable wind that it was believed they would reach the Canaries. Since the Dutch squadron has been in the Strait, most English privateers have departed; indeed it is assured that there are only 2 or 3 left at sea. In this bay various ships have arrived, and on the 25th departed Carlos Brelok, [bound for] Hamburg, a Danish [ship], 10 English, 12 French, and 2 Dutch ships.

MADRID, 11 July. New express news received from Cadiz, that the Spanish galleons, which [sailed] on the 21st, [with] two advice ships and four warships, under Admiral Don Balthasar de Guevara, have been found. [It] confirmed that in the same are laden goods of East Indian manufacture. The French ministers continue their conferences with those of our court; dispatches from Paris arrive daily; so that one believes matters of importance are under discussion. It is not certain when the mentioned reform order concerning the troops will be issued. It is now thought that this might be delayed some time. The King has recently honored the Marquis d'Asrin with the dignity of Grande of Spain of the first class, for him and his male descendants. They are very eager for the regulation of the Secretary of the Grand Inquisition and the treatment of [?], which with the private tribunal regarding Gibraltar, etc., has been sent to London.

FRANCE.

Extract from a letter to Middelburgh of 26 June. Commerce and navigation are becoming more and more closed here: meanwhile people are in perfect health; at Aix and [?] most of the nobility are well and departed; at Toulon preparations are being made for the ships which in short would also be ready; but there [?] the cold [?] at the banks, and at Aix; the plague in the forests [?]. Daily many pilgrims come here, as it has pleased the Crown, to repopulate this city again. In this healthy time, no one with his loved ones [?] would be received, if he dared to here, according to reports, and those coming from suspicious provisions, recently appeared at [?] on this day this matter.

PARIS, 23 July. Outing of the King, accompanied by the Duke of Villeroi and the Duke of Montemar, his chamberlain, to the Benedictine [convent], [place] of Saint-[?] and Port to see them play, with the Messieurs la Faille and Lirbault against the [?] Verdin and the [?]-Samuel, [?] and against [?], to this last 300 much [?] to [?], after the [?] was [?] was not [determined?] by his Majesty.

Monday's Courant.

to Bercy in the laboratory of Mr. Pajot d'Ozembray, for his great supply of saltpetre, which in the mentioned [?] was found, and thus [?] well executed. It is said that his Majesty will not devote himself to exercises nor [?]. Yesterday was the annual [meeting] held in the Palais Royal; the Controller General [made a report]; but it appeared that this was a matter for the great ones, and therefore to be resolved. [It] was therefore postponed to the next assembly, and the Duke of la Bezomme, but could not obtain this) the mentioned come with their sentence with more cheerful countenance and practice. The Messieurs de Susy, da Fourant, and Thiery have been called by the advocates of the Council to [?] Madamette to all the clergy of his diocese to admonish [?], to the exclusion of necessity in the mentioned health [?]. The Mr. de Polignac, Captain in the Regiment of the King's, his Father, who is an officiant in the court of the Duke Rome, has taken much trouble to overtake him and to arrange, as now the order has been given. News has come that the Congress at Cambrai will not be held before September. The [?] of Thiy and Velay [?], in the church of the Novitiate of the Dominicans. The Mr. de Fontet was married Tuesday to Madame the Mr. Colbert du Terroa, with Miss du Gaufies, which but [?] [...]

GREAT BRITAIN.

LONDON, 22 July. On Saturday the Commons made the first reading of the bill, forbidding the secret importation of goods to prevent smuggling; it came before the Lords the first reading of the bill for the relief of the original holders in the South Sea Company, and ordered the second reading on Monday, when all the Lords shall have the opportunity to be present. On Sunday the King, accompanied by his Queen, Brother, the Prince and Princess of Wales, went to the Chapel of St. James, where the [?] is raised, with a fine concert of instruments, to which the music was newly composed by Mr. Green, organist of St. Paul's Church; the Lord Howard of Effingham carried the sword of state before his majesty, and Doctor Headly delivered the sermon. [On?] Ledbee, who from the lighthouse [?] only read private bills; the Lords made the said reading, and passed the bill concerning bankruptcies; after which they submitted a request to all the directors of the South Sea Company, the Messieurs Grighby and Sutman, which requested by Admiral [?] to be heard against the bill for raising money from their goods, so that they might not be understood by particular accusations in the bill but in general terms, but this request was after much debate rejected. Then their Lordships read a request from the Messiour Aislable and [?] of the deceased Master General Craggs, in which requesting among other things to be heard by their advocates, over which wisdom a debate arose, which was adjourned to [?]. After which they made the second reading of the bill.

Left Column

[This section appears to contain heavily corrupted or unclear text regarding debates in what seems to be an English parliamentary or governmental setting, with references to various lords and officials including Lord Townshend and the Earl of Strafford. The text is too corrupted to provide a reliable translation.]

Stock prices from London: Bank shares at [?], Lottery Annuities at 57½ and 95, Bank [?] 135, with various other financial instruments listed including Annuities at 96 and other securities.

Foreign News

AMSTERDAM, July 25. A Dutch galley, which arrived at Algiers unmanned, belongs to Captain Jacob Groet the Younger. In April it was dispatched by Cornelis Stercksp with vessels from London. The reports mention Madagascar and a French ship from Mocha bound for France with cargo; ships from Suriname are expected shortly.

UTRECHT, July 25. The following persons have arrived in this city: Hymen Verfleeg, Koin. Bosmain, Jan van den Berg, Abraham van Capped, Gerit Olban, Daniel de Bont, Huibert van Sarde, Lublit Tigen, Ulke Sukkes, Ary Tercits, Koinfelt de Druyn, Aart van Proye, Adriaan de Lange, Jan Goedt, Jereon Goort, Richard Colius, Andrew Hancil, Benjamin Brown, Guy Buriet, John Burridges van Albion, and Johan Ciuog van Bordeaux. In exchange, the following departed for Thames: William Lewis, Brian Cunlley, [?] John Nealon, and others.

AMSTERDAM, July 26. The ship Wilminas Catrina arrived from the East Indies. Various other ships are noted with cargoes from Antwig, Danzig, Hamburg, and other ports, with destinations including the Maas River, the Vlie, and distant ports.

Right Column

Stock quotations: East India Company shares at 798, New India Company at 152½, English Bank at 131.

THE HAGUE, July 27. Prince Kurakin, Ambassador of the Czar of Great Russia, met with several gentlemen of the government yesterday, as did Baron Goder, Minister of the Elector of Cologne. News from Copenhagen and Hanover suggests the King of Sweden has recently departed, though reports remain unclear. No further dispatches have arrived from the North.

PETERSBURG, July 4. His Czarist Majesty has recovered from his recent illness and has returned to his residence with Her Czarist Majesty. A nobleman from Holstein is expected tomorrow. No further news has arrived regarding the Nystad Congress; all parties maintain their positions pending its conclusion.

VIENNA, July 16. Saturday brought news from Croatia that the son of the Croatian Count [?] has fallen victim to smallpox, such that the family line now faces extinction unless the elderly Count recovers. Three days of public entertainment have been arranged to divert the people. From Turkey comes news of a second fire in [?], which destroyed four houses and killed 22 people and numerous livestock. In Hungary, the rebel Emericky continues active in Transylvania with several hundred followers, having recently attacked a village.

BASEL, July 21. Word has come that Stanislaw of Welenburg has departed, regretted by all. It is reported that the Emperor has ordered 12 regiments in Italy to be prepared to reinforce those in Lombardy.

FRANKFURT, July 23. The Elector of Mainz has not yet returned from Bohemia to his residence. The Elector of Düsseldorf will visit the Landgrave of Hessen-Kassel next week. The Elector of the Palatinate is said to have made progress in ecclesiastical matters to secure complete accord with the Emperor.

LILLE, July 25. The Emperor's Minister at the French court has been succeeded by Herr Santini, who is daily expected in Douai pending his formal appointment.

Year 1721

PETERSBURG — [Text regarding the coronation of His Czarist Majesty appears severely corrupted; mentions artillery salutes and various dignitaries, but exact details cannot be reliably extracted.]

STOCKHOLM, July 16. There remains some hope regarding the King and the provinces, with various articles under discussion. Prince George of Holstein's minister is expected here.

STOCKHOLM, July 16. [Additional text regarding mineral waters and expeditions is too corrupted for reliable translation.]

Germany

BRUNSWICK, July 21. [Text regarding Swedish positions on the Rhine appears incomplete or corrupted.]

Course of Exchange

**EXCHANGE RATES** for the day: Madrid at 90, Cádiz 115½, Seville 113½, Bilbao 50, Lisbon 44¼, France 13¼, Livorno 91¾, Genoa 94¼, Paris 40¼, and London 34 Sch. 5½. Various exchange premiums (Agio) are listed for Antwerp, Ghent, Zeeland, Rotterdam, and the Bank at 5½.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 28th of july, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 91.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 30th of July, 1721
England

PETERSBURG, 2 July. Yesterday the anniversary of the coronation of His Czarist Majesty was celebrated here, it being the occasion on which His Czarist Majesty was crowned on that day in the year 7190 according to the Creation of the World, by the Russian reckoning. [Editor's note: This refers to the traditional Orthodox Christian chronology, which counted years from the Creation rather than from Christ's birth.] After the service at the Church of the Holy Trinity was completed, a report was delivered concerning the Expedition which General Leffy conducted in Sweden, accompanied by a threefold salvo of eight cannons against the Wetting, the Admiralty Citadel, and the galleys, during which the Te Deum Laudamus was sung. This done, His Czarist Majesty proceeded to his Court, where the Czar's residence, arranged in a fine open field, fired three salvos upon his arrival. The following day at 5 o'clock, all the Russian Generals and many other persons of distinction, as well as the Foreign Ministers, called upon His Czarist Majesty at his country house, where the aforementioned Majesty provided a collation until midnight.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 16 July. The King finds himself in excellent health at Karelsberg through the use of the mineral waters of Medwig, which is why His Majesty will continue there for some time yet. There is great hope that the King will be restored within a few days. It is assured that the preliminary points of peace with the Czar have already been concluded, and some report that the remaining articles are being regulated, including among others the marriage of Prince George of Holstein-Kätsel with the Czarist Princess. A Minister is expected from Denmark to negotiate, as opportunity permits, regarding an alliance between the two Crowns.

STOCKHOLM, 17 July. The King is in excellent health from the use of the mineral waters, though the tertian fever has not entirely left His Majesty, yet it is considerably improved. Regarding the negotiations at Neustadt there is no further news; however, an Express from the Swedish Ministers at this Court has arrived, but brought nothing more than duplicates of the dispatches which an Express some days before had brought to this Court, and which, returning to Neustadt, encountered it on the way back. It is said that the Muscovites are busy with the isthmus in Finland, having a bridge there to transport it, from which it is inferred that in the forthcoming Peace, that land will be returned to them. However, the Finnish nobility greatly fears that matters will not go so well for their Province; nevertheless they are preparing by all possible means to be maintained in their rights and privileges in the forthcoming Peace.

Germany & Adjacent Kingdoms

BRUNSWICK, 21 July. Count Welling, Plenipotentiary of Sweden here, has felt much better since he used the red bath waters at Wendhausen; he has betaken himself to the country house of the Duke of Blankenburg, and intends to return here within this week to continue his residence. Hope is beginning to revive for the opening of the Congress, since it is understood that Count Bellonon, as Plenipotentiary, Count Golofkin, will be sent here again, which Count is preparing to come from Berlin here with all speed. Meanwhile he is preparing by all possible means that they may be maintained in the forthcoming Peace by their government and privileges.

REGENSBURG, 21 July. Yesterday and this morning the Cardinal of Saxe Zeyst, with the Ministers of Bohemia and Munster, was again laid in bed; it is hoped that the Bavarian Count von Konon will go, though they are expected back again tomorrow, when they will spend 3 to 4 days in the city, and subsequently return again to the monastery Berlit, in order shortly to resume the Imperial Diet; as it is said that the Ministers of Mainz, [Bengaore?], (as already departed from Germany) and Hessen-Kassel will shortly return here. It is understood meanwhile that the Abbot of Fulda for the supreme regency of the chambers of Werzellaar, in place of the Prince of Fürstenberg, does not come much under consideration, and that this considerable office might well be entrusted by His Imperial Majesty to a certain Westphalian Prince. It has been said that the Commission of the Imperial Diet would be entrusted as a Commission of the Imperial assembly to investigate in the Palatinate whether the Elector has properly and fully removed and annulled the Grezes or Printhames, according to His Imperial Majesty's intention; however this news is not much credited by the Court of the Imperial Diet. The Württemberg Minister has undertaken the journey to his Court, but will not be long absent; and that of the Bishop of Würzburg has been postponed for 3 to 4 days.

DRESDEN, 22 July. Besides the Crown Prince of Denmark, who has been received in this city and taken up his residence in the Castle, von Weinfgel, an Envoy of Denmark, has also arrived here from Petersburg. It is understood that the French troops ordinarily have had orders [?]; which they will now meanwhile observe.

LEIPZIG, 23 July. The Crown Prince of Denmark, who is traveling under the name of Count Herthorn, arrived on the 20th at Fleisel, and Vilette [?] gives an audience to the Queen and Princess Margareta of the King; this Prince did not depart from there until the 11th toward Dieiden; here it is witnessed that His Highness will also soon be at Kassel.

HAMBURG, 25 July. The Burgessry of this city was completely assembled yesterday, and has consented to certain impositions, which are from the payment of the sum which must be given to the Emperor. The Deputies of this city are expected to return within 14 days. From letters from Copenhagen of the same day, we learn that the Court of Denmark, the same day as the Queen made her entry, betook itself again to Frederiksborg, where it still finds itself. Mr. Berkentien departed in the capacity of Envoy to Stockholm, and Mr. Reiffen went as Secretary of the Embassy with him. Mr. Beenfeld, Fourier of the Court, has been appointed by the King as President in Fredericksborg, in place of Mr. Dukes.

Great Britain

LONDON, 25 July. On Wednesday those of the Lower House postponed the following consideration of the Report of the Secret Commission for a whole month; after which the House, in grand committee, took the King's message of the previous day into consideration, and after a long debate it was resolved by 111 against 110 votes to authorize the King to raise 90,000 pounds sterling for the discharge of the debts of the Civil List, upon the revenues of the same, at an interest of 5 per cent per year; the salaries, pensions, &c. shall be burdened with a tax of one penny per pound, with which the aforesaid interest will be paid, and with the remainder a portion of the principal sum shall be paid off each time, until the same shall be wholly paid to the Crown; and the report of this resolution was entered today. The House of Lords, considering that the procedure of the Lower House, regarding Mr. Aislabie and the deceased Postmaster Craggs, could not be well reconciled with the actions of the former Directors of the South Sea Company, upon which the Bill is grounded, had in the conference on Wednesday with those of the Lower House requested that the Commons produce all the documents concerning the aforementioned Aislabie and the deceased Mr. Craggs. Yesterday the Commons had the second reading of the Bill to prevent the introduction of fraudulence through the practice of smuggling; and it was committed for 3 days; thereafter the report was considered regarding what passed in the conference with the Lords; and it was promised that the members of the secret committee would be appointed to a committee to draw up the substance of that part of the Bill concerning Mr. Aislabie and the deceased Postmaster General Craggs; in order to have the same drawn up in a conference to be given to the Lords. Yesterday the Lords had the second reading of the Bill for the discharge of the Army debts, laid the same in new committee, and postponed the Bill to raise money from the goods of the former Directors of the South Sea Company until Saturday. Today the Lordships again had a conference with the Commons, from which they received their reasons regarding Aislabie and the deceased Postmaster General Craggs; containing in brief that the allegations in the Bill against him had been proved by the verification of the former Directors under oath, to the satisfaction of the House of Commons; which reasons will be taken under consideration by their Lordships tomorrow. Those of the Lower House heard the report of the resolution regarding the discharge of the Civil List, and resolved by 113 against 89 votes that the salaries, pensions, &c., instead of 12, shall be burdened with only 6 shillings, of which a Bill is to be brought in. Tuesday morning Their Royal Highnesses the Prince and Princess of Wales went to Greenwich, where they walked in the park for 2 hours, and returned at midday to Kensington House to take their meal. On Monday very many stockholders in the taxable funds gathered together, and unanimously resolved to present a petition to the King, to show to His Majesty the disadvantage that their state has come to, as they would have to submit to the arrangement that has been made regarding the South Sea Company: it will amount to this more than 30,000 in number, and among them many widows and orphans are included. Discussions continued regarding an address of Parliament, and some even wished that the King would declare his opinion on that and other matters. The prices of Bank shares are 130 and one half to 31; East India Company books closed at 133; South Sea 133; the Million Bank 130; Lottery Annuities 98 and one half; Bank Annuities 97 and one half to per cent 91; African Company 29 and one half to 30; the Exchange Affidavits 7 and one quarter to 5 [?]; of this Land 6 and 1 [?] to 1 quarter; and J.B. 23 and one quarter.

France

PARIS, 25 July. On the previous Tuesday the King, accompanied by the Marshal Duke of Villeroy, his Governor, went for a walk to the Castle of La Muette; after His Majesty had attended the hunt for a while, the King found himself somewhat indisposed, yet this was of no consequence. The same day the Turkish Ambassador, the Archbishop of Camerck, without any ceremony visited, as was also done yesterday by the son of the aforementioned Ambassador. Beyond the presents which the King granted to the aforementioned Ottoman Minister, which in previous accounts consisted of tapestries of Gobelin, pieces of gold brocade, various Eastern silks, and other costly items; yet now it is said he is also being given a very fine book, [authors?] named, written by an Arabian author, which treats very much of philosophy: the aforementioned Ambassador had since his arrival in this city sought after this book, but could not find it, but encountering it in the King's library in Latin, he showed such great inclination toward it, upon which His Majesty undertook to have it bound very properly and his coat of arms placed upon it. On the coming Sunday Abbot Bignon will present the same to His Turkish Excellency, and it is not doubted that he will be highly pleased with it, because it is so rare to obtain: it is assured that besides this book, there is but one other copy to be found in France, which will be brought in its place to the King's library.

France

The project promises to be extensive and very well organized. The Jesuits have worked for a long time to obtain permission to establish a University at Dijon, but since the people of the University of Paris opposed this proposal with all their strength, they have not succeeded entirely. On the 21st of this month, the Lord Joulain, Count and Priest, brother and advocate of the Court well-disposed towards judges, formerly Syndic of the Sorbonne, Count, was ordered by the Government into the Bastille in Ostend; it is complained here that he had engraved a plate that was not well-suited and has since been withdrawn: it is assured that upon investigation, suspicious items were found at his lodgings, for which reason he was released on bail on Wednesday. The same day the Duke d'Aumout was seized by a stroke of apoplexy; he was bled twelve times, and a purgative was administered, but there is great concern for his recovery. The Steward of the Marchioness d'Ethizes has received a pension of 100 livres, as well as a commission as Lieutenant Colonel with the Cross of St. Louis; Commissioner Cailly has also received a commission of 200 livres. The Naval Commissioner, the Lord Dormellon, Commissioner of Finance, has received the position of Counselor of State, a post vacant through the death of the Lord Trudaine; and the Lord de Gau from Babylon, also Commissioner of Finance, has received the expedition [?], which the aforementioned Lord Dormelton previously held.

On the 18th and 23rd of July, which was a Sunday, many Bishops, judges, came to the Palace Royal for the Confraternity procession, which after the Portuguese ceremony of the Confraternity was to be presented by the Duke Regent to the Court, but this did not happen. It is said that the Duke of Bourbon intends to be married soon; namely, they say with a German Princess, who was formerly betrothed to the King of Great Britain, to which conclusion this Duke would have come, on the urging of his mother and of the Great Aunt. The Count of Toulouse will soon in the College of London teach the Turkish and Arabic languages; this education, which until now will be called a Tasal-Children, and they shall be designed to be sent to Constantinople and to the Courts in Asia. The Turkish Grand Vizier goes with his retinue to France for a secret purpose to Chantilly, to amuse himself there with hunting and English hunts; the Duke of Bourbon has made great preparations to receive him on horseback there; the Court will lodge there quite magnificently; the inns at Escouans, which are halfway, have been reserved; the entertainments are said to be extremely grand there. The Butchers of Paris, who do not accept the Livery, shall only transport the meat during the night. This Minister's journey to Fontainebleau will take place by coach, when he departs from here. The latest letters from Rouen say that Cardinal Albertoni, to appear in state before the public, has had preparations made for his equipages; that he has arrived with his servants, and having added several others with him, so as to have great state. The Duke of la Force has arranged a few days for the wedding party here; his nephew, the Count of Roure, has splendidly entertained, and has arranged to his green house to marry his brother and to keep children, during which time they will house and maintain themselves. Monsieur de Hayes, Mistress on this, bought a small mill with 3,000 livres in paper securities Saturday evening, and having attempted to hide it, this was finally discovered on Monday. Several children, recently arrested while playing in the streets around Charleroi, were forced under a [?] that fell in. On Tuesday a [?]

Italy

NAPLES, 7th July. The four merchant houses have, through an agreement of 15 months' duration, sent 200 guilders to end the States; this General Furaan had sent, the said Galeas, which the preceding of the Zijlinger and said shipyard clarifies, that which the said Fanassen carried with the fort water and also the confirmation enlarged was but Ko Mas. The Marquis, pleased with the Decree, which will be commanded in the name of the King in the name of the Republic, General Arch-Judge of the Doge held [?] the King of him Jubilee-Feast done [?] His Court: on XL return give; and His and their all agreement forgiven holds to her Excellency with all [?]. The Viceroy has held the word of his Death thrown the 3 Spaniards, mutinous their Officers coordinated tried and West off-weighed.

ROME, 12th July. For the Princess Benedictste will be received as the Viceroy her Chamber tomorrow morning the matter will be in [?], likewise the afternoon at the [?]. That morning the Fleet of the Italian will also great solemnity of Cardinal Marcivitti. They are already, who have themselves some in molestation, consequence are of ticket Adei, who at Rome for great a [?] the Cardinal must [?] Secretary of State the H. Valde Arandien, which said states Death [?], in the Church heard inserted [?] is Sister of the Magratas

England

BRUSSELS, 28th July. On the preceding Thursday, the Abbot Spinelli had his first audience there by the Marquis de Prié, to whom he gave his letters of credence; and thereafter the Pope's functions as Minister began. Against this, his predecessor, the Mr. Sontini, after having taken leave of her Excellency, the Duchess of distinction and Esteemed Ministers, departed for the nunciature; but his nunciature in Cologne; it is said that the mentioned Minister at Louvain, where he spent the night, made magazines to continue the Prince of Baden with all his handsome arrangements to amuse; Saturday was shot at the gun, which had been erected on the great square before the Town Hall; there was a great crowd of people, and an unpleasant bird filled with firecrackers was [?], so no lovers could [?]

The Director Capus advises that the exchange of the Lotteries of the Land, subscriptions of this current month of July, shall be completed; and will not the it according to a beginning, shall be made.

This Saturday at ten o'clock, being Wednesday, the 30th July 1721, the Broker will sell on the Singel at Cologne English hats, silk and sateen goods, and silk stockings from Robert Price; furthermore a party of hand-rottings, Cowsleys Hegtice, great sweepings, buttons, metals, and very many such goods, all from Matten Koelenburg, and Peter Onkerboer Brokers, shall at Amsterdam in the White Swan, a party of extra thick fresh [?] and Belgian white white breads, thick from 2 and 1½ to 4 inches; a party of prepared [?] and [?], on the Muyder's gate, by the Muyderpoort; and the further beams, planks, [?] from Stettin, and Semillon.

Peter and David Radet, brokers, shall on Wednesday the 6th August 1721, standing in storage bedsteads, beds, walnut chests and cabinets, porcelain and decorated, and great party made linens of all sorts, a party of fine [?], women's clothing, copper, tin, etc., jewels, a party made for the sale to be heard.

The sending in the Embassy of their High Mightiness, I can in half be by a Coffee-School to give instruction in the grounds of the Landvelt, Flanders, bookkeeping, and bookholding [?], also in the grounds of religion, presents in [?] in Voorschoten, for which he also has great and delightful provisions of Holland and [?]

At Leiden printed by FELIX DE KLOPPER, on the Bredestraat.

The 30th July 1721. With Privilege.*

Notices & Advertisements

On Monday the 14th August 1721, one shall in The Hague in the [?] greatly pleasant plantations, wooded grounds, and further lands, great with the other around 30 morgens of land, named Allemgeelt, named land, 1500 guilders at annual dwelling house, from Voorburg, opposite this side, at the canal of [...] running out onto the Wael, and at Rotterdam at the Mr. Pieter van Kouwenhoeven.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 30th of july, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 92.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 1st of August, 1721

ITALY.

NATELS, 8 July. The four Maltese galleys, which are in quarantine, have been in great danger of perishing due to a severe storm; aboard them were also goods, mostly belonging to the state. During the same storm, news was received that corsairs and other pirates had forcibly seized some vessels, and some have been lost at sea. The Cape of Bon has recently been reached at sea, and near the Cape of Tunis they have again appeared without regard for the quarantine.

Piety in the Church of the Vatican, for the deceased observation of Pius XI. [Editor's note: The reference is likely to papal ceremonies.] The equipages of Cardinal Alberoni are expected at Civita Vecchia; the Congregation of the Consistory has given His Eminence permission to have them brought here from Genoa.

GENOA, 11 July. Tuesday morning, two of the English ships which were cruising off this harbor sailed toward the west; the third was also sent in that direction; they were stationed lying [at anchor] the previous night.

BOULOGNE, 11 July. Sunday morning there was such a tempest, which occurred in such a manner throughout all parts of the houses at Cherbourg, and churches were severely damaged; in the countryside, over an area of 15 miles in all directions, all field crops were destroyed, trees uprooted, and fearfully, large numbers of livestock have perished as a result.

VENICE, 19 July. Monday afternoon, Signor Stampa, the Papal Nuncio to this Republic, made his public entry, which was the most magnificent ever seen here. To this end, the Venetian nobility proceeded around midday with his escort to the Island of [?] in 4 gondolas; in the first was the Nuncio with the Venetian nobility; in the second, one could see in relief the Religion, and Neptune with the image of Rome holding a scepter in hand, having the Tiber before him, and some other statues, which a [procession] proceeded from the wagons of the House of Conti; the third was entirely gilded, and the fourth black, like the ordinary gondolas of the nobles. These gondolas were followed by those of the nobles. In another gondola one could see the Patriarch, the Imperial Ambassador, the Receiver of Malta, and other Ministers of foreign Princes. The Nuncio, having made the customary obeisance, departed at a certain time; but many Triestine [?] who had come there to present their compliments to the Minister remained with him until the arrival of the [?]. Signor Morozini, who had been deputed by the Senate as introducer along with two other senators, all in hastily prepared clothing, and each in his position. All having come to land, a Secretary spoke on behalf of the Republic with ordinary compliments to the Nuncio, in which the introducer also presented his compliments. Subsequently the Nuncio went in the introducer's gondola and was conveyed to the Palace of the Nunciature. The escort of this prelate was in the gondolas of the aforementioned senators.

Tuesday morning the Nuncio was led in like manner to the College, being accompanied by the bishops, the reverend Patriarch of Aquileia, and many other distinguished foreigners. Having arrived at the College, he delivered his letters of credence, which were received at the table; after which he presented his commission in Italian very briefly and eloquently, to which the Doge and the senators responded. In this ceremony of the Visitation, he was brought back to the Palace of the Nunciature, where he was presented with all manner of refreshments. In the afternoon the Government sent the customary gift to the Nuncio. On this occasion the Palace of the Nunciature was illuminated for three evenings; a fine fireworks display was set off, and the crowd of people was very large.

That same evening the Government received letters from the Cordelier [?] dated 16 June, but it is not yet known what may have transpired from that date. Thursday, the feast of St. Martha, the Doge attended chapel with his usual escort. Thursday was also the day when a solemn procession was held, and similarly one from the brotherhood of Padua, which took place on the same day in the year 1509. That morning the Nuncio, with his escort, was received in the College to discuss the reply to his letters of credence and commission.

SWEDEN.

STOCKHOLM, 16 July. After the British Admiral Nottington arrived with the combined fleet in this city, he departed with the Great British Minister Finch to Carlscrohn, where they were received in audience by the King, and the Senate conferred regarding the commission on behalf of the H. Doge, and commissioners were sent for this purpose to Her Majesty. The reception did not go well in the evening; the dispatch of goods in Livonia has been received, and everything proceeds as hoped. It is reported that a courier has been sent to Nice. Letters have also been exchanged between the Count and a Governor at Court. There it is said that the Lieutenant has departed, thinking, as the entire Court does, that [?] and the Danish Court has been notified.

GERMANY AND RELATED REALMS.

VIENNA, 19 July. Yesterday His Imperial Majesty was brought into the secret council, and has also recently been deliberating on the matter brought forward there. According to reports from Hungary, the king had a dispatch concerning two infantry regiments and two dragoon [squadrons], and [?] a party of simulants [?] from the municipality enlisted.

Regenswragg, 24 July

Last Monday evening, in the Church of Saalen-Zeyts of Egelzheim, the Ministers of Bohemia, Momlini, who had recently returned to attend the Diet, which will convene again toward the beginning of the coming Month; most of the Ministers were questioned beforehand at that time, and the names which were held in reserve by the Emperor's Committee were prepared to expedite the deliberations: His Eminence afterward entered into conference with the second Committee, which had been sent with the Austrian Minister Jodolie for the hunt, and subsequently gave audience of various kinds to the Baron von Geisdorf, Minister of the King of Poland, who on account of a confidential negotiation had imposed upon himself to remain a quiet time with His Excellency, and departed the following morning toward Dresden. It is said that the Baron von Geisdorf, the Klein-Saxe Minister, received news from the Count of Keck that he had journeyed from Swalbach to the Font and had arrived there.

Great Britain

### LONDON, 25 July Our aforementioned 3 East India Ships, namely the Duke of Cambridge, the Cardigan, and the Craiggs, have arrived from Plymouth in England; with the same, no other news has come except that the Ship the King George, which was to come this Year also from Fort St. George, has proved unseaworthy for a voyage and has run aground there. One sees here the Cargo of the above-named Ships, consisting of 4313 pieces of Addaties, 4665 Arquebannies, 1963 Allibannier, 3400 Bafracs, 7667 Carndaries, 2461 Oriconocs, 1546 Chillacs, 15551 Chuften, 200 ditto Morcas, 1445 Copecs, 50514 Collacs, 9289 Cushties, 1362 Cuttannees, 2394 Chuc les, 1780 Droffelics, 2361 Eirchees, 2000 Emetiettes, 6012 colored Gingans, 25577 Gurrahs, 2139 Humburns, 2082 Jamparie, 5103 Lacuties, 16580 long Cloths, 480 ditto Blue, 3464 Lungeces Hsiba, 15217 Multimuls, 1337 ditto flowered, 8808 Nillaees, 2659 Penistcos, 14840 Photacs, 77793 Romais, 1600 Sunmoes, 1579 ditto Blue, 20793 Salampoureies, 1163 Shalbafts, 503 Sceiberjee, 4360 Seerluckers, 8823 Serica, 10715 Tafftaees, 8174 Tansjsups, 604 ditto flowered, 2930 Tepoys, 2384 Terindaens; 24200 pounds Carocn Garein, 81400 Cow heffe; 33200 ditto pounds raw Bengal Goods, 162000 Logwood, 710800 Saltpeter, 5300 Shellac, and sundry Goods for Private persons.

France

### PARIS, 24 July Monday, the Duke du Maine inspected the Regiment of Swiss Guards of the King in the Plain of Saloons. It is said that shortly 2 Ordinances are to come forth; the first of which shall ordain that all those who have claims on the Royal Treasury for Offices &c., shall be paid with Letters of a particular Print; which he shall also be able to seize from the Commissioners if they are set thereon; these Letters shall be received in payment of Rents on the Provinces, and further Offices, which the King shall declare: the other shall be advantageous for certain Persons, who with a fee of two per Cent, have registered in the Royal Treasury various new Enrichments, which ought to have no lawful share in the 25 Millions of Rents, which the King has created on St. Aydes and Gabrielien. One still speaks somewhat of Royal Letters under penalty of a fine, but no one shall easily attempt to make them signed by persons of name and reputation. Notwithstanding all the Attacks, the Pope maintains his Character of absolute authority, the Constitution being preserved. In the matter of the Settlement, one maintains here such a strange course for the salvation of the Church of Wiankrirk, it will be difficult to separate them in the Hovels. Some days ago the Jesuits in the College of Clermont defended a Thesis of 67 Propositions, on the Foundation of the Bull. The Pope has taken as his Official Theologian the Dominican Peter Ceji, previously Professor of Theology at Trier, but who under the reign of Louis the XIV departed from this Realm, and has since held himself at Padua; he is held to be the most capable Theologian of his Order. Tuesday, the Abbot Mettia Chercier, Curate of St. Niecy, Nephew of the Abbot Vivant, new Dean of St. Germain l'Auxerois, being conveyed by the Lord Dorfanne, Official of Paris, took possession in his own name of that Deanship. The King has permitted the Vermis to begin a vacation, so henceforth during the day at one o'clock in the afternoon, a Coach will depart from The Hague to Maaxten Koekienburg, and Pieter Onkelhoer Makclaafs, will at Amerdyik at the White Swan, offer a party of extra choice Julische and Berlinsche Book Pieces, thick of 2 and 3 and a half rot a daim; a party of sawed Planitsch, on the Muydegarff, by the Muydeport; and the further Barlkm, P Moelicns the Sheep, the Unicorn, and Samion.

One is of opinion that on Wednesday the 13th of August of this Year 1721, Houses in the Heirberg named the Star, below Ouisfhoorn, publicly to be let with three Morgen, two Hood, and 54 Roeden of good meadow land, under the Jurisdiction of the Noble and Mighty Lords States of the Land. On Monday the 25th of August 1727, at 6 o'clock in the evening, shall be let out, a Superior Cottage, Stable, Coachhouse &c., which is further carpeted between Maastricht and Zuilen, on the River Vegt, named Engcin on the dike at [?] hours, in the Doemc Tower, a fine Farm Dwelling, with Hay- and Meadowland, and 2 small Orchards, along with the aforementioned Homestead in Werland, and an Orchard, situated about the Zuisle Corn-Mill [?]; anyone desiring further information may address Amsterdam, or Erzbhard Vlaar, Notary and Broker at Utrecht.

Printed by S. Luchmanslii is printed and in all Cities by the Booksellers &c. Also I. II. of van der Heurefj Redaction on the necessity [?]. At Amsterdam by the transactions of Abraham Exemplary [?] Charles XI and one sees the entire War of the North, as well as the Materials & Figures, &c. 6 voll. Life of Sixtus Pontifex Roman written

At Leiden printed by FELIX DE [KLOPPER]

---

New East India Ships

AMSTERDAM, 30 July. The Captain of the Bark, last departed from St-Giequ et Livorno, reports that 2 Barbary Rovers, which had previously been on his voyage therefrom by guard, had come thither with violent tribulations; among these were the 2 Dragons, Minnor Peyl, at the Scyt Mazy, which one must have heard of, and where the foregoing Italian [ship], with 32 guns, which had slept while guarded with the Hamburg Ship the Sonika Galy, is so affrighted that previously one could not lodge in the six [?]; it has been noted! With the mission at Algiers things were so dangerous both for Marseillaise as well as Dutch War ships, since some of their Rovers remained at Sea and the city expected some, 2 Caravels were to depart. At Livorno, Captain Charles Brockmond, heading thither, but waiting for the Wind, and Bliond by Eigt. Wijman of Duysburg, who appeared to become violent; also the Locations of Captain Arn. Kanmer were taken in other fashion and presented, likewise arranged, and one may have arrived the Ship Plymouth, Captain [?] from here. With the Ship the Anna Maria, Pieter Cora. Blom, which in the Meuse was sold on the 18th of this month by Tour & Noor, one hears of the Ships that having to go toward Rio de Janeiro, the exciter of these at Sea should have run aground, but whether with the same, the Ships returned to Fetsembuerg, one hears no more news, only that there an old pirate [?], who has not captured any ships. Ship of Skipper Hlouwer, departed the fourth of this from Lisbon, and which is very near the City, one has heard of the War ships the 25th before last of the River of Lisbon being stationed, and betaking themselves to the Count by the Lord of Sommelyk, dike; that one hears the fleet from Bahia there partly still [?] 2335 to the fleet of the fleet toward Eternambueg, and the Ships toward Angola [?], having to go, yet 14 days they departed, and one hears Skipper Brouwer that he on the Portuguese Coast by 2 Rovers, which they knew of, was pursued, and one said as if there on the Coast might be even more Rovers; much news concerning Skipper about Cape Finis Terra, [?] the departing East India Ships in good State arrived. At Batavia arrived the Ship the St. Andries, Gyebert Jantien from Zeeland; at Bordeaux arrived the Drake, Corn. Draak from the Meuse, Pieter Adi. and H.Ed. Sievert from the Chelfse, and also others from the hands of the Dublin from Hoorn, Fougers from here; at Plymouth Captain Wys with his Gold-colored Ships, and Seahorse, must have been besieged. At Marseille the moving Ships from Vlastvijk, the Drake David Xc. came from Greenland departed, with the news that besides the 3 already mentioned Ships, 2 others would be away.

England

PETERSBURG, the [?] one of Ceeriafs Second Lower by Fulk [?] of the Militia [?] and [?] those [?], the foremost [?], in Hanno van Holland [?]

Denmark

VRIMON, the [?]

Anno 1721.

Tenth day of August 1721.

Course of Exchange

There it has been plundered, and in the course of time, having gained knowledge, they sought to secure themselves; through this, such tumult arose among the inhabitants, as if the Enemy were on the Border in violent fashion: wherefore the Imperial General de Guast Goudricourt, who commanded there, sent out some deployed soldiers into the woods to spy out the rebels or the fugitives; one now held that these were the same bandits who had attempted to rob the Count of Viertond, General Field-Surveyor, in his journey through Hungary, and for which reason he ought to have provided himself with a stronger escort.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 1st of august, 1721. — With Privilege.

No. 1721.No. 93.
