Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 4th of August, 1721
Muscovy

Moscow, 9 July. There is held here a pleasant summer festival. One finds no shortage of provisions here, and on this River (which is entirely free of ice) one has obtained Sturgeon from the White Sea, and some other fish from Boiea.

At Cadiz

Cadiz, 9 July. Since the 30th of the previous month, the following ships have passed through this Bay: the St. Michael, Walter Robbers from Hamburg; the St. George, Mathias Flemyng from Brouwershaven; the Joanettica, Robb. Lamberty from London; and the Arent, Lileman Welden from Breyderdam. In total, the following have departed: the John, Benjamin Havens, and Francois Nicholson (Canarian); the St. Francisco Jaufre, Captain Annous bound for Tortola; the Diamant, Th. de Jong; and the Arabella, Will. Hamilton bound for London; the Maria, George Morris bound for Teneriffe; l'Aimable, Captain Bocanegra; Mello; and the St. Josephe, Jan Noorman bound for Bilbao. There still remain anchored a Hamburg vessel, a Dane, 17 English ships (among them a merchant vessel coming from Dublin and English ships bound for Ostend), together with the Spanish St. Vliet, Picter Molenaar, sailing toward Amsterdam.

Naples, 13 July. The King and his entire Court are still engaged in the Carnival; His Majesty and the Princes frequently take the opportunity to hunt. It is confirmed that the King has appointed the Marquis de Leede as General over this Province. It is reported that the reformation of the Troops is being put forth and made public, though the overall Plan has been changed and put on a better footing. The Officers must conduct themselves according to their Companies or the same. It is said that the French Marquis de Milly and Biliarp have been appointed by the King as Lieutenant General over the Spanish Troops. The Herr von St. Michael is deceased, at the age of 39 years. The changes regarding the coming Ministers of the Court, which have been much discussed, appear to make no further progress at present. One sees the French Ministers and others from Cologne and States [?], which now [?], has not yet returned. The Family Ministers continue their Conferences and Ministry these months. An Express has been sent from Rome.

England

Petersburg, 11 July. At this Court, the anniversary of the glorious Victory has been celebrated with great joy—that victory in which the standard-bearers of His Czarist Majesty's forces had command of the regiment at Pultawa. This was celebrated with cannon fire from the City, the firing of Salvos, and with drinking and festivities, as on the day of the Battle. Throughout these celebrations, Her Majesty the Czarina with her Family and the nobility were present. At the same time, the Duke of Holstein arrived (who also participated in this Victory with the Officers) and took possession of the Palace of the deceased Lieutenant General Bruffe, where his Ministrations had their beginning, with cannon fire all around, conducted very merrily and gaily. His Highness then soon thereafter embarked in a boat on the Neva and went to pay his compliments to Her Czarist Majesty, who received him very graciously. A Ball was held and fine Fireworks were discharged. Two days thereafter, His Highness celebrated the Name-day of His Czarist Majesty, also with the assembled company, and for two days His Czarist Majesty had Salvos fired by the Soldiers. Her Czarist [Majesty] had the Troops make a Citation, and on His Czarist Majesty's [day], together with the Duke of Holstein, the foreign Ministers and the Nobles of both States participated in a feast. The Cannons were fired and that night fireworks were again discharged. The entire Expedition was sent to Rome. The Secretary of the Great Britain Ambassador, Colonel Staahype, who was sent to London, has not yet [returned]. The Secretary of the Great Britain Ambassador has [?] in the age of 59 years and [?], it appears for now there is no [further] progress. The Secretary of the Great Britain Ambassador, the Colonel, [?].

France

Paris, 28 July. An Arrest of the King's Council of State of the [?]th of the Month was issued, regarding the payment of accounts, according to the Commission of the Council.

Paris, 23 July. The Knight of Lorraine, who by his marriage has been deprived of his Ecclesiastical Benefits, has in the meantime resigned his Priory in favor of the Knight de Pina, descended from an old Family in Gascony, but in order to retain his Pensions on various Abbeys, has not declined to enter the Knights' Order of St. Lazare. The Mr. de Catclau, former Governor of the Palace of the Tuileries and former Captain of the Hunt in the District of Genouilly and Paris, died on the 24th, having bequeathed by Testament to the Hr. Meaupcou a Land Estate with 40,000 Livres annual income. The Architects, in order to obtain profit, have built many Houses for some, and for others, which were not in a state [to build], have had them built, and have been forced to post Tickets for them. They want Huon for those who wish to complete them. In the Cloister of the Premonstratensians, a great Theft was committed on the 3rd of the month. In the Council meeting: the decision regarding the drawing concerning the Papers was postponed until tomorrow. The Vicar of St. Etienne Hurlent has had a dispute with the Bishop of Soissons, who had refused him the verification of the Papers, which he presented for a Cure near Compiègne, under the pretext of Complaint regarding the Constitutio: the former appealed to the Court of Rome against this Chicane and there obtained his verdict. Against the Picardies, the matter caused him much harm, but besides this, he has another greater concern, in that the Hr. Petit Pied has in the critique of a pretended advertisement very badly attacked him—thus he will remember that his Name and Episcopal Title were lent to a writer [?] and good faith. One sees here the Address, delivered by the Lieutenant General of Nevets, to the High Marshal of Villars, how he in this present month of July, returning to the Court, passed through the mentioned City.

Our Company, divided in various Places, could not have the Honor to show you our great esteem, since You passed through this City; we are delighted, My Lord, that this damage can now be repaired, to see you, and in Your Honor the formalities which We have so many times heard proclaimed.

This Kingdom is indebted to You for its celebrated Victories and foremost Conquests, of which the brilliant Peace would still owe much; that is the Peace which has been preserved. You brought our Enemies to this; what do I say, You have forced them to surrender themselves to You.

May the high and glorious Success of Your Arms [continue], and You yourself have brought this great Work, and the product of all Statecraft, and beside you great General, great Mediator or Protector, great Statesman, and great Minister, You unite, My Lord, in Your Person all the highest degree all the Qualities [?]. The Hr. Neverts have long found incompatible in the genius of the Nation; and which have never been found together, unless some of them were diminished.

You possess as well as they the high degree of that natural and Social propensity [?], which knows how to make itself worthy of all the Lords and Communities, and which are all apt to your deeds as worthy to be expressed.

Portugal

Lisbon, 12 July. The Marquis Cusador has arrived here on behalf of the King of Strategy, as well with Letters from the Lord and the Infanta Donna Francisica, His [?] Holland the Prince Eugene, and the Infanta Donna Francisica.

---

Great Britain

LONDON, 29 July. Yesterday two unexpected ships arrived from Germany, among our beloved ships, namely the Cardonel, the Darby, and the Mary, from Fort St. George and Bengal, and the Sarum from China; so that one now eagerly expects 2 more ships from Fort St. George and China: the aforementioned four ships come last from St. Helena, and bring no other news than that they have passed the African coast; however, it is said here that they were at the Cape of Good Hope on the 18th of April; and where the aforementioned 19 Dutch [ships] named thereof on April, or as others say the 26th of the same sailed toward the Fatherland, and that they were followed by 7 other ships [?]; yet it is said that this last news is not entirely certain, because only some of ours have come in. Here one sees the cargo of the four aforementioned ships, consisting of 16 chests of Indian goods: 2530 Alibianunies, 500 Allingans, 126 Allibanunies, 1129 Raffias, 500 Bebilles Pullicats, 3527 Carrundaries, 500 Callowaypoetas, 1932 Chillacs, 477 Mocha Chitlenn, 3700 ditto Pattana, 763 Copperas, 7336 Coffees, 1600 Cusbaces, 1136 Cuttanoes, 312 Blatchès, 2100 Emierties, 114 Gorgoras, 7300 Gurahs, 1814 Humhums, 4108 Gunnillas, 1700 long Cloths, 1350 ditto Blue, 1030 Linges Hetba, and 30 Moiteen, 4842 Mulmuls, 1012 Nillacs, 132 Peniacoes, 137 Pautolas, 1850 Photas, 3000 Romauls, 9770 Salampouros, 500 Sara Toiles, [repeated entries] 500 Sara faccus Heiba, 504 ditto China, 2089 Tanisjepa, 50 ditto Tonquin. Tribundalies 1700 general Cabeca Gareu, 15000 Cowiles, 1043 great pounds of Fine Silk, 165000 Red Cloth, 63000 Silpelet, 3000 Sago, 11000 ditto Boe, 11000 ditto Congo, 7700 ditto Peloe, 1100 ditto Siglio, 1300 ditto Bing; 126 Chests and 12 Bundles of Chinese Goods, one kilt and 4 doors with Wares, 23 chests and 3 doors of fine goods [?], wherein 5 chests and one box with printed Wares, besides 5 and still various Goods for Private Parties.

The Admiral of the Bank are 119, E.I.C. Stock closed, [?] 1130, the Mil. Bank 110, Lottery Ann. 98 and 1 half, Bank Ann. 97, ditto 4 per Cent 91, Afr. C. 25, the Exchange Ad. 9, ditto Lond. 7 and 2 half, J.B. 45.

---

The Netherlands

DORDRECHT, 2 August. For this City have arrived the Ships, the Pottas-Vat, Ryckert Hiddes from Olioslotis, the Rachtsholte, Thomas Sanerson, the Henriette, Gilberth Hairiston from Liverpool; Corn. Spiangenis, Jan van Lojendaal, and Jacob Jancke Spaan from Dordrecht; the Salvy, Henibel Hecking from Newcastle, and the Justw, [?] Dick Janssen master from Riga: on the other hand departed the Primeculo, Richard Giecuw, and the Good Goodwill, John Booth toward Whitehaven, the Kaatship, Rich. Pontsby toward Liverpool, the Warwich, Guy Butler toward Sunderland, Thomas Hlammtton toward Liverpool, Add. de Lange toward Bremgerworp, and several others toward Dordrecht.

AMSTERDAM, 2 August. The Ship the Geelie Henght, Commander Pieter Campe, did not remain in Greenland. Gilded [?] are five Greenland [ships] in Tesisl returned within: Klaas Saef with five and a half Fish, 150 Qu., Abr. Ravenby 4 F. 300 Qu., ghette F. 160 Qu., van der Plaats o. F. 270 Qu., and still several Ships from other Places; also for the City Hidde de Vries still one fourth Fish from Davis Strait; and from Greenland Maries Zeeman with 7 and a half Fish and 280 Qu., and Tennis Stoop with 5 F. 300 Qu., with news that the whale fishery is ending, among whom Jacob van Keet, and Albert Schaap toward Smites; Coert Jaspble Knoele, David Davidie, and Iak Koeloflie toward Cadiz and the Strait, and others toward Guinea &c.

Burgomaster and Governors of the City Rotterdam notify [?] all matters August 1721. can [?] City of Justice thereof, shall not be Ardeers, Dayborders, and similar.

The Directors and Head Participants of the Company of Commerce and Issued Project: participated the first July of this Year 1721, by some Period notify, that all the continuing desired, [?] depth. Wednesday, is on Thursday, in the morning at ten hours, where [?] there. There will be offered an Obligation to the Common Office of Leiden, at The Hague register No. 11. Fol. 46. Verio, and concluded on the 11th of June 1708, of 200 Guilders; which the same is not sent to the Author of this Courant John Hoop, Director-maker, Daniel d'Asvedo, Willem van Laren, [?] Huffley shall at Amsterdam sell on Wednesday the 6th of August 1721, in the evening a certain pile of Velvet-Tobacco lying on the Hiedegraght between the Uystregaete Widow's Poldoijsch.

Hendrik Karfyol Miskelayy, shall at Amsterdam sell on Wednesday the 13th [?] a party of European Linens, and Silk Stuffs, 1080 pieces of Anjali Cottas [?], 30 fl. [?] Tansijreps, 10 fl. Therodotus, 210 fl. Douriislden, 470 fl. [?], 480 fl. Amierties, 960 fl. fine Nicquanias, 280 fl. fine finished Batten, 3 [?], and diverse [?], and 380 fl. fine Almoxiynen; beginning at the [?]

One is of the [?] on Wednesday the 13th of August of this year 1721, [?] High in the Heeren-graght West-side, with division Morning, (we Hood), and 24 Roeds good [?] of Wei-Hoo Werder, under the Jurisdiction of the Edie Mog. Gentlemen States of the Land of [?] All the [?]: Are-and-Stay-plays, 13 [?], besides the [?] the same to Speak on [?] Violins and a Bass, under the [?] FUAGAT & Deylige Wei, in the Green Berg, at Amsterdam; by the same Organ, and that [?] and belying Maerder Goeleuten of the King of France, [?] the 10th to East and West Indies, 10 fl.

---

France

FRANCE, 30 July. Saturday evening at 10 o'clock arrived here the Imperial Chamber Messenger with a Courier from Puttenbergh, who the following day being complimented by the Magistrate, continued the journey to his Majesty. At the Holy College a Courier from Sweden has arrived, with news that the King of that kingdom had recovered from his indisposition.

---

Cologne

COLOGNE, 1st August. In due course the Margrave of Brandenburg Baiezth arrived here by Water with his wife. [?] at an inn, and also took another [?] the following day to continue his journey. On the 10th of this month, the Lord Santini, [?] by the Bishop of Roerdam, [?] the Collegiate Church of Trebhold was consecrated, [?] to come hither to take possession of his Nunciature. This week a Courier from Landen, via Kilrath to the Emperor.

---

Hanover

HANOVER, 10 August. [?] of the 2 Foremost Lieutenants of the Emperor, with a Writing for the execution of the Debt Relief, by [?].

---

Great London

GREAT LONDON, 1st [?] Tuesday, Lord St. [?] yesterday [?] by the Lady Jacoba S.H. of the King's House at [?] in the Palace of the Lords in the Walls, where his Majesty [?] given to have, and which he still [?], [?] and the [?] thereof the following, [?] the London and Leyden [?] [?] are [?] to [?] by the newsagency [?]

---

Course of Exchange

The Course of the EXCHANGE is today on Madrid 90 and a half. Cadiz 115 and 9 fourths, Seville 115. Bilbao 90 and a fourth. Lisbon 44 and a half, V. Re. Venice 80 a half to 3. Livorno 92 and a half to 3. Genoa 94 and 1 fourth. Paris sight 41. London 14 Sch. 4 gr. Leipzig current 19.

Notices & Advertisements

On Friday afternoon at 5 o'clock, a Courier arrived from Rome with news that the Archbishop of Cambrai had been elevated to the Purple [Cardinal's rank]. It is said that the Comte de Morville will take the place of this Cardinal as Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs. The Cardinal Archbishop of Cambrai, the Abbé of Coenan, who is said to receive between forty to fifty thousand Livres annually. This evening, the body of the deceased Duke of Bouillon was brought with much ceremony to the Theatines, where the body will be kept for 14 days, and then brought to St. Evreux and buried. It is said that he declared by Testament his Title, Charges, and Estates to the advantage of his Grandson the Prince of Bouillon, in place of his Son the Duke of Alburet; however, it is thought that this Testament will meet with much opposition. The Turkish Ambassador went to Chantilly to see the body of the Duke [?]. On Tuesday, preparations were made to undertake the journey at the end of this week. The rumor that had circulated for some time—that this Ambassador did not receive his passage sufficiently liberally—has been addressed by him in recent days in the presence of several distinguished gentlemen. He invited them for a closer visit, where he first asked them if they also had any debts in this City, upon which they assured him very gently that they regarded themselves as ungrateful, over the provisions which had been given for such stay, to make known that they were all very well cared for, well clothed, and well fed. One now sees the Revenge Arrest of the Parliament of Paris in the matter of the Duke of la Force, which will be reported here below. The Court has finally agreed that those who find themselves on the Ship Pegaffe on the roadstead of the Island of St. Marcoul, on the coast of Normandy, may come into the Kingdom, provided that they are sheared from head to foot, their clothes burned, and they observe Quarantine. P.S. Letters have been received from Marseille, which report that the contagious Disease had again appeared in the City, occurring in 2 Houses next to one another; some persons died, which there again caused good nursing; several prominent Persons had thereupon withdrawn (among them the Brother of the Writer of this Letter) and betaken themselves for safety from the City to their Country estates, and whether this sad plague would again be followed [is uncertain].

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 4th of august, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 94.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 6th of August, 1721

GERMANY AND ADJOINING KINGDOMS

VIENNA, 28 July. A secret council has been held, in which the Emperor was present, and in which matters of religion were deliberated upon; His Imperial Majesty has however [text unclear] so that one would doubt whether his Majesty's resolutions will be of more effect than the previous ones.

REGENSBURG, 28 July. Since the Elector's Minister, Director of the Imperial administration, has not yet returned from the Emperor's court this week, all preparations have been made in his absence to effect the church visitation against England: and meanwhile some Commissioners have gathered at the court of Ministers in great [confusion], on account of the return of the Herr von Reeck from Heidelberg. The Palatinate Minister has so strengthened [his position], that the Direction of the government of Cologne, Director of the Protestant Corps, wishes to have a categorical answer, whether the aforementioned Herr von Reeck has returned to the Palatinate upon orders of the Protestant Corps, or whether he has obtained permission for his departure; the Roman Catholics appear to be embarrassed about this, because they relied upon assurance given by a Roman Minister that His Imperial Majesty would not have approved the transfer of Herr von Reeck to Thaltz, since his return demonstrates that his Commission is no longer valid. The Protestant Minister, in his absence, is expected back in the coming days, as is also Count Metternich, who [text unclear]. The ordinances against the mysterious sickness are executed so strictly throughout Germany, and particularly at Nuremberg, that no soldier shall enter the city without proper documentation; it is even reported that when a few days ago a peddler tried to bring smuggled goods into the city, the watch turned him away with threats, so he [text unclear] among the sentries. A few days ago a Danish Colonel was announced here, who in that Kingdom had already [text unclear] as Lieutenant Colonel, having shot [?], claiming that it was a matter of necessity, and that he would obtain a pardon. Meanwhile the magistrate gentlemen of the city and the judge of the Imperial marshal have received great reproaches on account of the conduct of the servants of the Minister of Bamberg, whom the Herr general [?], which against the government of Bamberg has rebelled, and to the military authorities has been written [complaining]; it is hoped that this matter must come before the Imperial Diet.

LONDON, 1 August. The day before yesterday morning, the Herr Jonathan Trelawny, Lord Bishop of Winchester, died very peacefully of a colic; he was the last of the seven Prelates who were tried by King James II. [Editor's note: Reference to the trial of the Seven Bishops in 1688] with his last [matter] at the throne regarding their conduct in the interest of the north. That same day the Lords of the gentlemen Waller have, and altogether assembled, the [text unclear] of the gentlemen Waller, have, and altogether assembled, [text unclear]...

gave evidence of his innocence; but if that should not be the case, he had still more testimony to make his innocence clear: for in the Council it was noted that the books of the Levant Company should be brought before them the following day; from which the members of the house had [?] taken over, which through the Herr Ailesbury had been reported for the assistance of the house against him before the court in the House of Lords, would be requested to lie there, and the Lords granted that we might see the books [text unclear]...

Those of the house in committee were about the payment of the bills of the [?] and the [?] on the side of all the salaries, etc., discharged with a clause to process [text unclear], which the [?] Company extended to the administration, kept in [text unclear], old [matters] of the [?] [against] [?] per cent interest, as when this Bill was passed, [text unclear] when the Lords granted the committee over the Bill against the [text unclear] discourse, and the books of the Levant Company having been seen, Waller [text unclear] their Lords [text unclear]...

Wednesday's Courant.

and were so haughty that they scarcely deigned to hear him speak, and having received the things which favoured him, which Sir John Blunt had sent to discuss with him about [text unclear], but that Blunt, instead of coming to him, had gone to the country; that he, seeing that Blunt would never come to him despite various requests, went to his Highness's house, that he had warned the Directors concerning the inscriptions of the annuities and purchasable debts, and these had answered him that they were authorized by an Act of Parliament to do so, and would find no peerage in it, and that they understood affairs better than anyone else; that if he had openly opposed their conduct, the stock would have been immediately called in, and he would have been presented through it alone as the defender of the Company; that he judged the Bank of England to be the great cause of all the misery, since the unfortunate investors have begged on their knees to be allowed to inscribe in the loopable annuities tomorrow, which he would have followed by thousands; that he was astonished to see so many wild witnesses against his son-in-law Waller, saying that he had been entangled with no one but him; and concluded by saying that he hoped the Peers would be willing to consider what consequence and what a dangerous precedent it would be for the future, if he should be condemned: but the Lords approved the clause in the general Bill against Ailesbury without any alteration; ordered that he be again taken to the Tower, and that the Advocate for the heirs of the deceased Postmaster General Craggs be heard. That same day Mr. Farrer on behalf of the great Committee reported to the House of Commons on the Bill to raise £30,000, covering the expenses of the Civil List and [?] the corn [?] per year; which with the alterations made therein was approved, and the House ordered it to be filed; nevertheless, merchant traders presented a Request to the House, and requested through their Advocate to be heard against certain difficulties in the Bill for the prevention of the importation of [bleached materials], etc.; which Request was referred to the Committee considering that Bill, and that they, or their Advocate, should be heard before it, when this was approved. Today the Lords examined Sir Carwall and various other witnesses, and heard the Advocate for the heirs of the deceased Postmaster Craggs: the arguments of this Advocate were a discourse on the danger of making an example of the case of the said Craggs, because it would not be consistent with the customs of the Nation to condemn a man after his death; but the Advocate having gone out, the clause against the said Craggs was approved without any opposition or alteration. Those of the House of Commons have passed the Bill to raise £300,000 and to pay the arrears of the Civil List; and have heard the aforementioned merchants on their request against various clauses in the Bill for the prevention of [bleached materials] being brought in by smugglers, etc. It is now expected that Parliament will be prorogued from this coming Tuesday. The stocks of the Bank [are at] 129 1/2, 131; East India Company 137; South Sea [Company] 126; Military Bank 110; Lottery annuities 29 and 1/4 to 1/2; Bank annuities 98; likewise 4 per cent 91; Africa Company 29 and 1/2; the Exchange [at] 9 and 1/4; likewise London 10 and 1/4.

PARIS, 1 August. Since Cardinal du Bois received the Cardinal's hat from the King's hands, there has been no peace in the Council, because he claimed precedence before the other Secretaries of State, and even before the Chancellor; which matter has not been decided here; and since the said Cardinal continues to conduct the ministry of foreign affairs, the Marquis de la Vrillière has since acted as reporter for him. And since there is some difference between the Herr de la Houllaye and the Herr Bon Parra over the plan for the redress of finances, and particularly concerning a point of the purchased goods and united debts since the year 1718, which the aforementioned wants to effect, or resign his office of Comptroller General, it might well be maintained that one of these brothers would succeed him in that dignity; although some say that the Herr Amelot might well undertake this charge, but that gentleman still seems reluctant to such a burdensome office. The Abbot Vivant, Grand Penitentiary, brother of the Abbess, having had the Abbess of Malnoue brought to Bruges two weeks ago, held a conference with the Abbess, in which it was made clear that a large hunting hound called Dragon should be removed from the cloister, because the great violence which it continually caused there was very troublesome to the said Abbess in her devotion, and about which she would probably have complained to His Grace. A female person, to whom her suitor a few weeks ago sent a surgeon [text unclear], of which mention was made in one of our previous [issues], died a few days later from the wound which she had received; but her husband, together with some others, in the presence of the doctor, having opened the body in order to make a report to the Procureur of the King, the latter issued a summons against the doctor, and against the Surgeon of him, because he claimed that the surgeon of the Châtelet alone had the right to make competent reports; and the Criminal Lieutenant condemned him [text unclear]. The surgeon having complained about this to the Faculty, that body took the matter up, and claimed reimbursement; of which two condemned surgeons, one a Commissary of the King's house, who assigned himself to the province of Valais, and the Lieutenant General banished him [text unclear] jurisdiction, the Procureur of the King condemned him in a fine of 100 livres. The place, which has been sold under the guise [of something else] for some days, was over the [text unclear] [?] not much discussion has been caused, and the [text unclear]...

Left Column

[The opening section is heavily corrupted in transcription and cannot be reliably rendered into clear English. It appears to discuss Italian affairs and papal matters under Pope Clement XI, with references to criminal activities and military precautions, but the blackletter transcription contains too many uncertain readings to provide an accurate translation.]

PARIS, 1 August. In the Council of Regency, which was held last Tuesday at the Palais Royal, regarding the most important matters under consideration, no decision was reached; for which reason the decision has been postponed to the coming Sunday. It is assured meanwhile that everything depends upon the Memorials which have been sent to the Cabinet of the late Monsieur Trudaine. The Abbot of St. Albin has been granted the Coadjutorship of the Bishopric of Saon.

Entertainments are here as abundant as if all was revelry. The Italian comedians have opened a theatre on the Pulley of St. Lawrence, and perform a ball three times a week. Many people attend, especially people of rank. Last Sunday the young Celebi was present, dressed in French fashion. It is assured that the construction of this theatre cost more than 40,000 livres.

The Countess of Conflans, who had amused herself for some time by receiving entertainments in her house, and where young people of the court mostly gathered, had some confusion and disorder in her house. Since the Turkish Ambassador arrived on Saturday, the people of the Ambassador's household staged celebrations at the place of the Palace. The Ambassador from Chantilly has returned to this city. His Excellency has been delaying his departure from day to day, but it is assured that he will depart next Monday. The young Celebi would like to remain here some time longer to perfect himself in the French language, but cannot obtain leave from the Ambassador. The marriage of the young Duke of Villars, son of Mademoiselle de Vilette, has been concluded, and the contract [text breaks off].

The Netherlands

BRUSSELS, 4 August. On the first of this month, the Burgomaster of the city of Rotterdam [text becomes unclear and corrupted].

By [publishers] at Amsterdam: The History of France by Monsieur de Mézeray, Historiographer of France, now reprinted with corrections according to the Chronology of the History of France, covering all the Reigns of Louis XIII and Louis XIV [further publication details].

In Amsterdam by Andries van Damme, bookseller on the Rokin: a Meter and Word-book, or an Alphabetical Collection of sacred, humorous, poetical and other materials, carefully compiled by several authors [further details].

Printed at Leiden by FELIX DE KLOPPER, on the Breedestraat. 6 August [1721].

Right Column

CURAÇAO, 5 June. [From Amsterdam arrived with the ship from the coast various goods and ships laden,] all [corrupted text regarding Spanish activities and shipping operations].

NAPLES, 15 July. On Wednesday [a religious ceremony] with haste. [The text becomes corrupt regarding a religious figure and public ceremonies scheduled for Tuesday, when she will be received by the Archbishop.]

ROME, 19 July. On Wednesday [a figure] had a long audience with the Pope regarding dispatches from the King of Spain concerning matters of concern. That afternoon the Count Kinski presented in person a formal letter to the Pope [from the Emperor, regarding his elevation and pontifical authority,] delivered in Latin; [the Pope's clarification was given,] which the mentioned Count then declared [regarding letters received from the Emperor]; Rome and that [an Angel] was welcome. His Holiness responded with observance; the audience granted the Count great reverence. [He visited] the Church of St. Peter and other notable sites [text breaks off].

[Shipping reports, various vessels arrived including]: Pieter Cloek from Riga; [and] others from Greenland with 6 fish from [?] vats, recently [brought]. News arrived that the catch of the Hamburg whaling fleet had been profitable. In the open sea are whalers from Greenland and France recently entered; likewise in the Vlie [7 whalers including] Klaas Stroerops and Swaarte Bok from Kombergen, and Adriaan Frederik Bakker with 7 whales from Greenland.

Stock prices for the Companies: O.I.C. [Dutch East India Company] 750-785; W.I.C. [Dutch West India Company] 103-105½; Subscriptions 970. English securities have no [trade] yet.

THE HAGUE, 8 August. The Lords States of Holland & West Friesland will meet tomorrow. Some news [arrives]: gentlemen from the Commissioners of the Western Company have arrived from Zeeland. The Lords of the Admiralties are likewise to be consulted. Mister Wenthoudt, having a seat in the meeting of the Lords States General, has departed [and] assigned [his duties] to Mister [?] from Zeeland [text becomes unclear].

It is reported that at the coming annual fair [?], there will be offered for sale a pleasant farmstead [in the manner,] approximately 30 morgen of land, called Almsgeelt, situated under Maelland [with further details to be obtained from] John Hogsfeld on the Wael, and in Rotterdam with the Mister Pieter van Kouwesboorg.

FELIX DE KLOPPER, on the Breedestraat

6 August 1721. With Privilege.

Course of Exchange

**EXCHANGE RATES** are today: Madrid 90½; Cadiz [?]; Vlissingen 9-16; Seville 10; Bilbao 90½; Lugano 90½ at sight; Venice 93; Livorno 92¼ to 3[?]; Genoa 9[?] half to 3; Paris [?] 41; London 34 shillings; Leipzig 36; Frankfurt current 20½-9; Danzig 290; Breslau 1 and 2 at sight; Hamburg 14 and 3 at sight; Antwerp 3¼ at 22 percent; Ghent 4¼; Brussels 3½ to [?]; Amsterdam agio (?); Rotterdam 3 [?]; Bank agio at sight.

Notices & Advertisements

On Friday, Madame D[avergne] gave a grand entertainment in the countryside near Paris, which is said to have cost more than 100,000 livres. The meal lasted until midnight; after which a magnificent fireworks display was set off, followed by a ball in a pavilion that was lit up. The arrangement of red lamps, lanterns, and torches, which were placed in countless numbers everywhere and hung in the trees to provide light, was exceedingly fine to behold. The banks of the river were lined with various fine carriages. The greater part of the notable people of the city had gone there, but no one could enter the house or garden except those who had been invited, as the approaches were guarded by a detachment of the Swiss Guard. It is said that Monsieur Davergne has left the Regiment of the French Guard, in Lieutenant's rank; that he has obtained the Government of Navarre in Béarn, and that this position is established at 11,000 livres annually, with the rank of Colonel of the Red [Guard]. The Duke of Aumont is now considered out of danger; on account of which a Thanksgiving was sung in music at his chapel at St. George's.

On Monday, 11 August 1721, there will be offered for sale in The Hague the large property, cultivated lands, and other holdings of approximately [?] morgen near The Hague; likewise two other parcels of land. Further details may be seen in the printed bills available in Amsterdam at the house of Mister Maarten Schilperoon on the Wael, and in Rotterdam with Michael Philips.

On Tuesday, 19 August 1721, will be sold in Amsterdam [various household furnishings], including costly rare Delft and Chinese porcelains, large copper vessels, passage doors, many gilded [?] in half [?]; silk fabrics, linen goods, and various fine worked pieces.

On Monday, 27 August 1721, at 6 o'clock, will be sold the estate of [?], including the manor house, stables, coach house, etc., further timber works, with [various components], named [?] on [?]; hobby-horse and pastureland, and 2 orchards, together with the aforementioned [?] pastureland and one orchard, situated near the [?], to be used as before. For further information, inquire in Amsterdam or with Everhard Visser, Notary and Broker in Utrecht.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 6th of august, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 95.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 8th of August, 1721
West Indies

CURAÇAO, 1 June. Word has reached here that no magistrates from Amsterdam have arrived; as was perhaps to be expected given the existing circumstances, the ship of Captain Cooy may well have been delayed. From Saint-Domingue and other nearby islands various small vessels have arrived here; as well as some engaged in trade from the Caracas Coast, since the Spanish Governor there has still encountered considerable difficulties in costs and initially could not get goods down from above: on the great coast there still lie the ships of Wijs, Hountaugh, and several English [vessels], Jan Corn., and some others; however the first 2 aforementioned ships are expected here within 3 weeks; and those of François Zermainois and Matthias Roos, which have already arrived from that coast here, [will] soon depart for Amsterdam and Zeeland. Since Spanish privateers still make the coast unsafe, ships bound for Amsterdam may well experience further delays, as has been experienced before.

[Text continues with reports of various ships departing for Guinea, Angola, the Coast of Elmina, and other destinations, including vessels captained by Mederik Souparé, Jaan Malveres, Joh. Porties of Amsterdam, Robb. Schollay of Hamburg, Jan Hol of Kristiania, Ant. Broug of Limerick, and John Henione, with others bound for the Baltic, Bordeaux, and other ports.] No report here of Sallee or Algerian rovers.

Italy

NAPLES, 15 July. Wednesday evening late, the [wife/widow?] of the Ostend merchant of Amsterdam arrived here: saluted with cannon fire from all the fortresses; the following day her return was complimented by all the ladies of this city: Sunday morning the appointed deputies of the city, together with the free commons of the people, welcomed Her Excellency; and on Tuesday she will be complimented by the foreign ministers and all the nobility. Prince Ottajano Médici finds himself still here.

ROME, 19 July. Sunday Cardinal Aquaviva had a long audience with the Pope, and there was read a letter from the King of Spain himself, written to felicitate His Holiness on the favorable developments regarding the Pontifical Dignity. That afternoon Count Kinsky had his audience of congratulation with His Holiness with the customary formalities: this Count presented on behalf of the Pope his contribution of respect, together with a gracious letter from the Emperor, to wish His Holiness joy with his election. This Cardinal—these presentations were sent with a fine marquee in the Latin language, to which His Holiness graciously responded with terms expressing much good will in prayers for the Emperor; [the audience concluded with] the Pope sending to the Count the customary present, consisting of two pounds of reliquaries of a saint, many other relics, a painting or tapestry with the representation of St. Peter, etc. [Text becomes fragmentary and difficult to render coherently] ...the Chancellery and Pontifical Table the order which he received from the Emperor regarding his offices; thereupon he is bound as Grand Chancellor. Meanwhile his successor; whereupon His Holiness with obliging terms regarding this matter answered: the audience being ended, the Pope sent to that Count the customary letters, including—and afterward the Cardinal visited the Church of St. Peter and subsequently Cardinals Conti and Bindo the Secretary of State were also present at the blessing; he prepared himself to visit with the Dignitaries. Monday the Pope held a private reception. Tuesday the Ambassador of Portugal delivered the letter of felicitation from his King to the Pope. Wednesday also...

Purple has been conferred on the Abbot da Bois, Archbishop of Camerlik; and [?] Alexander Albany, and Canon there; but since His Holiness is the successor of Clement XI, by request it is given according to custom regarding the deceased Pope's nephew—over the arranged inheritance of the Emperor, to preserve the right to maintain the excellency. The Pontifical Ambassador at his court has been sent there with the Purple. The Office of Clerk of the Chamber, [?], the Pope again held a consultation in which His Holiness appointed to the Cardinal Albani. The copresidents Cardinals Albani have agreed to clear up all misunderstandings on the understanding that the eldest henceforth shall be the Cardinal Camerlengo, and the other the Cardinal Albani. Cardinal de Rohan sent the Pope a letter of felicitation regarding His Holiness's appointment to the Pontificate of Cardinal de Nullem over [?]; but it is not yet known whether His Holiness will answer the same. Today Cardinals Conti and [?] had their first audience with the Pope.

GENOA, 20 July. The Empress is glad finally to depart, since the Justiniani family declared all their debts settled, and did not request delay until he had sold all his palaces; meanwhile he strives to pay off as much as possible, having placed his son in London to that end, to conduct and [?] his business there.

The United Provinces

25 July. The previous Sunday, the third in this month, the Doge with the customary accompanying chapel heard mass in the church of the Capuchin Fathers, [?], to pray for the guarantee of the Republic's finances in the year 1575, when this fine report was brought to the Fathers; and this ceremony was followed by a procession in the church. The Signior Doge, several brigadiers, [?] Ambassador at the court of Spain, has prepared himself to depart shortly. The noble Francisco Petaro has been appointed by the Senate as Admiral of the Ship-Fleet. Word comes from [?] that considerable damage was caused by [?], with golden [?] recovered.

Portugal

LISBON, 17 July. [?] Two from Bahia [text breaks off]

---

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 23 July. From Neustadt a courier has arrived here again; what he brings is being read, and the coastal plenipotentiaries are requesting certain provisions. The peace remains steadfast by continuation, with only two points still in relief, concerning the Limerick question, as well as otherwise. Admiral Norris has sent a courier to the British court, with which the French minister the Honorable Campredon has given a large packet of letters for his court to the French minister the Honorable Poufsin. Their Royal Majesties find themselves in perfect health. Regarding the propositions of the Neustadt congress, the Senate has held some consultations with His Majesty, after which the said courier with dispatches has been sent toward Neustadt; it is now held firm that the preliminaries will be signed by both sides. The Honorable Mornier has returned here from the baths of Aachen. Count Horn still keeps to his chamber, due to a complaint in his leg. The French and English ministers frequently gather together and await couriers from their courts.

Germany & Neighboring Realms

REGENSBURG, 31 July. The day before yesterday there was circulated here a lengthy memoir of all the grievances of the Protestants in the House-domain of Rechtsbourg, along with the reparations that, according to the order of the Emperor, by the Hartog Gustaf Samuel Leopold were established there on the basis of the circular letter of 3 April last written to all the offices; which is quite a large matter, as the continual assertion of the Electoral Palatine court [claims] as if everything had been unsettled in the Netherlands—although in truth one finds such matters contrary in regard to several unmentioned points: which gives reason that the said Hartog regarding the religious execution of [?] old heretics is ordered to proceed further. Also there has come out the printed grievance of the Knight of the Stead Body in the Empire of Worms, containing that a certain preacher of Waldenhottelheim, called Casty, has not hesitated, some years ago, with the farmers of his village armed to overcome the same; and therein [?] to cause to grow up, not regarding the instructions, [?] all Reformed; which deed, in the beginning concealed by the Roman ministers, is now fully reproached. Some days ago the Countess of Lesinsky from Italy, and last from Loreto, arrived here, and since she is related to prominent imperial ministers, much honor has been shown her: by the Count of Wratislaus and the Baron of Plietenberg she has also been visited, banquets and musical concerts having been held; the Cardinal of Saxony-Zeitz having been present there with the young Prince; this morning she has arranged the journey toward Bohemia, having been escorted part way by the said count. Some days ago a chapel fell in with planks for some distance, by which a child of [?] years was buried and killed. The Electoral ministerial master is expected to be greeted in the beginning of the coming week.

France

PARIS, 6 August. The Count de Rouffy, whom one thought [?], has been reinstated. The vicar of St. Paul, being an appellant, brought on the 28th previous the sacrament to the Abbot Mousier, almoner of the bishops of Xaintes, who is a confessor; before the sick received the sacrament, they protested that they would [?] the valley [?], that in the confirmed rule of the [?] for all Catholics [?], One expects that today at the Sorbonne the meeting of [?] of the month, which is very rare, may be held between the 3 parties. M. de l'Orangerie, clerk of the Honorable de Tarmenis, Guard of the Treasury of the Morning, in this field in the conservation of [?], so that by [?] was shared with certain notables of this city a certain forbidden [?], which they had demanded from the particulars, who did not wish [?] in the annuities that the King has raised on the Gabriel [?]. The Honorable de Pontcarie, first president in the settlement, takes all conceivable precautions on the communication of the contagious disease to prevent it, to which end he has magazines with provisions erected to investigate the necessity, which cost will amount to more than 30,000 livres. The court of Spain has caused some troops to march toward Biscay, Navarre, and the other neighboring provinces of this region, to improve the families, and to prevent the communication of the contagious disease. The King of Spain—in this knowing the reform to be done he sent his troops and those in company to bring them, to always hold 15,000 men on foot and 15,000 on horse in service. The Honorable de Vaurray, intendant of the Marines in Provence, who finds himself in this city, in these days in the church of the closed Augustines with a celebrity was overcome. Follows the report Anselm of Walch, the Parliament of Paris in the matter of the Hartog de France.

THE COURT, the same [?], and having observed all things, as well as the request and supplication of Deans and headsmen of the spicers and apothecaries' guild, without [occupying itself] with the petitions of Charles Crever, Pierre Beynard, Pierre de Prat, and Pierre Landoyt, declared the command of purchase through them [?].

Since Leiden, in the matter of the aforementioned Charles Orient on the 22nd of January of this year 1721 before Notaries and Witnesses, [?] has been restored with full remedy, and as regards the consequences flowing from the legal proceedings, after the aforementioned Charles Orient was brought before this Court with proper documents, he has been found guilty, condemned to pay 100 Livres for the benefit of the King, declared stripped of his [?], and forbidden in the future to meddle in any business [?]; and after the aforementioned Bernard Laulatis and du Part, for the same reason brought before this Court, have likewise been found culpable, the Court condemns them each to 20 Livres fine for the benefit of the Prisoners of this Court, and forbids them on pain of heavier punishment from [?] in the future; condemns the aforementioned Orseint, Laudats, Bernard and du Part, each for their own account to 6000 Livres for damages and interest for the benefit of the aforementioned Deacons of the Apothecaries and Druggists' Guild, as well as each individually in the costs and expenses of Justice [?]; declares the request of the aforementioned Deacons and Head-men of the Druggists and Apothecaries' Guild regarding the goods seized on the 5th of February last to have been properly and rightfully done; and in view of the great number of goods included in the aforementioned total [?], and since a list has been drawn up by the gentlemen Commissioners Rapporteurs on the 1st and 2nd of May last, these shall be sold, and shall be sold by Chouds and Rojoit, Bailiffs of this Court, in such place as they lie, under the supervision of one of the Substitutes of the Procureur General of the King: and from the proceeds of the sale, precise account shall be made of all that shall have come from the boxes of Tea, from the box of Candy, Sugar, the 30 bales of Galagas, the 19 bales of Mobolain, the 2 vats of China [porcelain], the 756 [?] of Zin or [?], and from the lists of leaves for [?] or dyeing, all as mentioned in the aforementioned Process Verbatim of the 14th of May last, and likewise of the monies proceeding from the sale of [?], specified in the same Process Verbatim; and firstly from the monies of this sale, there shall be paid in advance the costs and expenses of Justice; which, after all accountings are made and distributed, two-thirds shall go to the Hotel Dieu of this City as to the Hospital; from which two-thirds shall be given a sum of 1000 Livres to the Franciscan Friars of this City, and an equal sum of 1000 Livres to the Abbey of St. [?] de la Villero; from which third part the aforementioned monies shall be distributed pro rata for the damages and losses caused by the aforementioned Tea, Candy, Sugar, Galagas, Mobolain, China and [?], and shall be distributed by the Deacons and Head-men of the Druggists and Apothecaries' Guild for the benefit of the aforementioned Guild of Druggists and Apothecaries, either toward the payment of their Debts or for the support of the [?] in Leiden in the same Guild, as shall be determined by this Court upon the transfer of the aforementioned monies by the aforementioned Bailiff to the hearing of the Court, hereby authorized, which being done, shall be declared to have been properly and rightfully transferred. THE COURT, having likewise heard the sworn Deacons and Head-men of the Glass and Earthenware-Guild of this City, as intervening parties herein, having based themselves upon the distribution of the aforementioned portion of monies proceeding from the sale of all the goods, has ordered that upon the respective request and reply-briefs by the aforementioned Deacons and Head-men of the Druggists and Apothecaries' Guild, and the aforementioned sworn members of the Glass and Earthenware-Guild, the parties on both sides shall appear before this Court to hear what the said portion, according to their circumstances, shall be applied to, either for the payment of debts or for the ordering of [?] debts, or for distribution to the poor or Deacons of one of these two Guilds, for whose benefit the said portion shall be assigned: and that the monies of the aforementioned portion shall be held in custody by the aforementioned Bailiff of this Court as Keeper of the Confiscated Goods, for the preservation of the law as it shall require. The Court condemns the aforementioned Lanann and Uruvel in the costs made by the aforementioned sworn members of the Glass and Earthenware-Guild; and that [?] may hereafter proceed with greater caution and refrain from [?] in the future, as befits [?].

---

Public Notices

It is hereby made known to all whom it may concern that the Cheese market within the City of Leiden shall be held annually on the 1st of July. The annual Sunday council [?], the camels [?] on the Monday following as aforesaid; hereto forward the Drovers' Monday [?], but on the 19th of July on a Saturday or Sunday next, and the Bear market on Monday eight days thereafter shall be held, and [?].

In Amsterdam there will be sold on the Nieuwedyk one lot of [?] timber, Nerva, Drontheim, 600 Cut Deals, 3400 Nerva and Wyburger Planks, of 14, 20, 24 to 30 feet; 1000 Fire Beams. [?]

---

Paris

According to reports from Paris of Wednesday the 7th of this month, the King of France has been indisposed; His Majesty, finding the opportunity, has summoned the Princes of the Blood to Court. The officers of the Admiralties are [?], and [?].

---

Printed at Leiden on the 8th of August 1721 By FELIX DE KLOPP over the Town Hall

With Privilege.

Notices & Advertisements

On Wednesday the 8th of August of this Year 1721, in the afternoon at the Inn called the Star, under [?], shall be publicly auctioned and sold, being Morning, two Hundred and 54 Cattle good for daily 100 Meadow Hay or Pasture, under the Jurisdiction of the Noble and Mighty Gentlemen States of the Land of Utrecht.

On Wednesday the 13th of August shall be sold in Amsterdam on the Emperor's Canal, opposite the Comedy, the household effects consisting of a new English Shop-Counter with Drawers, various rare Cabinets, Walnut, Olive, and other wood [?], handsome Mirrors, Writing Benches, Chests, and other Chairs, a fine Harpsichord &c.; curious old Chinese and Japanese Porcelain, of all manner of Types, and everything necessary for household use [?].

On Wednesday [the 22nd of August 1721], in the afternoon at [?] o'clock precisely, shall be sold the Seigniory of Darthuys with all its dependencies [?], together with the [?] Dikes and [?], located in East [?], with its [?] [?].

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 8th of august, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 96.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 11th of August, 1721

CURAÇAO, 31 June. Since the departure of Captain Pieter [?] for Amsterdam, it is reported that we have received two ships: one in May from Virginia, another on the [?] from St. Thomas, and with it Captain Thomas Brenning, whose ship is named [?]; on the [?] a bark from Coro, [?], and Philips [?] from the Curaçao; on the 12th another bark from Coro; on the 13th Pieter Breckman; on the 17th Jan Hoorn in a sloop, and on the same night a bark from Puerto Rico; 2 French barks from Martinique; on the 21st Samuël [?] sloop; and a Spanish bark from St. Domingo; on the 23rd a bark from the aforementioned islands [?], and Juliaan de Poort from St. Domingo; on the 24th a bark from [?], and on the 26th another bark from Coro; on the 27th Frans Fary, on the 28th Antonio Middelburgh, and on the second from Puerto Rico; and from Tobago, and have Jan Bolt, also from Puerto Rico; and these goods are not new, except [?] traffic; since then toward the aforementioned coast sailed Captain [?] and [?] and [?] have come Mathys Kegnes; toward Zealand must still go the captain [?] Barnadet, whose ship is yet three days' journey away. Within 4 to 8 weeks this ship the Prophet Daniel of Pieter Luckhousberg, Pieter Bergen, and Dijk Houthuizen shall have to show themselves here yet shortly.

AMAIN.

RADIX, 15 July. To facilitate bringing the new coined money here out of the mints, it is handed over to the proprietors, or those who have borne the expense thereof, such money being dispatched. On the ships of Pieter Breckman, on the [?] his [?] in this bay cruise the 2 Dutch warships; to which Norrbandel Lammeln came to Guinea, and forthwith this time bringing galleons with their warships, and the advice from Vers Crux, the sloop; and from the Guarantee had been set: All notable persons here yet first came from Sr. Louis; Benedetto Penelaro, the St. Bernardo, Francito Rubaim; and Sr. Saporini, all from Genoa; and departed for Panama and Caravacho toward Sr. Sublaris; and on the 15th St. Thomas Roe toward Sr. Lucia; and the Pearls; Captain Ronbound from Porphelen; so there Buoch be his [?] own and Hamburg; 2 Decades, so English, there where the warship of Captain Edward Selingaage and sloop, under which Captain Gomes toward [?], and Captain Radix for Amsterdam, which, together with the Dutch ship the Spanish [?], are Pieter Dickelman, also toward [?], must [?].

FRANCE

Monday's Courant.

It is to be noted: today the King has taken a purgative. Afterwards on Pieter His Majesty the Duke of Orleans in his 43rd year [?] and second [?] (being born on the 2nd August 1674) [?] by which [?] Thomas, by all the Grandes &cc. has been complimented: yet it is reported that the King's indisposition caused that day all [?] to be passed hastily.

The meeting of the Sorbonne was postponed on the first of this Monday because it was forbidden by a Letter de Cachet:

There is whole movement of violence among the Doctors; who have presented a petition to Parliament, underwritten by the circumstantial support of 4 of the principal Advocates; this petition was brought into the Parquet and would be reported prevented. After the Turkish Ambassador had concluded at Chantilly, and there magnificently had been entertained, he went thence to the Harbor of Cette, to board ship there: the King gave him and his son magnificent presents. Madame de Brouwde, mother of the Mr. du Brome, Governor of Brittany, and mother-in-law of the first President, on the 8th [?] the first and was blessed [?]. The guests at the ceremony that Madame d'Averneèdes and against such [?] the 13th [?] had given, were the Duke Regent, the Marechal d'Estrées with his wife, the Great [?], the Vendôme, [?], du Dessont, and the Lord de Pargis, than Duke of Brancas, the Marquis de Biron, Madames de Flavacourt, and [?], and some other persons of the court. Since several days a new list of appellants has come out, wherein are various communities, and a great number of worldly priests, who had not declared themselves by that time; one sees a [?] concerning the audit of the second order, regarding the deduction of the church, on his [?] a work that is very well executed and is orthodox. The marriage of the Marquis de Villars and Mademoiselle Noailles was solemnized on the 15th at Conflans. The utmost ministries sent express couriers on account of the King's health to foreign courts. The funeral of the Duke of Bouillon was very splendid, such as one had not seen in this city for a long time: at the same were 400 children from the hospital; 400 priests, the first each with a torch, and the others with burning candles in hand; and more than 300 of his household, 200 on horseback as on foot, all with burning torches. According to letters from Madrid, the secretary of the British Ambassador Stanhope [Editor's note: John Campbell, 2nd Earl of Stanhope] there on the 20th July last arrived with the ratification of the convention between Great Britain and Spain, concerning Gibraltar &cc.; the said minister departed thence to the Escorial to communicate the same to the Spanish ministers. One writes from Lisbon that the marriage between the Prince [?] of the said Prince of Piedmont, which was with universal [?]

On the 4th of August, the King Thursday mid-day relieved himself, wherefore His Majesty, who was seized with great [?], was thereupon conducted to the palace, where the King on Monday showed remarkable [?] in riding was in the morning not overtaken by it, because one believed that the sickness was against [?] or the Aisle. Besides himself His Majesty at night very much [?], one a double working in the King, in which further yet his [?] one, therefore [?] was smoked, what was brought about [?], one saw [?] for the discovery of the entrails: Majesty, an eagle on the foot to open, which lieutenant for the other generals right so [?] as the bleeding of the evening about ten times was continued, whereupon the King a slow [?] heard, so that His Majesty found himself the following morning considerably better: yet [?] that the sun the great bleedings, and therefore much after Ly-Medicus is of the King, because the execution of his [?] the Majesty and; so comfort holds unfortunate it; 3 days Tuesday night the Regent in the palace of the Tuileries slept, to be able to [?] all the necessary old [?]. Friday morning was of the King's His Majesty to Parliament made, one mentions healed: himself was that then fourth morning one the Sirs of the Packet from the seat [?] themselves thereof the Sirs of the be found health reposed, and learned [?] that not [?] King doubled, and one that all the next morning further good felt, cared let the [?] be ordered by an interval valiant report was made: on the other hand ordered the King by the Noailles the other division, and against the Lower-right from His Majesty very good being malingered; yet the King found himself better through the great evacuation, so of under all of each and then to the King, whereupon the Sirs Southing evening [?] [?] said; that the King outside were ready, so more serves to always the [?] against further is fled, the Monday past morning more attended be. Sunday morning learned [?] Majesty of his healing as a miracle of all, which evening at 6 o'clock [?], that the King outside were ready, so more serves to always the [?] against further is fled, the Monday past morning more attended be. Sunday morning learned [?] Majesty already much better found, and therefore [?] lain, as when the King awaiting being, and sick without King caring and food to feel. Departure around invited; one hoped that Parliament report was made: one the other hand ordered the King by the Noailles the other division, and against the Lower-right

GREAT BRITAIN

LONDON, 3 August. The indisposition of the Princess Amelia, second daughter of the Prince of Wales, has finally improved. Majesty has been, but because the same are without any malignity, it was not necessary to separate the Princess Caroline from her; the Princess Anne is completely recovered therefrom. The sitting of Parliament continues and it is firmly believed that the King will on Saturday next repair to the House of Lords, to prorogue it himself for some weeks hence. On Saturday the gentlemen directors of the South Sea Company, and the time on which the gentleman Aislabie, and the executors of the effects of the late Postmaster General Craggs their estate were to be settled, have extended the time until the 3rd of October: the same days have been appointed by the lawyers, and several alterations made in the manner of importing of goods from the South Sea. At the same time the owners of the public annuities, a gathering of about 300, presented an address at St. James to the King; who received them graciously, and having thanked them for the fidelity they had shown toward his government, declared His Majesty to have already granted patents to several of his subjects, who are interested in the public funds, to do the same in loyal justice. Yesterday the lords finished the discussion concerning the confiscation of a part of the effects of Mr. Aislabie, the late Postmaster General Craggs, and the late governor and directors of the South Sea Company; wherein they made two resolutions; one to better examine their predecessors, or whether perhaps some fraudulent dealings had been made by any of the former directors of the South Sea Company, and to request the return thereof to the Company; the other on attention to the 200 [?] mentioned team, which one can [?] (Rhine) two dealings, which with [?] by the Lower House [?] from their will; the Lords themselves at that time thereby [?] that all that Bill not but as a Money Bill is remarked, where in the Commons [?] not golden would at this some alteration to make. Under [?]

DORDRECHT, 9 August. The following ships have passed by this city: the Paradise, Dirk Hentjes from Hamburg; Thomas Reuts, Stroopsluis, Corn. Botsman, and Gerrit Obbes, all three from Duykelcen; Jan Lugten from Nieuwport; and Jan Thomas and Catharina, William Lewis from Ryck Rulelt [?]. Ready to depart were the Mary, Hannibal Hecking, bound for England; the Jonghte Kind, Jan vander Waal for Utrecht; the St. Pieter Gielly, Corn. Demstede for Doornegen; the Pacht Guly, John Ruirjde for Ablon; the Henriette, Gilbert Hamilton for Liverpool; the John, John Cloake for Ireland; Michiel Lugten for Goch, and several others for Duymlkeken.

AMSTERDAM, 9 August. According to letters from Cadiz, the following ships have sailed thither: between there, ships are departing for Buenos Aires, for which the daily publication was expected from there, as well as the arrival of the Fleet at La Alicante. Last Sunday the Zoological ships, which had brought the Spanish Cardinal's vessel to Livorno, [?]; one also hears that His Excellency Vice-Admiral van Sommeville with his Squadron has entered Alicante, without having named Algieste Roeuvie. At Grand Canary has arrived Capt. Jacob Harmanalt, and at Tenerife Jean Baptisllo Simon from here; at Livorno Capt. Christoffel's Kanis from Petersburg; at Bordeaux Piet. Jacobte van Stetyng; at St. Malo the ship l'Heroicux, and his Daniel and Raphael d'Amelia Machariis, shall return to Amsterdam on Wednesday the 13 August 1721, in the evening at [?] hours, in the Nee in the Risk-flukt through Olivier Codhain. A quantity of extra fine new Varinas Tobacco and Havana Snuff Tobacco. Also a quantity of Lemons and old Yellow Cheese, under the house of the Lord Resident van Oliveira is Royal.

Hendrik Keiby, Broker [Editor's note: auctioneers were called "makkelaars" or brokers in Dutch trade], shall sell at Amsterdam on Wednesday the 13 August 1721, at 2 p.m., at Ground, a quantity of Caroche Lysaten and Silk Stuffs, 100 H. Douriasfen, 120 H. Douriasfen, 120 H. Theindains, 120 H. Douriasfen, 470 fl. partly, 400 H. Amiptjeer, 900 fl. Fine Nicquanias, 200 H. Fine striped Batten, 200 Blaagdoeken, 2000 fine Dagues, and 200 H. Armozynen; lying in the Buakke C.

One is notified that on Wednesday the 13 August of this year 1721, in the afternoon, hopes in the Heuberg named the Star, under Outshoorn, will be sold and disposed of; including owned Lands, two Bond, and 54 Cords of similar good meadow; Hay-Meadow, under the jurisdiction of the Noble Mighty Lords States of the Land of Utrecht.

Jacob Magnus [Broker], shall sell at Amsterdam on Wednesday the 14 August 1721, at 2 p.m., the Estate of, not from the Comedy, divided remainder contents, consisting of new and several Sitting-Comptoir with Drawers, various rare Cabinets, Notewood, a Piere, a rare Fine Mirror, Buffsacks, Coffee, and other Chairs, an extra Harpsichord, etc.; curious old Chinese and Japanese Porcelain; of all kinds of Wine, and what else is necessary for a Household; all viewable daily before the Sale there.

Jacob Magnus [Broker], shall sell at Amsterdam on Thursday the 23 August 1721, at 2 p.m., a good quantity of Colourente, Broade, Blue Silk Broches, and various other Manufactures; as well as Gold, Silver, and Iron, calquoene Jes [?] Glasswork, Etamifes, Sergie du Roix, Suge.

---

Switzerland

BERN, 1 August. Several French Clergy, who have been [?] during the organizations 1000 nearer have been, write here: that the Condition of Ganglione shall motivate to undertake, that one can far support from their Neighbors shall receive; Middles in the open Field, to then Commission Doctor, which a Germany healed lord, to then inform, that in May, days. The true stand is received, and one must give all Volle after, as the intermediaries have been delected, one has no Pastors as that Gustaf Majesty, is departed to Herenthals, after that the Ease morning shall follow. The Duke of Holstein finds himself still here, but shall very soon go to Huningen. Mo. say there the Coalition, which from our Huguenottists at Niuvenlijd stationed is, further there is departing. The combined Peace now is.

---

Vienna, 29 July

Sunday their present Majesty, the Emperor and Empress attended Chapel, thereafter all Apparent, Auditor goes by the Emperor, willing rides toward Hungary to travel, but his Emperor Majesty has given him [permission] rather to wish that he might himself still well hold where from his honored Cardinal the Gual bred already a pledge not deeper: and then great leisure through hing. Reust, Stroopugg on the Bionganje and so Prooinciee. The King Luyten of Poland has already the Emperor clothed to the Ordinances more and more irrevocably over [?] on the Genaasten of the Realm; his Majesty, that newly renewed Trappui veninations also in the is, the beforehand in that so are also the imperial forwarding more first pipetted than before. The outlet as if it would and on the Tolde Frontieres now volgen and is word dored [?] of the Letters of Belgium against gelpooluch; inponderd with some Dime Gode [...] Brandwyir already a arranged on thy ride Kadis, upper Constantinople riding with wapgenmu for ewas [?] the or much insurance of Ardy Person, as a uics. even Cay in Livorno, and on the departure of the Letters cape still a schr [...] in the Lands Deep at Anchor, that one to England se [...] in Sea [...] gone, [?] old [...] Piette Bak toward Archangel, Piettty Baudy toward the Strait, &c.

---

Constantinople, 17 January

The Ministers of the Czar of Great Russia, the [?] on the promised Treaty with the Ottoman Porte wishes to there more slowly liebreide, has itself thus [?] to the Great State expanded, and State remain by the Great Vizia i audience to have.

---

Petersburg, 20 January

His Czarist Majesty has departed to Peterhof, after which the Ease morning shall follow. The Duke of Holstein finds himself still here, but shall very soon go to Huningen. Mo. says there the Coalition, which from our Huguenottists at Niuvenlijd stationed is, further there is departing. The combined Peace now is.

---

Germany & Adjoining Regions

REGENSBURG, 4 August. Yesterday was set by the Ordinary of Bavaria and Biene, if tomorrow to the Cloister of Bretelinge to be present, and this although the houses, to firmly the Heaven out remain. Sick may stop. The Sour-Master will in said Society, since very happily dealt, and tomorrow, and the following day fresh from Prussia, and from the Bishop of Warcelshavn certain by [?] in the Leiden. The determination on the Prescription is.

Since the bekertunstanking, which the Lord very good in the Fallire, has his Dorslag received, which [?] makes fear [...] menignaams might have befallen know [?] Evaluations, and other opportunity [...] it erected is, what to do mean the Manifte Ministers good observe, and good new Instutshheid can tegscuudde the [...] it is even drifty, and goes over Medical gentles din doce, dum bekoording Caractcctcerte, ich bern against Exe cutie van de Vigente [...] which [...] would.

MANNHEIM, 5 August. Today parted a Keisel. Minister [?] the draw away because of the done Religion.

FRANKFURT, 6 August. Saturday the Brunswick Wolfftz-lew was buried.

BERLIN, 6 August. Yesterday various Coaches with Domestics and Baggage of the new Natu Sanctili were here arrived, and his Eminence was a gladness festooned through lying.

Wednesday the 13 August 1721, in the evening at [?] hours, in the Nee in the Risk-flukt through Olivier Codhain, [?] Anugurius, [?] Two subnuyuon = 2 o [?] Douaidieu, 2 70 [?], dit-veel hertiules 130 [?]. Heynnaans, 330 [?]. Benegais gu- [?] Ballen; 200 [?] Finaucoes, 200 it. elle Ballen; 130 it. Alba-[...] elte Ballen; 130 it.

28 June 1721, at 9 p.m., a house of Hugo van Asdrasl [...] to be sold and disposed of an extraordinary Quantity of [?] too der Land of Utrecht.

Wednesday the 13 August 1721, at the house formerly of the Widow Jan Tonros, on [?], consisting two beautiful Leidkanten with their Appurtenances, Inside, Tuchincren, Notewood, Olive, and other Tables; a hay and a fragt Muer-other Chairs, an extra frlacye Tuyg; of 13 cde leg ee Tuyg and half a bisca uiclynen, of all kinds of [?]; Paintings of forward Meditees, Apsl-uage for the Sale viewable there.

[?] and this entertainment for hire of the Widow Diogys Pauw, in the Herberg de Doelduys, with all its Seigniories, Pitelmeuite, and Umobianseere; as well as [?] oo aighunek, with his fine House, Bordeaux new, [?] one appointed Zecvau, Danae Doeadie, Prunale and Notaing in Utrecht, or by the Merciful Lucas de Schojpers.

and Burg of the City of Leiden at this moment to be sold and [?] the measured [?] in tos sem, consisting of a modern House with several Rooms, all their outfittings Stalling for two Horses, great extent to the third day, shall [?] ahead, lying in [?] Porsebe-Bomea; must lie under [?] yet more [?] Land, great extent [...] more Kertel is, and to then include the [?] Reykroop, and [...] do [?] the Church, and the sais-orden.

Thursday the 14 August 1721. The Old House Logements, the morning shall [?], Atode, Bistouer, and Black Cloths; everything merchandise, side Gold, Silver, and 2nd Linden; Frainde caamertandakada, [?]...kagie and Linen, Satins, Nicette, Umpitfen; [...] alongnt somptre until hopes on't Weeft, is deemed to exod uytgcaiten, s itiae in the bould hecte otsaame auld fir Revolutiena stuiten, and divieres tron, that inmidaaayse gebeeuhe none experience dis Ruttabuus above the [...] and [?] the limitaeten matane fetua de Gregorio Lisi oraste di malte, figured.

At Leiden printed by FELIX DE KLOPPER, on the Breedstraat. 11 August 1721. *Privilege granted.

Course of Exchange

**The Count of EXCHANGE RATES** is today at Madrid [?] and one half a quarter, Cadiz 11; and one quarter, Seville 111; Bilbao [?] and one quarter, Lisbon 44 and one quarter, Venice 11; Livorno 91 and one quarter, Genoa 94 and one half; Paris [?] and 7 agile, London 84 Sch. 4 gr. Hm-neve pi and one half, Leipzig current 44 and one agile St. Ilan-zou current 44 and one half Rysdl, Ditto current 44 and one quarter, Danzig current 44 and one half Rysdal, Ditto current 44 and one quarter St. Hamburg 44 and one agile St. Am [...] Fredieln se, Hamb Hamb di heyoen 1 and one agile pt Cent. Gent 2 and one quarter, Rotterdam 1 and one [...] Zeland cent. Gitterotm quarter. The Agis with the Back 2 and one year.

Notices & Advertisements

On Wednesday the 10 August 1721, in the afternoon at [?] hours, shall be sold at Zeoon, the Ambazin Lordship of Darthuys, with all its Seignorial rights and territories, by Sir Jantgelow, which in Over-Loonhuys, with [?] distributed Billets seen, and by the Lord Jacob vande Doefl; Further information can be obtained at Amsterdam.

On Saturday the 8 August, in the month of measuring on the Burg of the City of Leiden, runs [will be sold], a mentioned property [?], named the Kaaams, consisting of a modern House, the Orranguye, Game and Tynmansluya, Stalling for two Horses, with Maids in the arranged Puorrt, under the Ambzie of Sortoestade, From new House Schuting lying on it, long measured one hundred forty [?], tiled with sarde of Soerrestade, in the Roosburger Padi; the further still a thick [?] in the Vloss in the Waard, in the Ambzie of Leisdorp; the buyers can see the said places and Forwarded on the common Piacts.

At Amsterdam at the Jakoous van Waerbergh, Corner traders on the Water, is the Ghiafe, u, and shall have the Guard of the North, as also the Kerole [?] in the Crown of Elzire, & deysm Hereditary & Perabilitaire of the power [...] & Figures, 12. 6 vol. Vita di Sitio Peniochas Romano [written by Gorgue [?]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 11th of august, 1721. — With Privilege.
