Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 29th of August, 1721

Friday's Gazette.

West Indies

SURINAME, 15 June. On the 11th of this month, the ship De Graauw-Oost, Captain Jan Bruyn of Amsterdam, was cleared from this port, along with all the other ships from our anchorage. On the 7th of this month, at 2 o'clock in the afternoon, an earthquake was felt here at the French Company's office and at other locations; it lasted about half an hour in the afternoon, which caused great alarm among the people, as such a phenomenon had never before been experienced in this land. Several ships anchored here, namely those of Seeven van Lind, Jacob de Jong, Pieter Cornelisde Olem, and the aforementioned ship of Captain Jan Bruyn, are scheduled to depart from here to Amsterdam on 17 July.

Italy

NAPLES, 5 August. Wednesday morning, the Duchess of Montalcone, daughter of the King of Sicily, paid a visit with a distinguished retinue to our Viceroy, on the occasion of [?]. The Viceroy assembled all the civil and military officers, and a ceremony was held in the chapel of the mentioned Duke. The fortifications of Gaeta and Cape [?] were inspected; orders were issued; and it was decided to send a good number of soldiers to those places. The Viceroy also gave orders that cannon and other military supplies be sent to those locations, which had been held in reserve for the war in Sicily.

ROME, 9 August. On Sunday, the day before the Feast of St. Dominicus and St. Ignatius, the Pope dedicated the church to these holy saints with great ceremony. [The text becomes increasingly corrupted here.] On the same day, Cardinal Conti received the insignia of Grand Penitentiary of the Church of St. Peter. Various ecclesiastical processions and ceremonies were observed. The Pope decided to send a minister to the Congress [?]. Cardinal Brancaccio and others were involved in various religious ceremonies at the convents and churches, particularly those of the Dominican Order.

9 August

The King, our [?], having made provisions regarding the death of the Prince of Piedmont, has for one year increased all imports, which the Magistrate of Rotterdam has raised by one third.

Milan, 12 August

Good news has been received here that the Emperor has reduced the daily contributions by 300 scudi; however, this reduction will only begin from the coming month of November. The King of Sardinia has ordered good horses to be purchased, which pleases certain speculators.

Milan, 13 August

By imperial order, all regiments quartered in this duchy must remain for the time being and not depart without further notice, for which the officers have received the necessary recruitment funds from the war treasury. There is some concern here that the Duke of Savoy, by virtue of his good relations with this State, may not prevent further French incursion into these troubled regions.

Above the city, a meteor was observed in the sky, which some people interpreted as a sign that certain imperial regiments from Misocolumbia [?] and other Italian places might be coming here to make way for other troops already expected from Tyrol. An imperial commissioner is busy assembling these troops and preparing them.

Bologna, 11 August

Sunday the Prince and Princess of Modena arrived here, and took lodgings with Senator Caprara; that same evening, and the following evening, their Highnesses attended the Opera; Tuesday evening they were entertained by the Marquis Benoiglioli and the community at the Opera, and from there they departed for the baths of Lucca. The aforementioned Prince takes the title of Count of St. Felix.

Bologna, 15 August

Sunday the Prince and Princess of Montecorona, and their Majesty in the State chamber of Capua [?]: that evening and the following evening, their Highnesses attended the Opera; Tuesday evening they were entertained by the Marquis Beauroglia at his house with a fine meal and concert of music; Thursday they attended the Opera, and from there they departed for the baths of Lucca. The aforementioned Prince uses the title of Count of St. Felix.

Venice, 16 August

For some days the weather has been calm, and now it is fair and warm, so that there is still hope of controlling the grain [supply?]. [The text becomes increasingly corrupted.] A procession was held with the Pope's participation on the entire route, ending at the Church of St. Mark, and today, the Feast of St. Roc, His Excellency [?] passed by in fine procession. The body of the saint, which is preserved in the church, was visited; among all the ancient and modern paintings, the church appeared splendidly decorated on this occasion.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 13 AUGUST

Regarding an expedition by Admiral Norris, there is considerable speculation, since it is said that not only has the fleet been gathered, but the King has held long councils to attempt [?]. It appears that some small galleys and other vessels with 300 men remain in the King's service; a plan is being made for 1000 men to join the fleet, and the War Minister has deemed it necessary to command this with full authority. Everything remains [?] as the Czar [?]; the King's command is firm, though it is said that [?]. The War Minister, Count Fyttig, has returned from the baths. The Great Britain Minister, the Messrs. Ducken and Dumeter, are [?].

Germany & Neighboring Realms

VIENNA, 14 AUGUST

Wednesday, Thursday, and [Friday?], His Imperial Majesty amused himself with hunting. The [?], with the name Maria, together with [?] and the widow of the Archduchess and the Countess widow of Lorraine, arrived at the great hunt, and the Imperial Majesty had a pleasant time. [The text becomes too corrupted here for reliable translation.] Various ceremonies and preparations were made regarding the imperial household and courts, but the exact details cannot be clearly determined from this garbled transcription.

Year 1721

OTTOMAN EMPIRE

Previously, it was reported that the Tatars continued their plundering in Podolia day after day, having not only pillaged various places and committed other uninterrupted outrages, but moreover carried off a great number of captives from Slavonia, without the Commandant stationed at Kaminiec being able to prevent it; the said Commandant has therefore turned to the Turkish Officer at Kocim with complaints, making clear to him that his promises had brought no peace whatsoever. When this matter was repeated some time later, it was found that he was obliged to employ reprisals against this rabble, [threatening] that all those who came into his power would be hanged. It is reported that the Prince of Borghese at Naples has acted with great diligence in his office, whereby he has gained the very strong support of the nobles, clergy, and common people; hence, at the beginning of his appointment, he has generously granted the privilege of double salary to all his officials and bishops. Nevertheless, His Excellency has been strictly forbidden by imperial command to receive any generous gifts or presents, &c., of any kind from any Neapolitan, whether clergy, secular, military, or common folk. The said Viceroy has by imperial order remitted certain extraordinary taxes which the common people had borne, and there is talk of forming a grand alliance so that, should the Turks make any advance, they might be opposed; thereby to be in a position to inflict damage upon them. All the Imperial regiments are being brought up to full strength with all necessary equipment; orders have been given to the Imperial treasury to furnish the necessary funds to the regiments for their recruitment. It is said that Prince Eugene of Savoy has gained permission to increase the Imperial force in Italy to 36,000 men. There is again mention that the marriage between the Archduchess Adelheid and the Crown Prince of Bavaria may take place; whereby the Electoral house might be all the more drawn into Imperial interests, so as to be in a position to take over troops of their own in case of necessity.

REGENSBURG, 31 August.

At the previous Monday sitting of the Diet, various matters of importance were discussed, and much was spoken of concerning great numbers of memorials which had been renewed and submitted by the Reich Cities and Monasteries for moderation; in their requests, for which there was willingness to grant, although such matters were contrary to the decision taken, they were deferred regarding the augmentation of the regiments of [?]. The Protestant ministry, which has newly pressed for the continuation of the residence of Lord Reck in the Palatinate, to the great displeasure of the Roman Catholics, has been notified thereof by the latest post and is discussing further measures to be taken. In regard to other representations which they have undertaken concerning matters in the Palatinate with Lord Reck, they still require some clarifications and are drawing up an imperial memorial to be forwarded; against which the grievances in the Palatinate have been set aside, and those which remain are to be brought in their prescribed representation. It appears that they intend to abolish certain banished persons who remain banned, in order that this matter might also be undertaken in the name of their principals. The Cardinal of Savona, who has not yet left this city, is in constant conference both with secular and ecclesiastical ministers, and it is expected shortly that His Eminence may make a proposal to the Diet toward this end.

In Poland, meanwhile, a Te Deum was also sung; in the evening [celebrations were held] and they were adored, with public ceremonies at Riga and in other towns. Some wished to assert that the affair regarding the Papists and the Commandant in the Council of Regency would be settled, while others dispute this; the rumor is widespread that some houses and estates shall not be sold, inasmuch as these are held to be such as to provide advantages to certain persons. The revenue of the provinces, which was anticipated at 6,000 livres in declarations, is reckoned to be around 300,000. The musicians who on Wednesday sang the Te Deum of Lord [?] Campan in the Church of the Cloisters, remembering their great master, Lord Lully, sang most movingly; here the De Profundis in music. The merchants of [?], and all their goods, nearly fell; a Te Deum was sung in the great church, and they began to sell their goods. On the nights of the 18th and 19th, the Duke of Maine held a celebration with many cannon discharges, which continued the following day; the Marshal of Villars was present. From Lisbon, letters have been received concerning the constitution, whereby the Marquis de Cantacuzene, Envoy of the Kings of Sardinia and Tuscany, to the King of Portugal, all have sent their responses; His Excellency is sending to the Infanta Donna Francisca the portrait of the Prince of Piedmont; which has been chosen with the King's permission; whereupon the marriage of the aforementioned Prince with this princess is no longer doubted. The Popes have had three Guardians. It is reported [?]

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, 27 August.

Letters were received from Lisbon concerning Pernambuco of 23 March from Bahia, that the fleet had loaded a very great quantity of goods, which was loaded in 4 convoys; but since this consisted of aforementioned ships, not as much sugar and tobacco, &c., would be brought as was first thought. From San Chint in Pariarem, letters were received of 7 July that the drought there had continued, and that this was the cause of a poor harvest of grains and other crops, which brought great damage there; whereby the inhabitants suffered greatly. The ship Elizabeth, Captain Mattheus Tuly, was expected to depart thence toward here on 17 July. At St. Martin, word was received that Thomas Jungsen of Bergen and Mattheus Blok of here, the schooner Wyngaard Hendrik Kieys, had departed from St. Martin toward Honfleur. On the Rhine near Rouen were 3 ships from Hamburg, as well as Frieand Mooy of Danzig, skipper Laurens Voorburg, lay there bound for Rotterdam. At Texel have arrived several ships, among them 2 from Greenland, both with whale oil, commanded by Captain Robertsen from St. Gme.

Spain

CADIZ, 5 August.

The ship Antelope and the mail vessel Kamalen on 9 [?], after various days between [?] houses on the routes have departed into the sea under sail. Francisco, of the captain Ginief, [?] Hope. Pedro Didak, the ship Nicolas, of skipper Edward Dondin, toward [?]; the ship Waldbon, both bound for Cork; the Margarita, [?] Elizah Robinson, [?], toward Madeira; [?] Nicolas Fordal, toward Antigua; John Tillund, toward Gibraltar. The English warship and 16 merchant vessels, which here sailed this year from the Cartagena, [?] and two other ships with [?]; and also the ship [?] intended to be [?] and their [?], and toward Morocco or [?]; and on the Cartagena again sent, but it [?]

MADRID, 12 August.

France

PARIS, 22 August.

GRAIN MARKET, 3 August.

The States of Holland and West Frisia have today, according to credentials, reassembled. Lord Badelcourt of Brussels was very clearly told how Gluck would be placed tomorrow by some gentlemen of the government to report from his committee [?], and subsequently to and said going to Amsterdam [continuing].

Renewal of the leases of the [?] class for the expiration of the leases [?] in October from his committee [?] to the examination of two [?] for the expiration of the [?], among which on the third [?] of the second [?] at the comp of his on three [?] which in October [?] expires, so order has been laid to a certain five morgen, and yet another one and a half morgen Luzerland, being one hundred and [?] repair of the house in the second August and of Cinck, in his Ed. lifetime burgomaster of the city of Gouda, and last placed as [?] of the bridges that thereupon fell due, since 20 January 1701, to Philippus Gertrun Verhoer. In the almshouse on Amsterdam, next Monday shall begin again on the first of September at one o'clock.

**EXCHANGE RATES:** The course of the EXCHANGE today: Madrid 85½; Bilbao 89½; Cadiz 114s. 3s. 4d.; Seville 110¼; Lisbon 44½; Livorno 92¾; Genoa 95; London 94s. 5s. 1½ gr.; Paris [?] 41; Leipzig cou- zalt; St. Danzig 191; Hamburg 12⅔; St. Gent 2¼; Antwerp 2 1/16 x ¼ per C; Brussels 2; Zeeand 2¼; Rotterdam 2 schf. Notices & Advertisements

On Monday, Lord Bairchet read a poetical composition before the French Academy concerning the King's recovery of health, the expression of which was so moving that not one of the listeners could refrain from tears. The pages of the King's great hall have yesterday [?]. On the part of Doctor Copyias, notice is given that the verification for the coming month of September shall take place on the known punishments [?]. Note: let every one be warned that the lottery of the Journal of the 1st Class runs [?] to the 100th Class of the Palace: which people deserve to have [?] to a certain good inherited freehold estate, located in below Hasnlt; concerning the seigniory of Vliet, registered by order of Lord Gerard Cincq, in his [?] with his legitimate son's daughter, the late Alicia Cincq, as well as [?] of the deceased Alicia Cincq, and Philippina Gerrus V[?] for Gerard Cincq, hereby notice is given that within the [?] weeks of the aforementioned city of Gouda they shall make themselves known, and [?]; and if no one else shall come forward, the aforesaid two parties of leaselands and the aforementioned fruits thereof shall be disposed of to the city of Gouda, as having married the aforementioned Philippina Gertrus Ver[?]. The coaches from Amsterdam to The Hague and from The Hague in September, twice daily, in the morning at 6 and in the afternoon at [?]. Daily, one is requested to take [?], and one is pleased to sell a new, neatly furnished modern house with kitchen, of which the [?] departed or in other manner [?] in a garden with two play houses and diverse [?], furthermore [?] approximately one morgen of land; standing and [?] the sand; someone [?] the house and conditions [?] the aforementioned catalogue, and the Leyden by Monsieur Hendrik Koot, residing in Rudiert Kock Niehoizlhai, shall be auctioned in Amsterdam at the White Swan in the afternoon at 2 [?] 6000 greens, new decels of 10 to 12 feet; 3000 [?] 3000 stuivers; ditto; 1100 Weyburger: ditto of 18, 20 to 24 feet, and [?] Gertri Shoemaker, broker, shall sell in Amsterdam on Monday d[?] Lemburg in the Brackish Ground, a party of extra fine new [?] Felkoe, being the [?] and Saturday and Monday for cash in the aforementioned. At Amsterdam at the Janlkoons of Wacibergen, book merchants on the [?] Vermillion, by Lord Abraham van St. Clara, second figure. The wine brokers [?] in Dordrecht shall on Friday the 7 [?] on the adjacent New Street sell a party of about 70 to 80 [?] on the quay-haven, which on the day of sale are free to [?]. The broker Dirk [?] hereby advertises likewise that he shall [?] [?] situated on the great market at Rotterdam, [?] departed. Among which some [?] the aforementioned two parties of leaselands and the aforementioned fruits thereof [?] to be disposed of, as he has been instructed, and done [?]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 29th of august, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 1st of September, 1721
Cadiz, August 5

On the 18th of this month, there arrived in this bay the Antilope, John Evans, the Thomas, Robert Williams, and the Matiana, Santiago Conway, all three English vessels from the Canaries. The English squadron rejoiced here under the command of Sommelsadyk, having departed from Alicante some days earlier between Malaga and the Strait, and in that vicinity the Algerine corsairs were encountered; of 300 Algerines, 200 were captured in the engagement, which prevented the corsairs from Algiers from venturing out to sea. On the eighteenth of this month also arrived the Jalis, Eugenia, Gran Martin, from Hamburg; N.S. de Misericordia, Captain Gimel, from Genoa; as well as Juan Bañutia Jordaan, the Hope, Pedro Delaya, both French vessels from the Canaries; and the French ship St. Nicholas from [?]. Likewise, the following departed from here: Edward Dondins, toward Bristol; the sloop Concordia, Elsevau Joly, toward Scotland; the Providentia, Diego Noland, the Arent, Ette-man Wellhoen, both toward the Canaries; the Anna Maria, George Brum, toward Cork; the Margarita, John Matthew, toward Stockholm; the St. George, Efraim Kibbington, toward [?]; the Providentia, Nathaniel Bilby, toward Madeira; Milia Audrik, toward St. Lucar; all English vessels from Cadiz. Nicolaas Fonthal, toward Amsterdam; and the English warship Trial, John Talland, toward Gibraltar. Remaining in this bay are the English warship Newcastle, E. Edwin Sullingthgam, and 16 merchant vessels of that nationality, 9 French, 5 Dutch, and 2 Danish.

The mentioned ship of Captain Daltira, departing at the beginning of August this year from Caracas, sailed with two other ships from Laguayta which must proceed toward New Spain, and another still lying there, which is to depart in July of the next month. There are 5 register ships [official merchant vessels] between them: one toward Cuba with cargo for Santo Domingo and Porto Rico; one toward Curaçao, protected at Margarita and Trinidad de Barlovento; one toward Maracaibo; one toward Campeche with cargo for Havana; and one toward Caracas. For the past four or five days there has been heavy weather in this bay, though one hopes it will improve at sea.

---

Madrid, August 12

---

Paris, August 25

The King proceeded last Friday to the Abbey of St. Genevieve, attended by the Duke Regent, the Dukes of Chartres and Bourbon, the Prince of the Count of Clermont and the Marshal Villeroy. The streets through which His Majesty passed were adorned with decorations, which were constantly being renewed. This music was a pleasing accompaniment. The King on the 16th of this month proceeded to the Church of Notre-Dame; His Majesty continues daily to increase in strength and vigor. This morning, which was the day of St. Louis, the Coadjutor of the diocese provided at the Church of Notre-Dame, His Majesty daily increasing in strength and vigor. This morning, the day of St. Louis, the Coadjutor of the diocese presented to the King a bouquet consisting of an orange tree in full bloom with large fruits. The flowers thereof were exchanged for rock candy, and the roots of this tree were preserved in rock candy. The Carmelite monks will henceforth daily offer prayers and intercessions for this tree in place of the former practice, where they will sing Mass for His Majesty. This evening an extraordinary fine musical concert with instruments will be held, which concert will be divided into two parts. At the end of the first part there will be a display of the Artificial fireworks, with arrangements and conferences; under the direction of this ingenious work of particularly inventive character; ordered by the Duke of Montenuir, Master of the Royal Chamber, and executed by Monsieur Fendin [?], superintendent of the King's pleasures. The structure of these fireworks is fifty feet high, presenting a chapel wherein the blessed sacrifice of the Catholic order is represented. The middle section is [?]; the construction is antique, low in its lower part, and above are openings representing a temple, which are adorned with the Palace of Vulcan; in the middle stands a Vulcan himself with his hammer in hand, looking toward Diana who appears on his right side in a cloud, and above named for the great Monarch. Eight bronze cannons, which are positioned at Montbert, are placed [beneath] the frontispiece of Vulcan's workshop, with above it the head of Medusa on a trophy. Above the cornispiece Mars on the right and Minerva on the left side, now with the arms which Vulcan forges under the direction of Louis XI...

[Text becomes increasingly difficult to read in original transcription]

---

Great Britain

LONDON, August 26

It is assured that the King will not go to Hampton Court this year as was rumored, but that His Majesty will spend the remainder of this summer at Kensington. Since the Prince and Princess of Wales have been at their country residence, the young Prince William Augustus is now in daily education. It is said that according to the advice of the physician Field Hamilton, the former of Mr. Whitehead, merchant in Lisbon, on [?] is placed in place of Miss Brown. His London Journal, which was published last Saturday, is the remarkable subject of discourse, in the manner of a committee, and what the preceding regulations therein establish...

[Text ends abruptly in source material]

ENGLAND

A certain paper having recently published some matters relating to the late Act of Pardon and Cadet Cameraltum, with tedious preambles regarding the Report which the King laid before his Secret Committee, with a view to continuing that Report from the last third. However, the greater part of the Inspectorate and half of the 40, East India [Company], was this morning in certain quarters or in the office of the Printer, who properly with the Publisher and several followers, which paper they offered for sale along the street, in custody until such time as it was secured; Yet the aforesaid Printer and Publisher, one Wilkins and the other called Pode by name, are still under the examination of the Secretary of State, with safe conduct having been granted; even some believe they know that a certain Lord, who is generally known as the Author of the letter from Cairo, would be one of their sureties. Before the week in the Prison House of Newgate, an experiment was carried out through the efforts of the celebrated Doctor and Surgeon, to carry out the operation upon children, in like manner as is done in Turkey. Two criminals, namely a man and a woman, have undergone this operation, with marks upon each arm and leg, concerning which tales nothing certain can be said.

BARBARY

ST. CROIX, 12 July. Two vessels have been stranded on Breda, from London to the Levant, the cargo from St. Ubes being destined for London, which is completely lost and now has been plundered by the Barbary Corsairs. The other ships of the same fleet have happily continued their voyage, even from Salé they have had to take themselves, to bring themselves out from the Greeks under that danger of other Nations named, although yet 2 or 3 small pirate ships of those are still at sea. The great scarcity of Candia has had to be endured through the dryness of war caused by the height of [?] and made large earlier lengthwise. It goes well with the duel and must here be noted through the dryness of war caused by the height of [?] and made large. Pray that here for it is much full why one hears of the powerlessness to hunger; and many inhabitants this place upon the Pirate Land, even that the grain can be secured, to bring themselves out from the dikes of Salé they have had to take themselves, to bring themselves out from the Greeks under which can now Pray that here for

SPAIN

CADIX, 3 August. The careening of the Dutch East India fluyt, under which all the freight has been brought to Amsterdam, proceeds so quickly, that the directors of some, pursuing those great ships, 300 in all outside the Strait, and afterwards to be brought to the great islands; among which merchant ships are gathered; thus the Sea-spoil is routed through the Narrow. The flotilla of Vista Coels, as they according to the large ones last departed from there in the last of the month of April from there, the master-week and Bildt also been. are furnished with good cash and Money received [?]

STOCKHOLM, 28 August. It is said that Peace is concluded; indeed they will even say that the Treaty having been drawn between New Sweden and now sent is according to the genre down in his one son before his Caesarean Majesty. [Editor's note: This appears to refer to peace negotiations between Sweden and another power.] Ly'l has been in witness as also Wyborg and Kertholm; provided that the concluded Land have, and the Place in political manner shall. Miss of the 200 strong in the Peace desired has, and this more people that one well have lain well.

THE NETHERLANDS

KAMERICK, 20 August. Only yesterday evening around two o'clock, there arrived here 200 saints Thunderstorm with Stones and Hail mixed, where all the Caldrons in which the Linens are fabricated here flooded, through which inundation many of the Working-people are in great danger of drowning; Hailstones fell there weighing one quarter pound [weight]; yet to abundance.

DORDRECHT, 19 August. Before this City have arrived the Pollieon of Bergerac, Simon Ham, from Chatante; the Leusglacht, Groiti de Hamm, from Candastrop, Corn. de Brum, Ary Perella, Jacobus, Corn. Bosman, Ary Kapitain; Jan van Kordendaal, Reyndert Pauliis, Dirck Rooyen, Aert Jan Moorer, Ulle Sikkes, Jacob Luile Saram, Gidrit Obbes, Segert Coenen, Roelof Likkes, Gertrit Cijoen, all from Duykerken; on the contrary have departed the Content, Joseph Hodgson, & William de Doockhem, William Clarke, the Joseph Benjamin, John Graves, the Thomas de Marty, John Scaup, the Mary, John Romige, the Jocq Williams Hamster, the Siebiana and Sophia, Mathias Misson, the Owners Goodwil, John Booth, and the Primeroele, William Davidom, all toward England; the Chandois Sloop, Tobias Joweis, toward London and another toward Dunkirk.

AMSTERDAM, 30 August. The Ship arrived from the East Indies is called the Standaerder, of the Cape of Wallenberg; from there two other Ships from the East Indies are still expected; namely the Ship Weenen and another. According to the Letters from Danzig of 20 August, the English Admiral Morris of the Jeffreys, who resided there, had delivered; to the Magistrate of [?] have presented, and he with the definitive Declaration of the Royal Courage upon the Road would come and satisfaction obtain, concerning the aforesaid affront to one of his Majesty's Ships. Yesterday 15 Experienced Merchant Houses, Simon Aunders, My Fielet, and Wippent Barentie, from Norway; Juraan Jantile from Bilbao, Jop le Fep, from Transeck, Pieter Kirtenen, with 2 Pelts and 200 [?] Barent Hencklikle's Pelts, from Greenland. It is said that yesterday should have arrived Willem de Heert, with 5 Pelts and 210 [?], and Aam de Jonq, both from Greenland.

Bilbao & Cadix together at 30 1/4. Venice 95 3/4. Livorno 95 3/4. Genoa 94 3/4. London 34 sch. 3 1/2. Geneva 96 1/2. Leipzig current 38 3/8. Frankfurt current 139 Rysd. ditto current 34 3/4; Breslau 35 1/4. Danzig 291. Hamburg 13 3/4. Groningen 2 [?]. Antwerp 2 3/4. Brussels 2 1/2. Zealand 1 1/4. Amsterdam 1/4. Bank Agio 3 1/2.

Yesterday's stock prices for the Amsterdam Brokers: East India Company 77; 8 to 70. New India Company 193; 370; 75. English Bank 179 to 148. East India Company 147. South Sea [Company] 143.

THE HAGUE, 21 August. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West-Friesland [repeats the account of the storm from Kamerick above].

Madrid 30. Bilbao 30. Cadix 31 3/8. Seville 32 3/8. Lisbon 32 5/8. Venice 8 [?] 3/4. Livorno 92 3/4. Genoa 94 3/4. London 34 sch. 2 1/2. Geneva [?] 1/2. Danzig [?]. Leipzig current 8 5/8. Yesterday's stock prices: Admiral to East India Company 277 to 89. East India Company 1 [?]; Securities 970 to 75. English Bank 139 to 140. East India Company 1/7, South Sea 141.

FORATEMNAGE, 31 August. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West-Friesland have this afternoon postponed to day after tomorrow 8 days. The licenses of the Chapelle and Jondes, have all been Commissioned to away the Council of States and the Maas departed.

WENEN, 10 August. Sunday the Count of Mikolusch departed from here to Karlsbad, to further the restoration of his health. In this journey his Excellency takes the Lady [?] Commission to execute, which however are not yet known.

On the Loons of the site and the side Glass, for the especially of the Military force after the striking of the old Glass, the 9 October 1721, the Broker Gerrit Schoemaker, at Amsterdam will sell in the spice new fire curious Green Tea, Emperor Tea, Tea Honey, Condoli for Money to be spent; p.m. worth for Money to be spent; and for Money to be spent, on 9 September 1721, in the evening at 3 o'clock, in the Coffee House the Golden Bells, one spice price Cognac Brandy [?].

Sales and Auctions:

Shall be sold in Amsterdam on Tuesday, 9 September 1721, this fine House from wine Bladeburgh, in the [?]; in the [?] street, on the [?] House the former Brewery the Arent.

In Amsterdam at the End these [?] welcome and curious [?] Bruegel, Nicalaaus Berghem, Georgion, Tenierc, de Kemp, Berp, Franckx, Cassels, Boone, Zwartyan, Vervellt, Brekelecamp, Mersn, Elie [?], that stone the Classden [?] is by the aforesaid Midefaelt is obtained. Abraham de Hoodt, sold the elegant libraries of the late W. Aelsdorf for the Court of Holland in Theological of Catalogus is obtained at Amsterdam. In Boom, Haarlem from Kilch. Utrecht from de Water, Antwerp Verdollan, Brussels Setheevern.

17 September 1721. In the following days, on Wednesday the verifiers Burgwelff, at the house of Evert Meys, in the Hope, a panty fine shaw, is another yielded artistic Painting from Cassiaicus, Needlebooks etc., a panty Painting Wall of the aforesaid Broker to be obtained, on the corner of the Weysistts.

It is thought that the Peace is concluded; indeed they will even say that the Treaty having been drawn from New Sweden as [?] [Editor's note: Text is heavily corrupted here] shall before Caesar's Majesty. REGENSBURG, 25 August. Before Friday at the Imperial [court], but after no whole brought forward, as only that new [?] had had [?], to of [?] his [?] [?] comes 5 such deliberation over the Cameras of Wetzlar - and the sought Moderate [?] Boylanden, welcome [?] [?] been met [?] [?] this [?] shall certainly the of the Religion upon the Tag[?] [?] has not same [?], shall of the Catholic [?] worded upon the Roman presented: that [?] Solution and Rome [?] worded upon care seen, as the whole [?] of the two Brothers alone apparel [?] surely lost. by Dike of the Neck from [?] such [?], that to dwell, of the Ditt of the Neck out The Mandates which have been wounded [?], in favour of the Mandates following their [?] the: Execution of this ninth [?] the [?] are of the old Bible in the circle [?].

HANNOVER, 25 August. [Text largely illegible]

Course of Exchange

**Exchange Rates from Amsterdam, 30 August 1721:** **Exchange Rates from The Hague:**

Notices & Advertisements

On Saturday last, an Express arrived from Paris with dispatches to Mademoiselle [?] from [?], with news that the young King was in good health, whereupon this Minister immediately proceeded to the Court at the Escorial to make known the news to the King. This caused great agitation at Court, but on Monday the said Marquis set forth another Courier, being concerned that the King of France should remain so far from Paris; although this has, as stated, greatly reassured His Majesty. The Court continues to remain at the Escorial. It was expected that the Marquis de Leyds would depart to effect the desired reform among the Troops. It is assured that there will remain 100 Battalions, each comprising 500 men, and 200 Squadrons; 200 cavalry units will be maintained, each consisting of 150 horses, totaling in all 65,000 men.

On Tuesday in the evening of the death of Mr. George Caldwell, one of the present Sheriffs, which loss is much lamented by those who knew him for his singular virtues upon which he was particularly noted. A certain wealthy man in the County of York in the Market of Georgetown, of very conspicuous estate, has been sentenced to stand in the Pillory. Yesterday morning at three o'clock there was a sharp encounter upon the River near the Bridge, between the Officers of the Customs and the crew of a vessel which had come from Ostend with smuggled goods, and which lay at anchor: But when these officers had taken aim at them, they cut the cable and spars upon the officers, in such a manner that one was killed and another gravely wounded, whereupon they went up the River through the Customs barriers and continuing were pursued to near Chelsea, where the smugglers passed from view, so that the pursuit was fruitless. It is said that this morning the Magistrates were made ready upon suspicion that they would be accomplices in this undertaking. From Plymouth comes news that the ship Mary from Jamaica has arrived here with 40 privateers and a great quantity of plunder from the Indies. There is here a general desire to know who worthily by the East India Company next month will be reckoned, as the distribution will, some believe, take place in India so that the Company might thereby increase its reputation to cover its Obligations; meanwhile within a few days one sees a general gathering approaching the merchants, and in the house of the aforesaid Company they are actively engaged in distributing the 70 millions profit which the same has received by the Act for the restoration of public Credit upon the approval and stated Expenses, which it is said will amount to about 3 per cent on every 100 in capital stock. From France word is received that there came news from some sickness at Cherbourg in Normandy; where the Intendant of that Place (where daily 3 to 10 people die) is at some distance outside the City upon public authority; however that news nothing certain can be said, that some precaution for medical help has been observed.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 1st of september, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 106.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 3rd of September, 1721

№ 1721.

BARBARY

SALÉ, 12 July. The peaceful merchant ships, one carrying herring and beer from London to Livonia, the other with salt from St. Ubes to London, which were brought into Salé and robbed, have not only been returned from their robbers' nests through other means of justice, but one of the Salé vessels has been driven away and is to be pursued on these journeys. To date, although 2 or 3 small pirate ships from the said Salé are still at sea. The great damage from the drought and other causes which here previously made the situation difficult has now been remedied, bearing great fruit. In this month the price in Triers is four times as high due to robbery, which is why one sees many people dying of hunger from the city; and many inhabitants of this city, as well as those from the countryside, have had to seek help on the borders of three territories, because the linen trade has ceased, since rain fell in that direction.

SPAIN

CÁDIZ, 5 August. The departure of the Holland East India ships, including also those of the College from Amsterdam, which are lying here, proceeds smoothly, and by their diligence 2 or 3 ships daily run to sea and are supplied with provisions through the usual channels. Before the ships, both within and outside the Strait, and even as far as beyond the Algerian rovers have crossed, they have not yet encountered any of those pirate ships. The Flota of the Vera Cruz is watched daily here; and it is said that according to recent reports they departed in late April and will arrive there. In the coming week a good number of ships will again be dispatched here, and supplies will be made ready.

SWEDEN

STOCKHOLM, 20 August. It is said that the Peace to the new town has returned safely; according to reports one expects to work from there, and especially Castellet and Majesty, since common shots, as well as Wieck and Ketsholm, with the flat lands, the last times of this Crown will remain in politics. But since one wishes to weigh the Peace here so powerfully, and this may create suffering and people from whom one has gained something, many people are found who; since this reason is not such, insomuch as the complete security and the further particulars of that Peace are still awaited in trust, many will follow. From Amsterdam is directed by Henry. Fielschle Calf.

GERMANY AND NEIGHBORING KINGDOMS

REGENSBURG, 27 August. According to reports of the Imperial Diet meeting, although no matters of importance were brought forth, except that the new Wolfenbüttel missive had a religious character, to work from its foundation in the College of Princes to take seat, since the deliberation on the matters of the Rhine of Wierschouk and the smoother necessity of further Reichshelds; was again postponed; until the next meeting, when new Expressen would come to complete the Triple, and from the side of the Protestants, the answer to the Royal Resolution of the Roman Catholics would be presented; to accomplish the same, and a given; as the assembled Corps of four or yet two Ministers absence are, shall be complete; meanwhile the whole the same war and plausage bear; the Richtlien daily to root, of the Lord of Keck from the Falls containing; and attending to the fullness which he to those cared for in the Paltz of yet two Ministers absence are, in favor of the Lustered Disorder; according has households, one Executors or huitor-marie to hear; why such is not clearer, one strengthens that those contained orative are on spy of the curleige of years that the Cerzeyliche Minister, and Count of Wels, at his K. V. D. of yet gone has, for the intense of the Kaiser, to the re-convey, since his Ply. Majesty, could not withdraw from following is seen by following huiffe and at Conseilliate belong has; the latest in the Palls made for the Peace, which change one of the Protestants must make Scheppes; that the World stands pal of the opportunities, and to those Protestants no reasoning of lualen over any aureligdaegde given; where to also already said would have been; sedol some duogne tnd best crests couaerd over the daligninge of a special Inchje in quarte, whereupon the foremark of the Authors is, to the Luterse from the Gene-reformed Religion to unite, was by jonunge Protestant All-nities, verigd the probability, much long had found, and ready by there in untersweije omdogten or pun in these conjunction time would one fullsait writ vagt the Protestantum not to be stuck.

HANOVER, 26 August. This morning Prince Frederick departed for Lübenburg, where he will stay for ten days. The next week, which one until now held in sligning, by the Caribersio.

Wednesday's Courant.

Behrens Jewish Bankers, to get them there so that they might discover there the place where they hid their goods, having been fruitless until now; one has dealt with more rigor and now eighteen; 300 that they are now bound hand and foot in one, much so that if they were to be brought to the wise petition or well to the Funibank, because one val tlied they sent other goods out of the Country, in the intention.

BRUNSWICK, 26 August. The coming Duke of Brunswick-Wolfenbüttel has this day moved to Celle, and the Dowager Duchess of that name to visit. Since the holding of also George's turning away from the success, of what is presently held at Neufeld one will soon know; whether it will itself take hand or not.

FRANCE

PARIS, 29 August. The King's health becomes better day by day; His Majesty is brought daily new and various conveniences before the eye. The twenty-fifth, Feast of St. Louis, the Name Day of our young Monarch, proceeded from His Majesty with the compliments of all the Princes, Princesses, and the entire Court. The procession of the Carmelites from the great Cloister, which in the evening of the same day was conducted, in which the Provost of the Merchants, the Captains came to the Palace of the Tuileries: the Clergy celebrated the Mass in the Chapel, where the King attended. In the afternoon the King, accompanied by the Duke of Clermont, the Marshal Duke of Villeroy Governor, and the Bishop of Fiefs Governor of Majesty, heard the Salute. In the evening the Concert began, which the Royal Academy of Music is accustomed to hold every year on this Feast: during this Concert the King held the pre-dinner, after which His Majesty placed himself under a Pavilion that was prepared for him on the Terrace: as soon as the young Monarch was seated, came he the all-joyful marks of subjection and respect of his People, with repeated exclamations for the King: in the middle of the Concert that was held here, the Fireworks were discharged in a good manner as in our previous account is mentioned: after the same was done and the Concert ended, the King betook himself to his apartment, under the same acclamations of the People, just as before which the Duke Regent also saw from his lodging in the Tuileries, under that of the King, where his Royal Highness was, some Ladies, the Knight de Rouillac, and some other Nobles. This Feast, all the Strangers and the Fireworks, is with complete pleasure and satisfaction proceeded, it appears that one of the fireworks makers had made a box with candles to have at Verdiulière, but one of them almost a candle in hand had, yet they dwell in the danger, and a moment there through the Wind in the Arrows gotten, whereby they went off, have various how un-lucky gone, violently in the Face seemed to be struck, and some the Eyes and Ears lost, and it is said that one of them in the Hospital there was brought has died. The same has the King, the 2nd born in the Church of St. Louis and there, where an eternal Mass was, went after His Majesty in the Garden of the Palace of Lee, galleries walked; and on the 27th has the King attended the Blessing, in the Church of the Floother of the Cordelier. The French Academy has, according to custom, celebrated the Feast of St. Louis in the Chapel of the Lion; during the Mass, which was performed by the Bishop of Soilbns; an Hymn in Music was sung; and the Abbot Ciles gave the Praise Beete of the Saint with uncommon eloquence. That evening the Academy the Prize for Poetry, to the Knight of St. Didier, in Music sung, and the delivery of that for the elegance, was postponed to the coming Year. The Academy of Sciences, that of Inscriptions and Bellelets, held here the Feast of St. Louis in the Church of the Priests of the Oratory; where the Bishop of Bion the Mass, and the Abbot de la Roche [?] did, the King has to the Lord d'Armentenuille, ordinary Councilor and Secretary of State, given the Survivance of his Office. Another part of his Son the Count of Noville, who Ambassador in Holland, and Plenipotentiary at the approaching Peace conferences could of the Congress of Camerik. On the 27th was held the Game of the young Boatmen on the Seine, to each other with a Rope-trick the Toads in the Water and on the River to fish, which the King's ships were not; notwithstanding it seemed to govern why his Man. Coming Sunday, the Boatmen themselves will perform this Exercise [?] at the Devil Kayaal at the Porte Neuf, when the King, along with the Princes and Nobles will at the Quay see such from the Pavilion of the great Gallery. Daily come here Deputies from the foremost Cities of the Kingdom, to congratulate the King on his recovery, to which Feast they are all invited. It is said that from the Italian in the field of Burgundy some complaints have come against the Dragons of the Regiment of Orleans, which by instruction Ambassador through Languedoc conducted having, in the said journey great robberies commonly committed have. It is said to say that with connection of the real third word is conceived that one continues the Ambulatia of War Ste. of St. Sebastian and Postrahien, after; that Magaya was rounded harpen on foot, and that one said the Prince Brittany there Haarlem and around his Forts to sail also the ways hoped to come, to the end the Spanish Troops and that was this Month political should take, which it was not to be believed, because already many Spanish Troops there evident were followed. Although was very diverse Conjunction become best that the [text cuts off]

PETERSBURG, 11 August. Nothing further of importance from His Czarist Majesty's hand; his journey to Königsberg will continue this week, though it will not come to Leiden as it is presently underway, and indeed that great honour [is] most welcome; though Menshikov was expected to be encountered on that road beforehand.

NAPLES, 14 August. It is reported that by the grace of a certain Royal Majesty it has been determined that [this occurred] this day under Guadeloupe [when it was] published, laying it out in confusion amidst the Government. In the evening the Church of St. Lazarus held service with sung funeral cantatas, and Vice-Chancellor Rardelaske [participated]. In the evening the Church gave [a service] by the Inspiration [?] firmly supported on the care of the Lords of Cassandra; today the same was cut [?] on the fort of the Lords of Casa wounds; where [?] the aforementioned of the Visit others [?] which also all directed forth on competent fields [?], certainly worked golden [?], 22, 3, 30 [?] and great [?] of welcoming joy which would take an Ingenie with him equal and following much weight found by two bed souls of the...

THE NETHERLANDS

BRUSSELS, 7 September. The ship Starrenberg, which arrived from the Malabar coast in the East Indies, was destined for China; but has had to turn back to the aforementioned coast of Malabar due to the loss of the Monsoons; the same has, by what is assured with certainty, a light [?] with it as well. The ships Vennen and the Stadt Oostindien, which are on their way here from the East Indies, are expected to arrive in this city shortly.

AMSTERDAM, 7 September. The S. Anna has arrived from Greenland at Bergen with 204 barrels of blubber; from Bordeaux [there] is presented for private sale a newly built modern House, [near] churches, on dykes that are either ruined or hung with [?]; together with a Garden with two Summerhouses and diverse Arbours, further furnished [with trees] planted many years ago; in total approximately [?] acres of Land; situated [?]; for particulars concerning the aforementioned House and Conditions inquire at Katwyck, and at Leiden with Mr. Hendrik Koot, residing on the Vism Head; standing located on the great Market at Rotterdam, [which] shall be enlarged for persons of high and lower rank, as well as for all other respectable Passengers, lodged and treated as before; furthermore let everyone take care as he has done hitherto, and [continue to do so]...

The Marquis d'Asfeld had completely subdued all of Catalonia, and subsequently moved to Aragon. The Marquis de Lede [?] according to Mr. Hendrik Koot, residing on the Vism, at Leiden; by Mr. Hendrik Koot, residing on the Vism at Rotterdam, through which some have the pleasant view on the aforementioned of high and lower rank, as well as for all other respectable Passengers, lodged and treated as before: furthermore let everyone take care as he has done hitherto, and done as ought... Notices & Advertisements

At Dordrecht by Johannes van Braam has been printed and is likewise to be obtained in Amsterdam and thereabouts: Godly Soul's Contemplation, before, during and after the death of Johannes d'Ourrey, Minister of the Holy Gospel at Amsterdam, in [?]; printed at Leiden by Joh. du Vivie and Joh. Arn. Langerak, shall on 3 November [be] of the Lord and Mr. Guilerus Eduus, consisting of various Faculties and Languages [?], at Hanover [?], at Hamburg [?], at Geneva [?], Leipzig [?], Antwerp [?], Brussels [?]; in Munster, in Utrecht, in London, Arnhem [?], Deventer [?], Alkmaar [?], Groningen [?]

Printed at Leyden, the 5th of September, 1721

France

PARIS, 19 August. There is much discussion here regarding the Oration delivered by the Bishop of Soissons; the Response by Monsieur Mallet, Director of the French Academy; the Compliment to the King delivered by Monsieur de La Mothe in the Vatican; the Harangue to the King delivered by Monsieur Mallet at the head of the French Academy; together these comprise approximately 30 pages in quarto. Some days ago, word was received here from Cologne that Monsieur d'Gravesande, Professor at the Academy in Leiden, has gone there to examine the celebrated Machine which was in the Cabinet of the Landgrave. It is assured that this skilled Mathematician, as well as the young Baron Fischer, have confirmed that this Machine achieves perpetual motion, which has been sought for so long. The aforementioned Professor d'Gravesande has accordingly determined to write his opinion to Sir Isaac Newton, President of the Philosophical Society in London; and Baron Fischer and Monsieur Delaguilliers, Demonstrator of Philosophical Experiments of the said Society. According to this report, the Machine is presently constructed in the form of a Wheel of 12 feet diameter, and is covered with bleached linen to conceal the mechanism. It rotates with such force that it completes a full revolution in one minute. In each revolution, one hears seven or eight weights under Slippers, depending on the side where the Wheel turns; and when one retards it in its course to the extent that it makes only 2 or 3 revolutions in one minute, it gradually accelerates back to its former speed. The continuity of the Motion, carefully examined by a long Horologium with Seconds, always shows the same 26 revolutions in one minute; and all the particular investigations which learned gentlemen have conducted on this Machine have always revealed the same motion and unchanged rotation as aforementioned. Some days ago, the Process was settled between the Marquise of Crèvecoeur and Mademoiselle de Vigabourg against Monsieur Samuel Bernard, regarding the purchase of land, to the advantage of the first-named parties.

From Port Louis comes news that two fine Ships belonging to the Company have arrived from the East Indies, and two others are expected momentarily. Since Cardinal du Bois can no longer discharge the office of Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, it is believed that Monsieur d'Armenonville will be appointed to this post. The Nephew of the Bishop of Chartres, who is approximately 17 years old and Colonel of a Regiment in Picardy, has with his Uncle's permission abducted a young Woman of noble lineage from one of the principal houses there, which has occasioned vigorous prosecution against him. The Edict regarding the Arrangement of Finances has not yet been published, and as this uncertainty causes great unease, many who are occupied with Papers find themselves in considerable difficulty. The Actions stand at 56 and the Bank Notes at 79 livres.

Great Britain

LONDON, 28 August. Last Monday, Benjamin Norton Defoe, for having published the Introduction in the established London Journal of the previous Saturday, was brought to the prison house of Newgate, but was discharged the following day under Bail of 500 pounds, which he acknowledged regarding debt, and 250 pounds by two new Sureties, to appear at the King's Bench on the first Georgian Term. The Government has promised a Premium of 100 pounds to whosoever shall apprehend a [?], in the fields of the Smugglers (mentioned previously), who witnessed that one of the Toll Officers at Gelten [?]; and on the Prophecies of the further deep [?], which were 20 [?]; a Premium of 20 pounds has been set; already several of these latter have been captured and locked in the prison house of Marshalsea, and it is reported that several have fled to the East. Tuesday, the Earl Cadogan reviewed in Hyde Park the 6 Regiments of Foot Guards; but it is not yet known when, in the presence of the King and the Prince of Wales, the general Review over the entire Household of the King shall be conducted. It is reported that the Princess of Wales is again Pregnant. Wednesday night, the Earl of Warwick and Holland, one of the Chamberlains of the Prince of Wales, died of the Cold at the age of 23 years, and is succeeded in his Titles &c. by Mr. R. ch, one of the Noblemen of the Prince of Wales. The youngest son of the Lord Viscount Townshend is one of the Gentlemen of the King's Chamber.

Regarding the Easter Capital, notice is given that the various Auctions in September, on the known days, must be held according to each individual [?]. Note: each is warned that the place of the auction of the 10th is received, as to the red Class. Daniel d'Asevoda, Nicholas Ens, and Rafael d'Asevoda, Inhabitants, dwelling at 6 houses in the Dykstraat in the Old Burgh, a sharp Sale of 1720 seized Princestaft between Reguliersgracht and Vryelut. Jan Piersdie Zomter Malisiam will, on Tuesday the 16 September 1721, at the White Mills on the Cinget, [auction] all the English [goods?] of W. Rodardy Medidus, 200 in Drawings as Primes, built Bricks, and carpets; [these?] may be obtained from J. F. Zomer, and Gysbert Hol, P. Ouiebolfon and D. Borckeningh, Factors, also in Amsterdam [?] in the White Zuwen, 6000 old Copperware, 4000 choice Christianware, 2000 G. new Nephews and Wyburger Spartem, 3000 Maarbkins and Barkones; 6000 N. feet, 1800 Goltsten, 3000 Mromhude and Oaricende Misses; 1100 Nera, wykie and Oaricende Fire Bilks, and 1000 Mardoulste Stolp Bilks, &c. B. Picari, hawker in Amsterdam over the old Luytersche [?], [undertook?] by way of Inviting, and which is under negotiation.

---

TEUAN, 22 July. The Letters arrived here from Fez and other Places make it known that the Moors have [received word?] that on various Places along the Coasts, other Ships of the Squadron under Command of the Count of Liefden are [operating?]; the other a Fluyt touching at Lifestaff there could not enter. The trade in the said Staples could not proceed there, concerning which His Majesty, [maintaining?] quite different relations with this Land for trade.

Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, 21 September. According to Letters from Danzig of the 2nd, the British Ministry, in conference with the Council of that City, had made it known that His British Majesty's determination well the great Ministry had advised; that Admiral Norris, for the obtaining of forces, had spoken to proceed to these [?]. The Government's State [?] that this was engaged [?]. According to Letters from Jamaica of 29 June, New Style, an English Warship that was [?] Porto Bello and Cartagena had [?]; as also two English [?] with [?], and still not returned Ships lay, which were in good condition to [?], which about newly saw, in danger of encounter by Ships of [?] arrived. From [?] were withdrawn the [?], Henry [?] toward the Maas, and the Ship the Falcon. At Boulogne we have here arrived the [?], and the cargo, being Corn, the Glick, and also [?], so from here all the Mast. At Boulogne have arrived Piel Michaelhele Ville, the Sara, Wolbert Naring, Corn, Turk, and Pieter Tusniffe have, with the Dutch Warship of Capt. Wys. At Nantes Jan Willemsk, Jan Jacobite Tiebbar van Hoof, Teude Gerbardt, Jan Blom, and others, 2500 from the Maas as Zeeland; the Ship the [?], Marten Klock, had departed for the Baltic; and the Ship the Dorp. Gerrit Rook, sailed to Stockholm. At St. Matten had arrived the Liede, Jacob Josale from here, and departed Pieter Hendrikske, and the Ship the Prize Dieden, Capt. [?] on watch on the Maas. At St. Malo we have arrived the St. Columbeau, Jean le Sage.

Spain

CADIZ, 13 August. The Commander, under the 14th or 15th of this, under Sea; of the Captain Gracita sailing over Ships of the Squadron under Command of the Count of Liefden; the other a Fluyt touching at Lifestaff there could not enter. The trade in the said Staples could not proceed there, concerning which His Majesty, maintaining quite different relations with this Land for trade.

and as a French Ship with three [?], at the same time, then had 200 [?] Letters mentioning, [?] named way. One [?] mg the & the Catholic Court [?] a [?] Frys, which the same religion, married, and the Skipper that his roofed City [?] in the [?] air all those [?] which [...] bring with death shall be by a Council. the same beholds [?]. According to 1800 [?] one has news that in Segger [?] Governing there by much wet [?]. With 2 14007 Men, 200 Roubles had [?], with order was by [?] to provision for Cogia is scattered, so not to from the same Tripoli go [?] Bengal and Desna, stained [?] under the hearing of the that [?] has not willing in a Fran [?] has Calvised the Providenta Diego No-Chipon [?]. Con. Perety of Genoa, and the Jat London at his greeting by. Walter Robert, but last 200 lay 21 shieller, amongst them in care of E. the Hollanders, and a Hamburger

MADRID, 19 August. The highest Monzal Family, itself even in the great Mule and there now in the Infusanere shall turn more toward Major. Don Francisco Joseph [?] Don Diego de Norja Marquis of [...].

LIVORNO, 17 August. Because the 3 August [?] Roeris, with 40 [?] about Tunis toward Ayes departing ships; as according Ships, as [...] had sorrow have, one will also today [...] which [?] goes to [?] for days sent, and that such ships of them in extending the George paille, of Cadiz and here in the Hand

PARIS, 6th of this September, the Salute morning in the Church of St. Denis, and gave [?]; and [?] house [?] on the Quay [...] one [?] and the Quay.

Course of Exchange

**EXCHANGE RATES:** Madrid: 30 and 1/2. Bilbao: 29 and 1/4. Cadiz: 13 and 1/4. Seville: [?] and 1/4. Livorno: 5 and 1/4. London: 12 Shillings at 1 and 1/2 to 6 groschen. Paris: 40 and 1/4. Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 8th of September, 1721

TETUAN, 22 July. The Spanish Troops which have been stationed at Ceuta have now been dismissed by order of the governor, and some are camping in their old encampment; however, since the new troops have also camped there, nothing of consequence occurs on either side, as both work remains at a standstill. By letters from Salée of the 17th of this month, we learn that the King there is pursuing a new cruise; unfortunate news regarding the inhabitants had occurred; up to that day no foreign vessels had been brought in. The buyers who were dealing with the 2 aforementioned English Pirates, and had dealings with them those days, cannot come to their livelihood there without having all their property searched, which is why money came in the customs. [?] good market.

Spain

CADIZ, 13 August. The Dutch Vice-Admiral Mr. van Somemel's Daughter is to choose the sea in the pleasantness of Honeyshouse on the 14th or 15th of this month. The two East Ships from Leghorn Lomillion & Oeleyndt, their rear made with fine ships; efforts continue regarding the remaining ships of England to be dispatched. From the news incoming from another source, all ships from Gibraltar of the third date brought word that the [?] of Salée had turned about and had taken two Galleys, ten from Brindisi, and likewise with Lisbon, was heading toward Lisbon; the other was a French Ship at 14 miles further, carrying in hold 20 England Seas, which Pyrop-Oir, 11 Vault Cook-Isle, 1000 Barrels of Accia, mixed Silver, 5 Cases drawn Silver, and 1000 Barrels of Accia. Good and Spanish French Ship with third Load at in deal hamel [?] [Editor's note: This section is heavily corrupted in the transcription and meaning is unclear.]

Monday'S Courant

It was made out great, and 5 or 6 persons were crushed by the collapse of a Scaffold. The latter is that Fort Saturday morning came out of the Council, in his Carriage carrying people, whereafter through another in second morning, whereby he was gravely wounded. This night between Friday and Saturday, two Musketeers were on duty; to such extent that those on their watch heard it; and after the Soldier had seized them that they were both concluded. A third matter greatly concerned those who might well have feared consequences, unless prevented, with the Guard at Stadiem, which the two Musketeers had killed, as the whole Corps undertook the matter. Last Friday one saw the fine hanging Porcelains begin to be purchased, which came from the Magazine of the Duke of la Force; namely, his secretary the late Retaud, and the entire Court departed that very day to the Duke's country estate, from whence they shall not soon return. Two were noble Princesses, which had been servants of the Monastery: they were brought before the Justice of the Châtelet. That evening upon the Place de Grève there was entertained the lord Philip Morleu, Knight and Governor of Starency, of Pakidvid, Circulus and other Lordships, a Pensioner of Anteu; he was aged. There came a sermon from the Overdict, for such pieces, because they nearly deserved Stamp: his Scaterie is drawn up for the lord de Chavreoa, Grand Provost General of the Constabulary, the alliance of the Council, ad hac qualified with so Auditors of the Châtelet, by the Council in Extraordinary Committee in Session, whether the other in Paris there brought, some Auditors, Friend of theirs appropriate, whereupon on the 17th July, by order of the lord de Grève, his ward is in the court was melted: he had before had much to do with the Rights at the Door Remdes, and the Probity which he had long maintained well, yet everything was burden to him, so also he went to the lord Audbert, his Friend and Colleague also well served him, on which Council he was sentenced to be Beheaded. Much Noble Folk came to said Audbert to Amsterdam, but he was captured. To the Stables gone would have had great mountains, and he verified with other richter in mufti in a Rogue all this; yet he took on the whole great, being very uneasy, and a Treasures broken off, was in litrels given by the Landeriche, which he had qualified in the Executive, and he was immediately brought to the Châtelet: and gone in the him in in all [?] happened; until so that leaned in him in em reaction to due Vacancy place in opened. Through man one is to [?] followed upon of 5 Sergeants, as one pole mountains, and the desired is great on action was the Lords verdict without have, either or they [Editor's note: This section is severely corrupted and largely unintelligible.]

England

LIVORNO, 15 August new style. By Express one has received news that on the 13 August new style, with 40 or 45 British vessels, which arrived about the 14th in the evening, in the Bay of Biscay against a Fleet of some Moorish resting near Medeo, an Engagement took place between Moors and British, though the common French Ship between Moor and Medeo is engaged, be about no Allied French Ship [Editor's note: This dispatch is incomplete and corrupted.]

France

PARIS, 1 September. Last Friday the King went with his entire Court hunting in the Woods of Boulogne in the District of Saint-Joules, in the Church where it was inhabited; and on Monday the Church lay there; and so the passage of the Abbeys of which the ceremony occurred there on Tuesday, Yesterday one heard in the Chapel of the Tuileries that His Majesty was at Versailles [Editor's note: This report is incomplete.]

The Netherlands

[No content provided]

Great Britain

LONDON, 3 September. Saturday the Widow of the Earl of Strabthope was delivered in childbed of a Son and a Daughter. On the third Monday in May, departing toward Dublin. Against Parliament sitting in late September the opening takes place. The Archbishop shall head the Clergy toward Dublin. May has ordained that we should [?] Faerd and to Foot. In the, People write that the King's figure has lived well in twelve different places, and the King's children are said to follow. Also it seems one is firmly convinced that in the matter of the Anteconstitutionis also such other subtleties [are involved]; this concerns still great accommodations with the Directrice, against which twelve ships have been sent.

PERSIA.

SPAHAN, 5 March. [The text here is severely corrupted in the original transcription and cannot be reliably translated. It appears to concern a Vizier from January 1720 and matters of treasure and appointments, but the specific details are unclear.]

TURKEY AND THE CARPATHIANS

VIENNA, 17 August. Following recent conferences at Kamenic [?] with new instructions regarding Italian affairs. It is said of the Army that it is quite occupied with inspecting the harbor, and there is a great gathering of galleys [details further corrupted in original].

GERMANY AND THE TAUNUS

VIENNA, 17 August. [See above—text appears duplicated and severely corrupted.]

BRUNSWICK, 2 September. The [?] Courier arrived here [text becomes illegible].

[ENGLAND]

[Multiple passages follow regarding English news, but the transcription becomes increasingly corrupted. Discernible items include:]

- An experiment conducted at Newgate Prison - A foot race near [?] on the road to [?], with races of 4 miles each; the first prize of 15 pounds sterling was won by a runner in the service of Sir William Blackett; a second prize of 15 pounds was won by one Bullet - The Prince of Wales attended this entertainment - Discovery of 30 large bags of candy and tea aboard Captain Coombs' ship - John Cooper imprisoned in Newgate on counterfeiting charges, with assistance from one E. Reeves - Various stock prices: Bank 135, [?] 133, Bank 122, Lottery Annuities 102, Bank Annuities 101, 4 per Cent 94, [?] 23

THE NETHERLANDS.

DORDRECHT, 4 September. The following vessels have arrived at this city: the Wepen of Amsterdam, the Anke Koning, the drit Gebiederes [?], Douwe Doreen, both bound for Christiania; Jan Lugten from Dunkirk; by chance the Endeavor, Gouben Robinson; the Providence, Leonard Henuis; the Phenix, John Coutes, all bound for England; the Judit, Sara, Dirk Jantse Kiredt, bound for Riga.

AMSTERDAM, 7 September. In the Sound approximately 10 ships [are noted]. [Much of the remainder is corrupted.]

Notice is given that on Thursday, 11 September 1721, the locks Jan Koster, Lucas Stutgart, and F. Raadesmasker will serve as brokers for new whole lodgings on the Haarlemerdijk, a party of fine quality coffee beans and a party of good quality coffee beans; [further details corrupted]; likewise on 17 September the same will occur on the Singel at the Berg, a party of 26 to 30 vats [of ?].

The Directors of the Commerce, Navigation, and Insurance Company of [?] are announced at the office of the aforementioned Company [details corrupted].

Jacob van de Houte, Messrs. Jeweel, and Cornelis Verlinde, brokers, both of Rotterdam, at the White Lion Inn, will sell a shipload of Bassy Tobacco from the 1726 crop, which is still sealed, from [?] in the plantation of the Gentlemen Jacob Seinfrif and Son.

Jan Wolffelen, broker at Amsterdam, will sell on Wednesday, 17 September in the Warmoesstraat across from St. James Street, at the house of the widow Piet Eve with various items, tortoiseshell, nutwood, ebony, glass, and other furnishings belonging to a mirror shop; all goods arrived by the ship the Fortuyp, Captain [?], within 14 days to be delivered into the hands of the aforementioned [details corrupted].

At Nicolaas of Hoorn, bookseller at Amsterdam, there is being printed [?] with an admonition to warn these two religious [persons?]; it will shortly be printed by the High [?] [details corrupted].

SCHAFFHAUSEN, 31 August. The delegates of the four Cantons, met here to discuss bringing the welfare into agreement through gentle means, have been entrusted [with negotiations]; it is hoped that means will be found to keep this matter within their sphere. The Baron van Weldteren has departed again from here; in return [?]. The Swedish Minister Herr Preys has been appointed by various gentlemen of the court. This night the Herr Tilenus expired.

FRANKFURT, 3 September. The same court [?] on Monday [?]; on the named goods, precise information was obtained. A critical patent has been published forbidding the export of [?].

DÜSSELDORF, 3 September. For the reception of the wild [?] of Germany, who [?] in their hunting [?], there come daily many Princes, Graffin, and other persons of distinction.

COLOGNE, 3 September. The Papal Nuncio here has received the Bull [?]. Rome has obtained the Jubilee Festival [details obscured].

[VARIOUS AUCTION AND SALES NOTICES FOR AMSTERDAM, 1721]

These pages contain numerous notices of public auctions and sales in Amsterdam for September 1721, including:

- Sales of coffee, tea, and other colonial goods - Auction of household furnishings and goods - Sales of tobacco from various plantations - Estate sales and liquidations

[Specific dates mentioned: 10, 11, 12, 16, 17, 18 September 1721]

AMSTERDAM, 3 September. [From the Republic of Genua, further details corrupted.]

LONDON, 3 September. [Details not preserved in transcription.]

---

Notices & Advertisements

On Monday, 4 October at 6 o'clock in the evening, there will be a viewing at the Hague at the Holland-Halle of the yacht against the Vogelkoy, measuring 22 [?], a sailing vessel, high and without mast; formerly belonging to the Honorable Licaat, Admiral of [?]. Further information is available at Amsterdam at Romvol & Co., at the office of Procurator Carel van Belle.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 8th of september, 1721. — With Privilege.
