Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 10th of September, 1721

FRIDAY, MARCH 6. The conspiracy that was hatched and discovered by the Grand Vizier in January 1720, which took place in the second instance as an attempt to establish himself as King, has had such great consequences that people have been implicated in it, and those of his followers have also been prosecuted. He is proceeding, and through the office of a new Grand Vizier, being held prisoner at Biaffa (the great one), old [?] received until the discovery of his hidden treasures has already been achieved with success, for in one chest alone 400,000 Turkish Ducats were found, besides three other wealthy men discovered, and also in three places very great treasures of Jewels, Gold and Silver were found, and afterwards, among various of his Followers, through which treasures were to come to them. The Pasha has again sent the Generals to various Places, 200 of the Borders as Officers, etc. thus prepared to occupy: still some Rebels remain in the Land of the previous District, who are making some movement, but such is known to be of little account, as the Roads at present are much safer for Travellers than before.

GERMANY AND THE ADJOINING KINGDOMS.

VIENNA, August 27. Following the holding of Conferences concerning the disputed Governorships and States of Brunswick, which one could hope for no more [?], an Express has been sent to Rome, with new Instructions from the Cardinal of Albano, to settle these matters and to help. It is said that Milord Forbes, Admiral of the Emperor's Navy, is to betake himself to Comacchio to fortify the fleets, and to make those agreements, in order to be able to hold a great number of Supplies in the same.

REGENSBURG, September 16. In the Diet assembled for this Session, matters of Electoral interest were discussed. The Pamphlet concerning the union of the Lutheran and Reformed Religion which was here before, is very much disputed and difficult; with this now quickly, are to be sent to all the great places the Plenary Assembly, while this matter is pursued through the Ministers of the principal Protestant [bodies], with which [it is] dealt, and of good expectation [?], innocently in this united Lutheran States made happiness [?] will boast, to the friendly spirit [?]. In Protestant Collegiate [assembly] will be presented, and [?] one can [?]. They have moreover made [?] that some of the Ministers have been appointed, with foresight that discussion will be had thereupon.

BRUNSWICK, September 2. The Baggage of Count Golofkin, which ten Posts came from the [?] in Moorish Saturday arrived in this City, as [?] that of Baron Sletnitr, his [?]

GREAT BRITAIN.

LONDON, September 5. The Magistrates of the health of the Republic of Genoa have between both healthy Mountains and a certain Place at sea caused to remain [?] the danger of being exposed to the health. It is said that the King over the thought of the New Town would be regulated, and that the Czar his vehemence has remained to advantages.

Wednesday's Courant.

Great Britain, Holland, and those of other Nations, which do not come forward with good Certificates from the Magistrates of the Place and Haven where they were loaded, that the same manner of infectious Disease, and that the Merchandise was properly loaded became brown [?] or Haven. Which remedy by order of the Secretary of State, Carteret has been published, first the merchants who trade to Genoa might conduct themselves according to this order. With the Letters of July 12 from Eustadon the defended [?] and Phases there were very great, in the entire course of the Year 200 turned out poorly, that Plantations which in other Years have yielded 160 Barrels of Sugar, now deliver none; 16 from it, wherefore the sorrowful condition of that Island was not to be avoided. From Sussex it is reported likewise that the Sugar trade there is likewise so violently attacked, and the Smugglers have become so bold that they have bound the King's Officers hands and feet several times and thus carried off the goods; in many places these Smugglers amount to more than 100 armed Men, which thus with the below a brought Load bring in Safety. From Horsham comes news that a party of 20 Grenadiers under the Command of Lieutenant Jekyll, out of the Regiment of Groves, having seized 3 Horses of the South [?], also 7 or 8 of their Heads fell and were also brought in there. Those of the East India Company have now received news that again two Return-Ships, the Caravan, the Duke of York, and the Bridgewater, have arrived in the Downs. Thursday the Directors of the East India Company were assembled, in which Assembly the distribution of the profits was to have been unveiled, but it is not yet made known, but some believe that a certain payment will be made, and in part. The Asia being not yet closed, it has been decided among the Proprietors to divide that which will amount to 50 per Cent, but all these Resolutions of the Directors will be examined within some days in a General Assembly with the Principal Shareholders. The order to appoint Dr. Green as Bishop of Norwich, in place of Dr. Trimmel Bishop of Winchester, has been sealed with the great seal. Yesterday all the Members of the common Council at Kensington were assembled, in which Assembly good order was conducted, by Proclamation to make known, that Parliament, which has been prorogued until October 10, will then begin to sit, to ventilate matters of very great importance. The Change which was contemplated at court, one now declares shall have no progress. The same day a Courier arrived with letters from Knight Sutton, Ambassador at the Court of France. Lord Irwin here is very seriously Ill, as also the Duke of Chandois. In the meantime, the Bishop of Bangor was to go over to the Archbishopric of Dublin. It is assured that the Experiment of the inoculation of Children's Pox, was practiced upon 6 Prisoners in Newgate, [?] which the same had never had, but now since they have been in that Prison for some months, has shown no other alteration, than a twelvefold cold [?] around the Incision. A Vessel with Fullage from Ostend, they were by the Toll [?] was, the [?]; it has been found beneath in the same a considerable party of Tea, all of which they [?], 44 [?] Chests, and two Baskets with Porcelain.

This Evening the aforesaid 200 Proclamation concerning the new sitting of Parliament was published. The Acts of Parliament 1717. Q. L. 1645, S.S. 140, the Mill. Bank 122, Lottery Annuities to 6 [?] 102. Bank Ann. 107, same 4 per Cent 94. [?] C. 29 and 1 half, the Heirs Asset. 9, same Lond. 7 and 1 half. York Build. 30 and 1 half.

FRANCE.

PARIS, September 7. Convened Monday as the Doctors of the Sorbonne held their ordinary meeting, in this Assembly notice was given by Circular from the Bureau, and [?] assistance of [?] by a sound [?] Letter de Cachet containing prohibition to speak of certain matters (which for these [?] are) equally to resume ordinarily the Exercise of the Sorbonne, in [?] Tenure to Athens and that until further order, and as in the third Meeting will be chosen, that which [?] the most common or [?]. Or a new Seat according to the Deliberations [?] well [?]. The same [?] decree those of the said Sorbonne in their Church a great Mass, the [?] [?] Boheens brought things, and without the restoration of the King's [?] health. The Mr. Jean Baptiste Mahus, Counselor of the Duke of Lorraine in his Council [?], where of Street, and the same [?]. And sent to this Court, 19 August and [?] the [?]

AMSTERDAM, 8 September. On Monday of this week, the Commander of the Lotteries departed [?] in good order to the Commissioners at the office to bring the plan into cakes and tickets, and to obtain the order of Buralle; to [?] on the other side. [The text here becomes increasingly difficult to parse with confidence.] [Editor's note: The transcription appears to have significant corruption in this section, making reliable translation impossible without the original source material.]

BRUSSELS, 6 September. The Mister Stormets, Minister from the Electorate of the Palatinate here, has recently returned from a journey to England, though he brings from there very fresh advice, that the [?] and the Congress at Utrecht would be [held]. It is said that one hopes that the difficulties between the Elector and the King of Spain shall be settled by agreement; at the last meeting, the counts of Utrecht and Great Britain took [?]. The Prince of La Tour de [?] Louis of Baden to Antwerp has sent shortly thereafter, a mentioned Princess there by the Governor.

By Nicolaus de Hoore, Bookseller at Amsterdam, there is printed [a work] concerning matters in [?], which [?] showing [concerning] Fortune and [?], [which work concerns] the time of Emperor Leopold I, Roman Emperor, and [?] [German?], the [?] of secret matters, and [?] [other historical details] [?]

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 12th of September, 1721

ITALY

NAPLES, 19 August. The Agent of the Duke of Savoy at this Court, who was arrested by the Archers last week and brought into custody without any justification being given for such treatment, has been released following his complaints to the King's government regarding such injustice; and to provide him some satisfaction, the Archers have been reprimanded. [?] The robbers in the German territories continue to trouble our Russians; recently they have [?] two men who dared to leave the church at Villiers, for which reason the villagers of Villiers dare not leave, and the Gallic Prize [?] against [?], whereby he lost the helm of his ship. Word has been received that the Lord von Melito also [?] at the siege of Königsberg [?] the widowed Choris on the ship was overcome.

ROME, 23 August. After the Pope last Monday, 23 August, the Cardinals and his Ministers, his Holiness also held a long audience. Meanwhile [?] the President, who very [?] audience still to Albania [?]. That afternoon the Cardinal invited the entire house of Albani to dinner. That same day the Cardinals of Albani and Colonius, and [?] by all the other Cardinals likewise the General Brigade who remained with the Cardinals [?] the General Brigade at the Pope [?] that he remembered him from the Battalion in the previous assembly. That day the Nuncio Bossu in Rivoli was, where he was received by Cardinal Salerno. Wednesday the Cardinal invited the entire house and the Prelates well [?] of the Subjects in the agreement that in Austria in an expedition on the agenda, with word that the King of France is in good health; for her Eminence made known from all other heads [?] that he shall have a Te Deum sung in the Church of the Holy [?]. It is understood that the Court also [?] his Clientele [...] the Answers of [...] of France, concerning the promotion to Cardinal of the Answer Bishop of Camerik; but since the genuine Cardinals within [...] [?] General goes from all other heads [...] thus with the Pope, not only for [...] who did not stand in the Father of kind Answers knowledge, Father of the Conscience; for [...]. Thursday when the Academy was held, the Tower of Martel [?], and the [...] still on the previous [...] which

---

SPAIN

BILBAO, 22 August. The ship of Captain Timmermans, having come from France for this port, has had its health letters and certificate taken by our Corregidor on the order of the Court, to be sent to Madrid, and there examined in the Council of Health. Meanwhile nothing can be unloaded from that ship until the Court shall have permitted it. Since the Plague continues in France, even greater precautions are taken here against the arrival of goods or persons coming from that realm.

SAN SEBASTIÁN, 26 August. Yesterday the Garrison troops were withdrawn from this city and the castle; likewise those from Fuenterrabía; which troops together with those in the Paliguia march toward Bilbao. On the other hand, the Spanish Dragon regiment, a great number of whom evacuated from Pamplona, consisting of about 800 men, has taken possession of this city and castle; all of which was done for the war. It is not yet known whether the customs houses will be restored shortly on the same footing as before the French came here. It is also thought that within 3 days a prohibition of trade with France will be published, just as at Bilbao.

---

SWEDEN

STOCKHOLM, 27 August. The King finds himself at Wermland by the use of the waters in very good health, and his Majesty departed from there. The Admiral Norris and the Great-Britain Minister Ritchis have been there several times with the King. No further news concerning the negotiations at Nystad; it is said that peace with the Muscovites still sounds good. The Russian fleet has returned to their harbors; in early September it will be combined with the fleet [?]. The Count Horn has departed to his landed estates. It is still said that the Brunswick Congress shall continue, whereupon the Emperor's Minister, Count Pflug, is initiating; the said Minister expects his sent expedition from Vienna is set aside, to secure from the ranks of peace this crown. P.S. It is assured that [?] of peace for this crown; which is daily [?], at the point of the Harling of Holland, in regard to the succession of the Crown. It is forgotten that his Czar Majesty (as soon as peace with this crown is concluded) [?] his high Mediastal [?] befitted.

---

GERMANY AND ADJACENT KINGDOMS

VIENNA, 30 August. Wednesday the Imperial Vice-Chancellor Count von Schönborn, 75 years old, came from Schloss Schönborn, where he had conducted his affairs, to the city to pay his respects to his Imperial Majesty, who received him kindly. Monday was the birthday of the Empress, who is now in her 114th year. That evening an opera was given at the Favorita, in which the entire court participated [?] the widow, who came late that evening to conclude her regency. The Prince Eugene and the Empress, her Majesty and her household departed, and in the coming week will depart to Hungary. Daily efforts continue to prepare what is necessary for the embellishment of the Auditorium of this city, and it is reported through the Bishop of Passau that [...] the Archbishop shall do [...] distributed patriarchs; moreover the florins yearly revenue [...].

REGENSBURG, 4 September. Since the indisposition of the Provençal Corps and the Hanoverian Ministry, and the Roman reply given last week, in the assembly of last Monday here no [?] was taken regarding the continuation of the Commission Decree of the Emperor concerning the way to Wetzlar. It is expected that the Lord von Rodt would be ordered to [?] the redress; which on the field of another day to send the restitution; which on the field of the royal [?] of the Palais Majesty, and the cruise to, some Commission having [?], shall pass through this city to have relieved the German courts; Commission to have [?] through this city shall pass to have relieved the German courts at Berenthal on Imperial order entertained.

---

GREAT BRITAIN

LONDON, 3 September. Word is that there is a letter here from the ship the Britannia, reporting that a East India ship has recently arrived with about round ships of the Company departed; but that of the other ships, also five Dutch ships from China have returned, which from China have returned; beyond the stools and calicos of Livatten; but and the good-natured Coast, and the Cargo still not separated, [?] Cargo still not dispersed. The ship still from Herford.

---

FRANCE

PARIS, 4 September. The King has granted free access in the matter of the Prince of Tonnay-Charante, his son, to the Palace General Leman of his Majesty's Chamber. The [?] of Hemesstem, the Bedsman of his Majesty's Chamber.

Printed at Leyden

France

The Duchess of Orléans has been delivered in childbed of a son; the Duke of Charillon and Their Majesties hold him for christening as godparents; the entire Luxembourg family has appeared in person. The Abbot of St. Albin, who resides near court, has obtained a directress of Taarau [?] in order to enter the priesthood. Our Bishops are departing to their dioceses.

The animosity between the two parties continues at the Sorbonne, and shows no sign of diminishing. In which assembly the King steadily favors one of these against the Patrols. There has been [?] and the assembly was opened by a Lettre de Cachet, in which it was stated that the same vigorous exercise was contained as [?] blood. The attempt was made to continue the differences of the Assembly, but this was not promised to the Loire, and the matter was deferred to another assembly. While the Corpus [?] sang the Te Deum, over the [?] and no great indication of the King's health. Monsieur de Malezieux, intended for greater military office, has become Chancellor of the Duke du Maine [Editor's note: Louis-Auguste de Bourbon, legitimized son of Louis XIV].

For some time the wine merchants have experienced a declaration named [?], through various judged Alicante wines, which they claimed to be warehoused, or previously stored, in much the same manner as those of the Dukes of La Force. This declaration, having been judged well made at the Châtelet, was therefore appealed before Parliament, where some matter never came to audience of the great Chamber. The Advocate General has concluded against the declaration, whereupon a judgment followed that said declaration is declared null, and the wine merchants lose such their case, after they had already made costs of some [?] Rixdollars. It has also been reported that this declaration stands in a sense with the previous one, and has on the whole been considered the same.

The Jesuits have for some time proposed various questions to be decided by their disciples, and thus created great zeal and audacity in French, in consequence of which for some days at their college a solemn affair was debated, the question being which of the following should have received charity, in a contested estate left to some pious person for the poor, namely the blind, the mute, or the mad, orphans, or the elderly. After the debate was conducted by four young colleagues, Messrs. Mathon, Patin, de Villeneuve, and Marly, it was found in common that the Sieur Peyden de Morville, who was judge, would have allowed the elderly or the orphans to the charitable goods; but the judge declared himself for the advocate, who for the mute in this case, and it was judged that these had the most compassion and greatest need, because he lacks hearing, which is the greatest and most scientific of all goods.

It is reported that the Duke Regent, together with the Duke of Chartres, is coming to Amsterdam to eat with His Majesty. His Royal Highness goes every week two or three days to St. Cloud to amuse himself, where he has been most magnificently entertained these days by the Duke of Vendôme. The Marquise de Perabère has returned to Beaumont to reside at her country house, which she acquired from the Count of Nod, and comes frequently to the city. It is reported that the Count of Morville signs all dispatches concerning foreign affairs. It is rumored that the first dispatch has already arrived. In this matter Charles is said to have gone to England or Geneva to find Mademoiselle de la Tremouille there to marry. A devout Carmelite brother, who served in the Carmelite chapel of Place Maubert [...]

On behalf of the Director Capian, notice is given that the drawing of lots lodged in September, as known on the Ponteleyen must be completed, and shall take place following [?]. Note: [?] the lots which in the domain of Dominiteium enjoy 10 [currency], as far as the lock inclusive.

Jac Wils and Jan van Blanckens, brokers, will sell at Amsterdam on Friday: broken ground, a party of extra fine puykA of 75 bales from Salonica and Smitde Catorena. Further for sale by hand [?]: a newly numbered mother's house, with six kitchens, decorated or painted; furthermore a garden with two playhouses and diverse summer houses, planted some years ago; together about great a morning's land; [...]

Barbardy

ALGIERS, 13 August. Since all the rovers have returned here without [?] having taken a Spanish ship, and the city [?] has necessitated that three of the ships went [?], carrying 30, 36, and 40 slaves' cannon, into the sea, where one might [?] 300 heads, since by trickery and this one heard it, since by ships [?] kept the hot doll thing. Names of the city on the same place. Foul preparations for a bombardment [?], are here in haste again [?] those who continue, one must three ships [?] supply.

Spain

CADIX, 19 August. In this bay are [?] Guilles pavilion, a great that the Go [?] Fnis of the vision of Sades, had the rovers that ship [?] Antoinette Go [?] of Hamburg [?] above him complained [...] People were already charged there, by [?] it was ordered, the same [?] were through salt [?] on slaves [?] [?]. Ship goods 1500 and [?] Salt was also taken [?] Door already more and [?] goods [?] through the [?] Salt was also taken, the same to [?] 2000 peals of contraband [?] then recovered [?] Drocth [?] about 50 or 60 boats of oil, as Alicante soap, the people were themselves to [?] as men were made into slaves, what merchant about 6 or 10 years old, who [?] the great [?] the [?] becomes [?] whole passengers laid, Spaniards of birth [?] through [?] Gulayens to Turcan and Stanley [?] affiliate: Finally the said rovers [?]

Sables 965-970. The English effects have no fixed price.

The Netherlands

GOVERNMENT of the States of Holland and West Friesland, have been met again today. The entire company is today or tomorrow to leave for Friesland. The two Commissioners of the Wedding Company have met at their house on Friday, 19 September 1721, at 6 o'clock in the evening, in the Admirals' Chamber, as shall be noted.

modern house, dwelling with no upper and bed-chambers understanding, and resting [?] 270; furthermore a room for 8 to 10 horses, coach- and garden-maker [?] sold with [?] and bridal goods, all since 1 day [?] located at the side door of Katscyk on the Aya, in The Hague their side to see, one stick [?] passing by.

Amsterdam [sold] on Monday, 15 September 1721, on the Nieuwwendijk [?], Tuesday [?], 2000 [?], 2000 spars, 3000 juniper, [?] 1000 cypresses, 300 birches, 2000 firebuckets, 2000 Marquat the three millions, of 22 to 26 feet. [?]

at the Hague likewise the preceding day viewing is, other prints; For the Mr. Abr. van Verden, [?] of the King, [?] in [?], in too much stated on Saturday at [?], the aforesaid [?] continue, [?] of which he the same [?] at the same shall lie.

England

Some news destined for Greece, but the [?] ship is already from Greece destined, but the [?] ship will not be named, the same in the whole [?] [?] on [?], a kitten drink awaited, Couchichille, about 20 or 60 rovers' oils, and Alicante soap, in the people of the said trade. 26 men were made into slaves, what merchant about 5 or te yachts old, which [?] the great to [?] the said 80 over [?] still two passengers likewise, Spaniards of birth [?] [?] whole [?] [?] great in which one [?] done that wine [?] of Turdan and Stanley [?]

The ship [?] is begun with a Canary [?]; finally the said rovers [?] very [?] weighed and Princess ship [?] [?], Boot, [?]. 20 From French locks is [?] here [?] war-ship, as today about eight [?], 200 [?] sought that [?] [?] captains and the [?] [?] lying still [?] [?] the passages and [?] [?] [?] not [?] [?] the [?] [?] [?]

Madrid, 26 August

France

The Netherlands

AT THE KLOPP and on the Broad Street.

Course of Exchange

The course of EXCHANGE today at Madrid 89½, Cadix 11, Seville 112½, Bilbao 90½, Lisbon 44½, Cadix [?] 81, Seville 91½, Hamburg 36½, St. Gent 2½, Brussels ½, Zeeland 1, Rotterdam 1 agio. The agio of the Bank is ½ agio. The shares of the East India Company 501½.

The 12th of september, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 15th of September, 1721

Anno 1721, No. 111

Monday's Gazette.

Barbary

ALGIERS, 13 August. Since all the corsair ships have returned from the sea here without any other prizes than a Spanish merchant vessel and a grey brigantine having been taken, and the entire campaign of Kamadau now being concluded, the Bey has found himself obliged to permit the rovers to set out again: 200 of these ships set out this day in the aforementioned corsair fleet, carrying 30, 15 and 40 cannon respectively, putting to sea, wherefore one may not long expect to hear news of German and other ships from these waters: nevertheless through careful family vessels one hears that the Dutch Squadron, numbering 200 in all besides the Venetian ship, and of ships through these waters. Many anxious for a bombardment toward the mountains have withdrawn, and in their former outposts there now come those by which one is not yet fully reassured. Since the equipping continues here, one expects that shortly more ships will depart.

Spain

CADIZ, 19 August. There has arrived in this Bay the Maria, under Captain Galiere, coming from the Strait of Gibraltar. According to reports from Salé, the rovers there had captured the ship the Antoinette Galy; at Hamburg: There was it seized, and here likewise arrested, the crew being already plundered, but Captain [?] Tormouy brought it back near Cape Montero by a small ship with 13 [?] and 25 men; in the said ship were left 120 [?] bales, some cargoes and damasks, and killings, 30 barrels and 70 [?] pounds net. By the Admiral of Salé a French frigate was seized, coming from this Bay and named the Guise [?], but the [?] of the [?] still here. Lisbon 44 and 2 [?].

France

News has been received that the French Ambassador at [?] in Stephano had found a great treasure, which had been buried there; whereover some difficulties had arisen, so that the said Ambassador withdrew himself there into quiet retreat. Young letters have been received from the [?] of this month from Smyrna which report that the Government there was unwilling to admit more Admirals, certain ships which from commercial places out of France might come there, and that the Turks had already sent back ships which had come there.

Extract from Marseille, 25 August.

Of these weeks there happened here at the Delf two families; as when the houses were being searched and laid open one found too much, that there in those houses which must be on the Street, and in [?] watch is. Those arising therefrom have thus made themselves secure, and though this is not lawful, it has nevertheless been better than...

MADRID, 26 August. Yesterday Her Majesties, the Prince of Asturias and the Infanta, the great Office Shoulders for the contract of the Lord and the safeguarding of the Prince of Asturias, who now in his [?] year is. The Ecclesiastical Ministers have thus made themselves secure, and though this is not lawful, it has nevertheless been better than one here in the Quarters otherwise would be able to become...

PARIS, 8 September. The indisposition which the Sieur le Pelletier [Editor's note: French financial minister] has caused by the overturning of his coach continues still, but one hopes nevertheless that he will recover. The Sieur le Pelletier de la Houllaye and the Secretaries there are conferring frequently with the Cardinal du Bois, which Cardinal it is said will shortly be declared a Minister there. In the Archbishop's Palace a meeting is to be held of all the Prelates which have accepted the Accommodation, and since the Pope absolutely requires that the Continuation be purely and uniformly accepted, without regard to any exceptions, it is perceived that they are resolved to retract. The two mutineers lately troubled by the [?] and since removed, are by order of the Duke Regent celebrated: the Sergeant and three persons of the Guard are placed in Arrest; nevertheless in the Musketeers it is ordered that no reprisals be used upon [?] and Jansen Prison. Since it continues good in the midst [of harvest], one hopes that the Vintage will be good. It is said that the Sieur Jean Law [Editor's note: Scottish financier and architect of the Mississippi Scheme] will shortly return hither, with the [?] as extraordinary Envoy of the Duke of [?] to reside here at court, this rumor of his return has caused some liveliness in the Action [stock market], as the same has risen from twenty-two to nearly one hundred per cent. Since the Ordinance for the departure of Officers to their posts, lying in the four Provinces, the following Articles have yet been issued. One dated 30 August ordained that the [?] from Germany and Flanders in this [?], buying in curtailed [?] shall pay. An Article whereby are annulled the two Sentences of the Judges at Amiens, and the same condemned together with the [?], Procureurs, [?] Clerks, with heavy fines, for fault of the Controlling of the Letters and the [?] Seal. Another concerning the Judiciary of the Consuls, the Commerce and the Merchants. Still those Regulations regarding the Division and Extent of the Capital [?] Keepers of Guienne, Poitou, Saintonge, and the Land of Aunis. And finally an Article concerning the Order of the Holy Spirit.

AMSTERDAM, September 15. Yesterday entered the port the Huybeit de Draak from Greenland, captained by Corn. Winter, and Pieter Vielander, both from Davis Strait. In the Lands Deep anchorage lies the Ship of St. Thomas, as well as the East India Ship Stadhuys of Delft, for the Chamber of Hoorn, having departed from the others around Pato. In the Vlie entered and moored at the city the Pieter Melder, coming from Davis Strait; the same vessel had anchored off the north point of Hitland with the East India return ships. In Meethuygem, for this Chamber, and the Stadhuys of Delft, for the Chamber of Hoorn, which latter has already entered the port: never the Ship 't Hays in the Vlie, for which 2 Ships were [?], but the Ship Middelstoor, for the Chamber of Zeeland, was left around Kro, and another the Ship Waddinzyen, likewise thereabouts, so it is expected that the Crown ships will come over the bar, and some of them will already be inside. According to letters from Petersburg, His Czarist Majesty, together with the Czarina and the Baron of Holstein, etc., had returned from Krononfost, and they held a most agreeable audience with the Swedish Envoy at Krononfost. There is expectation at Petersburg that the Peace with Sweden will be concluded. On July 7 there departed from Riga the Sara Galy, Captain Burpan, who from the 7th or 8th of the month was to sail shortly from there. According to letters from Cadiz, the ships of Christoffel Boeyel and Jan Brink that had arrived there would have to await further orders from the Court before they might be unloaded. At Nantes a ship from St. Domingo had arrived. At Bergen [?] Bur, Hans Gabriel, and Jan Meyer the Younger of St. Marien, and Barent Fruyken of Königsberg, Sictje Teunis from Bordeaux arrived on the Elf. In the Vlie entered several sloops from Königsberg, Gerrit Slap from Greenland, Dirk [?] Kuypen, Klaas Cornelis and Jan de Buren from Davis Strait. To Bordeaux had come the 2 Gezalters, Corn. Walp and Willems Jacobis, and 2 Marien and de Hoop, Leendert Jacids, all from here. Yesterday sailed out from the port into the sea Gillis Pekel [?]

CUMARIA, July 16. It is reported that the Cape Jaia Brittas of Amsterdam has arrived there, from Rio de la Hacha and others; however regarding the Fort of Looisbaever, Three Bloedhargh were to depart for Amsterdam, such that the Bark de Diamant on April 16 in Havana [?], has safely passed through etc. The Second Ships of the Altevied etc. were in Porto de Porto [?]

PORTUGAL AND LISBON

LISBON, August 21. The fleet from Bahia and Pernambuco [?] on the 19th came the Forerunners, on the 20th [?] etc. The Second Ships of the Altevied etc. were in Porto de Porto [?] Inhabitants within the [?] ordered that ever and ever in the said Balding situation arrived Ships from Bahia and 2 from Fort à Fond-hayz [?], together with 1 or 2 other Ventos de Misregenta still a vat of cargo with no certainty likewise [?] in 2 or 2 good [?] Ships besides all the other Goods, and still 5 both minted and unminted Gold. Y Gold sold, including all other goods [?] so that this Fleet is most unusual. The Fleet, which has remained here so long, will depart from Hamburg; the third made haste in [?] August. N. de Bos [?] the Merchants Capt. Manol de Silva Bar Maya, Joao Baulista Ribeyro, Pedro dos San dagoga Roa, and Francisco Alvez Motila Xavin, Antonio Dias Fedroiro, and Faulino de van de Keysen, Antonio da Coida Lisboa and Bento Fereyra, which also from Bahia must to Jeronimo: Capt. Vital Coutto, and [?] etc. [?] have [?] but the Islands of Teu [?]

SWEDEN

STOCKHOLM, September 23. The latest [?] of this fleet from Marsts [?] was held up [?] [?] of our Plespo passed. O [?]

COLOGNE, September Here [?] Cologne [?] Kurya Direction, the [?], and Ministry Deputized, the Gentlemen Neuman and Derter, on [?] [?] City Delburtien [?], and the report comes as soon as possible will be brought to conclusion.

Wednesday, September 17, 1721, in the morning at 9 and in the evening [?] in the Hope, a party [?] [?] Leyden Lake, and common [?] hals 2 guilders, and Wille [?], Emaldyne, common Kirsch, Cotton, [?] etc., and party Painting [?] [?] in [?] [?]

STOCKHOLM, September 3.

LONDON, September 3.

LONDON, September 13.

At Leyden on the Breedeltstraat, September 15, 1721. With Privilege.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 15th of september, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 112.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 17th of September, 1721
West Indies

CUMARIA, 16 July. News has been received here from Curaçao that the captains Marcus Starema and Jan Braam of Amsterdam have arrived there, as has the Concordia under Jan Geurtle from Rio de la Hache, and others. However, it has not yet been discovered where Pierre Loefhouwen, Dirk Houthuysen, and Klaas Keytop of Amsterdam, who are pursuing gold, have gone. News was also received that the Bark de Diamant on 10 April lay in great distress near Havana in a [?] well, though the coast has not yet [?]. The Zeeland ships under captains Hendrik Endt, Jan Akerveilt, etc., must depart from the port of Porto Cabello.

Portugal

LISBON, 21 August. On this date we received here the long-awaited news that the fleet from Bahia and Pernambuco was before the bar. We beheld the forerunners, which consisted of 2 ships, and on the same day all the remainder arrived—namely 2 ships, 2 East India vessels, and one Pirogue or ship from Goa [?]—to the great joy of the inhabitants. This being the largest fleet that has ever come from Bahia. Moreover, there have arrived on this river 2 ships from Bahia and 2 from Pernambuco, which [?]. Because the navigators have not yet come ashore, the cargo has not been precisely measured; however, it is estimated at the same to contain 22 to 24,000 chests of sugar, 26 to 21,000 rolls of tobacco, along with all the other spices, and also 8 to 9 million crusados of 200 in minted and unminted gold, which amounts to approximately 70 to 80 tons of gold, all of which is valued with the 2 East India vessels for 2,300 which this fleet has brought up. Now in order to inform you who commands this fleet, which has been so long awaited, I shall in a few words relate the details, being in the East India ship under Captain Juan Antones, N. S. de los Vales, which must put in at Bahia, and the merchant captains Manoel de Silva Barros, Manoel Rodrigues Maya, Joao Nuncinta Rubayra, Pedro Nunez Rodriguez, Pedro Goncalves Aloza, and Francisco Alves Moni. Those under captains Dias Nuno, Antonio Drus Paidoto, and Fandito Soares bound for Angola; and those under captains Antonio da Coeña Lisboa, Louis Fereryta Salgado, and Bento Fereryta, which must also put in at Bahia, then to the port of Recife; Captain Vidal Corea with N. Michel and Captain Franc. Co. elho de Oliveyra to the islands of Terceira.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 9 September. The last expedition, which was sent from this court toward Neustadt with later-resolved dispatches, has been detained by contrary winds. A courier from our plenipotentiary in Constantinople has arrived at court, bringing the duplicate of the instructions concerning the Swedish proposal. Therein also came a courier from England to the worthy minister Finck and Admiral Norris. He has delivered some answers to the former regarding his king's concerns, as well as some orders from Admiral Norris concerning the disposition of the fleet in the Baltic Sea, upon which the same has also made some movement in his squadron to [?] to Helsingör.

Great Britain

LONDON, 9 September. Last Sunday, Doctor Rurough preached in the chapel of Kensington before the King, the Prince, and the Prince of Wales, and finally well the Frederic [?] Her K. H. returned to Richmond to hear afternoon prayers. Yesterday the King and the young princess dined with the Prince and Princess, their father and mother, to pay them a visit. And yesterday, when wine and the Order of the Bath [?], in which the Bishop of Winchester participated; the solemn ceremonies were performed. The King here at London on [?] Nicholas Lechmeyere as Earl of Great Britain, with which it was charged to elevate him to Viscount, and 100 [?] will He have given to the nobility likewise, and Burlington has also joined the Order of the Bath; Earl, and Mecrore Klimanteck and a Countess of Ireland. Friday evening the Lord Mayor went to Lambeth to appear there.

LONDON, 12 September. Yesterday the last stone was laid on the tower of St. Michael's Church, [?] also on the tower of St. Michael's Church [?] this rich work well on. This is considered to be one of the finest ornaments of this city. The Order of the Bath has [?] to the citizens and the directors of the East India Company, an agent is expected here to settle certain affairs with the said company. [Text becomes increasingly garbled in transcription]. The bankruptcy has not yet been declared, for the Admiralty has not yet [?]. The admiral on [?] the chapter to be preserved. Yesterday [?] on the Bredelstreet with privilege.

---

Wednesday's Courant

of the Camps, treaty with Spain and the West Indies, and has moved His Majesty, the King of Spain, to compensate for the damage which the Company suffered during the war through the retention of its effects. The captains and [?] committee have finally been printed and are sold for 2 Shillings and [?].

The shares of the Bank are at 132; East India Company 145 and a half; South Sea 145; Million Bank 122; Lottery App. 101 and three quarters; Black at 3; Bank Ann. 101; [?] per cent 94; Africa Company 23 and a half; the Exchange [?] 9 and a quarter; [?] 9 and a half; Dock Build. 31.

France

PARIS, 12 September. Monday word came that Cardinal de Mailly, being overtaken by a serious illness, found himself in a dangerous condition, which is why his niece Mademoiselle de Nieles hastened to His Eminence; however, Tuesday news arrived that he was better, and since then we have learned that the apoplexy has turned into a paralysis, on account of which His Eminence was confined to bed for some time. The Sieur le Pelletier des Champs finds himself much better. When the King last Monday returned from the Palais des Harbors to Burgundy and again to the Palace of the Tuileries, it was observed that many of the Guards and Pages had drunk so heavily that they were scarcely in a state to properly perform their functions. It is said that the Princess of the realm wishes to hold a celebration for the King. The Duke of Villeroy, in the form of the Duchess, having inquired whether the Queen would also not wish to receive, it is said to have been answered that the small rooms were too small to receive so great a guest. Although the plague in Provence is over, the King of Sweden's captains, without certificates from there in Piedmont, have had their effects burned. Ships depart from Marseille to some other ports, but mostly to the Levant, and Venice [?], those of Genoa and Livorno have offered them their harbors. In the Cevennes there is a lack of money, provisions, and workmen, such that there and in Languedoc things are in a deplorable state.

England

STOCKHOLM, 3 September. The last dispatch, which was sent from this court toward Neustadt with later-resolved orders, has been [?]. Two of the [?] couriers of our plenipotentiaries at court have arrived, bringing the duplicate of the issued Wisdom-Proposals.

The Netherlands

BRUSSELS, 15 September. The Marquis d'Artagnan, Governor of the State in the County of Ghent, who despite his advanced age had gone to Aix, has died there. The venerable Gerard [?] was buried yesterday in the Church of Sablon. The officer whom he had taken with him to Flanders died yesterday afternoon of an apoplexy. The Baron van Wintenfeld, Governor of Dendermonde, who had also set out for the water route toward Brussels, was likewise overtaken by an illness, from which it is feared he will already have died. The Marquis Rubij, Governor of Antwerp, departed from there yesterday toward Vienna. Yesterday evening around ten o'clock the Marquis de Frie arrived here by post. The Governor of Aix, who had been away three days, returned here yesterday to his government.

Precise orders have been sent to the governors and magistrates of the Franche Comté frontier concerning the renewal of precautions which are customary to prevent the importation of French ships and goods from infected regions, with the persons being ordered to maintain strict adherence, such that people will have to observe quarantine.

AMSTERDAM, 15 September. Yesterday there came into Texel the East India ship the Huis te Victoria; the other ship Merhuisen is also said to be near the coast; and that the 2 other ships Middeloeuer and Waddinxveen were dispatched toward Batavia, having departed from the Cape on 2 May homeward bound.

THE HAGUE, 16 September. The gentlemen of the councils of Holland and West Friesland have assembled again today. [?] The chosen ministers and Baron Ulset have gone toward the embassy, carrying [?] high principal [?], and will return about the end of October or beginning of November. Another extraordinary envoy regarding the [?] has been sent to the respective provinces to be published, wherein it is stated among other things:

"In [1721] We have taken it upon ourselves to inform you that the [pestilential and secret] sickness, which in the preceding year [?] has [spread] greatly in Marseille, has already extended to [?] and several other places in Provence—spreading from time to time here and there in the neighborhood—and we have just grounds to be concerned that the plague of the inhabitants or [?], we have just grounds to be concerned that [?] sickness should not be brought into these lands, we have deemed it necessary to use all possible precautions, [?] all contained in our preceding [decrees], so that so great an evil under God's gracious protection be prevented. FOR THIS we have deemed it fitting to forbid, so as we forbid by this [decree], that no ship or ships coming from Marseille, Toulon or [?] from the coast of Languedoc or Provence, and coming explicitly from any province of the Kingdom of [?] on the Mediterranean Sea, shall enter the Kingdom of the English and at [?] located there, shall ship with their cargo wholly or in part—

without exception; and the people punished with death. [We forbid] sea ships at all harbors [?] Britain, from any ships coming from the aforementioned quarantine areas, to allow entry or to receive them in ships and to admit them; &c.

That also good goods coming from the aforementioned provinces of France on the Mediterranean Sea, which may be imported into these lands; on pain of death &c.

End of our report

[Left Column]

[The original transcription of this column is severely corrupted and illegible in the source document. The text appears to be administrative regulations concerning maritime commerce, quarantine procedures, and trade restrictions related to ships arriving from the Levant, but the transcription errors make reliable translation impossible. Key fragments suggest regulations regarding:

- Penalties for smuggling goods (1000 guilders reward mentioned) - Restrictions on goods from France and French-controlled territories - Requirements for ship captains to provide detailed manifests of cargo - Medical declarations and health certifications from ship's officers - Procedures for unloading cargo at designated ports (Texel, Vlie) - Lists of permitted goods from Levantine ports (wine, brandy, oil, salt, dried fruits, spices, timber, etc.) - Requirements for lighter ships and crew conduct

Without a clearer source transcription, a more precise translation cannot be provided.]

PROPERTY SALES NOTICES

At Amsterdam on Friday, 9 October, there will be sold a fine pair of Cyprus and Surinamese [goods], and E. Thomas d'Estoe, 10 September 1721. On Wednesday at [time], in T. Peter Greene Desbalkgn and Foirels, of 40 to 44 feet...

The Executors of the Testament of the late Widow Lucas de Leneery intend to sell by public auction a large quantity of Rooms, consisting of the following goods: [details to follow] daily near the aforementioned Widow's property at Hoogewoerds Poolt in the City of Leiden.

[Right Column]

### PORTUGAL

LISBON, 21 August. Today there arrived Joao de Simas Cameliera from Mr. Allen of Cork, James Burne and Mathias [...]. Four English [ships] Edward Falkingham, Eduard Pulthan and Charles Ber[...] Baal bound for the East Indies, Christoffel Thomas to Eyrona towards Galloway, Tolbio Dorfels towards the Pa[cific] towards Falmouth, etc. Today the ships Joh. Lei to Christianias and the Chan[...]. Old Bergentonia [reports] that 200 [vessels] still lie on this river, including one with the East India goods of Capt. George Pursîs, lying at Hamburg, etc.

### INGERMANLAND

PETERSBURG, 22 August. Tomorrow [the Tsar will be] in Rusterlandia, and the entire Court in the Church of this city with the Swedes, [while they] make new preparations for certain places in Livonia. [The same reports that] His Grace [has had] good fortune on the course, and one would wish the same to be repeated. [Someone] arrived from Newl[and], and [is] proceeding toward his wounds, but [other details are] not clearly legible.

### NAPLES

NAPLES, 26 August. The Marquis van Maoîs [has] recently appointed from Ampi [...] the Woodville [...] Herren Taletti, Giovine, in the duchy of Circelli, [and] to this the Court [has] nominated him; and if the order does not go [correctly], the Hiroen will be sold by his K[...], Magn[...].

### ROME

ROME, 30 August. Saturday, being the day of Joeemis, Cardinal Cînfugetularis of the Church went to the Island, where his body is preserved, [still] in deep care, with [reports of] winning in hand, which would number between 50 and 40 Plenaries. Monday Loâns, [when] the Cardinals [gathered], [and told] of the P[...] National Church of this Holy [place]: Cardinal Maie [and] family made it known that they would not [participate]. Cardinal de Rothan [appeared] with a [?] to the aforementioned Church, [concerning] the Foundation of the Sacred College and the [?] [which] provided the occasion for [viewing] magnificent new Carriages, [which] [had] 40 Staffpagrs. All of the Cardinal's [retinue] was [present], but they held [it] especially by Gold and [...]. Mass was said in Music and thereafter the [service concluded].

---

Printed by KLOPPER, on the Breedel Street, September 1721. With Privileges.

Notices & Advertisements

On Monday, 4 October in the morning at 6 o'clock, there will be sold in the Hall on the new [location] the estate HAASWYC with meadow and arable lands within the bounds of this city, consisting of a front and back house, and previously cultivated fields of 11 morgens and 165 roods, located in the lake; bequeathed by the late Lord Lieutenant Admiral van Almonde.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 17th of september, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 113.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 19th of September, 1721
Left Column

PORTUGAL.

LISBON, 21 August. Between the 12th and 18th, the following have arrived here: Joao de Siluos Corneliu van Michael, Hendry Allen from Cork, James Boyne, and Mathieu from Caleta Port; and the English warship under Captain Edueard Falkergem departed, along with Eduard Nidmels and Christoffel Baal bound for the Baltic Sea, Christoffel Thomals for Youghal, Will. Hyson for Galloway, Munnelly Yodella for the Levant, James Boyne for Falmouth, etc. On this day entered the Teyde: Iob. Ees and Jan Bergman for Christiana, and the Charitas, Djof Bergelen for Gothenburg. There remain in port on this fluyt 45 English merchantmen along with the warship under Captain Dorus, 7 Frenchmen, 3 Hollanders, 3 Spaniards, one Hamburg vessel, etc.

ENGLAND.

PETERSBURG, 22 August. His Czarist Majesty and the entire Court are expected to arrive in this city tomorrow. It is supposed the Swedes would undertake more expeditions, taking several places in Livonia; Count Nicjael Repuslew of Newstadt has arrived and proceeded to His Czarist Majesty's presence, though no news of [?] has been heard.

ITALY.

NAPLES, 26 August. The Marquis of Malleone, having resigned from his office as Lieutenant of the Royal Chamber, has had Messrs. Tuttini Gioruito and Haring well Clirceli Eluardi of the Carnici nominated by the Court to that position; and it is awaited with great expectation which of these three gentlemen will be promoted by His Majesty to that office.

ROME, 30 August. Saturday, being the day before St. Bartholomaeus, the Cardinal-Chapters visited all lands of the Church of that Saint on the Island, where his body is preserved. The Church was visited by the Cardinals as well as other church scholars, who sang the Mass; there were present between 200 Penitents. Monday, the Feast of St. Louis, the Cardinals held a general ceremony of 37 Chapels in the Church through the Church of the Saint, and Cardinal Marcosoli and S. Capit. D. ci. Pamill announced that they could not attend; one went to the aforementioned Church to attend the function and receive Sargo Collegio: on this occasion His Eminence held discussions with the Cardinals, some of which were reported to be difficult. The Seventh Mass was sung in music, and thereafter the Te Deum was sung in thanksgiving for the good health of the King of France: at the end of this function His Eminence felt no discomfort. [?] Daughter of the household, and distributed Scudi to a poor Jew. The third [?] sang the Church of Sr. Louis for the first time. Tuesday, the Marquis Spada, Minister of the Duke of Lorraine, had a brief audience with the Pope. Wednesday, Cardinal of Rohan took the entire Casa Albani. Thursday, the Cardinals expressed their respects at the arrival of the reigning Empress. [?] of all the Cardinals, Ministers of Princes, [?], and the appointed [?]: The most humble was His Church of St. Augustine, each Feast was celebrated there: in the valley below, the Father Patrimony was evident to all the Poor, which His Majesty attended. After the V. of these matters, the Ministry heard [?], and he gave to the Cardinal the R. Band the Abdication, which a [?] had requested against the day: on account of the aforementioned Duchess which he had received from the Patriarchate: That afternoon the Pope went to visit the Card. of St. Catherine of Siena. There the Relic of Sia Rola de Lima was liberated: after His [?] the Chanceli Coirbaa in [?] with the [?] Pall in the Cloister; and gave remedy there to all the Cardinals who gathered at the Door, [?] the French and Rhine [?] in the aforementioned Cloister so that Cardinal Huntbel Albani depart for Soruansio, and subsequently the Dames [?] who gathered at the Door, [?] Cardinal during this absence, with this [?] oversight of the function of Kameling where [?].

LIEGE AND OTHER STATES. There are furnished the Alphabet, Captain Giusepp Critifton from Dublin, the Europa, Captain Thomas Townaid from Genua, the [?] from London, [?], Giacomo Cardin from Algiers, and Giusepe Richard from Alexandria. The Ship Coscaldit: Ter. Holder, from Amsterdam toward [?]; it encountered a Storm twelve miles from here, the ship being damaged, but the [?] saved it, now the aforementioned ship repaired. The [?] ship of Captain Wilkl from Amsterdam bound for Genua on [?] August. Since the King of Great Britain has authorized his Resident in this City to resolve the differences between the Nobles [Jalliniani], it is hoped that he will be agreed for mutual convenience.

MILAN, 27 August. Upon receiving orders from His Imperial Majesty, work has begun to repair all the pioneer places of this State, and daily wood-work is noted therein, or none in the fortress [?] and forts of the noted, Thursday all [?] here from Smirna are the Governor of the State: therein the third [?] Talla being [?], [?] shortly.

AMSTERDAM STOCKS.

CARGO of the time CARGO of the day of [?] of the [?] Seats. conneet, 1613. Ch. 1663 College fa..., 1683 Collage III..., 1793 [?] Raijs, 1723 Lumis [...] & [...] [...].

Right Column

SPAIN.

ST. SEBASTIAN, 2 September. In this City and Province, since the French militia departed from it on the 25th last, nothing has occurred except that an Alcalde from here was elected to Hood; for the administration of the tolls there, as well as to oversee the export of silver. Since our new Captain General arrived yesterday under cannon salute from Madrid, it is expected that he will issue new orders for various changes. The ship of Christian Bakendorf, arrived here from Amsterdam, is having its goods discharged. It contains a mine supplied with gold and silver, which mine was discovered on the aforementioned island. Yesterday an English ship from the island arrived in this harbor, whose Captain reports that the pelts are most abundant.

GERMANY AND NEIGHBORING REGIONS.

VIENNA, 6 September. Wednesday night at 12 o'clock, the Emperor appointed Count Coprnaad of Staremberg, Privy Counselor and Count of His Imperial Majesty, to undertake the journey to the court of Great Britain. That same evening, the Prince of Passau had an audience with the Emperor, to whom he submitted a memorandum in his own behalf and that of his bishopric of Passau, showing how gladly the aforementioned Bishop and High Diocese would comply with His Majesty's wishes, yet this could not well occur, being bound by old rights of the Church for some centuries (to great prejudice), and that it would not be possible for posterity; but all this has not and will not be able to help, for the Emperor is firm in his resolve to press the claim of this City, whatever it may cost. Prince Eugenius's journey to Paris is postponed for this year; and since the last conference, where his mandate held, it is reported that not one man, let alone the projected 12 regiments, needs to be discharged—it is promised that all regiments will be placed in complete state.

REGENSBURG, 8 September. With letters from the Palatinate, Fryburg, it is learned that the Elector sent a Mandate to the Reformed Church Council, accusing them of having issued sharp letters, with [?] to the Secular Corps and its Plenipotentiaries to inform how much of this non-restored ecclesiastical revenue amounts to; to which the aforementioned Church Council responded in writing that it could not provide this to the aforementioned Elector, nor had his Ministers been satisfied. Furthermore, all in the previous Council, and it was thought that Count of Wels had demonstrated so clearly that the Emperor's wishes were being obeyed, that by the Emperor it would be [?] such would find much work to prove to the contrary. Meanwhile, the Protestant Ministers confer daily on the second letter to his Imperial Majesty [?], which despite the term being extended multiple times, the matters of the Palatinate remain unresolved.

REGENSBURG, 13 September. Monday, on account of the Feast of the Birth of Mary, no Imperial Diet was held. The Protestant Ministers, placed again in conference over the Rehaltsstaken matters with differences, have adopted measures not pleasing to the Imperial Ministers, and this had already caused some commotion. The aforementioned Imperial Ministers are ready to receive the Reply of the Protestant Corps to their Last Answer, according to the various articles of the Rehaeltszaaken with great expectation, to renew the Conferentiaries thereover; but for this further preparation is still needed, as one wishes to see the implementation of the orders that the Emperor has so often repeated through the Ecclesiastical Commissioner, to cease the grievances in the Palatinate, and to proceed with the [?] in The Hague, where the Imperial Diet will be held, is being pressed so strongly that further preliminaries will be [?] before winter. All the Ministers of Würtemberg and Holy Cassel, returned here, it is said, will undertake to mediate the union of the two Protestant religions, which by some is judged fitting, and by others unfit. The Count of Wels, returned from his Commission to the German Courts, has conferred with the principal Commissioners and other Imperial Ministers, and was entertained by Baron Kircheler at dinner; he will remain here a few more days and then continue his journey to Vienna: nothing can be ascertained as to what report he has given regarding the religious matters in the Palatinate, though new civil matters have recently come from there, which are being examined, and among others one from the Bishop of Osnabrück against the Chapter, which is again being strongly investigated by the Imperial Court against the Protestants: this matter being brought before the Imperial Diet.

GREAT BRITAIN.

LONDON, 12 September. Besides the ship Erford, which is daily expected here, there are also awaited this year the St. George from Bombay and the King George from Borneo.

CARGO OF THE EAST INDIA RETURN SHIPS, namely the Duke of York from the Hague with Bengal cargo, the Catgarini from [?], and the Bishop-setter from China and the T...n St. George; consisting of Hoks Addidels, 2149 Archebamides, 1058 Alibelles, 1133 Allabennies, 3 Arctic Sieur 1400 Basnáas, 1387 Cartidures, 1131 Cherocannes, 141 Chilias, 1632 Chicoo, 300 [...] Mosters, 121 Copela, 249 Cottumees, 108 Churchles, 911 Cutlees, 10661 Cullaer, 1603 Donphonies, 2214 Elatches, 399 Ementjels, 1380 Gelo-, 2147 guileudie Gireens, 230 Gurgones 4) Gordes, 1430 Guli-rah, 1088 Hunbains, 500 Latcories, 14190 long Cloths, 450 of Disayme, 1391 Lunger Harb, 11818 Mulmaith, 1449 thereof bleached, [text continues]

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, 17 September. The ship of Captain And. wil Roeck has arrived at Bilbao; the St. Joseph [from] Jan Romme [is sailing] from Bilbao toward Rotterdam.

Goods [cargo], consisting of the cargo of the overseas return vessels, have been sent to Middelveerse for the Chamber Zuidland's warehouses; for the Chamber Amsterdam [to] Noordwaddingveer; for the Chamber Rotterdam [to] Quadeluyn of Delft; for the Chamber Hoorn; and The Hague for the Chamber Enkhuizen; these aforementioned five ships having departed from Batavia on 12 January and 12 February 1721, and all arrived safely in the harbors of these lands.

191,380 pounds brown pepper, 6,190 pounds white pepper, 314,425 pounds of poeyu-soyeu, 406,000 pounds saltpeter, 206,000 pounds Japanese steel-copper, 6,756 pounds Java indigo, 11,850 pounds sappanwood, 31,950 to 200 pounds [?], 392 Japanese [goods], 2,700 pounds benzoin, 600 pounds red sealing-wax, 1,214 pounds floret gums, 7,625 pounds Java cacao [?], 2,900 pounds ale, 3,780 pounds red anise, 1,200 pounds rattan and rottan, 17 rotanaise, 2,400 pounds bandanous, Banda or taffia de poule, 1,520 pounds sweeties, 300 pounds [?], 167 pounds nekaas and other goods, 2 large spoons of grain [?], gurans louven, 1,310 pounds various bethils, 1,640 mallemoelers, 2,950 Bengale coffee, 476 doegallen, 1,600 thernsdains, 850 tanjepers, 3,000 hamvans, 960 various pedal [?], 3,700 solotopie, 3,600 schiazen, 9,600 griffoen, 900 tellerie ghoctan, 420 various dongys, 16,800 various roemalis, 1,700 phoilafen, 6,000 various gingans, 8,160 various salempouris, 19,740 various guineas, and 1,600 various zeploques.

HAARLEM, 11 September. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West Friesland convened today.

[The following passages are heavily corrupted in the transcription and cannot be reliably rendered into coherent English.]

---

In England

[Transcription too corrupted for reliable translation]

---

France

PARIS, 15 September. Since the Pope continues to refuse acceptance of the Bull Unigenitus, [which is] particularly contrary to the body of doctrine of Cardinal de Noailles, it is proposed to hold a meeting tomorrow of the aforementioned Cardinal and other accommodating prelates at the archbishopric to establish their opinions on the retroactive effect of the steps they have taken toward this accommodation. Sunday the matter of the [?] ecclesiastical goods passed in the Council of Regency, and yet the ecclesiastical estate of the Regency is limited to certain monies, which are to be paid by the farmers-general; for thus one hopes to remedy the losses; the misappropriated barrels of 1700 [?] and must be sold. The young Marquise de Villan has shown evident signs of pregnancy, and the entire family is rejoiced. The gentleman de Thierly and an attorney at law who signed the petition for the release of the Duke of la Force, and who on St. Louis's day at the burning of the fireworks in France [?], has died. The Marquis de Sommety, son of the Count of Sommety, Under-Governor of the King, who had withdrawn from Burgundy, was appointed on 30 May [?], because he obtained the reversion. When the King attended the hunting of the Duke of Bouillon and the Count of St. Maure, ordinary Master of Horse to His Majesty, wished likewise to attend, he sat on a small cushion; but the same was dropped by the aforementioned Master of Horse on the ground, which caused general laughter, though no harm resulted.

Regarding the Director's lottery, it is announced that the drawing of the lots for August and September [will be] on the known day, [and] a complete drawing of new [tickets] will take place. Note: it is reported that the tickets [drawn in] the freedom of drawing of the lottery gentlemen, as those in the second class inclusive.

As it is reported that at the municipal cash office of Beaulieus, [reference] No. 21490 has been given on the Lottery A.V.H., and [those] who had not received [theirs], the aforementioned ticket (which is undersigned in H. Witte and the date left blank, to be completed with the 15 September 1721) should present [it]; the same shall be done as on the last ticket, as directed above.

As the Council of the City, regarding debts within the City of Gouda, by [?] witnesses there, has appointed as his universal heir his next friend for the first half, and his next female friend and heir [?] in the capacity of mother and executor of the aforementioned will, not properly [?], of 100 of the father named as intestate [property], 200 [?]; each in the quality of executors of Cornelis de Vori, are hereby notified to present themselves before January 1723, or otherwise the aforementioned executors [shall be] duly appointed.

[Multiple auction notices follow with corrupted text that cannot be reliably translated.]

---

Spain

CADIZ, 16 August. The much-desired news is universal. [It has] brought successful conclusions, Seville, Segovia, etc., receiving new orders daily; Celestine [ships?] are all in order for the loss of the ships in which fine goods or merchandise are contained, and all shipmasters, Holland, [?]. It is requested that an order be sent [?] from Vera Cruz shall be [?], [and from this without] the Spanish Admiral: Mister Kyp; [?] named, and Spain competitors under Don Antonio de [?]; the following day [there] arrived in this Bay [?] Holland, Captain Kismith; sailed in hopes to meet the aforementioned Dutch Admiral; [?] under Spain [?] through [?] and [it] is known that of safes [goods?] were to be [?] from the foot [?] to reckon, which in this Bay said Exguia the lords, [the] Samuel, Tourzoa, Concordia, Captain Podeftas of Lisbon and Pegeltya of Cork; [then] departed the Maria, Will Grace, Diego Muquier and Bilbao, the Spain, Francisco de Leon toward the Crown [?] remain against the English, in Flanders, or by [?] toward Dunkirk and Ostend, toward Bordeaux, 7 Holland, [and] 2 [?] ships.

---

By Privilege.

Course of Exchange

Exchange rates today: Madrid 90, Cadiz 114½, Seville 113½, Bilbao 90½, Lisbon 94⅔, Venice 81⅜, Genoa 94½, Paris 41¼-¼, London 34 shillings 8 pence; Leipzig current 13-12 rix-dollars; St. Danzig 202; Rostock 93⅛; St. Hamburg 12-2 rix-dollars; St. Antwerp 2 per cent ¼; Brussels 2½; Zeeland ¼-1; Rotterdam 3 rix-dollars. Bank agio ½-1; the English Bank 113, East India Company 141, and [?] 141.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 19th of september, 1721. — With Privilege.
