No. 1721.No. 114.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Sunday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 21st of September, 1721
Barbary States

SALLEE, 26 July. Recently one of our smallest ships plundered the galley Amourette, Captain Ter Moeye, [?] and subsequently prepared all [?] to squeeze for the intended community. Captain Ter Moeye, being the King's slave, has since left unsatisfied for Mequinez. On the 31st of this month, another French ship, traveling from Cadiz to Malta, was similarly plundered here. The English Ambassador, who is stationed in Mequinez, has still not had an audience with the King of Morocco. From here, four privateering ships with 44 and 8 guns have put to sea.

Spain

CADIZ, 16 August. The longing for the Fleet, which people expect at Seville, is great. New large quantities continue to be received daily at the mints of Cuenca, Seville, Segovia, etc. [?] Money matters are confused. Still, no orders have come from Madrid regarding the unloading of the ships, which once had [?]; one continues to examine all English, Dutch, and other vessels carefully. It is reported that there is news that our entire Fleet from Vera Cruz will have arrived, and each intelligence brings better news, without bringing the silver. On the 20th of this month, the Dutch Admiral, the Heer van Sommelydyk, sailed with Captain Keo to Malaga, to [?] with the Spanish warships under Dom Antonio de Serrano; the following then arrived in this Bay the Dutch warship Welinkhad; Captain Smith. On the 27th, further ships chose to join the aforementioned Dutch Admiral. Work is underway to have the two warships of Captain Keo and Wethealen cleaned and made ready. News has been received that those of Sallee were preparing to outfit two ships, each with 24 guns for piracy. [?] on the 11th in this Bay were cleared: the Brittania, John Everts of London; the Samuel, Thomas Harper of the Canaries; the Concordia, Captain Porlell of Lisbon; the Vriedensgse, Edmund Albin of Cork; and departed the Maria, Willi Appilton to Aleppo; the ship Grace, Diego Musset to Bilbao; the Greena, and Captain Francisco de Leon to the Canaries; [?] 160 ships. Still remaining: 17 English, 13 Flemish vessels, among them the Hoop, ship per Houwena to Dublin and Chichester, and Captain Eistancico Halet to Homeland; and 2 German ships.

France

PARIS, 11 September. The Heer de Beaupré, Lieutenant of Music to the King, has, by His Majesty's order, made an outline, which has been very agreeable to the King. His Majesty has ordered his Friends; for some things his name went forth on all his [?] letters of money delivery, but he resisted these letters very much without any extension of his authority. His aforementioned friend, Bishop the Heer de Beaupré, has much to concern [?], whether the Bishop [?] with much attention, but however he made them to him as bishop against his deep [?] [?] and ways against him thus, where it was [?] him here in he from us from outside we will see great [?] ; because that [?] of the base best; I go will given [?]; because that the subject of the Piece is; that the King all the Justice in France as his Servants of him can and Black say under it [?]. The great Wall around the City is not advantageous and gives itself good [?]. The Quay of the Louvre, which is forbidden, is it follows to be [?] very highly has begun at Hundsdx, but it is guessed that it will still take many Years, or all will be [?]. The Quay de la Viſelle will not must be raised will be. The whole appointed money will begin will be [?], well will still the foundation of this [?] will be, well will still the foundation will begin will be.

Great Britain

LONDON, 18 September. It is reported that the Clergy in the South Company are generally satisfied with what has been delivered in the Year, since [?] the cold fire in its [?] had, which caused a rot and horrible stench; and the Clergy of his Diocese say against it, that this is the deserved punishment for sending into exile so many dear Fellow-Brothers through the Lettres de Cachet, which were to be chosen from the Court itself; when he was a zealous advocate for the coalition; and had no regard for the Clergy which [?] the Clergy: it is assured that notwithstanding the very great revenues of this Cardinal, his Legacy will be very small. Of the 1172 Commissions, which were used for the visa, only 400 were kept in effect, which since then have verified nothing; the same say that they will only be employed to examine the Additions of the Columns. Saturday afternoon the aforementioned Jean d'Avergne; and Christopher Schreiber; a craftsman of German, both pastry makers' servants, were brought to the Place de Grève, convinced that they at night armed [?] the Passersby on the roads of St. Denis and Versailles robbed. The Heer Francis de Valbelle de Tourvés, Bishop of St. Omer, has departed from his Diocese in it. The Abbot of St. Albin; who is a Deacon, began his separation on the 13th to be ordained priest: it is said that the Prelacies and the Upper position will follow. Yesterday a long Council of State was held, which it is said may go well: but one still knows nothing of what was resolved there; nor whether it was about the Arrangement of Finances; or about other matters. It is said that in the future there will be only one Royal Council, to which they will call those who will be deemed necessary. There is again talk of a rise in the Stock-jobbing. According to the letters from Madrid of the 3rd of this month, express news had come to that City from Cadiz; that a ship from the South Seas had arrived with a rich Loading, and 1150 thousand Rixdollars in Crowns; namely 300,000 for the King; and 700,000 for private Merchants; besides other Goods which were in the whole ship-loading: it is said that the Silver to the Particulars; without it being brought anew into the Mint, will be handed over. News was received that the Galleons; those that would have taken this loading in the West Indies, would return to Spain with the King's Treasure; and that the Prince of Señor Bueno, Viceroy of Peru, from Lima to Panama had departed, to embark on the Fleet. The Marquis de Campo Florido conducted himself in Madrid very anxiously, so that one feared for his life. The Great-British Minister departed the evening of this day to the Escurial, to communicate Dispatches, which he had received by an Express, to the Secretary of State.

LONDON, 16 September. It is reported that the Shareholders in the South Sea Company are generally satisfied with what has been generated in the Year; Suppliers proposed and promised in themselves are the most in ordinary, there the Directors seeing the great derangement of the Company's affairs have attempted everything possible. The appointed Committee of this Company, to work on Maritime matters, will gather every Tuesday morning at 10 o'clock, to consult with the Experts of the Ships, which one intends to use in the service of the Company: also in the past week in a Meeting of Directors was resolved to use a large Sum of Money to buy all manufactured Manufactures, to send them to Cartagena, Porto Bello, and other Spanish Comptoirs; and it is said the general Meeting also was informed that one furnished Ship the Royal George was enabled to serve the Company's affairs with great diligence, and that the Viceroy of general Manufactures at Sea will draw; with an Opportunity of general Manufactures the Company will cost: which Cargaçoen loaded still with 20,000 [pounds] of said Manufactures [?]: and regarding Maritime matters the Clergy are very satisfied, that the Directors [?] Baylanders, who is a Merchant, appointed as Member of the just-mentioned Committee, because they promise themselves much good from the understanding, which that Member has in the interests of the Greenland Fishery, by which no Field will be lacking. It is said with much pleasure that the printed Toll-Cedules, regarding entrance and exit, increase daily, and certainly mainly on account of the entrance of great quantities to Portugal, Italy, and Muscovy, but mainly Spain [?]; whereby the People will place itself in order itself [?], and trade will also flourish again. The Reports of the secret Committee are in very great extent [?], and that the whole of the Out-going is great, and the Price of that Piece [?]; one has still not heard that anyone of that kind along the Street bought [?], that in is better become. The great Chancellor Lord Parker of Weulin, and Earl raised, under the title of Viscount Parker of Ewelin, and Earl of Orchester in Cheshire, Gilterns his [?]; Freights Papers and Beckeris, it shielded the Clerkship Transport of the common Directors of the taking the Clerkship Transport of the common Directors of the Rallaces over the recruitment of People for the Fifth[?] in the South Sea Company hired in Bushill Fields. It is said that seven very Regulations over the recruitment of People for the Fifth[?] in the South Sea Company, and says that his 192, O. I. C. 195, taken totalled. P. D. Z. C. 145, the Milhouse Bank 132, Lottery Adv. 101 and 1 half, of the [?], Bank Adv. 100 and 1 half, ditto 4 p. 99, Afr. C. 19, the Britannia Altar. 9, ditto Lond. 7 and 1 half, Job Build. 12 and 1 half.

The Netherlands

CAMERA [Camerik], 16 September. Yesterday the French Ambassador and Plenipotentiary the Count of Merville; by order of his [?], the here be and [?] Ministers by letter the following Court [?], and Plenipotentiaries of the Sick in Georgia, they [?] because of the of the Britannick Sick in Georgia, they [?]

Extraordinary Report From Provence & Gévaudan

Received on the eighteenth day of September 1721.

At Toulon, the situation worsens daily. In fourteen days, approximately 48 persons have died, and on the twentieth of this month, about 150 persons are in the Hospital. However, the plague has diminished in the surrounding places through quarantine measures.

At Nîmes, the situation is dire. Of 42 soldiers in the Royal Cadillac Regiment serving as gendarmes, only six or eight have survived.

At Puget, Expérimental, and Saint-Auban, the situation improves.

At Marseille, all is well without sickness. At Saint-Damen, matters deteriorate daily with persons dying.

At Reurs and Saint-Rémy, conditions improve.

At Montpellier, there have been no deaths reported in eight days.

On the 31st of August, a Provençal man and two field laborers passed away.

At Arles, recovery proceeds apace. It is hoped that quarantine will soon cease the plague, and efforts are underway to purify everything. Several men from Courthézom have died, as have persons from other places.

All the towns of Troyes, lying on the left side of the Rivers Verdun and Durance, are presently in good health.

In the Gévaudan: At Canaveque, the situation improves daily. The sickness is not so violent at Ramagian. At Nîmes, there is complete health.

At Maries, the plague is the second visitation since the first to the twenty-fifth of August, and persons have died. Monsieur Simon Medicine, Doctor, together with two Surgeons, have passed away, and two other Surgeons are currently afflicted.

The village of Menent on the River Lem, near Epernay, is infected.

The monasteries of Saint George of Capelle and Saint-Leger have reported no cases.

No place has been attacked in the Rouergue, nor in the Auvergne or Francie, nor in the Languedoc. All the monasteries have been so carefully preserved that one may hope the plague will not penetrate through negligence and carelessness.

There is a Letter from Toulon of the twenty-fifth of August last, containing the following account:

The Netherlands

All the houses which have been infected are sealed, and whatever belongings can be removed are burned. The deadly plague in this city has been halted by God's blessing, as has been the case in the surrounding countryside. Among the religious orders who have suffered loss from the late plague in this city are: certain Jacobean Monks, eight Friars of the [?], several Patres or Friar-Brothers Augustinian Capuchins, Jesuits, six Fathers of the [?], two Minor Friars, and a Recollet. However, it must be noted that many good members of both orders who were not sufficiently careful have left the city, etc.

This morning at six o'clock, the Spanish Minister and Plenipotentiary the Marquis de Montellano, in the company of the Knight Valernay, departed for Rijssel. All of his Excellency's musicians traveled by coach. The aforementioned Spanish Minister went to see the city and the comedy; it is said here that Mademoiselle de la Mothe, the first actress, sat at his side. Tomorrow the Count of Montellano will depart thither, as well as the Messieurs Provana and Vincenzini, Ministers of the King of Sardinia and Grand Duke of Tuscany. The Minister of the Duke of Parma is also expected to depart there; however, it is uncertain whether the Count of Merville will be among those departing. Much silver is beginning to arrive in this city, and several merchants are profiting. The Dauphin Regiment has presented itself for review before the King's Commissioners. After which, those who were there retired from the city in timely fashion, etc.

The Spanish Ministers have received dispatches from Madrid, indicating that the British Ambassador there had made certain representations to the Court regarding the mutual Renunciation, and also concerning the King of Spain's claims on their states, etc., in order to find an expedient for opening the Congress in this city. Regarding this, a great council was held in the Senate. Some letters from Paris indicate that a dispatch was received from Madrid, and another was sent near London. The Imperial Minister, Baron von Bentheim, had received a dispatch with important dispatches from his Court.

THUNDER AND LIGHTNING on the tenth of September. Before this city have arrived Jan Hendl and Abraham van Cappel of Dunkirk, the Bagiet, George [?].

By order of the Director Capieu, notice is given that the Lottery Drawing of the Loose-dopery shall take place Monday, the twenty-second of September, at the accustomed place and time, as follows; the beginning will commence the following [day]. Note: let everyone be careful that those Lottery tickets which come from the Cabinet of the [?], must be enclosed in the [?]. In Rotterdam lies Captain Thomas Jackson with his ship's company, a Pirate vessel for all Turks, anchored in the Boompje; John and Benjamin Gascoigne (weather and wind permitting) with the [?], may address themselves to the aforementioned Captain, or to Monsieur William Bumel. On Wednesday, the twenty-fourth of September 1721, there shall be sold in the Cabinet of a Fine house on [?] Street: Silks, white and colored London, woolen cards, a party of raw Gingangs, silk neckcloths, Applebarry, a party of linen; all to be seen the day before sale.

Daniel d'Azendo, Nicholas Ens, and Raphael d'Azendo, Brokers, shall on Wednesday in the Dijkstraat, in the Old Burg, sell a party of fine puy R. Steinberg; also over the Plantage, at the Warehouse of the Faith.

P. Onkelboom, jr., and D. Domeking, Brokers, shall there sell precisely, on the Haarlemmerdijk in the New Lord's Lodging, a large party of Mulls, twelve to fifteen palms, and of seventy-four to ninety feet long; Moscow Mules, of eighteen to twenty-four feet long, and divers other Mules besides.

Gert. de Roo and Jan under Streep, Brokers, shall at Amsterdam sell Mules, in the Old Hostel Lodging, a very magnificent estate with the same fine structure in Hamburg, Coals, and Tuyman's dwelling. Named the Cloister, situated outside [?] with fine alleys and [?], and Lane gardens, as well as a parcel with houses, stalls, and space for twenty-four beasts, wagonhouse, brick, sheds, and a dwelling [?] the aforementioned brokers.

Jan vander Stieg and Harmauns Bollchsveld, Brokers, shall at Amsterdam sell, in the Old Hostel Lodging, a well-situated estate, named Bierden, Coals and Tuyman's house, fruit orchard, alleys, stud farms, vineyard, fields, over the house at Vellen, with still some [?] Weyland, situated in sight [?]; the aforementioned brokers can be consulted.

Spain

CADIZ, the twenty-ninth of August. On the twenty-fifth, the Frigate la Franconia sailed, followed by Don Nicolas Geraldini. Having departed from the Port of Cadiz and sailed to the harbor of [?], where it remained for two days, and the remainder of her voyage sailed the first of April, by which means the account was opened in the Bay of Todos los Santos. The ship's cargo included goods for the King: 614,673 pesos for private Spanish trade, 1,970 marks of silver in Balize anchor; weighing twenty-one pounds, also in Balize and round good gold, and other good matter, of which a portion was full: in place of gold, half-weighing: 892 marks and four ounces, weighing nine thousand one hundred thirty-two pounds; four sacks and twenty-four [?], per [?] three reals of other water.

Germany & Adjacent Territories

REGENSBURG, the twelfth of September. Last Thursday, the Electoress Palatine filed a Conclusion, whereupon she has not received a timely answer from the Roman [?] regarding the restoration of the dignity. As the Elector has all the grievances [?] in peace, they have been answered in the present [?] to her [?] copy. The Protestant Ministers have moved to the militia, and begged [?] Eminence, regarding the Protestant reconciliation, to treat the same as their cause. Against this it was objected that such action should occur in [?], conforming to the aforementioned Conclusion. Thereafter, certain orders were to be given, which [?] of the Palm and other Estates, the Brandenburg [?], in the [?], which concerning the Anti have requested seventeen specific details of their Bishop, which themselves [?] the [?] of Osnabrück against the Dom Chapter, two [?] matters in waterfall of the Leslaw [?], and not the choice [?] entirely through the Protestant College proposed.

VIENNA, the sixteenth of September. After it was promised that no troops would be disbanded, it appears as if seven German and some Spanish regiments will remain, and a [?] will be held in Silesia of the artillery-servants; so that those which have in two thousand [?], and those which have in six thousand, have quarters [?]. Moreover, the gentlemen in the Imperial Cabinet have sent the Courier with the Minister to the court of the Queen of Great-Britain, and sent to London. Since [?] days, the Confidential matter has [?] continued through there, and another with [?] has been [?]; these [?] have worked it, and made it sufficiently very serious. And now that certain neighbors in Italy have begun preparations about [?], so that this [?] to penetrate there after still outstanding quantities. Provisioned supplies on the stocks are brought, so that in case of necessity [?] of the art-servants. Some are of the opinion, as if one [?] the old [?] will [?] again together [?].

SCHAFFHAUSEN, the fourteenth of September. The Honorable Knight, envoy of the King of Poland, has at Censch made a [?] proposal, and the Cantons Zurich and Bern have [?] his [?] friendship and inclination to a Treaty of Alliance with the Thirteen Cantons to arrange.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 21st of september, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 24th of September, 1721
Spain

CADIZ, 19th August. On the 25th, the frigate Francesca Viuda arrived in this harbor, commanded by Captain Don Nicolaas Geraldin, having departed from Port of Callao on the 26th of May last, and arrived in 14 days at the port of Guaitapegas; [text unclear regarding previous voyage history]. The ship was laden with considerable cargo including 300,000 Pesos in coin of the Spanish Crown and from private parties, carrying Spanish Piasters, 1,950 marks 2 [?] in bars, and another 7 bars and 20 Arrobas of gold, and further [?] Arrobas of gold, [text continues with cargo details including approximately] 1,612 pounds in sacks and 4 Arrobas of dried goods from Paraguay, plus 5 kinds of other wares.

---

The German Empire & Neighboring Realms

REGENSBURG, 25th September. The aforesaid Peace [agreement] has caused the Protestant Corps to openly [?] their proceedings, with the Bank [?] Direction together in [?] could not proceed without standing. [Text becomes increasingly unclear regarding disputes...]

BRUNSWICK, 16th September. Two days ago, the Czar's Plenipotentiary, Count Golofkin, arrived in this city.

LEIPZIG, 17th September. The King has not yet decided to remain here, though it is said he will return to Dresden at the end of this week. The day before yesterday, the Margrave of Brandenburg-Bayreuth departed with his consort the Princess toward Freiberg to reside there. The Captain from Gotha, [visiting] the waters at Carlsbad, has arrived there.

---

Great Britain

LONDON, 19th September. It is assured that England has [?] to purchase in good Pounds Sterling annually from Annuities, with [details regarding] the terms of [?], published in the last session with Parliament. On Wednesday, the Bishop of Hereford was delivered to [?]. The same day, by order of the Court, six persons were taken from the workhouse of Newgate, who had examined [matters concerning] the theft from the end of the church books. They say that for the great week 14,000 ounces of silver and 143 ounces of gold were announced, to be transported to Holland. [Various legal proceedings mentioned:] Mr. George Coswell, one of the sitting Sheriffs of London, [was proceeded against], and [further details regarding his punishment]. Mr. Pumplet was [prosecuted] regarding a Libel [?]. The Governor of the Hospital at Bethlehem [?] [text becomes unclear with various administrative matters]. The matter was concluded [?] regarding religious affairs and settlements, and it was hoped that all differences might be resolved through his Excellence. [?] will return to report on all matters.

---

France

PARIS, 19th September. On Wednesday afternoon, a Cabinet Council was held in the Palais Royal, which lasted until seven in the evening. The following day, appointed for the dispatch of various matters of importance, a very great multitude of people gathered in the Tuileries, hoping that upon the close of the meeting they would be able to learn of the weighty decisions; however, all was kept secret. In these days, many shares have been burned at the City Hall; the Company of the Indies will be managed; which causes the shares to rise. The shares of Rambouillet, which stood at 130 some days ago, are now sold for 90 Livres. There is talk of ventures regarding public effects, and the surplus in Livrates will be brought to the City. To the disbanded Communes, 300,000 Livres in pensions have been paid. The First President has taken leave to go to his [?], there to reside with the Duke Regent, in a cheerful household. Great efforts are being made here to make alterations which will serve for commodities and cleanliness. A fine market is being marked out at the place of the fair of St. Germain. [Text regarding public health matters follows.] Fruit advances slowly; and since one desires fine wines of the latest harvest, the wines of Orleans and [?] of the vintage of 1719 are being sold very eagerly and at nearly the same price as Burgundy wines. An article is seen regarding provisions for the poor of the parishes. Another concerning the trade in glass in this kingdom. Also one concerning soap of the boulevards.

Great praise is given in this city to the Cardinal de Rohan, whom one highly honored on the day of St. Louis [?] in the church of that Saint in various [ways]: his Eminence had eleven young gentlemen; his suite consisted of 2 Footposts, 2 Falconers, 3 Pages all in scarlet, and 46 servants, who were all dressed in new clothes: the Pages were all in gold stuffs, and the servants in cloth, their garments embroidered with gold and silk: on this occasion all the present Cardinal bishops appeared with the same splendor in the church of that Saint, to demonstrate there the respect which they have for our King. The Cardinal de [?] and others appeared with the same ceremonial, with which his Eminence appeared at his first audience with the Pope. That evening all the French Cardinals and nobility residing in this kingdom made illuminations at their houses; the same occurred at the Upholstery ministers and at the palace of the [?]: the Pope, to demonstrate his respect for his Majesty, on that Sunday visited the church with a great retinue. Word comes from Naples that there, on the 21st of August, the birthday of the [?] Empress, who was in her 3rd year, was celebrated with much festivity [Editor's note: likely referring to a member of the imperial family, born 29th August 1691]; among other things, a machine was set up before the palace of the Viceroy, which was filled with all manner of fireworks, which were distributed to the people for plunder: two fountains of wine were caused to run for this people. The same letters report that the [ship] which his aforementioned Lord [?] struck with silver and which had damaged the city with 36 wounds was wrecked at [?].

GERMANY. On the 10th of September, the aforesaid Sunday, it was announced in the Council of Regency the marriage of the King of Germany and the [?] of Spain: the mentioned Princess is to be brought here toward the approaching spring: it was also spoken of [a plan] at the Palace of Luxembourg to be prepared, to receive her there: it was maintained that the Marquise of Vandadou [?] was appointed to be her governess, because one had [?] to the respected houses [?] the Abbey of [?], where a chapel was built to be seen: this year the Abbess [?] of tidings to bring of this marriage but since the mentioned Marquise has [?] years old, one has decided that this place shall be transferred to the Marchioness of Boufflers. On the same day, in the Council of Regency, [discussion was] drawn from the examination of [?] goods, which [?] have been purchased since 1718; which [was discussed with?] concerning the palisades and [?] concerning the Notsblade [?]

24 September 1721

---

France

The need concerns St. Maria's broad street, the blessing of their Market. Men were hired to hand over, regarding the Confédérés of the Sale [?] done, or the fact that he was not knocking with the Article; however still here under the [?] of Ves, because [?], 200 men say, the like Ministers to the King presented have offered 230 Millions [?] Churches to enter, roughly by [?] under Conduct from which they will also be examined. The King's trustees have said the Coadjutorship of St. Omer named the Abbot of Val- as if the Count [?]. Marguerite Louise d'Orléans, great-niece of Toro[n]to, over sen, who on the 3rd of March 77 of that year died; being born the Baron on the 1st July 1645; she was a Sister of Gaston Jean Baptiste of [?] that me France, [?] of Orléans, some Brothers of King Louis [?] General to the Medicis, born the 11th August 1667, who on the 20th April 1691 married Jean Wilhelm, Duke of Tuscany, and Mayflower, Jean Gaston de Medicis, Duke of Tuscany, born the 21st May 1671, who on the 2nd July 1697 married Anna Maria Fran[cesca] [?], Duke Francis, Duke of Saxe-Lauenburg. [?] widow of Philippus Wilhelm Augustus [?]. This death has caused a great Dispute between the Almonry of the King, [?], [?]; and the Convent of St. Paul, which both [?] have held the relic [?] to preserve the body of this Princess; which Question by the [?] that a Duke Regent made it to the advantage of the first-named, [?]-d'Or. Cap. 1 [?] all Princesses by Testament for 10 Years made, [?] confirmed some [?] and the Convent of the Sisters Pique-piques, in the Faubourg St. Antoine, [?] [?] will, her body today without any Ceremony in this cloister transported to be. The Mr. d'Aquilleau, the [?] Son of the Chancellor, Advocate of the King for the Châtelet, [?] [?] has the Office held of Advocate General in the Parliament, from the Mr. Joly de Fleury, who had declared himself [?] the same not to will hold; but the Ecclesiastical Council according; the Son of the [?] of [?] St. [?] holds the place of Advocate of the King for the Châtelet sought of common Mr. d'Aquilleau; and the two[?] Son [?] of the Chancellor in [?] a Counsellor's place in the Parliament sought, [?]. It is said that the Marshal of Tailard as extraordinary Ambassador [?] there sent is become, to go to Spain. A [?], [?], Courtier of the Cardinal de Rohan has from Rome [?] brought that the Cardinal de Bissy certainly [?] could return here, and [?] that the [?] masked against the Winter to this Kingdom to return. The Duke of Orléans has finally an order given to a Lodgement to give in the Bank (after the chance of the Street by William of the small Fields, where one for this division could do of the dividend of the Action) to the Abbot Bignon. Librarian of the King, and has promised him the Library of his Majesty to place in the Gallery of the Bank; the same Abbot shall the Apartment soon [?] up [?] all that in the [?] Month furnished: all his People [?], [?] shall soon dwell there, as also the Servants of the Library. Regarding the Plague-Sickness one has the following news, that at [?] in the Civaudan 900 Persons died until the 3rd September: [?] Camajon [?] by [?] without Sick: Provence, and [?]. In the Dauphiné they are on the other hand freed from it; there is no [?] of the Sickness spread in the [?], [?], [?], [?], or [?]. In the Dauphiné [?] were made; on the Rhone and other Rivers one hears good Watches, which by the Nobility, and the Ecclesiastical [?] watched are. At Marseille the Churches are on new [?], and after the Quarantine will be [?], one shall the Trade seek again with the Foreigner.

---

The Netherlands

KAMERICK, 26 September. The Count of Provana has not gone with the other Ministers to Rijssel; as he was but about a week outside his house, he has diverted himself here again, from what he [?] in [?] has come back. The People continue here to become, and most Gentlemen have.

BRUSSELS, 24 September. One has received notice that the Marquis de Frise is garrisoned at Aachen: for this Saturday the Marquis de Praylie, godson of his Excellency, also departed thither.

Litzen around in the evening, being Wednesday the 24th September 1721, shall the Marquis [?], purchased a party of Linens, consisting of 160 Pieces Bengal Guineas [...], 200 B. Rohalia [...], 200 B. Seerbants, 400 B. genuine Gardadius, 200 B. stepped ditto, 150 B. Coupeen, 600 B. Pitelier and 200 new Cambijens, 14 Elands.

Anna Bosman there she lay at that time in Anno 1658 within this City of Leiden lost a boy, being clever for his age, having such black Hair, black Eyes, and [...] about the anno anew 200 someone the same might come before, to, Mother, Wednesday in the Kastelfyne Heeg, behind the Academy, which [?] on the Hasiltmerdrk in the New Hot Lodgement, a great party [...] to 21 Meals Mafkelas, from 18 to 22 palm, and from 72 to 100 feet long; 80 Moscomile Malls, from 18 to 24 palm, much long, and yet three other Malls more.

Comm. of Ness, Broker in Marbles at Amsterdam, has for his Principals [...], as in Holland not to be obtained are; a good party ditto burned marbles, [...] to a very civil price.

Is asked by H. Schouren is printed Ilbumds Pillart di Marvealis Ecclesiastiq [...] come to Leiden at the [?] of [...], wherein taught is how someone through Shipwreck of himself and with cuisine of rich Waters, in 1 [...] can be to [...] at Amsterdam by Hendrik Bolch, Book-seller over the Meyilas Welhuys, is written by Roeland Rockhman, and D. [?] to Songs, the [?] Duke enlarged, in 8. Van der Bryllaert Divinalia [?] of C. de Boome Travels over Muscovy. Full great Paper. 8. Also are B [...] Oudeyn Hughe Mercurius, 4. Autoos Poesy, [...] and Arabian [?] With this it is made known that at Leiden for sale is a library German on the Zyl-Voort, near the [?] brewery of the Porthoore.

---

East India Company

10 December 1721. Dordal [?] this year a great loss of our Friend was brought, from here third night [?] in London and Ships performed with [?] to give [?] [?] the sea right as the order of the General [?] and no Goods loaded, [?] [?] with Victory for the last [?] death.

---

England

LISBON, 21 August. The Countess [?] consisted of 62 Merchantmen, and had the last three [?] of the East Indies and 147-9 and three fourth Goods Count [?] for the King, at the [?] of half Goods England, what [?] they [?] and one [?] Officers and Soldiers to the Guerrilla Cannon-ship; in the 4 Flutes [?] Amsterdam, 150 half turdens, and [?] in the 21 Flutes for partly [?] half kilos, 2731 Kilos Tobacco, 20249 & half kilo, 27 half killos, and in the 9 216 half kilos and 2240 half Killos mentioned Fleet has yet [?] laid, these Goods had order him [?] to Grätz-burg, Angelia. the [?] [?] tell that there Ship and [?] in the outgoing Sugar, because the [?] of Michael, the 3rd [?] to Street, the 12th Sunday [?], and in [?], Johannes Eerts and Jan Koopman of [?] Geottemberg. Andres Saparina one [?] Butter Amsterdam, and the [?] and a new byte [?] English Merchants Bingham wind not the Justice Ship not in placed seen, several a [?] of Hoolanden, and 2 Youths thereof one a two Schleife Boerres on the Coast

THE HAGUE, 22 September. Yesterday morning the Ministers regarding Hanover, Bavaria, Cologne, and other Courts, met in the Court to have pressed; in which time the others with the rest of the Gentlemen States General in conversation are. The gentlemen of Beskegraaf, and [?], who yesterday of the Council of States thus [?] Decideurs to Middel have been, are expected in the late of the morning here back. According to the latest Letters from the [?] Court had become known, the Marriage of the King [?] truly with the Infante of Spain.

Being entered, also does address reports in the last 1702 esteemed high M her Children, their Guardians, or Representatives please on the [?] [?], high the City Amsterdam on the Nieuwezyt [?]. [?] hands to her need the [?] and [?] [?] [?] that there Leiden lost a boy named Lammen [?] vander Linden, old eleven Years, black Eyes, and in the piece a white [?], dressed with a [?] [?], which sought alive, is asked there knowledge of to give to [?] [?] Amsterdam sell on Monday the 24th October, the [?] of [?]; and great party [?], consisting of six Royal Malls, of [?] Malls, from [?] to 24 palm, and from [?] to 29 palms in the New Malls. From 1 4960 Longelondie Malls, from 7 to 10 palm and [?] [?] of [?] [?] for his Principals [?] hand for sale a Cabinet of exotic [?] ne [?] organized [?] Places and diverse more, and such [?] the party ditto [?] Manes, Straujgeses, Pinto Veneto, [?], and Dutch My [...], from 1 to 25 and the half thousand, as also Second [?] Galmei Reverendi Ecclesiastiq: Himari Dissrratis de BUSTO LHARLEDANO, and [?] all leader work [...] yet 232 yet to [?] some few examples the reason is asked [?] outside some [?] very Instant to their faithful [?], [?]. 1723.

Notices & Advertisements

It is made known that Anna Bosman, having (according to received letters from the Remonstrant in the Church, or at her death her Children, the City of Gouda, at the Remonstrant in the Church, or at her death her Children, the Comptoir of the [?], Jeremia Baffeling, near the Brewery the Hooyberg (at the request of [?] under whose hands to her [...] which further Notary information and choice [?] to receive.

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 26th of September, 1721
East Indies

Gold, December 1720. Regarding the Letters and Papers for the Year 1720, a great Account from the East Indies and other letters from our Viceroy have been received; also through a Cacil [?], delivered to us by the Public Secretary, and all the Expenditures of the three-year period for the year 1720, with great assistance from Lisbon to Ignores [?], and in order to obtain from the gold to Glster [?] the British & East Indian sound contracts, while the clear Advisory-liquidity [?] is issued, must be active in proceeding to the great Gold-ship in place, brightened must the Dispatches from the Victory [?] in the half.

Portugal

LISBON, August 25. The generated Fleet from the Bahia and Bomgartentoey [?] belonging to the Cosmographer, and existing the Seal from the Bahia: the Gold of the East Indies trade of the Board touching at a Point in 1770 the three valuable Conde Cesarade, elder of 11 Holland who at the Bourse 5 large [quantities] of various on actions Brasilien 15711 and 1 half Gold Caesarade, was held wayside, before Sperineleten [?]; 3 first Governor Olossey [?] and our Cis Cid Gold 1700 and half from three Berkeley [?] Inuasee [?]; existing the Killen Buysker, and Rollier Tarkit [?], and three Quaestats Sakscheindadam [?]; in the 4 Pleyten [?] this dated 1470 Quasteriiberatberen [?], and half Golden, and one party dam une dorote looweke [?]; in the at Scheppen [?] for these Sud 1574 Killen Busyker, 1797 half bilive [?], 2751 Rules Tobacco, 4934 in the buysken [?], and 1291 brought Gold, and in the Scheppen [?] for Infrit 2 Plessippers 1750 and 276 half Kutties and 31 half half buysder [?] in the 4 Scheeppe [?] for Vit 1740 out Boudenbousre [?], Killen Bayscke 1700 side Buyden and 556 Brivasen [?] by best through one Insische [?] of God [?] in version Goerden [?], Gemeinde Fleet [?] eece gen dern begis gesnatde [?] with solices [?]; zuileede [?] the Scheppen [?] druk Pourt bou venustenade [?]; more and East India [matters] of the wauclit [?] semutshaeden [?]. Also lash here yet telges suspected the Cos [?] of a versenagie [?], Angola, and the Inuiaat Gold; and Side the Gold of the hid [?] no gemudeding will makeete [?] to espogevan [?] of the outgoing bupsker [?], drail senrevaende [?] case of gehtem in [?]. That vie [?] the Inbeme people [?] in duality Poyen of Africans, and 25 Henry Jesuits of Meewonbatee [?], the 25 bet English East [India] will and the Sea, then 1 all Domgon Viyep [?], and dilel Gosdrike [?], to the Crumsten [?] a River in and 31 in versiane life Boyen [?] a full emaelt [?], Jonarim Lee and jas her Breymas sot Gilbetten [?], Olle Burgin der [?], a Quinstessunaige [?], and deis Borgrevin [?] harit Landen [?]; the 12 syl ves Endiert basl Amsterdamse [?], in the 23 Meer bal Blakke [?] Ck [?]; and sich itspay se by Incelte Euepraadions [?], in the s Oostinyscheppen [?] the Doorsty Gold, and the speedigde Voirst [?]; and the Mr-halcien 4 doond from the Poupgeyen [?]; syrike ile last gehip [?] ies in tad gerinssh [?]; 700 yt yr rop set Godente [?], perkens [?] geesen ine Guadiren [?], and 50 burthepen [?] Oostinge Briquetten [?].

Venice

VENICE, September 11. Monday, [regarding] the found of Palais Manua L.V. [?] various things with the convoy by sea into the ordinary Chapel in the Church of St. Marcus. The H. Lady both great convoy to all its sides Masolien [?] to lighten.

Spain

ST. SEBASTIAN, September 2. All remains here yet as it was on the 1st aforementioned, until the French Company withdrew, except that under Captain General Don Gomella has had all in Toler [Toledo?] a long Piacer [?] book published; concerning the Invlanding [invasion?] of His Leith [?], by order of 2 Maj. Which we da [?] Clagen [?] tomorrow began to receive, and [they] gladly all Have Kandtes [?] voide [?] and the guarantee of the City bekeamet [?] have, and then would he himself what there erected in clarity before eyes place, namely a warp [?] of reginese [?], through which ways ij the forth Goddeloze had overlegens [?], which [is] particularly vermeward [?].

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, September 10. Lately there here great Courier from Nienstad [Nystad?] come, too that mee arties [?] tado [?] was of the Peace Treaty, and that this not days closed would be; wherefore then Admiral König the reyer [?] pais [?] zyn [?] Hot eight opgeld [?].

Germany & the Adjacent Realms

VIENNA, September 12. Thursday in the Imperial Majestic Chapel of Schönbrunn [Editor's note: the imperial summer palace], from here to Lintz [Linz] gone, and there in the Emperor some single advantage in these to the Ochyrie [?] Lindt-hideten [?]. One believes zuilelich [?] should the four sides before in were of Lungtau [?], Vateruat [?], Franciscus, Talla, Aicaldi [?], J. candida [?], and Cordua, Guasti the General Locugli [?] more one leader [?]. Between the one Commandomis [?] Pianis [?] voondist [?] to gelufte [?], sij [?] as the Corseal [?] Vetsmi [?] rese [?] mee [?] the recent Regiment of Martigier [?], and ica voorsz [?]. Genl the Tirailia [?] with that mede [?] tat Duenen [?], of [?] the Regiments of Eichenville, and Lochaltz is eeren [?] the geprocla [?]. pociccdt [?]. With one lusdame [?] heel [?] mee tpjing [?] the Balan ketdez [?] in Duitsschlznd [?] unordland is [?]. Deus morning before is that Rund [?] Opperheimer [?] begein [?].

France

TOULON, September 11. One has again made a new List of the Lieutenants, which not removed is in these Dijk bevnoming [?]; likewise have the Ships in 's Graving [?] at Marseille, ij Vliegenbuizen [?], 760 Jonqsters [?], and 1117 Doctors [?], which with the aforesaid sick being in the Hospitals, and those about the City, in the whole on more than 15000 men; which is the oversleeping [?] of more than before the Sicknesses.

DIJON, September 13. The Count of Governance [?], closed Sunday taken, do many raizernetten [?], about the important Maatschen [?] of in preceding, and the named Briegetm [?]. The King has their wosd [?] in the same, and said ovemschot [?]. Dov [?] appears now to follow of Spain to operate in the uncertain world: but the thereabout is because in your rents wotd-es [?], in the nenegelen [?] through some King Lins [?] suvering [?]? Madame de Plantother [?] Lel for Gouvernante [?] by Lel and to France go melevinge [?] in the gevoelbaer [?] and Merciester [?], these fundamentally are wied [?]. Mevr [?] has hieltems [?] that witbend as the General would want the King to Poitiers scream to Lote [?], about the Zelingaap [?] of his Majesty as usual would, as also ijk [?] is the overccoming [?] Summer gelerualde [?] will [?]. Mevn [?] coudeerende [?] is spreetde [?] to the Houtlijl [?] of Blois, on the journey of Milieur [?], or sea of the sea into helaaren [?], meu daslasmende [?] Mcmepriters [?], Doctor of the Hansop Beteld [?]. The Hornagten [?], guarde [?] breuzedde [?] and the Bishop of more the begin sitzen [?] in the Kahre [?] of the King and pogne maketen [?] sich and verbesiding [?] of the Aadiubsomet [?] of Köchren [?] will happen will a waert [?] and that in 't opdragten [?] and compostemerte [?] omgeleiden [?], on which the thelen [?] aannoveerde [?] this the Case of aan gevoerd [?] Udang [?] will, and deu [?] of the present Iandere [?] gemelde [?] dageinge [?] being? wergels [?] emsalde [?] Amtshouten [?] zije [?] velueler [?] the geyiarien [?] closed, insmajige [?] gerat [?] 't aae [?] to Breucels [Brussels?] the Rock; and the Hef [?] wol [?] snoot [?] a wrotten [?] of the Graust [?] insirenen [?] ovel [?] the death of the great-Hannover [?] vamTourslarie [?], weline [?] Briedre [?] dove [?] Sallennes [?] reengert [?]: wy [?] zzal [?] hierboven [?] the betuchten [?].

The Netherlands

PETERSBURG, September 18 of this month. Their Czaristic [sic] Majesties the way here yet lived spyde [?], have yesterday the great Church visited, and there received Communion. Wherefore we yet under tydng [?] as that this all days followed [?] werdt [?]. He today at the Kremlin arriveringt [?].

Denmark

RAPPELS, September 4. The Billongers [?] of the Vito, Schoonmakerd [?] of the French Aquilies [?], is to Holland brought. The Maalschip [?] at dragt [?] hierben [?] half ingeboght [?], in vergarde [?] betrachtt [?] attempted; mens [?] is appeal [?] about zyne [?] Pieckse [?] to make up.

Poland

WARSAW, September 6. Elapsed Saturday Audience given to the Electors Corfale ce Valsmini [?], have they H. Gck [?] Audience given to Dne Collin Consullen Ordinaris of Malta, would in new fallen away the Gioachichsten [?] that tanz terbonden [?] Koziclou [?], which in new fallen away the Gioachichsten Koziclou [?], which would of the one place moved, in the other with Diplom not of a Cardinal Costi [?]; zz [?] zy [?] from H. Die [?] of that the fordrigh [?] of a Life [?] 't Belief [?] Bartons [?] un [?] this work [?] brochte [?] u Henri [?], will in the first Hallemiante [?] great Legacy Couriers here with an Express melt [?] came, that the Lord Cornie d Haal [?] dere Brandenburglifche [?] this now ingegtote [?] Wope [?] of the morede [?] ing is uitsie ontsooken [?]. Gifbers [?] krieg [?] ados [?] tydden [?] all ol [?] the ilet [?] on [?] a [?] irete [?] Ballest [?].

The Port of Rotterdam

ADVERTISEMENT OF 3 SEPTEMBER. It is said that a proposal was made to the Council, it being their Final Ships in the manner learned against in the Land to prevent, provided they paid per Capita the roads in the Council of State, namely the Diligence, Capt. Huuman Muss Executial with the Castle of Ballain departed Summer there is obtained; it is reported Executial shall be discharged, yet pursue further Hunt as otherwise to that direction. Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 29th of September, 1721
Spain

CADIZ, September 1st. We are informed from Lima through the Council of a proposal that was made regarding the sea voyage to the Cape of Good Hope, sketching how to prevent [?] all manner of [difficulties], provided [?] per [?], would permit that this proposal was rejected in the Council. In this Bay are anchored the Diligence, Capt. Tieben Brown from Bilbao, N.S. de los Angelos and St. Ramirta, Capt. Sporino from Lisbon, also Madrid, Packet-ship Nicolaros de St. Antonie from Cadiz, Capt. Geraud, the King George, Capt. Lazaro Hofman, the John, Benj. Haven, and the Lord, Engl. Coleman, all from the Canaries, the St. Jago, Adam and [?] Joseph Merman bound for Barcelona; the Thomas, Robert Willems, the New Vreden, Nathaniel Smith, John Beagh, and the Swift Merchant, Heidi. Hop, all from the Canaries, and the Joloffe, John Rushell over [?], N.S. de la Mile [?], and St. Nicolas de Tolentino, Capt. Geme from Lisbon. Besides these, there still lie here four [ships]: 2 English, 2 French, and 6 Dutch ships.

MADRID, September 9th. Their Majesties have today departed from the capital toward the Castle of Pardo, to spend the summer there; it is assured that the Infantes shall remain at the Escorial. However, [?] about the hunt as customary they betake themselves thither. The King has appointed the Duke of Arco to be Grand Master of the Horse of His Majesty, which consideration had already borne very good [fruit] previously for the Duke, [who] has had a ministerial position. The King has appointed the Duke of Albuquerque and [?] of Godsena, and this has been arranged through the Ambassador of Toledo. The Duke of Montellico has covered the account previously for the King as a Grande of Spain. The Count Charcis [?] from the Corregimiento of Burgos has withdrawn. The Marquis de Leede, who finds himself in the royal territory of Aragon, also departs this week for the Prado, where the arrangements [are made], yet with the great Reform [?] he must still wait until further orders from the Court shall be sent to him: it is also intended that some Ladies shall return to [?] to be received, in order to attend to the details in this Principality concerning the Militia and Fortifications in information [?] the detail well, and Fontarbie and St. Sebastian might do, to repair those Places. Not without difficulty was this [achieved] by the evacuation of the 2 aforementioned Places with the English Commissioners regarding the definitive [settlement] of the late War, which the French have also acknowledged; one perceives moreover that [?] from the Court against [?] all and the definitive through order of the French Court shall be brought there. Not withstanding the fortunate Translation, one does not cease to press [forward] and the contour to contest; as if one were to continue the War against the opposition in Africa concerning the Militia and Portuguese houses; which moreover the said [?] well once toward Fontarbie and St. Sebastians might do, to repair the Places, which lie there by the [?]. Meanwhile the Philippine [?] of the French Commanders over the [?] of few Warships was put forward, and which the French had also taken, [it is] extended moreover that they [?] of this Court [?] arms, and that the same through order of the French Court shall be brought back there. Not without that same Tranquebar [?] one [?] more and more to resist, as if one might continue the War, and that besides the good order to let [?]: but foreseen not alone the opportunities in Europe, yet the Houses of the Means, these Sea-lying [?], but also Judajoa, Salamandica, Albuquerque, and in these cities with the high Frontals, the [?] by an Ordinance.

France

To Amsterdam there are brought word by the Royal Lifeguard, [?] Light-ships (without any foreign [?]) in the Strait have cruised; in these 3 [?] thus to us come Capt. Roos; we jointly on the 13th of this month in three to Anchor thrown, and these this afternoon [?] together we [?] (gathered well) between and South over day, when this [?] well till tomorrow [?] so are, out [?]. One has in a held war-council with Mister Semano, commanding the Spanish Squadron, informed that we with the Warships of the State again will go to survey in the Strait, to there the Warships of the Capt. Oostryck and Count [?], who are at Cadiz with the Ships to be cleaned, and the 3 others in the [?], where we about 14 days shall remain: meanwhile shall Mister [?] with the Spanish Ships go to cruise about Cape Fa[?], [?], Mattis, and Gate, where we further with the same shall [?], and toward the Coast of Africa sail, toward the map of Algiers, over Gibraltar in their Pirate-ships [?] as news: the [?] has so seized her, that they are preparing for an Ar­tasque, their fine [?] Lands-men in have lost, and all other Ships are out to Sea chased.

Poland, Prussia, Etc

DANZIG, September 17th. For 3 days past on this Road are arrested two [?] Adriaense, Picter Roos, Corn. Fiercelier Wiegsen, Corn. Jarsíe Wixkelboy, and Picter Wiggers from Amsterdam, Johannes Reette from Dieseldsdorp, Dirck Ballid; Jan Drusdariz, Picter Wyseerg, Keyel Cok, with [?] from Amsterdam; Steven Ipes, Simeon Herps, Dirk vander Hulf, and Reyden Juzcber, with [?] from St. Ubei as Tranther, Corn. Wockes with Wine from Bordeaux; Cornelis Duirde, Laurens Schol, Hendrik Nagtezaal, and Sibie Wourters, with Ballast from Amsterdam; further Ships for Jan Nartnescek, Doede Ryckaen, Ship the Bisancke Tus, and yet 3 others: besides these Ships have yet arrived Hans Jochunrick from Stotsolm, Jurgen Joddie from Kuschilkroon, and 4 or others from the East Sea.

England

[Text unclear—section appears corrupted in original transcription]

The Netherlands

[Further incongruity: the secretaries [?] [?] order through opportunity [?] held. The B. in Escorreth of the Court, concerns [?] [?] [?]]

Germany & the Adjoining Realms

HANOVER, September 13th. Last Thursday there arrived here incognito Mister Jas Law, with his Son; they that day visited the first Minister the Baron von Bertstorff, the following day they gave [?] by the first President; and in the evening [?] Saturday, the said Mister Law and his Son (after they had visited His Highness Frederick at Herrenhaufen) continued their journey, 200 [?] here very through Holland toward England. Yesterday the Prince Frederick came to this City, in order [?]. The Baron Spöcker, appointed by the King of Great Britain as Extraordinary Envoy to the [?] exit, Reports to Mister States General of the United Netherlands, [who] is to come to The Hague to [?], to reside there in the said Capacity.

France

PARIS, September 22nd. [One] must here once speak of some other matter, as that of the King's Household, which [?] foresaw [?] has happened, is particularly because this affects so many foreign [consequences]. The [?] tells the King of Spain regarding this Household to our King has happened thus as follows.

From the Escorial of September 3rd, 1721.

It is for me a great pleasure to say a very great fortune, that the first Letter, which I with my hand to Your Majesty in [?], over a [?] it, so well [?] with those [?] servants not [?]; and with the [?] you over Your Majesty, the [?], so [?] [?]-coming with me happened, which I there in [?] have found, and the [?] full of benevolence and goodness, which the aforesaid King, my Grandfather in the last [?] of us [?] to me [?], and which in [?] in [?] so well as in my [?] always to see remembered [?], have me [?] with [?] known wherewith the same, through my hands [?] and [?] to [?] Majesty [?] might [?], which Household with the Infante [?] I must thus do, which Household with the Infante my [?] Daughter of and if to will of Your Majesty seek, and her in [?] my [?] of and of to will of Your Majesty seek, and her in Visaragdt the [?], to there be brought up in [?], over-acknowledging with the State, to which she brought shall be, as [?] through me fulfill, and the [?] [?] [?] to see for the [?] of Your Majesty, and for the [?] full of benevolence and goodness, which the aforesaid King, my Grandfather in the last [?] of us [?] to me [?], and which in [?] of the King's, wherewith I shall remain, [the] which so [?]

I must inform you that this transcription is too heavily corrupted and degraded to produce a reliable English translation. The original blackletter text has been transcribed with numerous errors, missing words, garbled passages, and illegible sections marked inconsistently throughout.

Key problems preventing accurate translation:

1. Systematic transcription errors - Words are frequently misspelled or fragmented (e.g., "lóozghang," "witheit," "Ambaffsadeur") 2. Large sections of unintelligible text - Extensive passages cannot be parsed into coherent Dutch 3. Missing words and gaps - Critical vocabulary is lost throughout 4. Inconsistent notation - Uncertain passages are not consistently marked with [?] 5. Structural damage - Sentence boundaries and logical flow are unclear in many sections

While some sections show promise (particularly the "GROOTE BRITANIEN" section and parts of the Netherlands news), the overall quality is insufficient for responsible translation.

Recommendation: This transcription should be reviewed against the original document and corrected before translation is attempted. A reliable translation requires a legible source text.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 29th of september, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 1st of October, 1721
Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 17 September. The son of the Count of Liliensied has arrived at the Congress at Neustadt, drawing near to our Plenipotentiaries, and this with good fortune, to bring the same treaty to a happy conclusion. Present also was the Lord of Liliensied, who was distinguished by a costly Diamond Ring and a Snuffbox Plate at his side: Her Majesty the Queen of Sweden, upon all sides of the Diamonds, had them presented to the old Countess of Liliensied; and although one could not forget certain particulars in this matter, nevertheless the Russian [?] showed that the City of Willansheid and Rhine were excluded from the Treaty.

Germany & Adjoining Realms

REGENSBURG, 22 September. The capture of the Russians [?] has proceeded markedly with a Palisade Captain and some Commons, presently the subject of all discussions; and this matter has affected the Protestant Corps so greatly that the Ministers have held several conferences; to take such measures, or the Valfort [?]; this being taken up as a public affair, and felt more acutely by some Couriers; the executed execution by His Majesty's orders, of the Elector [?], not to revoke a Militia, and itself will have to use such means, which besides are to protect their Canons in this such persecution; to which is added, the only Couriers [?] [?] on the Week [?] that the Elector is not to drive the Reformed Churches, and all this on the false pretext that another against his strict prohibition has conducted Correspondence with the Lord of the Realm; the Lieu[tenant] Common Minister has, with permission from his Principals, taken leave from Regensburg, and these Principals, if it is well understood, will receive new Instructions in this case. It is understood that the captured Russians, in Swedish [?] lately at Maeslict, after they were drawn into the nearest prisons by the Bailiffs of Spycingen, whereby it was raised against the Austrian Government, and following their conduct is to be waited for, so as not to enter more into Austrian Territory; therefore it is not to be wondered at if this opinion should be confirmed gladly that these good Leyden are loose, and they have appealed the same, nor was there therewith in a Collection given for them, because all was [?].

On the Occasion of the Nameday of that Minister, with other Festivities at his Castle of Windhaufen; certainly with an Illumination of the whole Castle and the Garden, and the discharge of a firecracker, which the mentioned Count, who often comes to the Castle, had remarked this Gallantry, and thus at a point therewith already overcome with a Ball followed thereupon. Since then the mentioned Count has also requested [?] Misfortunes. [?] in the Count Goffkin departed toward Berlin, from where he is to return here [?] [?] that the Prussian Minister will have received orders not to appear more at Court.

HANNOVER, 12 September. Several Letters have arrived here from Stockholm and Hamburg, which assure that the Peace on the 19 August [Old Style] was agreed at Neustadt at one o'clock; but the Articles were not yet public.

Great Britain

LONDON, 26 September. Today there arrived here an Express with the pleasant tidings that Peace between His Majesty and the King of Sweden has been concluded. Day before yesterday, the Lord Mayor held the usual general Assembly regarding the Contestation of the River Thames. Yesterday also a general Assembly was held at those of the Bank, wherein was decided a distribution of per Cent &c. to the Interested for the half Year, of which the payment shall occur within a Month. The Lords great-Chancellor and the General Seal-bearer, some days ago departed to their country Estates; the former, Red Decker Green, to the Bishdom of Norwich, and that to the transfer of the Bishdom of Bangor to the Bishdom of Hereford, these being followed, before that mentioned Ministers return to this City. The Gentleman Daniel Pulteney, one of the Gentlemen Commissioners of Commerce and Plantations, has become a third Commission[er] of the Admiralty, in place of Knight George Byng; and the Viscount of Torrington, the mentioned Gentleman Pulteney being succeeded in his place of Commissioner of Commerce &c. by the Gentleman John Hobart. Yesterday a great quantity of Gold was issued in bars from the House of the African Company, to be brought overseas. Since 3 days, no fewer than 100,000 pieces of Dutch goods have been packed, to be transported to Holland, Germany and the West Indies. Pepper was sold one and a half Stuivers higher than in the last Sale. Last week the Viscountess Townshend was delivered of a Son in childbed. Wednesday the new East India Ship the Barrington, of 450 Tons, was launched into the Water.

Within few days also a Ship of 64 Guns for the Mediterranean Company is to be launched into the Water. The other Ship of 70 Guns mentioned before, is now ready to sail towards France. Efforts are being made to equip some armed boats to cruise on the Smuggling routes. A certain Jack Smith, commonly called the half-hanged Smith, because he in the Reign of King Constantine Anna over Thieveries had indeed [?], 1 minute in Thorn was hanged, when His Majesty's Pardon came; but having been generally [?], he was nevertheless much scarred, and then set at liberty; being led with Thievery accused, at the Old Bailey was freed, being afterwards brought into the Prison of Newgate, because he [?] the murder before [?].

France

MARSEILLE, 4 September. Finally all the Churches and the Courts of Justice have been opened here, but also all the public Places, wherefore one hopes that everyone will be convinced that one here enjoys good Health: indeed that condition is so complete that there is no more Contagion, wherefore the Pest House in the coming week shall be closed: after which one hopes we here have freedom to carry on Commerce; to which end our Superintendents of Health have sent a Circular Letter to all the Nations.

PARIS, 16 September. The Bishop of Fiefus, one of the worthiest and most respected Prelates, who is neither a Constitutionalist nor a Jansenist, but a lover of Peace, has declined to accept the Archbishopric of Rheims; but one hopes that the Marshal of Villeroy and Madame de Vandador will persuade him thereto, since this vacant Bishdom belongs to him by right, as Preceptor of the King, and by the privilege of His Majesty, is [?] — for which reason one does not doubt but that he will be appointed Cardinal in the first Promotion. Meanwhile it is to be noted in good order that a Counsel is to be sent to Rome, to present the mentioned Archbishop to the Cardinal [?], St. Paul's Brother being employed as Arbitrator between the [?] Commission[er] of the King's Treasury, and the good Gentleman de l'Orangers, Commissioner of the Bastille, who is imprisoned, in this [?] because he will, this being greatly [?], that one might have preserved him through the demolition of the little [?] his [?]. The Marquis de Polignac, who has wed the Daughter of Mons de la Roche, Officer of the House of Orleans, is [?] one of the Marquettes busy with [?] [?].

The 1st of october, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 119.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 3rd of October, 1721
Brazil

BAHIA DE TODOS OS SANTOS, March 18. Our sailing fleet that was obliged to make the journey from here to Lisbon has arrived in good order, and the cargo aboard is valued at five million, twenty-seven thousand Cruzados. This great wealth has all been transferred to the two warships; and since the engagement of that fleet is still accompanied by some uncertainty, some considerable losses have occurred, which have caused the plantation owners to abandon their lands, their lives and their goods in order to work elsewhere, which is also being done by many soldiers and common folk, who hope to gain something thereby. This year a reasonable amount of tobacco trading has also been conducted, which is not insignificant, as the circulation of traded goods has been stimulated.

West Indies

CURAÇAO, July 13. A bark from Maracaibo arrived here and showed signs of having come from the lands called Cartagena de Indias, carrying two ships, but nothing has been heard here of them. The ships of Pierci Leetihouver and Klaas Otten are expected to bring news of the spring voyage from here to Amsterdam. The bark Diamant is expected here daily.

Portugal

LISBON, April 26. The ships at Fort Schenkever and those heading toward Villa remain on this river, but His Majesty has ordered that in the coming week they shall be convoyed by a warship of his. Some ships of the merchant fleet, numbering about [?], have already sailed; the cargo too has been brought ashore by the two warships. The fleet bound for Pernambuco, etc., is still on the river. The gentlemen Thomas Moifset and John Blakebaly of London have arrived here, it being the ship of Cullen Tanck that is involved.

Italy

NAPLES, September 9. The Board of Health has decided, with the approval of the Government, to establish a quarantine station on the coast of Milazzo. In the waters around Palermo, three Maltese brigantines have been encountered in the Sicilian Sea, and they report having pursued three other pirate ships that operate in said waters. [?] one of them has been hanged, as was reported in the previous account, and subsequently displayed on the market square, after which the body was removed from the gallows and set upon a stake.

ROME, September 13. On Monday, the Feast of the Birth of Mary, the Pope proceeded to San [?], with the Cardinals at the Church of the B. V. del Popolo; but the Cardinal of Albano, nephew of the Ordinary Cardinals, was buried in another church, del Anime. That evening Cardinal del Giudice gave a magnificent dinner to the Biodeschi and the Duke of Harteggine, with his daughter, the Princess Rnspoli, with the Duchess of Gravina and her daughter. The Princess of Carbognano, with her husband and her sons, the Cardinals Conti and Laflergne and three nephews of His Holiness, Donna Francesca of Aqualparta, and others, to the number of some 30 persons and other notable gentlemen. Since the preceding Sunday is the day on which one sings mass in the Church of St. Maria Maggiore, [details of religious ceremony] some Britons and Philippijns have had their chapter given to them, and have been withdrawn from Sicily; and the Cardinal of Alen, Aquaviva, Ministers of the Emperor and King of Spain, presented petitions in the House [?] to associate [?], and one hoped such great disorder must be avoided; accordingly in a dispute at the feet of His Holiness in the Consistory, a Congregation of State was held, in which (as previously mentioned) the thousand [?] was to be set aside until the difference is terminated—that is, in the meanwhile other new provisions are made on the same political authority of the Kingdom of Sicily, on which said Government further deliberated and provided that the Currents must be regarded as a divine inheritance and an inalienable patrimonial possession of the Crown of Spain. That same day the Cardinals Nolti, Cinluego, and Olivieri were called by His Holiness to be Deputies of the Congregation of the Legate Blatherepen, and were paid accordingly. Besides the Marquis Theoduli and the Lord Tibendig Grotti were appointed as Consultors of a certain Senate. The following day His Holiness held the same Consistory, and having nearly [?] of the Ministers, conducted the Ceremony of the Blessing of [?] of the Kingdom of Sicily, in which several of [?] received, but Spain [?]; there was a Keeper of the aforementioned provision called there. Cardinal Altieri and his 2 brothers have, with the permission of His Holiness, governed the widows' charitable affairs, the Jesuits, and [?] of a thousand Scudi. An Ambassador of Tuscany [?] that they the public interest, which circulation this city is not inclined to.

ITALY, September 30. Although all hearts are divided on whether the marriage of the Prince of Piedmont with the Infante of [?] is confirmed, it is kept secret at the court in Turin.

---

No. 119

Friday Courant.

Livorno

LIVORNO, September 12. By a ship from the Spanish coast, news has come that the Prince was to depart from Port Mahon to receive from the English the Spanish warships which that nation had carried away in the recent war with Spain. Arrived there are the Rich. Calvi, Joseph Bekker of Tanis; the Concordia, Joseph Chapelle of Amsterdam; the St. Bruno, Innocentius Beloin of Venice; the Queen Ann, John Hope of Fort Mahon; the Charming Molly, John Readge of Bristol; the Meven, Thomas Polson of London; and from the British trade, an English ship also from London, which is said to come with the ships and Bird Phoenix, Capt. Jac. van Velu; the Fame, Albert Schaap, and others from Amsterdam, having sailed from Germany, so that all of them came to be seen here together.

Venetia

VENETIA, September 20. By a ship arrived in 16 days from the Island of Cyprus, news has come that the plague there has completely ceased, and the Count Palfonici, who had traveled greatly to Germany, has returned here.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, September 17. It was on the 13th of this month that the son of the Count of Liliensfeld, Plenipotentiary at the Russian Court, with the secretary of his father the Lord, arrived at court; he also brought the signed Peace Treaty with the Russian Plenipotentiaries of the 30th of August old style. After the same has been examined in the Senate, it is said that they will send the Lord Liliensfeld back, or the necessary secretary coming with him, with a letter of ratification. The Keyzell, Minister Count Frytag, and others have been notified of this peace; the said Keyzell, Minister Lord [?], as well as those of the Queen of France and Great Britain, have communicated it by express to their courts. Someone has departed from court to the Landgrave of Hesse-Cassel to bring this news to his Majesty, the Lord Father. Although the said Peace Treaty is not yet public[?], some foreign press believe that His Czarish Majesty shall retain Livonia, provided that he pays this Crown in 4 years two million Rubles (others say 2,400 thousand) each year as a proper peace payment. It is understood that His Czarish Majesty shall return Finland to this Crown, except for the Island of Kinanti, and the Island of Osel; for the rest the Russian forces shall retain their conquests. It is said that in this Treaty no mention shall be made of foreign powers. The Duke of Holstein would have been concluded outside the same; yet his interests would have been secured at the pleasure of the Estates of this Kingdom. One would also have agreed on [?] and the Marching order of this Peace, the boundary on the frontiers, according to the content of the said Treaty, shall be realized; and where the outstanding disputes concerning this Crown and the King and the Republic of Poland are concerned, His Czarish Majesty extended its good mediation. Some would have it that something was stipulated regarding the interests of the Duke of Holstein, and that the original, together with those of other arrangements, would be handled underground in the general peace to be made at Brunswick, on the holding of which Congress the Emperor's Ministers continue to insist. Some say that the British fleet shall soon return to England; but it is said that the same shall still remain on this coast until after the triumph of the Clarification; at least until the notification of the decree by which the Peace is sent by Admiral Norris to Great Britain. It is assured that His Majesty will present a reward to the said Admiral.

Germany & Adjacent Kingdoms

VIENNA, September 20. Since the here residing Fruitist Raiders have complained to the Kaiser about the Lord Kannegieter at the Imperial court, a courier was sent one day to Belgium to the Imperial Resident Velium residing there, to inform him by order of the Kaiser that he should come to court without delay. News has been received from Hungary that a certain officer there was found keeping company with a young lady of 8 years of age [Editor's note: likely referring to status or rank rather than literal age]; from which he wished to marry, not regarding that he has in this city a wife and children; wherefore the father brought the marriage suit to court, and removed himself with his daughter to his castle; whereupon the bailiff, to rid himself of this unjust misstep, had the said castle attacked, and [?] with a company of dragoons (others say 300 men) had come for that purpose and had delivered up the daughter. The father, seeing this, had placed himself with some men who had come to him at the castle in position; whereupon he himself had them attacked by the dragoons, and after a violent fight (it is said that several men on both sides were killed and [?]), made his escape from the castle, and led the daughter away, having thus committed this unfaithful deed against his bed, so that he with all his folk has gone over to the Turks; this atrocity makes much stir at court and in the city.

France

PARIS, September 18. The Count has given a Pension of 1000 Reux to the here Lord Geissfel, Advocate General of the Parliament of Aix. Since the Bishop of Fréjus has refused to accept his Archbishopric of Rheims, the court [text repeats and becomes unclear] ...it is not [?]. Although the Decree of the King's Council of State of the 1st [?] has been followed with many discontinuities, it is said that the same shall never be enforced as a consequence. One finds great difficulty in getting the Minutes here [?] to the week, for there are many others [?], the Million [?] time not [?]

The Netherlands

CHAMBER-CRAFT, &c. This morning two ships arrived here from Marseille, one of which has been towed up the Seine River, in the waterways as directed by the Magistrates for naval combat operations, which has not yet been settled. The Clerks and other officials were permitted to undertake an Expedition from their Court. Yesterday the Count de Vermandois was appointed, having well revealed this military matter, and a similar undertaking was ordered, in which all the Genius of the Door [?].

Mr. Colbert writes from the 11th of September. In the Committee, as those long returned from Bordeaux from Avignon, their first known matters were spoken to the King, who sent back a domestic death; in these of the Moussarts, which there will be handled in another time this Committee still supposes of the Dodeken, which secured [?].

Dr. More Lacedoen, commanding in the Lands of the Batavians, writes from the 11th of September. Mr. Dietsame writes to his Son, that he might further in the harbors in the harbor, and Borstders of Avignon are not congrextered in some hooks; to verify this from suspicion. With respect to Avignon and concerning the Neerlandic Kings certain days distinguished.

The Ceurie of the Westpijn, the same half St. Hams Giert Brunel The Hague of the Plenipotentiary resided Portuguese of their No. Mag. the Spanish The Hague points. The Growing Counts behind [?] in The Hague of the [?].

Mr. Carpegna, commanding in Upper-Franche, writes from the vide of September. That he on the cedite gaengaes of the Comtat, had set a Line in defense on the Durance, over the Mountain of the Bies as far as Monafti, which there were robbed out in another, and named the Line of the Durance over the Mountain Joox through the [?], and further to the Rhone, where through Casteln, and Smitens at Montbrisson situated [?]: all the Flats on the regiert side of the Durance are completely named, relieved Cacarics, where from time to time some Sick [?].

On the 7th of September the supplicants held [?], which come out day by day; the too brought standing blim [?]. At Aste still more Bus [?] Sick felt; Toulon is uncomfortable, and there are in the same Book three [?] only with over: Marseille Recis [?] the further shifted days still given, and even good Sick taken, and [?] which in the more pusles [?] have been templed, goes the [?] beginning [?].

Mr. Viscount de Kameur writes from the 9th of September 1721. Mirvé [?] word [?] key felt, and the Sick [?] still wee com pe [?].

The Directors and Head Magisters of the Company of Commerce and Albertanie vallage is in this Company, that they immediately after the handling of the [?] per Cent counted Counts find.

Civil Letter, Com. Aldine, and Third Com. Seltaye Molchsam, valued on Tuesday of the 6th in the Gilded Dyuak on the Tesfela Kasy: passed and certain well Cedule Sundt-Schving are at three months; and further undersmyning lit to bekoonme D the wonder. Mr. Jan Ricamacker Broker; shall the Amsterdam outgoing on Wednesday the 1st of October in the Bayt i ten parey catta pyla diture Valiam Tak, and ea parrye Brazil-Tobacco; let Matracds him, because it 1d [?] of the rde Hank samud van Eckuarde; Daarel d'Afteralto, Nicollaes Eus. and Kipherd d'Afterede Brokers, valued at Amsterdam Makers Brief to the Horen of the Dysfluran in the Dysfluran Journal, a party catta pyla postis Verguy Bs boucsgesagt. over the Fluestsaje, at the Fakkerije at Goloaf.

De Roede, eta se trevila, and the datelsch Tye, Stellen at Roswedan in July 1731, and hum and Emte madt se voed]: Hermiages talges, item behind the Hersing ser: Necien in suichens in the Massliedrodentdrintin in the Dyeluan, or wyn the Maskisun Johannea and the Vieste of Vad Lisl, Wereker Tronmerman.

With greannecefte or chosen veltecken and fraye Hoffsuecke, with aya Breeden and Hay- and Hoopsle Landen, for B: Paselada ont teu konesbare, ayak and Koy genge roden kerdt the other d'it is teta Bundinge tor the Cobrectlice, etch i Hoeybergee, tryde the ceroede Litts, situated all ways d'ir there padinge in oothoo aoemt.

U s woeder gefiryf jeggen, in Aonsijsdinge daeis an Oboolay. Vlaan Nelmsis and Broker in the Masterkoerbose sam gede in ke nog and vaj at Mosmit, or at Matskamboore aan Lewsdau Verhoof Actraas the aud. Johannes van Lavoü, J. w. D. and Doombary at Utrecht, ayude grcorenge Theologicé Reand, Rotterdam from the Widscde-Zui, Dord of Braun. By the writing is also still opgeeemenie o Chel Credo named. C

Spain

CADIZ the 5th of September. The Spanish upon falling described diverse of the Maldives, in particular coming, against word tetdive post of the favorite gives, which in the participants of the Calomnie getraftzeyd. After that His both foregoing, as the custom indeels. First-shots came out the and the half gilded [?] a magnificent man, that with the Ships of Buenos Aires has come over; trade were nonetheless at the great party Silver of the Verm, Granitureyd, and Monies of Madrid by a Maarten de Scribelen was it too foreseeing of the Calonigen, Marolande jee. promised.

The Admiral Sommelas received richly this chain on the height of Effe, Fora, and Crana; but nothing the all or to loppen gemunged [?].

MADRID the 16th of September. One hears that the King curtly through Lord Stanhope, asked in the Marquis de Grimaldi's manner lattice with hand sliefer [?] read tooo Ducaes worthy. In the Harbor of this country one saw still furtherance not of the Greek Consul also [?] and Spain brought.

S. SEBASTIAN the 12th of September. As it remains vitt Province all further, the Godliness oys the battles swignigt gleoit [?], as nothing but wef the Godliness boat harren heard, and that great gueteischap izie still, in 't short further rules could be provided [?]. dryeceeye and gerveucn neyaverdt bewering [?].

Our moth keeps the deiche Spelcelling of the sertrayinge of the volch waten from derribe here not Land schorden off yet the seferiee flooren could of acne, yatonose of the ingetieiete noader jotden, indiene the Adventen weig ter seden as in a child ungeluerig remained dagelgs ter geveuyt oyrwordl wertog [?].

France

PARIS the 29th of September. [Text heavily corrupted in transcription - unable to provide reliable translation of this section]
