DE KLOPPER, at the Breedtstraat, 5th October 1721. With Privilege.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 3rd of october, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 120.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 6th of October, 1721
Spain

Cadiz, 5 September. The expedition concerning the Dutch ships sent to Madrid has not yet returned, and word is that it will be received favorably. Tomorrow morning the individual shareholders of the warship, furnished from the hold, will each receive their silver, with none other than the healthy Indians. Some are reporting from the place, with beasts bearing a load of 4 new currency, as has come over with the provisions from Buenos Aires. In the following week, moreover, a great quantity of silver, also from Vera Cruz, is expected to arrive from the ports of Madrid and Spain; however, the miners in Sicily are not proceeding so hastily to send ships to Chagres, Maracaibo, &c. Admiral Sommeldyck is examining those deceit Colon and Barbacu, and the ports of Santa Fé and Cruz, though it is uncertain whether they will be recovered.

MADRID, 12 September. It is reported that the King of Great Britain, through Lord Stanhope, his ambassador there, has had a present delivered to the Marquis de Grimaldo, worth some thousand ducats. In the ports of this kingdom, inquiry is made, in the audience of the merchant ships in the corona, whether 3 warships have also been laid down on the stocks.

ST. SEBASTIAN, 14 September. All matters in this city and frontier are proceeding; the goods from the ships are being unloaded without any difficulty, provided that the same, of green conveyed blue boxes and with great ceremony in letters, [?]; so that it is hoped that the goods, which have been brought into this city and [?], (and at such time as the French not much and British not entirely make war,) since the Adams here in the land ought to have been given away, and in the harbor by the city our warships are stationed and Madrid it is reported that the opening of the Adams here in the land ought to have been delivered, and is being conveyed to the King, from whom daily a report is expected. Our warships have delivered this specification of the discharged goods.

France

PARIS, 22 September. Last Friday the King appointed various officers to knights of St. Louis; the ceremony of their order being placed for tomorrow. In the same part of the militia the King has made a proclamation that the lands on his lands are to open; though he himself would not use such right. Though he then proceeds with held. Now in Boyer against the [?] for his majesty, reckoning such great ballet of the four elements, in four elevations, to be sung.

---

No. 119 — Monday'S Courant

Council of State for the reduction of the incoming fears of the state coal from England coming. Another concerning the modification of the incoming duties on malt and cheese. Another of 9 September from the fair market of Bordeaux from 17 October until next March. Another of 16 September forbids individuals from receiving and issuing old currency species, and other gold and silver coinage, the same being able to be received by the money changers and in the mints.

Extract letter from Paris, 23 September. I find myself obliged to advise your excellency that yesterday the ministers of the Queen of Great Britain and France signed the act of guarantee concerning the abdication renunciation of the Emperor on the kingdoms and states which the King of Spain possesses, and those of the Queen of Spain regarding the kingdoms, and that the Emperor [?], which the day after tomorrow shall be handed to the ministers of the Emperor and the King of Spain; at the same time it shall at the British court in London the publication of the said acts of renunciation be facilitated, where this crown and Great Britain are guarantors thereof; so that one may believe, in this considerable point the way is cleared, the Imperial Diet [?] will be opened first, &c.

Great Britain

LONDON, 30 September. According to letters from Dublin of the [?], the meeting of the parliament of Ireland began the previous day; at which occasion the Duke of Grafton, Lord Lieutenant, appeared in the usual manner in the house of Parliament, and the Commons having come there, an address was made to both houses of Parliament; which was presented to the King, who, desiring the welfare of that kingdom, has thereby ordered that parliament take such measures; as could best serve for the ease of government and the welfare of the people; that his majesty with tender feeling over the public calamities which that nation might have suffered, was now exceedingly solicitous; that the best means would be employed to protect that kingdom from the damage afflicting neighboring lands; that subsequently the necessary means be considered, and the extraordinary costs in that parliamentary session be ordered so that the nation be placed in a flourishing state; for which his majesty has again applied all his power, allowing himself to receive contributions toward the erection of a bank. The houses being well conducted, they were allowed to receive contributions toward the erection of a bank. Communities would be assessed; so that matters would be submitted to the house of Commons &c.; that through the same the necessary subsidies might be granted &c.; whereupon the Lords and also the Commons resolved to thank the king by advice, and to testify the zeal with which they would apply themselves regarding the erection of the Bank &c.; and that finally to both houses this thanks be presented. Monday a requisition was presented to the King for the re-establishment of a colony in [?] New Scotland and the province of Maine, being land which the Dutch previously possessed; but in the wars conducted thereupon, during the time of Charles II, which they held and which was assigned to the crown of England, and afterward by the French with the Indians of Canada was [?]. The statue of the Lord Chancellor Hyde, grandfather of the worthy Queen, has been sent to Oxford, to be erected in the hall of Christ Church. [Several items regarding business transactions and colonial matters follow - text becomes increasingly corrupted here and difficult to parse with certainty]

The Netherlands

LILLE, 10 September. Our intendant, concerned about the contagious sickness reported in France, has ordered that all the wolves in this city, as over the German borders are needed to the city's gate, to open a lazaretto nearby, so that quarantine be maintained there. The beasts expelled from there and from the German borders are held half [?], and from Dunkirk are held and the ships with wolves [?] or Lydagen [?] are not yet decided; Friday is it the following Friday the following Monday, in accordance with our ordinance of the 30th of this current month, [text becomes fragmented] ...

Spain

...outside, in the [region?] that we do not further concern ourselves with going from Spain to broaden [trade], in order to establish that no woolen goods in Flanders should be imported, which would otherwise be allowed; but without [?]

Dordrecht

Margriet, Ralph I Thomas Pennabi Will. Davidson, al Sud Drudgett, al Corn. Fran dois, Tobias Jeffe tegencerd verhokl Joseph Hodgkinum

[His Royal Highness], being informed that some merchants, somewhat too eagerly importing foreign wool, bring [goods] through the harbors, canals, and rivers of Flanders, in agreement with foreign merchants, their wool from Barbary, and even from the Levant, making it pass for Spanish wool. His [Royal] Majesty, holding all time dear the preservation of his Majesty's Underlying [territories], and wishing in such a good time to avert from these Frontiers of the Kingdom alone useless and uncomfortable disorders for unworthy sick persons, has been compelled to send us his order in this open fashion.

FOR THIS REASON we do most expressly issue injunctions and prohibitions to all Persons, of whatever State and Condition they may be, to bring in any foreign wool, from whatever hand it comes, in the entire extent of our District, until otherwise ordered by us; rescinding thereupon all Reports and Permissions by our good [authority] issued up to this present day; forbidding the Director, Commissaries, and Officers of the King's Pages to allow any Foreign Wool to be received upon pain of [?] of their office. Commanding the Magistrates of the Cities and Villages of two Districts to keep watch over the proper execution of our aforementioned Ordinance, which [shall be] published and affixed will be, at the place of our good [authority], which we equally command to keep watch over the Execution thereof; done at Ryel the 12th September 1721, by [?] Nimmd, and by the Lord Remond.

Brussels, 4 October

Cithereon, chased from His Imperial Majesty's [residence], happily arrived [?] at the state, as when in his [?] year is passed, on the [?] 4th October is [celebrated], the Count of Windaguetz [?] took the occasion of a magnificent Festival or Ball at the Castle of Tervuren; of which Your Excellency, still and thereafter Nobility [?] of this day, excepting a great multitude of Admirers, which had gone there to take part in that Entertainment. Music was there maintained by a fine Symphony of Kettle-drums, Trumpets, Hunting Horns and Viols; during the Meal, [?] from his Health was [?], one fired the Cannon off at the Castle placed; [a] Triumph tree that was erected, and richly adorned with art, and which in the evening with a considerable number of Lights was illuminated, the bright beams hung beautifully bright so that the Company, on account of the [?] entertainment held this longer; not until morning promised well [?] to the utmost satisfied over the entertainments, which the aforementioned [person?] his same Consort a gentle Greeting have seen.

Amsterdam

Michael Cadogan [?] also the Polar co

Petersburg

Peterhof also to the Polar co

Rotterdam

Free trade men; men expect [?]challen are C° at which time the Lord [?] his Czaarte h [?] High Ministers h

Court magnificent to Hos magn

Mosjeldorp

General and Govern

Keulen [Cologne], [?]

Aelpshaul Areyt

Comp well-built Sma Book sold and other [?]Prink-cake 2 also other [items] Crochet and Dou br J. D. Bepaan, Utrecht of de Water, Rotterdam J. D. Bemain, Amst.

Amsterdam by Steenhouwer en Uitwerf, Book-seller

P.S. One writes from Moçara that in very good fashion of September, the Ministers of Great Britain and France the Act of [?] had taken upon the promised [?], which shall be placed in hand to the Ministers of the Emperor, and King at Lisbon [?] Wednesdays and Thursdays forenoon a [?] toire of the Westphalian Treasury Court.

By [?] mid-week on Wednesdays and Thursdays forenoon a dragon on the Table Quay, selling an extra well-prepared Smal [?] on the Table quay, sales an extra well-prepared Smal Wednesday the 8th October 1721, shall at the house of Steenhouwer and Uitwerf, Book sale forth; a worthy collection of Spanish, Italian, French, and other English, as also some Italian and French Prink-cake [?] also other [items] Crochet and Dou br J. D. Bepaan, Utrecht of de Water, Rotterdam J. D. Bemain, Amst.

Amsterdam by Steenhouwer and Uitwerf, Book-seller

Jamaica, 16 July

There 16 ships [have passed by], namely the Carlos, Capt. Neckina from London, the Peps, Capt. de Dore, the Harris, the Johannos, and the Haerferd, Capt. Lilley from Bristol. Capt. Harris, and the Liverpool Merchants forth. Capt. Hiam, from Connee, the Corolling falls, Capt. Harris, and the Liverpool Merchants forth. Capt. Hiam, from Connee, the Corollindale out. Somers and other from the Island, named and forth, and from the French coast, Somers and other from the Island named and forth, and from the French coast, Somers and other from the Island named, out of Vera Cruz, which later and further to Cadiz to be changed 1700 t. the Admiral Gibson, whom we not further [?] was. Notices & Advertisements

At Leyden by S. Lucktheiman shall on the 14th and 15th October sold be called- ferven [?] on the Island Marken: and on the 21st and 24th October the angular [?] of three to Collsidam; consisting both in very fine Theological, Historical, and [?] appendix of Illustrated Books; of which the Catalogues is to be seen at Doid by map, 's Hertogenbosch, Amsterdam, Wetterns and Boom, Haarlem of Kelter, Gouda State &c. Uneasy, J. and D. Book-making Makarts, shall to Amsterdam sell on pearls, on the universal in the New Lord's Lodging, a great party Mats and 14 to 2; palm, and of 70 to 100 feet long; and 8 Muscovite Mats, of 18 to 24 palm, 12 to 19 palm, and of 74 to 90 feet long; 4 Malerecds; 4500 Long-Hondie Mats foot long, and still diverse other Mats more.

On Thursday the 9th October shall at Leyden by Johannes van der Linden, the young, [?] the Mr. Johannes van de Leene, J.U.D. and Counselor to Utrecht, further arranged Theological [?] and other Books; the Catalogue is to be had to Utrecht by of de Water, Amster[dam] Bouquet, Rotterdam and the Widow Bos, Doid by von Braams. By the same is also come out Cau's Civil as Criminal &c. in Octavo.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 6th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 8th of October, 1721
West Indies

JAMAICA, 16 July. Eleven to sixteen ships have arrived here, namely the Carlos, Captain Nekkin from London; the Constantyn's [?] Strand; the Hope, Captain Harris; the John and Betty, Captain Wimmore; and the Hardfield, Captain Lilly from Bristol; the Phenix; the Endres Voir, Captain Harris; and the Liverpool Merchant from Barbados; the Delhi-gaste, Captain Nics from Guinea; the Cathelian; and also the bark of the Spante Full, another [ship] from the island of Cuba, bringing the welcome news that 13 Spanish galleys had been encountered, to be shipped together to Cadiz; [?] it is said that Admiral Chrifton [?] in Rio at the fore. On the Spanish coast several [?] ships are lying.

Spain

CADIZ, 9 September. Señor Giraldi, who departed from here on 11 October 1719 with the ship [?], bound for the South Sea, arrived on the 26th of the previous month with the ship St. Franciscus Xavier, which was formerly a French vessel, in the South Sea, in this bay; the same [ship] having departed on the 11th of December from Cathao, and had encountered [?] in the bay called Cornucopia, [?] departed 30 days thence, and subsequently suffered leakage [and spent] 14 days in the Bay of All Saints; as this ship [?] in the waters of [?] ships and [?], the St. Joseph Royal, the Neptunus, the Teris, and the Prince Charles; besides at Porto de S. Bonaventura, ships [?], one Spanish ship departed again from the bay of St. Cornucopia, they had yet no business to transact, and through the [?] again in the waters of Lima [?], and no French ships or goods are to be admitted. Generally [?] departed on 17 February with the [?] goods of Lima, and also with goods from Lima, and [?] not to Mexico, and also with the fleet of [?], which departed from London in the beginning of May [?], and [?] to embargo [?]. Before the [?] departed, one had at Lima the [?] mark and the commerce, in hopes; but the ships from Leiden [?] long [?] on the coast, and [?] had, the goods [?] [?], so that loss [?] would not [?]; three the ship [?] likewise fate in that [?].

BILBAO, 15 September. The ships Daniel Godelinck and Dimmerinck from Amsterdam, arriving here from the French harbors, have as yet no permission to unload; also shipwreck goods [?] the harbors, Antonio has also entered here.

England

BILBAO, 19 September. The sad event [?] shipwreck of the ships Danish Gooderkin and Thonisman from Amsterdam, coming from the French harbors, have as yet no permission; also arrived here Captain [?] the Ursent, Antonio.

Italy

LIVORNO, 12 September. News has been received here that the Algerian rovers have also [?], pressed comes here in the sea still a French bark from Algiers, which [?] that the most in sea waters, so that the [?] there still lying buyers go in sea waters, [?] small, and well [?]) against waters no Hollander [?] Hollanders. The ship Andries Listing has departed well from here with [?] to Salonica.

Portugal

PORT [?], 13 September. From Lisbon [?], the third shipwreck to Ireland, and the mariners to Viana [?], the third [?] departed; but one is troubled, and the ships [?] days at sea struck, and through that they [?] [?] work of a night [?] down, while that [?] for the Saturday the Algerian rover, [?] [?] well from here with [?] to Salonica departed.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, 24 September. The ratification of the concluded [?] is 12 [?], in which arrangement from here [?] again is [?], so against the [?] [?] one says that the [?] [?] out 24 other families in the palace no justice that in not [?], there still [?] in the land of [?] in the [?] through have not right more doubted [?] above sheet [?] or [...] in binding of a million [?], which often in the [?] Sweden holds, shall in the [?] of [.....] sea be held. [...] well can shall be established and well paid. 970, one says that 13 [?] [?] March out this royal Parliament [?], ships shall go where there [?] [?].

Dyodelet and the surrounding Kingdoms.

REGENSBURG, 13 September. Not withstanding that in the whole arrangement [?] once a week [?] and the religion [?] arrangement is well here week of the religion [?] [?] days in week of the religion [?], and to narrow inheritance no justice named, through we more [....]

---

No. 141

Wednesday's Courant.

and thereabouts one wishes that several Roman ministers from [?] 1573 departed. Word has been received that the plenipotentiary of the Protestant Corps of Frankfurt at Heidelberg has come, and [?] according to the lately concluded conclusion of his principals, with the justified church council had mentioned, in order to obtain a new [?] of their income, of which [?], which by the Elector are to be restituted, to the Lieutenant-overseer of the [?], thereabouts some [?] was made, but one [?] equally not; or approximately after the instance of the Protestant corps. The elector's here residing minister says to the Protestants that [?] short all the [?] shall be finished, but one believes; if it should come to investigation, that much shall still be wanting; such shall now soon reveal itself, since the last term set by the Elector is but of 6 days. P.S. One says that the Elector's [?] would have answered under the Elector of the Palatinate.

Great Britain

LONDON, 9 October. This morning an express arrived here with the articles of the concluded peace treaty between the Count of Great Russia and the King of Sweden, so that one shall soon see those. Monday, the Mister Louis Button was promoted to Lord Mayor over the municipality of Portsmouth. This day the Bishop of Durham, who had held that bishopric for 47 years, he was beforehand already 2 years bishop of Oxford, deceased in the age of 88 years; having left the greatest part of the ornaments [?] which he possessed, to their use. Some wish that the Bishop of Salisbury would have the apparent [succession] to that bishopric; in which case the Bishop of Gloucester might go to the bishopric of Salisbury, and the Doctor Wilcox might obtain the bishopric of Gloucester. That same night an elderly woman in Love Lane, who had lost her senses since the evening of Monday night, threw herself from her window above and fell dead in the street; a daughter, who was with her, was [?] all the [?]. This week a Mister about [?] likewise fell in the street, so one wishes because of the great hurt he had in his fall; but surgeons think they shall be able to preserve him. Wednesday also died the comedian Daggert, [?] over his dutiful submission to the regent [?]. Tuesday, the Knight Edo. Northeys, his son, who is member of parliament, married a daughter of the Knight [?], who brings 3000 p. to the marriage. Word has been received that at Wexford in Ireland 25 persons have been arrested and put in jail over their role as soldiers for the [?] house [?] over their role as soldiers for the Protestant house.

[Several lines of heavily corrupted text follow concerning a woman jumping from a window, various appointments, and commercial news about goods and shipping.]

The Lord Herbert, son of the Earl of Pembroke, has become captain of the first company in the guard at court, in place of the Duke of Montague. The [?] place [?] the Algiers [?] city, which has been vacant for some time, was [?] [?] in place of the duke; [?] the Montague the [?] place [?] the commodity at the merchants Symonds for 3 s 10 f. 19 shillings, [?] the [?] [?] brought to the chamber [?] city, because the Lord [...] the duke of the chamber [?] city brought, because the Lord [?] the relief has sold. The shares of the bank books closed, I.C. 149, Z.Z. 131 and 1 half, the million bank 114.

France

PARIS, 30 September. These days the Queen was at St. Cloud to visit the Duchess, with whom she [?]; whereupon the King amused himself with walking, and came the following day at 10 o'clock well back in the palace. Yesterday was the [?] on the hunt, and returned not before late to the [?]. The [?] of guards and with the reception of the [?] [?], to Bayonne to [?]. The Duke of St. Simon shall go alone and well proceed; he takes his sons of the knight order of the golden fleece to be confirmed; and that his sons are in time to be elevated; at the retinue of said duke Spain and are elevated; at the retinue of said duke Spain and are elevated; at the retinue of said duke [?] and all 200 people. The chapter of Rheims has on [...] named the following officiants to serve during the visitation, Mister Fillou officiating of the [?], Mister Fovatt [?], Mister Viliot conservator of the privileges, Mister Milter secretary [?], Mister D. de la Fore overseer of the fabric of the chapter, Mister de la Salle [?], and Mister bourgeois for the chapter.

From other courants of the world, one may read further.

PARIS, 3 October.

It was on the side of the certified Month, and Cardinal du Nois, a Lord of trust in the hands of the King; stood ready to lay [something], where [?] had already arranged everything necessary; but at that time when His Eminence wished to go out riding, there came an extraordinary Courier from Madrid, whose Dispatches occupied him so much that the aforementioned Lodge had to be postponed to another time. However, it has been said that the Gentleman of the Letters between [?] here in held [?] the Agreement of the Son of Spain, and his Court, the Marriage of the Prince of Asturias, with Mademoiselle de Montpensier, in the beginning of the House of Orleans; it was said in the beginning that the Marquis de Biron would have been nominated by the Duke Regent, to compliment Their Catholic Majesty, and the Prince of [?]; but now it is learned that said [?] through the Marquis de la Larre shall take place. It is now questioned whether the two religious Marriages were the cause of the journey of the Gentleman de Soundrval, who departed to Madrid for this purpose some time ago. The Duke of St. Simon leaves [?] toward Spain [?]. The King gives him [?] and pays him 500,250 Livres for the expenses of his journey, and besides this Sum the said Duke has also taken up 500,250 Livres on his own credit from Monsieur Samuel Bernard; the Count de Longe the same Nephew, and his Sons, will go there with him: they each make a small [?] over 15,000 Écus. The Suite of the said Duke shall consist of 12 Gentlemen, 12 Pages, and 24 Officers or Servants; each shall receive from the King 1,000 Livres as a Gratuity, over and above their ordinary Treatment; and it is expected that the same Duke shall remain no more than six weeks in Madrid; [?] upon departure from Madrid of Spain toward [?] to take [place]. The Cardinal de Biffe shall return from Rome on the 12th of December. One was greatly amazed at the news that the Duke of Brittany has retired so piously into the Abbey of Le Bec, to end his days there. It is expected [?] this coming Sunday; the King and Madame Royale, under the sponsorship shall hold [the baptism] of the Princess, Daughter of Their Majesties the King and the Queen, which child the Ceremony of Baptism never received.

ITALY.

NAPLES, 13 September.

Last Wednesday there arrived here a great British Fleet, which happily [arrived] before the Feast of Saint Januarius on the next Friday, at the same time that the [?] Insight was revealed. On the way one hoped that the Grains and Warehouses, so that the same might be [?]; a number of ten, [?] Thomas of the Bridge, in the Strait of [?] is greatly lamented. Yesterday our Authority [?] the end of the mentioned [?] [?] those who have recovered from their indispositions.

The Barbarille Romarin have [?] of the Work, here to [?].

ROME, 20 September.

Sunday the Feast of the Sacred College, held in general Diet at [?] in the church of St. [?], where the Holy Anne Daughters were distributed. Yesterday the public Ambassador Austrian with His Holiness; after which the Church of St. Eustathius was visited; a Holy [Mass] was celebrated. Monday morning had the general Audience of officials by the Pope. Tuesday the public [audience] with His Holiness. This morning the Cardinal of the public Audience with the Pope, in which Conti and Spinola Secretary of State [have...]

The Cardinals Aquaviva, of Rohan, Qualtetti, did not [participate] in the [?] of Prombeo, however said they gave to the President, who soon [?] all prelates. The Cardinal of Schönborn, did [?], made a displeasure of [?] & Colonna, the Prince [?], [?] Lord...

Although and concludes, the Holy Father granted [honors] to Antonius Conti. One sees here [?] number the Cardinals Aquaviva, [?] two groups, to whom the [?] on the Royal Chapel

[Editor's note: Old Style calendar] believed [?] His Majesty and the King of Sweden; by which [?] to the Czar, by Sweden for [?] shall be done. Provinces Livonia, Estonia, Ingria and [?], Narva, Pernau, Wyborg and several other Cities with his [?], as well as a portion of the Kingdom of Finland; and the City of Kexholm; shall on the part of His Majesty the Swedish be returned to the Swedish [?] the remainder of the Kingdom of Finland. In the same Treaty are also included the King and the Republic of Poland, of which highly esteemed [?] General for the Provinces of Groningen, has given notice, as well as to the Foreign Ministers [?] the said Lord President has therewith [?] use, in the name of High Powers, the said Prince [?] was thereupon complimented. Yesterday morning Monsieur Dreyes, as Colonel of [?] before the Board of the Admiralty was [?] here.

THE HAGUE, 7 October.

No. 1721.No. 122.

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 10th of October, 1721

ITALY.

NAPLES, 18 September. Last Wednesday, the solemn ceremony of the Feast of St. Januarius was begun in our Cathedral Church, in the Chapel of the Tiefor, which was concluded on the following Friday in the usual manner; and during this time such a terrible storm with thunder, lightning, and heavy showers occurred that the gutters and vineyards in many places [?], causing considerable damage. On the same day a fierce storm in the area of the fortress Gaeta sank a ship that was laden with coal; however, six men who were aboard managed to reach land. The newly built palace of the Marquis del Vasto has been graced by a visit from the Under-Governor in the name of the Emperor, the Princess Casiani, from the Family of Borgia, [?] of Spain, by order of the Regent Empress. Her arrival and gracious reception have been certified by the Imperial Court, according to proper ceremonies, and was further honored by the clergy of St. Clara.

ROME, 20 September. Sunday, the Feast of the Exaltation of the Cross, the Sacred College, numbering 9 Cardinals, held services in the Church of St. Marcellus, where charitable gifts were distributed to poor young women. The Venetian Ambassador had a public audience with His Holiness; the latter visited the Church of St. Euliachia, where the Feast of that Saint Cardinal Agnes was officiated by the Pope. On Tuesday, Cardinal Althan held a public audience with His Holiness attended by a large retinue. This morning Cardinal Althan again had a public audience with the Pope, where His Eminence Cardinals Consil and Spinola, Secretary of State, paid their respects. Cardinals Acquaviva, von Rohan, Contuliesi, and Orsotomi, and the Princess of Piombino, traveled to Albano to pay a visit to the Pope, and he received all with great affability. Cardinal von Schönhorn entertained at table Cardinal von Schorsombach, the Venetian Ambassador, the great Constable Colonus, and Friar Ordechiuki, along with eleven other gentlemen of distinction. The Signor del Giudice presented a very fine and splendid horse to Prince Don Melchior Aquaviva. A Manifesto was published here under Cardinal Aquaviva, containing the King of Spain's act regarding his pretentions on the Crown. Regarding this Manifesto and at the request of the Pope and the Lord of Florence, a formal process was drawn up of all that was done by the Canons of St. Maria Maggiore [?]. [Editor's note: The text becomes increasingly corrupted here.] Cardinal Althan subsequently forbade all trade in doves, which had been practiced in this city during the time of the late Cardinal Althan, when he had his last public audience with the Pope.

FLORENCE, 13 September. Regarding the marriage of the Prince of Piedmont with the Infanta of Portugal, it is now determined that it shall take place here in the coming summer, which will delay matters for some time, until such time as one shall have news from there, which is still eagerly awaited.

FLORENCE, 20 September. At the home of the Signor Philippe Gentilli, a ceremony was held in the hall, with the daughter of [?], a lady without dowry, whose name is Palavecinr. [?] During the festivities, such a violent hailstorm fell upon the palace that all the ladies in the vicinity were terrified, and in the confusion they hastened back to the city. We have experienced an unusual abundance of hail this summer, as has also occurred in Italy: at Leghorn such hail fell as was never before seen; this gives us cause for concern. The vigilance of the health authorities at Leghorn and the diseases reported in France cause us daily precautions [?], and such diseases in France cause us to [?], for should they [?] his borders, we would have reason to fear [?] and his territories [?] in quarantine [?] his territories lost [?] in the territory of our [?] we might [?]; thus the same ship [?] lost [?], so that one [?], which is [?].

MILAN, 20 September. The picturesque fortification of our [?] [Text becomes too corrupted to continue reliably].

VENICE, 27 September. The stormy weather continues here still; and word has been received that the storm in the region of Padua has caused great damage. The rivers of Terra Ferma have swollen so greatly from the continuous heavy rains that there is great fear of the dikes breaking. The Noble Brigade with his wife has departed for Genoa to continue his journey to his post as Ambassador at the Court of Spain by sea. According to letters from Constantinople, the Porte continues to demand satisfaction from this Republic regarding what occurred at Dulcigno, though the Grand Signior was unable to grant protection to those Corsairs.

SPAIN.

ST. SEBASTIAN, 20 September. It is said that the late inspections of goods, as soon as the French have occupied this province, were only intended to prevent French goods that are still here in the land from [?]; since one cannot obtain permission from the Court to send them further into the realm, except only into our Province of Biscay. Those of Vitoria say that we shall not obtain customs houses here; but regarding this no decision has yet been taken by the Court. It is reported, though not yet confirmed, that the Spanish fleet, coming from Havana and other islands, as well as from the North Sea, provided they are in good condition and have healthy certificates, shall be permitted to enter.

BILBAO, 10 September. Until now the ships that have arrived here from Joost Waag, Damian Gonzales, Michael Timmerman, etc., have not been able to obtain permission to discharge, but it is hoped that this will be granted within 2 or 3 days. From Amsterdam [?] that this will be granted within 1 or 2 days. Those of [?] James Glutter, Jouwe Douwes, etc., and those of Mauritius Feolaat, Master Keurs, etc., shall depart first within 2 or three weeks. According to the latest advices from Madrid, there is now great hope that the customs houses established here shall be abolished shortly.

GERMANY AND THE NEIGHBORING REALMS.

VIENNA, 17 September. The day before yesterday, the Deputies of the [?] Eighteen left the city of Hamburg after taking leave of the Emperor; and since they have already obtained the assent of the Ministers, it is held certain that they will depart tomorrow. [Text becomes increasingly corrupted and unreliable].

REGENSBURG, 28 September. [Text too corrupted to translate reliably].

FRANCE.

PARIS, 1 October. The season continues to be pleasant.

LEFT COLUMN:

[The following section is heavily corrupted in the original transcription and cannot be reliably reconstructed. The legible portions appear to concern French court matters including:]

...pardoned to work with the Cardinal, the King, and [?] who has not yet been declared First Minister. When the Duke, as Regent, and the aforementioned Cardinal had received word from Rome on Monday, regarding the Prince of Albuquerque, with Mode- [?] and the Provider [?], the King was very satisfied. The Duke Regent said, "Sire, there is yet another [?] in your [?]..."

The King was very pleased, as he had heard of the victory in the campaign, over which His Majesty was quite joyful. The Bishop of Fréjus requested a few days' time for deliberation, but there is no doubt regarding acceptance. The Duke of [?] departed with the Marquis de la Porte, Captain in the Guards of the Duke Regent, to pay compliments to the King of Spain regarding the marriage of the Infanta. However, this journey is expected to cost this Duke some 200,000 Ecus, who along with his cousin, the Duke of Quinzo, have had their sons named as Great Regents by the King. The Duke Regent and the Duke of Bourbon are in such harmony that they have had but few disagreements and have even maintained a friendship. The Duke of [?] and [?] of the six Commissions of the Lords of Paris is with the Duke of Bourbon in the capacity of Intendant. The Archbishop of Rouen has been appointed to Guise. Monsieur la Perronie has removed Mlle Quifanne of Courland from cancer. The Lord de Purion, Court Master of the King, has died. The Count of Raubauprié and the Lords de Mailly have each received a pension of 2,000 Ecus on the Abbeys of the deceased Cardinal de Mailly, of which they are heirs. The aforementioned deceased Cardinal left many debts, including 1,000 Livres lent by [?] a nobleman under the care of the Jesuits, which while he was ill, the Capuchins came to collect because of the loss to their funds. The Intendant of His Eminence replied to these Fathers that they were already well compensated, or indemnified for the loss to their funds, with the Seminary that the Cardinal had given them. And to a Doctor of the Faculty at Reims who had served him as Assistant in the Execution of Letters of Cachet, who had also transferred 1,000 Livres to His Eminence, the aforementioned Abbot Morel said that the Canonry and [?] its revenue had brought him 1,000 Livres annually for several years, such that he would be paid within a year. On Sunday, the Scottish Earl Gordon played a game of chess against the Duke of Brittany, who lost the game and 100 Guilders besides. At the meeting of the Sorbonne on the first of this month, the discussion continued further, the Doctors wishing to have a Syndic, as the Council had done, and absolutely refusing to choose another. At Rouen, the trial was prepared of the Marquis de Giffard, who, robbed of all his goods while ill by a gamester to whom he had lost much, was then drowned. The nobleman who had lodged in a furnished room at the Gate of St. Michel and, while the procession of the Cordeliers was going toward St. Michel, was insulted some days earlier, was arrested that same evening by the people, who were full of vengeance and [?] considerable. A Royal Council will be established to judge the confiscations that might arise on the occasion of the [?] liquidation. The speculation whether the Grand Duke will marry again has given rise to wagers. Hunting has begun hereabouts. Since [?] weather conditions are expected to improve, the warmth will soon [?]. The Agent of Prince Ragorski, who was placed in the Bastille because he had transferred much money for the Prince, died in his bed through suicide, having cut his veins. The Officers of the Mint have [?] a quantity of counterfeit gold coins from the house of Lord Robin regarding gold coins of a second Mint, and with the banker Cottin some sixty Louises of a third Mint, along with a large sum of old specie. It is expected that an Edict will come out here to stamp and remelt the old gold and silver Louises; however, private individuals would pay for the experiments both at the Mint and at the Exchange with Bank notes, which would certainly render money more [?] and place it in better condition in Provence; but it is said to be much so in Avignon, and that the Vice Legate [?] the gates of that city have [?], which have also been much lost in keeping to his house...

The Directors of the Company of Commerce and Navigation of the City of Monnikendam [have presented to] the Lords Burgomasters and principal participants [a proposal] upon which they have resolved to grant to the Gentlemen Leaseholders, who [have received] 6 per cent to reduce to 5 per cent, of which the distribution shall be made on the fifteenth of next month, and shall be available daily for this at the office above, where the proprietors of that [?] might become acquainted in the future, like all others, with the distribution that was resolved; those who have transferred their money in payment and partly in their name shall have their shares [?] from the transport money, namely 1 per mille for the Company and 5 Stuivers. [?] P. Uekelhoet, J. and D. Bootskonning Brokers, shall at Amsterdam in the White Sea [sell] a quantity of timber goods, consisting of 6,000 Frederick-rix Deals and Eastern Deals, 6,000 Spruce [timber], 4,000 Fir, 1,000 Couriers, 1 Twat [?], 600 bent planks, via 12, 3, 4, 5, 6 to 7 inches, 30 to 60 feet long, 10 feet long, 10 Hamburg Beams, smooth and fine, 600 Fire Beams, and 1,000 [?]. Jac. vander House, Martenus Jewel, and Cornelia Veilinde Brokers, [shall sell] Virginia [tobacco] leaves of tobacco of the harvest of 1720, and this with Gaveling tobacco, in the Whitstreet in the Inn of the Black Lion. [?] Jacob vander House, Martenus Jewel, and Cornelia Veilinde Brokers, shall [sell] on Friday five houses precisely in the Whitstreet in the Inn [?] of Bladeren Blay tobacco of the harvest of 1719 and 1720, and this in casks in the storehouses of Mt. Alexander Gaullaggs. A certain Adriaan van Thol in The Hague died in the year 1717 on the [?] by Magdalena vander Notten, for some days previously, whereby [?] to his friends, for [?] his last [?] blood relations Brighs-man and Dominicus Diderik for children. Lith, Minister of the High German congregation, by the magistrates could not yet be further verified; [it is] 200 [?] that he is one, of which not only will notice be given; [it is] 200 [?] that [?] has been brought to their L.d., that you alike also [?] none, where of announcement shall come to be made.

---

RIGHT COLUMN:

LEYDEN

LILLE, October 3. This morning an express arrived here from Paris, bringing word that yesterday there came from Madrid the news that Admiral Cherton, who on August 21, 1720 departed from Cadiz with 2 ships for Vera Cruz, on September 19 or 20, 1721 happily arrived at Cadiz with 13 ships. The aforementioned fleet had 11 million pesos of Peruvian silver aboard, and it is said that the Prince of Buenos Aires, Viceroy of Lima, had returned with it; and although the aforementioned fleet had suffered some losses in 1721, nevertheless in real returns from the previous fleet there would be [profitable gains], of which one was eager to see the particulars. At Dunkirk they have also published the joyful news that the ships of Normandy that were mentioned there might be brought in. The day before, they had begun to heal the ships of [?] Lankhuysen and [?] Sail Rogh.

AMSTERDAM, October 8. According to letters from Stockholm of October 15, the King shall remain [?] as before [?] and toward his Chamber built; but H.M., and the day before it appeared in public, and some 200 [?] [?] made very many visits. The Admiral Maria yesterday-[?] departed from his court, and is preparing himself to return soon; but blood [?] postponed the willingness of the Ratification. What the General Admiral Board had sent regarding his confident [?]: complaints arose there about the [?] Vice [?] to conduct. At the Canals has arrived Corn. Colmao of [?]; the Liverdy Th. Poole of London and the Cape, Michael de Matega in 7 days from Algiers, with news that two eight [?] were ready there to go to sea. At Bayonne are lying hereward two [?] with Capt. Bernd Argens, Leon Cialuebourg, and Jaques Didug, and also the ship of St. James, Capt. Etienne Pinckele, toward Martinique. At Bordeaux has arrived the [?], Gerrit Leendersz from the [?], while from there the Dense [?] Michael de Wilde had departed; one saw there daily to meet [?] from St. Domingo the ship the Golden Fish, [?], which from St. Domingo just departed. From Danzig has hereward departed the [?]; and Jan Bakker, Olof Gertridie, Tekke Tjerkis, etc., are also lying hereward [?]. At Boulogne [?] the ship of Anne Hefikels, from here to Colbergen departed. At Bordeaux have arrived the Anna Margarita, Pieter Klyn, and Wilhelma, Steven Gertridie, both of here; on the River of London the Prince Frederik, Ulding Janijtaen of Hamburg, and the Rose, Samni Eyveel of [?] and Adriaan Corn. of Dieppe. At Bordeaux Niels [?] of Copenhagen; at Crudtfs the young Jan, Pieter Pieterele of here; and Bhendam Kaap, Francien Willemse of London: from this river departed John Maastiers and Heent. Berg toward Hamburg, and the arrived ship of skipper Hop departed on August 2 from Archangel; Corn. de Boer, toward Lisbon: a great [vessel], but [it lay] in the White Sea from which it departed. In Texel lay yesterday: of ships: the Juffer, of Symen Symente, the 7th and 8th of September [?].

ENGLAND

SPAIN

CADIZ, September 12. Here several [ships] have [arrived] according to [?] and more Joost Hooy of Anne, here [?] and to the return, [?], Barye of [?]; the Factory [?] 11 at St. Lucy departed from [?] Bilbao, Frigate [?] T. Th. Broere of London, the [?] of the Golondrinas. Th. Barton of [?] Maritano 4 various [ships], the Dina Holts and Marlas Welei, all of Hamburg [?] Palmer of [?], the [?]; the Richards, Jan Francis of [?] and Canaria, and the St. Franz, G [?] the [?] at St. Juan and [?] Jan; Capt. [?] toward [?] Christe, the S. Estien; Jan Friend, all toward Malaga's; 1 [?]. Upon the departure of the letters, a [?] came and the [?] [?], but the Frandie [?].

CADIZ, September 19. The Vera Cruz sailed hence [and] consisted of 13 ships, and [?] Capt. Arnour. The return [?] so [?] to the [?] warned individuals, and [?]: little [?] time [?] the other from Vera Cruz [?] thick [?] [?] Ecotic; [this] bray the Anna, Capt. MADRID, September 11. The Margrave Marguelia, Amsterdam, Cabinet Courier, who [?] off days [?], give books to the Duke Regent of France, and one [?] all three [?] other [?] King [?] [?] [?]; which bliss [?] here. The [?] [?], [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?].

AMSTERDAM, ROTTERDAM, DELFT, HAARLEM, UTRECHT, GOUDA, ALKMAAR, ENKHUIZEN, AND SNEEK.

[Concerning the] good progress [?] of the embassy between England and Russia, the [?] has received the order: [?] day to prevent that daily [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?]; [?]; [?] it [?] burg; it [?] in [?]: [?] [?] in the [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?]; [?] also [?]; S. at [?] [?] Williams the Prince Fowel [?] Sem [?] [?] of here; c the river [?] [?] with [?] was in [?] ships of [?] [?] [?] [?] Lisbon

THE HAGUE, October 9. The Princes of Orange, having [?] of Cattiel here water [?]. The Great-Britain Miles [?] Witworth has of [?] come from [?]. Some cities of the general [?] themselves there. The Generals Count of Tilg and Hompfch are here [?] which [?] in conference [?].

Upon this they have made a request to the King's administration and authority, and [?] before [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] Monnikendam [?]; so that this [?] which no [?] [?] [?] [?] January 1722 in [?] money came to [?]; even if the Company 5 months mille for the poor pay, for on alter resolved; that after this mediation and [?] [?] on Amsterdam and [?] p. and 3 ft. for the poor, at the buyer and seller on May land; and the ambush on [?] on May land and [?] on the [?]; as both 6,000 Frederick-rix Deals of 10 12 for [?] 1,000 [?], [?] and 1 00 [?], 1,000 Twelve-ells, 1,000 marble [?], 1,000 brickstone, 1,000 [?] 3 to 60 feet long 1 60 Green shutter, of 1, 3 to 3 and 1 half inch of 4 40 [?] beams, and 1,000 green beams, etc.

...

MADRID, September 21. The Marquis de Maulerizet, Ambassador [?] Cabinet Courier, who [?] some days [?], his council with the Ambassador [?] Duke Regent of France, and [?] all three [?] other King [?] [?] [?]; which blessed [?] here. The residence [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?].

MADRID, September 21. That [?] [?] good [?] Gouela [?] [?] I say one already [?] on the [?] [?] [?] sees one [?] France adhere [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] mentioned. 1,700 times; were great joy caused. The great [?] married [?].

Course of Exchange

**The Exchange Rate of the EXCHANGE is today on Madrid 93 and 1 quarter. 117 and 1 quarter at 12. Seville 92 and 1 half. Bilbao 88 and 1 quarter. Venice 93 and 1 quarter.**

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 10th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 13th of October, 1721

LEIDEN

SPAIN

CADIZ, 16 September. Arrived in this bay: the galley Galy, Captain Joseph Radden from Genoa; the Sivilia galley, which ran away from Amsterdam bound for Sicily, which is being unloaded and reloaded here. The Retourne, Captain Hays from Martinique; the Dorothea, Captain [?], bound for St. Lucia, has departed; the Kircop from Bristol, Joseph Lond from Bristol; the Charles VI, Captain William from Genoa; the Chitry, T. Rioum from London; the Gorgone, George Dornford from Waterford; the Golondina, T. Banno from Dublin; and the Indianite, Jean Claude Dancheran from Martinique; the Warwyck, William Schor; the St. Pierre, Peter Haeriche; and the Moufes, Jochem van Cologne; and the Johanna, Matias Wolens, all from Hamburg, of which the Nanthemel, Captain Andreas Hand from Ostend; the Tenerife, Maurice Marchandi from Bilbao; the Richard, Jan Francis from Plymouth; l'Esperance, Andrew Poves from the Canaries; and the St. Francis, Cipry, Peter Baudyn from Amsterdam, have departed. The St. Jan from Dublin, Samuel Leg, bound for Dublin; the Sr. Jaq., Captain Touceline, bound for Minorca; Jagole Koopman, Adam Clarke; the Andalusia, Dan. Manamara; and the Samuel and Silvia, William Friend, all bound for Malaga, and Dutch warships cruising, etc. At the time these letters were sent, we learned that the West Indian Fleet is still expected, and the Holland ships that recently arrived will be unloaded tomorrow, but the French goods must be sent back.

CADIZ, 9 September. This morning the fleet from Vera Cruz arrived here, under the command of Admiral Don Ferdinand Chacon; consisting of 13 ships, among which the long-awaited French [vessel] Captain Lurgo. The return cargo consists of three million [pesos] in gold as well as silver for the King, besides eight million for private parties, and eight million for the guardians. It is not yet known exactly when this fleet departed from Vera Cruz, but it is known that eighty [ships] departed before the new harvest had yet fallen. Also arrived in this bay the Arras, Captain Auzeyollen, nine days from Amsterdam; they report having seen bad ships on the way to [?] the sea; Captain [?] the Lord Count died in his ship, and after the [?] the following day in the great bay the ship came entirely [?] Captain Godyn, commanding the ship Veere; on the 13th, three Holland warships, the Ter Meer, Kampen, and the Huy te Neck, were sent to sea, and under the command of the Holland Vice-Admiral Lord van Sommerlyk to sail, which also in the straits [?]; holding [?] the next morning, Captain Godyn to follow.

MADRID, 15 September. [Text unclear regarding the Marquis de Maulevrier, Ambassador of France to this court, receiving a courier...] which brought a letter from the Most Christian King, from the Duke, Regent of France, and one of the Council of Regency, this arrived at [?] [?], and also the tidings of this marriage between the King of France through its good choice and acceptance; which joyful news provided here extreme joy and gladness, and joy shots, in which all the people of all conditions, [?] Lisbon [?], but the like the. In Toldeo were various and Spanish regiments sick on the 13th [?]

MADRID, 25 September. The day before yesterday in the morning, the Marquis de Maulevrier, Ambassador of France to this court, received a cabinet courier, who had flown from Paris, and brought a letter from the Most Christian King, from the High Regent of France, and from the Council of Regency, being all three matters recently secret and important matters have been dealt with. Marriage between the King of France through its good choice has been accepted, and to the joy seen here has thrown out and much celebrated. The King, the Queen, and the Prince of Asturias wrote each one a letter in their own hand to the Infanta, to give her knowledge of her doings, and place with opportunity chosen would be. These 3 letters were that same afternoon by the [?] of Paoli already by post from the [?] Escorial to the young Queen brought; where she the King, the Queen, and the Prince of Asturias went to the Church of [?] Florence, where they will have enjoyed.

FRANCE

THE archbishopric of Rheims has not yet been disposed of. The Archbishop of Rouen, who lived at Gaillon, is the deceased one, sought this church for his bishopric. The Bishop of Laon is deceased, and placed by the Abbot of St. Albin, natural son of his regent, who was already Coadjutor of that bishopric. The Abbot of the Order of Citeaux in the bishopric of Rheims, is this other [?]; being by the deceased King taken here out of the bishopric of Pamiers, but has the Bull at the court in Rome [?] been able to obtain. The Lord Grain, Treasurer, departed at night, because that place from fear of contagious disease has already been shut up. The Lord Benthier late President of Toulouse simply became, the Lord de Manibon la Peleriet of the Doubs that office with the King's approval obtained. On the 2nd of this month the Juillacates and solemn funeral rites were held for the deceased grand-Duchess of Tuscany because the House of Medici that Order always has been favourable. The Duchess of Vausradou will not leave the King, for which reason Madame de Bouilene went to the borders of Spain to receive the Queen. The Dowager Princess of Conti has the Surinamese over the spoiling of the young Queen has not yet accepted. The Lord Bondia, and the physicians [?] was of the late Dauphin, is thus to be of the young Queen. The [?] for the Prince of Asturias to the wedding departed. The first President at the head of some members of Parliament made an address to the Duke Regent regarding the required consent of the King by anticipation to his marriage with the Infanta of Spain. The Countess of Marr has arrived in this city. The Abbot of St. Simon goes with the Duke of [?] to Spain. The Lord Arouet de Voltaire, author of Oedipus, has received an honorarium of 1000 livres from the Duke Regent and [?] is at work in the old Louvre on the compilation of an alphabetical dictionary of the names of those who have their declarations registered in the [?]. There has come an order that all the jewelers and goldsmiths must render an account of all the gold they have sold since 2 years, and [?] in the handling. The Knight of Langeton has been provided with the great cross of the Knight Order of Malta: his brother has had much part in the double marriage, as well as the Lord Robin of Montpellier. Since some time on Sundays again the [?] medicines have been taken up, which in the chapel of the Holland Ambassador the Lord Hop have come to church. In the Orleanois the vintage is very advanced, as well as in Burgundy; one finds also in this thought, and it will be good. The [?] in the Gevaudan: the surrounding places are very alarmed.

GREAT BRITAIN

LONDON, 7 October. By the letters from Dublin of the 26th previous, it is reported that each of both houses of Parliament of Ireland the previous day had handed over to the Viceroy an address to the King, as well as to the said Lord Lieutenant, the Duke of Grafton: which four special addresses, along with the replies to them, are too long to find place here. That same day was ordered by the Commons to bring in a bill to have ships coming from suspect places held in quarantine. The following day was ordered the bringing in of three bills, of which the first is to regulate the night watches in the parishes, the 2nd to bring convenience to the subjects in the enjoyment of the rights and privileges [?], the third to encourage the culture of the lands, and to regulate the sale and purchase of grains in the markets; subsequently it was decided to request that lord lieutenant by address, that his excellency might order that the public accounts of the nation for the house be laid. It is said that some prominent gentlemen in the proposal would be the King to propose to unite the kingdom of Ireland, or its Parliament, with that of Great Britain; that is, the last peers and 20 members of the lower house would have seats in the Parliament of Great Britain, and just as the Scots, it is said that the eyes have little foundation.

The previous Saturday, the commissioners appointed for the sale of the goods remaining from the former director of the E[ast] [India] Co[mpany] were gathered in the house of that company, and examined all their accounts, and namely this last year and all the previous ones, as [?], which they actually and in all [?] or handwritings, as proved, which they actually and in all previous October; the said commissioners by the closest [?] are many of the said documents closely tested; but on a bed for them elsewhere, in consequence [?], namely that they had brought in [?] from [?], which in a certain time, refused a) all those [?], under foreseen its [?] was too strict, and that his these them might not [?] the divide: yet they have to, namely the Kiddesman Ingram and Francis Exles that bed Saturday done; and anno the 1st author Ingram, and there certain times had: Gillarcen have those Greenland [?] not emigrated in the place of Sir [?], where therefore they [?].

Anno 1721

Theft Notice. Thursday morning, a fine Staverly Pony, approximately 5 years old with a long tail, predominantly brownish in color, was stolen from Stilherd. Whoever returns it shall be rewarded for their trouble. The gentlemen Tillard, John de Flint and de la Fon are requested to examine the aforementioned Pony and send their Certificate regarding the same to the Treasurer of the Exchange.

Gaston Greville is authorized, and according to a Letter of [...], betook himself the day before [...] Port.

DORDRECHT, 11 October. Arrived before this City are the annual Matthys Romont from Dunkerque, merchant Burevelt from Nieuport, and at midday their respective Keys. His Majesty in particular [?]; but after a charming Serenade was sung this evening, His Majesty and Messrs. [?], together with the Archduchess held evening Meals. Saturday morning in the Keys. [?] The Count of Wels [?] most delighted; and with the pipe several of the retained [?], report was made to the Emperor, and the Cash-box is delivered; Moore, the municipality Amsterdam has been divided in parts.

Only on Wednesday the 27th October 1721, following days, at the Market-halls, located in Damascus, Armenia, Georgia, [?], [?] and other [?] with, moreover Japanese and Chinese [?], Iron Spool-Vessels, Coffee, Chocolate and Tea and other [?], an uncommonly great [?]; and other Goods moreover: All

at Amsterdam sold on Friday the 17th October, in the afternoon at two o'clock, two hundred Lake Boards, measuring 45 to 65 feet in length, and 200 Lake Friesland trees and Fireballs, and the Mills from [?] and [?].

at Amsterdam sold on Monday the 28th October 1721, in the Wine [?], at the Copper [?], 1000 [?], 4000 [?], 1000 [?], and 1000 [?]-Planks, of 5 and a half to 6 and a half inches, 1000 [?] and Sharp lot Spruces, [?], [?], [?], [?], and diverse other Wood-spruces; and the rest in the [?] &c.

Various notable sales on Wednesday the 22nd [?] extra fine white [?]; as is visible by Notice.

[?] shall be sold at Amsterdam on Wednesday the 21st October 1721, in the evening fine and fresh Mill [?], Spruce Larch, and other [?], a fine lot, and other [?] &c.

Thursday the 23rd October, in the afternoon at two o'clock at the bureau on the [?], a very fine collection of Paper-goods, 200 Drawings and Prints, and [?] Engravings either printed or unprinted; the particulars are set forth in the Catalogues, which are to be obtained; consult the other Notice herein.

The Brokers shall sell at Rotterdam on Tuesday the 23rd October 1721, in the Second [?], a Ship's cargo of curious fine lot, from newly arrived and at [?] as is commonly said at the Timber-dock.

The Brokers shall sell at Rotterdam on Wednesday the 21st October 1721, in the Second [?], a Ship's cargo of curious fine lot, &c. and 1720, and at [?] as is commonly said at the Timber-dock.

The Brokers shall sell on Thursday the 23rd October 1721, in the evening at 2 o'clock a Ship's cargo fine lot all sorted new [?] Leaves [?] a curious lot [?] Tobacco and at short Timber-dock in the Warehouses.

on the 3rd November and following days, shall be sold the considerable Library of [?], whereof Catalogues are to be obtained; by [?] J. [?] in [?], [?] [?] Frankfurt am [Main]. [?] [?]. London [?], [?] [?], [?] [?], [?] [?], [?] [?], [?] [?], [?] [?], [?], [?], [?] and [?], [?] and in the [?], by the [?], [?] is somewhat [?] and the [?] [?] [?] printed in great [?], with [?] [?] [?] [?] [?] [?], [?] Goods for a short time, [?] [?] but a brief [?] printed shall [?] [?] [?] here [?] obtained; [?] [?] [?] also as the [?] [?]. [?] obtained.

[Report] of the building of the new Organ in the [?] Church at Amsterdam. is [?]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 13th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.
