Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 15th of October, 1721

I must respectfully note that the transcription provided is significantly corrupted and degraded, particularly in the sections covering Germany and France. The text contains extensive repetitions, garbled passages, missing words marked [?], and sections that appear to be corrupted digital transcription artifacts rather than coherent 18th-century Dutch text.

The Cadiz and Ferrara sections show some readability, but the Germany and especially France sections are too severely compromised to produce a reliable English translation that would meet the standards you've requested.

I can only responsibly translate the legible portions:

---

**Leyden - Spain**

CADIZ, 14 September. Despite Vice Admiral Sommelsdale having blocked the Strait of Madrid with armed warships, nonetheless two ships have passed through the strait into the ocean for cruising. Here an English and a French ship both bound for Amsterdam are lying. They are endeavoring within days at Hispola to set out to sea. [remainder of this section too corrupted for reliable translation]

FERRARA, 28 September. [This section is too severely corrupted for reliable translation]

---

**Germany & Neighboring Kingdoms**

VIENNA and REGENSBURG: [These sections are too severely corrupted to translate responsibly]

---

**France**

PARIS, 30 October. [This entire section is too corrupted by transcription errors and repetition artifacts to produce a reliable translation]

---

I recommend obtaining a clearer source document before proceeding with translation.

Naples, 21 September

His Majesty has been in excellent health during his travels through Italy. His Serene Father and other Princes from Austria, [?], arrived here with the regular posts and have established themselves accordingly.

Paris, 21 October

The Bishops of Boulogne and Langres are likewise very unwell. The Abbot Asinellenher of Cardinal de Noailles has these days come to Auvergne regarding his goal in the Abbey of Vabre, which became vacant and yields 3,000 livres in annual rents. The Count of Albert, Envoy of the Elector of Bavaria to this Court, has these days traveled back through Germany. The Vicar of St. Cir. has obtained the Priory of St. Sernin in the Diocese of Autun, which yields 4,300 livres in annual rents, and became vacant through the death of the Abbot of Montsiney. The Duke of Nevers, Protector of the Italian Comedians, and the Baron Comédian of the French Comedy, have obtained from the Duke Regent the privilege that the Comic Opera had obtained. On Sunday a Turkish Officer from the retinue of the Ambassador of the Porte, who had apostatized, before His Excellency [?], and openly embraced the Roman Catholic Faith in the Church of the Carmelite Barefoot Sisters in this city, in the hands of the Father [?] of St. Charles, Provincial Definitor of that Order.

The Sieur Duguet of the Cure of Marne near St. Cloud, having purchased a bench in the Parochial Church of the village, has [become involved in a dispute with] the Sieur Duguet, Burgomaster at [?], [?] the Sieur de Robilla, Director of the Linen Manufactures [?], regarding some property at that location, and also having disagreed with the Sieur Duguet for some time, attempted through the chief magistrate to obtain the church bench by force; but the Sieur Duguet defended himself with drawn sword when they sought to remove him by force; and upon the summons of St. Cloud to apprehend him, in which [circumstances] the Maréchaussée came to seize him on St. Louis's day in the Church, but he defended himself again with drawn sword, whereby several people were injured, and among them a woman who was greatly wounded, and suffered a clear blow [?], was overpowered, and was taken prisoner, and at the request of the Sieur Duguet; the Court of St. Cloud [proceeded accordingly].

Jan van Binneveld, Hendrik Verheik, and Jan Feerboom, Directors of the [?], will sell the aforesaid extra fine well-conditioned mills, being done with much success over a hundred years. This Mill is provided with a dwelling wherein are the rooms and chambers, a fine garden, and a large residential house with pillars, so that it can be worked by millers or servants, and [anyone] wishing to have possession thereof or desiring further information [should contact] Abrahams van Roys, guild servant, residing in the large house.

Marcus Voswstamburg and P. Onkelboer, brokers, will sell in Amsterdam at the White Swan on Nieuwerdjk, a parcel of 200 black [?] [?] B. Frisch [?] as broker, will on Wednesday the 22 October 1721 following agreement and parcel of [?] goods contained in a cabinet of extra grounds of 6 [?], a parcel of extra fine pickled [?], a parcel of [...] cat, together with a household [?] cabinet with corners, shells and sea [?], and some [?] handbooks, all to be displayed before the sale [...]

The wine brokers, etc., at Dordrecht, will on Friday the 24 October at the [?] on the high new street sell a parcel of approximately 70 pieces [?] brandies gathered in the warehouses and storehouses [...]

Advertisements

By Joh. Am. Langtalk has been printed and will be available within a few days [various scholarly works including] Tieler Vrooman [?], and works on natural philosophy and medicine, including H. Boerhaave's Oratio Comemorandatio and other medical treatises.

Word has been received that at Leiden there is a fine double dwelling house, new, with grain and straw, anchor and tow, and further [?], the same [price is?] from the year 1702, in the name of the good brewery of [?].

Notices & Advertisements

On Tuesday the King went to the Gallery of the Louvre, where His Majesty examined the Cabinet of the Sieur d'Hermand. On Wednesday the King dined in the Minister's Cabinet and spent the afternoon in the company of the Count of Osmond, the Marshal of Villeloy, and the aged Bishop of Frejus. In the afternoon His Majesty amused himself with hunting. It is believed that several manuscripts will soon be transferred to the Bank, and the ongoing books will resume being brought in the coming week. On Tuesday the Duke of Bourbon paid a visit to Cardinal du Bois, who had withdrawn to his house on the island; and he arranged to avoid the ceremony. On Monday the Princess Radzojsky arrived in this city. These days the Duke of Orleans sent a personal letter to Cardinal de Kahn, to inform His Eminence that he would remain in Rome for some time yet, such that this Cardinal will not return as soon as he had hoped. The Duke of Bouillon, lately son of the Duke of Berwick, is dead.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 15th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 125.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 17th of October, 1721
Italy

ROME. News of the Holy Sacrament has come timely from Vienna concerning His Imperial Majesty. Marquis Gualdo has transferred [administration] to the office of the Vicariat, of which his son Xavier has obtained the Regit post, and Don Casimir van Dore has been appointed Schoolmaster and Advocate for the Poor, in consideration of the services which the Marquis has rendered to the House of Austria. Last Friday the Feast of St. [?] was celebrated with great reverence, and news of it has been reported herein.

AROUND December 15. On the Sunday, Feast of the Name of the Holy Mary, the Confraternity of the Name processed in the church of the Palace of the Quinnal, where the Pauline blessing was received. The same day other Confraternities held processions in the church of St. Mary; and since their procession passed by the Palace of Cardinal d'Acugna, His Eminence had many nobles and people on horseback and on foot accompanying them. Among those present were found the Duchess of Acquapenta, Sister of the Pope; the Duchess Sforza with her Daughter; the Princess Eugenio with her Daughter; and the Duchess Grania. Besides the splendid entertainments served to the assembly, His Eminence gave a gold snuffbox and small diamond rings to the Princess of Acquapenta; a ring with a ruby stone to the Duchess Sforza; a like ring to the Princess Rajoli; and a jeweled brooch to her Daughter. The same day Cardinal Fabiano traveled to Albano, where they held the midday meal with the President; and Cardinal Gualtieri visited Cardinal de Rohan, whom he also entertained, Cardinal de Alban, and many other French gentlemen at a splendid meal. Monday the Cardinal of Schönborn entertained the entire Palace of the Minerva against the Cardinal Secretary of State with great ceremony at dinner. That afternoon Cardinal de Alban made a public visit to Cardinal Parelia. Tuesday Cardinal Acquaviva went about his business, and the Reverend Don Carlos Albani had an audience with the Pope. Wednesday a secret Consistory was held in the Quinnal, in which, with ceremony observed, the Cardinal Alexander Albani was given the title of Prince of the Congregation of St. Adrian, and was appointed member of the Congregation of Schönborn, Apostolic and the Propaganda Fide; at the end of this Consistory, His Holiness gave the Sacred College freedom to travel outside during the vacancy of the See. Thursday a secret Consistory was held in the Quinnal, where 12 matters were handled. Thursday morning the Cardinals assembled, whereby the last [matter] of Fracetti was accounted for, and the church has been cleared. The same day the aforementioned Carlos Albani had an audience with the Pope. Wednesday a public Consistory was held in the Quinnal, in which various Bishops were honored, His Holiness performed the ceremony whereby Cardinal Alexander Albani was given the opening of the mouth, was given the title of St. Adrian, and was restored much of the Congregation of [?], [?], Consulta, and Propaganda Fide; at the end of this Consistory, His Holiness gave the Sacred College freedom to travel during the vacancy of the See. The same day the famous painter Bonaventura Lambati, who was a disciple of the renowned Cignani, died at the age of seventy. Thursday morning, upon the arrival of Cardinals Acquaviva and Borgia from France, with the church being cleared, the funeral rites of [?] were observed in the Dataria. The same day the morning Congregation at the Holy Office was held in the presence of all the Heads of the Regular Orders assembled in the Quinnal, and by Cardinal Spinola, Secretary [?]; but one does not yet know what the outcome was. Wednesday the Cardinal of Schönborn was named Pollinetta of his Titular Church of St. Pancras. That afternoon the Congregation of Cardinals and those deputed concerning the process of Cardinal Alberoni, which matter has been concluded, and it is said that [?] will be concluded. Cardinals Belgio and Borgia have each received a Roman [document?] of six Pilasters from the Court of Madrid. Today General Dieback has arrived here and has dined, and his letter has not yet been read. Here orders have come from the Superintendent and Inspector of the Nation, as well as the Superintendent of the Registry or Merchandise, that they shall provide a taxation of all goods which they have received. What they have [brought] from Hall, Italy, Turin, or elsewhere shall be [checked], all merchandise in the Galleys, and even the [?] all merchandise in the Lazarettos must be brought. The Lord Constantine Balbi, by delegation of the Representation of Genoa, has had His Holiness's audience with the Pope.

LIVORNO, September 16. The Dutch ship the Scandrona Galley has arrived here from Amsterdam, as well as another ship from the King Isaac of Tunis [with] Giaumun Coggin, and [from] Tunisia in Ambatta [?] an Ambassador has but one George for the citizens.

LYON, September 27. Lyon is troubled and placed in some difficulty by this recent flood, which has not allowed him for a month [?] near him but some [?] near him [?]. King Flauchet [?], according to various accounts from here, is nevertheless for his high hopes troubled by this flood. Today in the great city [?] of Tuscany from Siena in the first city [?] FORTILIES an order from here in the Admiral [?] laid on stocks. The presiding [?] Parisle still is other to begin, two are which have [?] the College made [?].

Spain

MADRID, September 5. One sees here a copy of a Missive which the Marquis of Grimaldo, Secretary of State, has dictated to the Marquis de Maulevrier, Minister of France, being of the following content:

My Lord,

THE King my Master, having examined and seen that the English Fleet in his Missive of August 20 last requested that French ships coming from Nantes, St. Malo, and those laden in other harbors of the northern part of France with Sugar, Cacao, and other similar merchandise be admitted to Spanish harbors, according to the prescription of English ships that are permitted; His Majesty has ordered me to tell Your Excellency that French ships, which coming from Nantes or other French harbors toward the side of the Ocean, laden with Cacao, Sugar, and other fruits of Martinique, with a declaration that the same came from that region, in the aforementioned ships or in others, from which they transferred their cargoes in the said harbors of the Ocean, the King has resolved that the Council of the Traded shall admit them for commerce, as well as all other English and Dutch ships; as well as those which come directly with Cod or other [?] from Terraneuf to the harbors of Spain, provided that they shall have no other goods than only freight from that land in loading; the said ships, after being unloaded, without any other, but only a Quarantine of 10 days must observe, &c. Dated the red September 1721.

Signed THE MARQUIS OF GRIMALDO.

ST. SEBASTIAN, September 30. Here nothing passes except that all ships coming from Brittany, the North, or Martinique, provided they are furnished with good health certificates, are not only permitted by the old ways of the Court, but also immediately unloaded. Since some small ships with grain come from Ireland, there is an abundance of corn. Since one has not yet received Resolution on the matter of money, both with respect to the Customs and the goods mentioned here, business stands quite still; but one sees the said Resolution expected daily.

Sweden

STOCKHOLM, October 1. After the King had recovered to his health, His Majesty this past week inspected the ordinary councils of the four Swedish regiments; which were afterwards made known in their respective provinces of that name. Yesterday the Queen traveled to Stockholm, where His Majesty, his consort, is residing. The French Minister Campredon intends within 3 to 6 days to travel to His Imperial Majesty at Petersburg, in order to present there on behalf of his court a certain commission. An express messenger has arrived from the Ministries of this Crown at Neustadt. Daily one expects an express with the declared ratification; when great rejoicing will be observed. Admiral Norris, for whom a present [?] Fleet [is to return to Great Britain].

Germany & Adjacent Realms

REGENSBURG, October 8. Since the Protestant ministries maintained by their unanimous resolution that they could not commit themselves to the proposals of Rome, in their answer to the citations on the proposals of Rome, in their answer [?], they held it not advisable [?] [text heavily corrupted]. His Majesty's orders to His Royal Highness the Elector Palatine and to other Roman estates, which on account of contravention of the Treaty of Westphalia, with respect to the exercise of the Protestant [religion] at Sultzbach and others in the realm, have been brought before the Emperor, in order to settle these matters [?], namely the Counts and [?] have [?], it is said that the Protestants [?] shall thus [?] in various imperial deliberations on religion [?], is in the case of [?] Mainz there again [?], so that the Protestant corps [?] other [?] [text heavily corrupted], and those of the Chamber of Wetzlar, which still the moderations through some ministers' withdrawal, and the same point being held outside, through some ministers' withdrawal, and the same point being accorded; all the others shall equally [continue]; guarantee [?].

France

PARIS, 10 October. After the King had made new efforts through the Bishop of Fréjus to persuade him to have the Arrest Book of Aubijn registered, the current report had concluded nothing other than that he would not be able to refuse it, wherefore it was firmly held everywhere that he would accept it, in order through the use of this illustrious Benefice to obtain the honor of the King's favor; which seemed all the more likely because the answer which the same had given to His Majesty at the beginning gave such indication: nevertheless, after a couple of negotiations, said Bishop has declared himself prudently, namely, that he could in no reasonable way without burdening his Conscience accept an Office, which as heretofore would entail the same consequences, and both [?] would be willing to undertake; yet as much depends on the King's decision &c., he must finally declare that he could not accept this honor, which His Majesty has taken in a tangible manner, and it is firmly held that he will not be spoken to of it again; and the King has given him in place (as already mentioned) the first Abbacy of Saint Étienne de Laan, which fell vacant through the death of Cardinal de Mailly: this Abbacy gives at present [?] yearly 30,000 Livres in Benefits, besides the 12,000 Livres Pension, which were given to the Count of R[?] and Knight de Mailly. These Ecclesiastical Benefices [?] equally with another Pension, changing Ecclesiastical Benefits which he has, and judged this to be a heavy burden; for these reasons he declined to the King once more the Abbacy of Tours, in the Diocese of Châlons, which yearly gives 13,000 Livres in Revenue. The Abbot Feriot [?] at His Majesty, has on the same occasion of the Abbacy of Peirbon requested 4,000 Livres Abbacy of Cyt (very experienced in this Art) [?] Monthiel; the Knight of Autun. The King on the 6th of this month walked in the Gallery of the Louvre, and at the same time visited the work of Hermand, in the employ of His Majesty, who showed him a very fine work, made in a Complement of two Legions in order of Battle, with all that belongs to it, and which was told through many Figures, which move through animated Wheels, in the same Machine placed which His Majesty and the Court viewed with much pleasure. (A Duke the day before had seen the same early in the morning) His Majesty went at the same time into the Royal Workshop to see whether there was being worked on the Models of the Barracks, which the Noailles must occupy. It is said that through [?] the Redoubt of [?] has been purchased by the Lord Crozyn, for 45,000 Livres. Madame de Rebourt, [?] of the Lord Roult de Rozanne, and a different Papers, [?] the same day. The Bishop of Agen is dangerously ill. The Knight de Till, who had yearly 30,000 Livres income, has died on his estates in Normandy. The young Lady Brujns, widow of the deceased Duke of Orleans, has also died on her estates near Palaise; through the unhappy fall from her Horse, [?] the Magnificence of [?] has requested from her Husband to be separated, and has already given herself for that purpose into the Convent of the Religious [?] [?]. These days one has found in the cellar of one of the new Buildings, which the Fathers of St. Lazarus are having built, the body of one who was mistreated, together with a [?], who [?] the Cruets with Wine, with a Table, and Chairs. The Archbishop of Laon has died, whereby the Abbot St. Albin his Coadjutor takes his place, and appears thereby to be desired for the dignity of Archbishop of Paris [?]. The Abbot Brigaut, who at the time of the affair of the Princes de Cellamare was placed in the middle, is dismissed. The Abbot d'Eutangues appeared here in public, and that the greatest first Men of the Court saw that he had again bent himself in the bosom of the Government, he was punished at [?].

---

Spain

CADIZ, 23 September. For the second time, the Million [?] Prince of Suess arrived from [?], which has also brought [?] in the Peace Crisis and was not favorably [?]. Said [?] and not very clearly [?] would follow. According to the Vice-Admiral Lord van [?], and on the 13th were [?] three reflecting Dutch [?] in 2 divisions [?] the English [?] likewise [?]. Previously [?] in the Bay arrived [?]. Here have arrived the King of Carambin, Samuel de St. John Baptist, Paul [?] Royse, Gerrit Hartmans, Nicholas Colombijn from [?] Martinique, Daniel Morton from Dublin, Rudolphs of [?], John Hendyks of [?], and on the other hand since the [?] toward the Carratien, in Berg [?] Theodorus Howens toward Amsterdam, [?] toward St. Lucia, [?] Tobacco, and other [?] particularly laden, [?] sea. Their Silver and the Good [?], are at the Egge [?].

MADRID, 30 September. The Queen entered as [?] His Majesty went toward the [?] concerning their Majesty [?] Spanish and Italian [?], it is assured that [?] at the end will be made, and the young King will be [?] another of the King's children, [?].

---

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, 15 October. Of the missing Ship of Olbert de Vries, one had no news, so that it is feared that it [has been lost] through the Rovers, who in the Red Sea have succeeded greatly. Captain Pieter Nauyen ran between the 14th aforesaid at Cadiz, that he had already arrived, after 3 or 4 months by the Algerian Rovers, and [?] indeed 24 hours to know yet some of their said [?] through [?] the Northern Ship misbehaved. At Rochelle lay [?] [?] Corp. Meynkom, Klaas Schelvis, Skipper [?]. From Names were visitored the Ship under Captain Stienne Guijsarft toward Naniago, Jacob Jansje Tiebbesin toward the Olifiere, and the St. Laurens, Marren Corn toward Stockholm. At Banjomne was cleared the Ch. St. Agnes, Mrs. Adriana, Willem Willl from Zeeland to the Boetgeur St. Anna, Mrs. Drank, the Eenboirn, Laurens Jansfen, and went down the River 5 or 6 Ships, among them the Hope, Gerrit Klok, [?] and the March in Nantes the 2 Shippers, Pieter Tranquille, and the Mrs. Wilhelmia, Hendr. Willeme to Chodelle the [?], Corp. Piercefe from the Sea. In Tessel are some Ships inside, and before the City [?] Merchants from [?] &c.

THE HAGUE, 16 October. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West-Friesland have today, after holding deliberations, gathered again [?] and made Election and Conference, and in that same Trade find themselves here. Yesterday morning the Prussian Minister the Mr. [?], with the Gentlemen Deputies of the States General in Conference. It pleased indeed very much [?] and Separated trained [?] and 8 Sweden toward France delivered, is with [?] and Divided toward Cologne, there are [?] on the same were [?] [?] the Lord's [?] Lazaret sent, where [?] [?] [?] and has taken all the necessary precautions; and after the same [?] Documents had [?] (Among the same precautions) are all our [?] [?] carefully examined, and nothing found [?] and again their Question on a Place [?] [?], which ground [?] 200 guilders to the Skipper his Documents [?] handed back, and from last Sailor the same earlier Sailors [?] removed. This night an Express from [?] arrived, and [?] morning another from Germany. The Lord Bisfeld as Colonel [?] the Bond of Neuspell. According to the [?] Lord Jan Law as [?] in that City.

---

LEIDEN, Directors of the Mill-works, [?] hereby announce that they intend to sell the aforesaid extra fine well-conditioned Mill-works, being over 100 years old with many repairs made. This Mill is provided with a Dwelling-house wherein various rooms and offices, a fine Garden, and a [?] for Dwelling-house, and can be inhabited through [?] old Kneeges; [?] will see the same having other undertakings [?] Abraham van Roocyn Gildereght, dwelling in the great House.

Jac. vander Houde, Marinas Juueel, and Cornelia Verlinde Brokers, will sell this evening at 8 o'clock precisely, in the Wine Hall, in the Heiberg the Black Lion, etc. [?] Virginia Blade Bay Tobacco, of the Crop of 1720, and that at Auction [?] Balthasar van de Stricven Ficketon, Docker.

Jacob vander Houde, Marinas Juueel, and Cornelia Verlinde Brokers, will sell this evening at 8 o'clock precisely, in the Wine Hall, in the Herberg the Black Lion, e[?] Blade Bay and [?] Tobacco, of the Crop of 1719 and 1720, and that at Auction [?] in the Warehouse of Mr. Alexander Casthaer.

Jacob vander Houde, Marinas Juueel, and Kornelis Verlinde Brokers, will within Rotterdam, in the Herberg the Black Lion, sell against [?] a Ship-loading [?] Tobacco of the Crop 1720, that a [?] to be become, [?] extra [?] Hagen, as the Buyer [?] when they lie in the Boom-grass in the Warehouse. Balthasar [?] Hoekvorke, at Amsterdam on the [?] of the Fleur over [?]

Also in the Bloem-street near Gouda, in the Winderdyk, and some Pales and Planks [?], one is to understand, to the Booth there promised that he for his bringing [?]

Printed at LEIDEN by F. I. LY DE KLOPPER, over the Town Hall. 17 October [1721. With Privilege.]

Course of Exchange

The Course of EXCHANGE is today at Madrid 94, Cadiz 118 and one quarter. Seville 117 and one quarter. Bilbao 93 and 7 parts. Lisbon 44. [?] 53 and 5 parts. Livorno 93 and 7 quarters. Genoa 3 quarters [?] 48. Paris slight 42 and 1 part. London 14 Sch. 1 and 3 half or [?]. [?] 293 parts gt. Biedau 39 and 0 [?]. St. Hamburg 92 and 0 [?]. St. Amsterdam 2 quarters per Cent. Gent 0 quarters. Brussels 2 and 1 half [?] quarter. Rotterdam quarters. The Action of the Bank [?] and 3 parts. The English O.I.C. 127. The Smith 126, and Z.Z. 95.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 17th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.
• • •
No. 1721.No. 126.
— ☥ —

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 20th of October, 1721
Spain

CADIX, 23 September. After the arrival of the Florida Fleet, which has brought in Gold, Silver, Jewels, &c., totaling 14 Million Pesos, this has been occasioned by the Treasury that the Prince of Guinca brought for the King and private individuals from there; a portion of our Navigators' gains remains at Vera Cruz because the market at Jalappa has not been favorable, and many goods have not been sold. A drought occurred here on 8 May in Vera Cruz which caused ruin; and it is said that afterward an Advise from there would follow hither. According to reports from Gibraltar, the Dutch Vice-Admiral Hoet sailed several ships from that Bay, and the 11th week went to Sea: the following day the three departing Dutch Warships arrived there together; Holland in a commission in the Name of the State. On the same day the English Ambassador Stuart with the captured Slaves from Tetuan arrived there. It is reported that the Silver came in with the last Fleet in the bay; and the Crew will be transferred.

There have arrived the ships of Arent, Steven Velde, and Raymond King from Comeragh; Samuel Anderson and Timothy Jaffon from Ireland, the St. Jean Baudelet, Paul Faulkilde from Hamburg; Ant. Moyer, Gerraid Auys, Gerrit Haiseer, and Wilt. Groten from Amsterdam; the St. Mertyn, Nicolaas Colombya from Norway; the Fame, Nicol. Dranth from Mysticco; Daniel Norton from Codt; the Mault, and Theed. Makkirick from Dublin; Richard Norris from Waterford; Jacob Kradok from Exon; John Hendiks from Plymouth; Conn. Valdeniet from Norway; the following ships John Haye and John Camie toward the Comeragh; Elias Bergeen toward Madeira; John Evans and Lazarus Hofman toward Lisbon; Salvador Carton toward Havana; the Hectop, Theodora Hueten toward Amsterdam; and the merchant ship Joofte Hoop toward St. Lucar.

The Silver, 200 coined as well as uncoined, along with Tobacco and other Goods landed in the harbor, belonging to the King and private individuals, amount to about one Million and 20,000 Pesos. The Silver and Goods which arrived with the last Ship from the South have been directed to the Warehousemen.

There have arrived carrying 560 Pesos; 5,916 Mark weight Silver, amounting to 79,188 Pesos; 240 Cans weight Silver and Gold, amounting to 40,000 Pesos; 512 Cires of Cochineal, weighing 40,000 pounds; 1,843 Cires or Cans of Indigo, being 200,000 p.; 130 Cans of Vanilla, weighing 73,000 p.; 1,494 Bales of Jalap, weighing 12,300 p.; 318 Cans and 150 Bales of Indigo Drugs; 47 Cans of Porcelain; 10 Bales and Cans of Contra Yerva; 7 Cires of Sarsaparilla; 123 Cires and Bales of Sarsaparilla; 11 Cans of Brazilwood; 46 Cans of Chocolate; 20 Chests of Sugar; 46 Cans and 180 pounds of Oaxaca Powder; 94 Cans with Feathers; 15 Bales and 18 Pots with Balm; 346 Pots and 2 Cans with Mary Oil; 6,711 dressed Hides; 160 ditto with Hair; 1 Can of Mother-of-Pearl; 31 Cans and Stores of Liquidambar; 3 Terlios; 2 Cires and one sack of Cacao; 89 Copas of Cavanae; one barrel of Tamarinds; 24 Pots of Turpentine; 24 Cires and one Bale of Silver Cochineal; 230 Bales of Seville Indigo; 2 Lumps of Tecale; 8 Terlios of Thazoile; 4 Cans of Savonette; 2 ditto with Indian Earthenware; one Case of Paintings; 28 Arobes of Brazilwood; one and 1 half Arobes of Achiote. Apart from what has already been mentioned as having come for His Majesty: 619,921 Pesos, 200 in coined and worked Silver and Gold; 572 Cires of Indigo, with 4,407 Arobes in 12 pounds, one Ounce, and 7 grains of Pearls; 714 Quintals of Copper; 600 pieces of Cedar wood; and 2,205 Quintals of Logwood. In Havana there remained moreover for the King 270,000 Pesos, together with a great quantity of Tobacco and other Fruits from that Land.

MADRID, 30 September. It is the Count of Olfun who has been appointed by the Queen to go as Extraordinary Ambassador to the Regency; to undertake the Command of the Infantry of Spain. With the King the Dispatches for this purpose are being prepared; he shall also take among other things three Letters to the young King, namely one from Alfonso [Editor's note: Alfonso, Duke of Anjou, grandson of Louis XIV who had recently become King Philip V of Spain]; the aforementioned Infante, whom one now calls Queen of France, has over the Royal Marriage been complimented by all the Grandes of this Court. The King has granted the aforementioned Count, with approval, an allowance of 5,000 Pistoles, and 1,000 Pistoles for Equipment; to which allowance one adds such supplements that it is worth noting that within his Lady's retinue, more particularly on the decree issued, he has been given a guide in the person of the Count of Medina Sidonia, who shall then return hither: he comes to the Italian Court with much splendor and pride in the continuation, having already sent considerable Remittances to Paris to there obtain the portrait of the Queen of France. The portrait of the Queen of France has been obtained. The Court itself, managed by Monsieur Blanchini, and there one piece of Alfonso in the seal, set with Diamonds, and at 1,500 Pistoles valued by the Knight Pigitano; one reports of the aforementioned Count of Orleans, having also been purchased for his use, whose fortune having already been found, that within an Expedition of his Lady, more particularly—by the Knight Pigitano; one reports of the aforementioned Count of Orleans, having also purchased for his use, whose fortune had then already been found, that within an Expedition of his Lady—one roman has been circulated, to be given to the Spanish Cardinal, couriers and messengers, and to the German Spanish Cardinal.

The Infante has already found that over public Joy is besieged by the Ballet of the French Ambassador, the Chevalier de Beauvillier, Knight of the Golden Fleece, and others; the Queen has presented him with it, with great pride certifying to him. The Queen presented him from the Head of the King's well to Lisbon perp[etually]—[?] as he returned himself in France for divine matters, the sick one more particularly in France and divine matters, is now by order toward the sick one—and the divine has been—. As he returned himself in France, it has shown against it in France, and made daily the Head retained on the Wellbeing, by which occasion it—and by which occasion it may be—the Infante and France is granted, to through Lisbon perp[etually]—since that this has led well under Catalonia and Portugal, the Knight Le East one torch and divine has been—. Don Ferdinand Chacart and Wellington, guided by the Knight Pigitano, and shall always have served in S.H. Highness, the Don Millions De Fortu in Silver, a Camel, and a Cow, be Good, 2,229 Guilders and Beaulijo.

Portugal

LISBON, 23 September. There have arrived the Ships of Jan Rietke, Pieter Valk, Dirk Goudsichalk, Jan Calmer, and Klaas Viles from Amsterdam; Hans Jac. Luwvig and Clemens Flikquier from Hamburg, the Ship the Benjamin from Rotterdam, and 3 other Ships from London. The Ships have arrived here at Port à Port and at Viana; and the East-Indies [ship] that has brought them, [?] for this River on the Algiers Covers.

France

PARIS, 13 October. Yesterday afternoon when the King held Dinner before His Majesty, a lovely grand Musical Concert was performed by many Voices and Instruments. The Princes and the Dukes have made known to the Duke Regent that it would not have been disagreeable to them had His Highness the King not chosen someone of greater quality than the Marquis de la Farre. It is said that the Knight d'Orleans, natural Son of the Duke Regent, had felt this keenly, that this Commission was entrusted to him, and that thereby he might possibly have obtained access to greater honor through the opening. The departure of the Duke of St. Simon has been postponed; another day shall be appointed for it; but one remains even in the agreement, which the Princes shall undertake for the coming Winter.

It is again reported that the King will come of age in the coming Parliament [Editor's note: Lit de Justice], that St. Mattio will again yield, and this being done, the Duke Regent might renounce the Lieutenant-Generalship of the Kingdom. The Cardinal du Bois and the Prince of Conti have some difference regarding the Ceremony that the former has not observed. The Duke Regent has relinquished his intendancy of Finances; His Highness the Cardinal du Bois has been honored with the same, whose income amounts to about 30,000 Livres annually; the lease of the Cardinal is not [?].

The Bishop of Frejus, who is the King's confessor, has not relinquished his Abbey of Tonmene to His Majesty, but he shall use the income thereof to establish Schools within the Land, from which other relief shall be given. The Bishops of Agen, of Tool, and of Langres are dangerously ill. The Body of the deceased Archbishop of Rouen is here buried and shall be interred at St. Cosme; this Prelate was in great esteem at Court, and had he remained living, he would well have come to considerable honor; the Abbey of la Gradey, vacant through the death of this Archbishop, may come to the second; Son of the Mealtime of that Name, given to the Abbot de Besons, Son of the Mealtime of that Name, and Nephew of the Deceased.

The Bishop of Nantes has received leave from His Majesty to manage household matters, which the Archbishop of Rouen had; since this Bishop the first Assistant-[?] of the H.w as well as Chaplain and in household-Ven. R. appointment made to 59,000 Livres household-[?]. He is busy selling the Moveable Goods which the great-Duchess of Tuscany has left; these are all fine—still magnificent; the great Duchess Agent, having opposed this, continues to assist thereat. Yesterday there was against the Venetian [?] a Cardinal employed, arranged in a new directorship, touching on the King's [?] how the Son of the Clouds and Mists, touching on the King's necessity. Today Evening the Opera of Phaethon, which great pains had been taken to rehearse until the half-King, over the Company of Musketeers all the Officers present with Debt-papers blank, which a dead person will wholly undertake to the operation of today of the Fithel. One shall undertake to the operation of today of the Fithel.

[Note: The final sections of the France entry and the conclusion are severely corrupted in the transcription and cannot be reliably rendered into coherent English.]

Left Column

[Note: This section of the transcription is severely corrupted and largely illegible in the original. The text appears to concern plague reports (the word "Ziekie" appears multiple times) related to French cities including Avignon, Orange, Valence, Vienne, and Mendes, with references to preventative measures and the involvement of bishops and nobility. However, the corruption is too extensive to provide a reliable translation. Key fragments suggest reports of plague spreading to Toulouse and measures being taken by local authorities, but the specific details cannot be accurately rendered.]

The Netherlands

DORDRECHT, 19 October. The following ships have arrived at this city: the Owners Goodwill, John Booth of Newcastle; the James and Elisabeth, Joseph Davidtson of Sunderland; the Thomas and Catharina, William [?]; the Chreds, Jan Schuyen of Ostend; and the following are departing: the Wigth, Guy Buret, the William, Stephen Dixburg's John and Thomas, John Alcuby, the Anna and Sara, Georg Jogling, the Suply, Christopher [?], the Mary Ann, George Sparrow, the Mary Gould, Thomas Warren—all bound for England; and Michael Lugten for Newport.

AMSTERDAM, 19 October. Arrived at Malaga: Juraan [?] Merchant; on the River of Bordeaux: Pieter Christheaarie and [?] Lolling, all three of this city; also at Bordeaux: [?] Espard, Kuyck, Ficers Tyewood from St. Domingue. The latter vessel was robbed on 21 August by a pirate of part of its cargo and ship's equipment near Cape St. Domingue, departing per express with the ship Le Toulon d'Or, Jonien le Voye, and others. [?] is outside the East Indies route of Cape [?], and two other ships.

Bank Agio: 5 and 5/8 East India Company: 970 West India Company: 100 [?]: 545 [?]: 50 English East India Company: 175 to 2 Bank: 126 and [?] to 2

THE HAGUE, 19 October. The Lords States of Holland were adjourned yesterday until next Thursday. His Excellency the Baron Spöcke has presented his credentials and instructions from the Government of Great Britain to the Lord of Lïcen, president of the Assembly of the Lords States General, in the capacity of Elector of Brunswick-Lüneburg, in which capacity His Highness is acknowledged; this Minister has been received by the Foreign Ministers and other notables. The Lords Commissioners of the Levantine Trade have departed for home. Herr Mynderhuysen, Minister of the King of Prussia, is being escorted by certain Lords of the Government, as is General Isrompolski, Minister of Great Britain, to take leave before shortly departing for the Court of the King of Prussia. It is said that the Great Britain Minister Godorp and [?] have been sent to Vienna. At the beginning of the coming month, the Palatine Minister, Baron Ulmer, [?] will [?]. Jan van Dunesvelt, Hendrik Verheik, and Jan Feercboom, Directors of the [?], are well-conditioned [?] for more than 100 years with great success. This mill is [?] a large dwelling house, as the mill-keeper or [?] can be housed therein; anyone having business therein, or under the management of Abraham and Boxem, [servants/?], residing in the great house. The estate of Samuel de Wit will be offered for public sale and auction on Monday, 27 October at Bruges, [a quantity of grain?] [...] and place [...], for the morning at 9 o'clock. [...]

Joh. Stavis, Jan Lemmers, and Hendr. Linquet, brokers, will at Amsterdam on the eleventh, in the Brakke Grond [...], white pepper- and tobacco-mats, a quantity of hides, and individual pelts. Property of Doyft, Jan van Raden, and Jan Wittigh, brokers, will at Amsterdam [?] party [?] in sorts. Jan [?] Steen and Harmanus Hoftcherveld, brokers, will at Amsterdam [?] corn and gardener's house, orchard, alleys, [?] hedges, vegetable garden, fields, over the Utrecht [...], with further information to be obtained from the aforesaid brokers. Philip Steer, auctioneer [?], will sell at Amsterdam on Tuesday, 28 October, 4 ells of cushion-chiffons, white goods; [?] and [?] chiffons, Japanese, by N. ten Hoorn, bookdealer opposite the Old Gentlemen's Lodging. In the lodgings of Manueldoon of the Maccabees, [?] will be raised in Rotterdam. The [...] over Court-farewell: [?] makes it clear that the Lutheran spirit rejoiced [...] In the [...] [...] this [...] to lease. Announcement of the Union of the Lutheran and Reformed [churches] at Amsterdam on the Dam at the bookseller J. Oosterwijck, with privileges of Hanning Josephus Judaic Histories, together with [histories] of Jerusalem, etc., brought into Dutch by W. Jowel, and offered for sale there and in the capital cities in Poland, on fine laid paper, [...] and from the printing houses [?] for the [...] in certain [...]

MALAGA, 30 September. [Text severely corrupted—appears to concern cavalry/military movements and magazines of provisions, with references to provincial orders from Germany.]

VIENNA, 2 October. Count von Weis to the Emperor [?] from the Flowers of Ungari, Württemberg, Ansbach, Bavaria [?]. [Text severely corrupted—appears to concern ministerial orders and execution of imperial decrees regarding coercion matters.]

LONDON, 13 October. Since my last [?] the following have occurred: [?] of the South Sea Company, its previous direction toward news; [?] St. Abr. and half, [?]. Genoa laid [?]; the Lords Jacobi, Gore, [?], Morley, [?], Raymond, and Gunna. Sunday morning at one o'clock, in the night, burnt to the ground; a woman of the Lady Essex [?] and a thousand [?] in the garden, as well as a [?] woman, who have broken [?]. This [?] is around Graveland at the port steered, and at Menkhuizen, which the resolution [?]. Yesterday one began to draw the Lottery of State; the same [?] on [?]. The Agio of the East India Company: 130; Bank: 125 and 1/2; South Sea: 0 and 1/2; Africa Company: 33; Bonds: 135; London: 131.

REGENSBURG, 13 October. [Palatine Minister at the Imperial Diet of the Reichstag?] [Text severely corrupted—appears to concern orders regarding Baden [?], places and ordinances against [?].]

DRESDEN, 14 October. It is learned that [...] the time [?], and [?] [?] paper, showing [...] there are some on royal paper [...] privilege granted.

LIX DE KLAA, on the Breedestraat 20 October 1721. With Privilege.

Course of Exchange

**Exchange Rates of the BOURSE:** Madrid: 94 and 1/4 to 1/2 Cadiz: 112 and 1/4 to 1/2 Seville: 117 and 1/4 Bilbao: 92 Lisbon: 45 Veracruz: 83 and 5/8 Leghorn: 93 and 7/8 Genoa: 94 and 7/8 Geneva: 89 and 4/8 Parma: [?] London: 3 Scudi 11 and 1/2 Frankfurt current: 129 Reichsthalers Danzig: 292 Polish [currency] Luisburg St. Breslau: 35 and 5/8 St. Hamburg: 12 and 9/16 to 5/8 Antwerp: 25 and 5/8 Scudi Ghent: 2 and 1/8 Brussels: 2 and 1/8 Zeeland: 5 [?] Rouen: 1 [?] [Editor's note: Large portions of this issue are severely corrupted in the original transcription, making reliable translation impossible for approximately 30% of the text. The legible portions report on ships arriving in Dutch ports, exchange rates on major European trading centers, diplomatic movements at The Hague concerning British and Prussian ministers, real estate auctions in Amsterdam, plague news from France, and London financial market reports including stock prices for the East India and South Sea Companies.]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 20th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 22nd of October, 1721

Monday, 12 September. By letters from Ceuta we learn that the Danes have completely withdrawn from the vicinity of the place; that the troops and garrisons which the [?] were obliged to leave there, and His Majesty has approved that the garrison in the future shall have all necessary provision for foot soldiers and mounted cavalry; and that the magazines of the place shall be supplied with provisions and war supplies for 6 months.

VIENNA, 8 October. We learn that the report which the Count of [Weick?] made to the Emperor concerning certain ships, which have caused problems at the aforementioned places, is now viewed in a better light by His Imperial Majesty and is being favorably received by all ministers; wherefore it is hoped that orders will be issued and that they have been dispatched with assurance of his support. The Imperial Minister has urged the Emperor regarding these matters, who had sent word that everything within a short time would be arranged further by the Protestants. The commission has made [?] to the Protestants. Herr de St. Sophonie, Minister of the King, was in the Diet at Brussels and has informed the Emperor of one of the peace negotiations between the Count and the King [?]. It is believed that the case of the Resident of Prussia in this matter was not otherwise affected by the connection of the band of [?] of the Count of [Schorbon?]. [Several lines heavily corrupted in original]. The Emperor is satisfied here with the conduct of the Count of [Wall?]. The Emperor has asked for assistance for the Prussian forces, to which he is devoted, and the Emperor has, in order that those who might help; and where the situation would allow him to send everything that they have sent him, an old man [?]; sent to the Imperial [?] of the Palatinate to encourage him to the speedy dispatch of the forces of the Protestants and [Suarces?].

REGENSBURG, 11 October. On Friday last the Elector's minister announced to the Protestants and members of his court that the Elector's [?] has been concluded.

ENGLAND, 14 October. Letters are seen here from Vienna regarding a private Diet in Hungary and the Emperor's intention [?].

---

Wednesday's Courant.

HANOVER, 17 October. According to the latest letters from Stockholm of the 3rd instant, the French Minister, Monsieur Campredon [Editor's note: French diplomat], departed to go to His Majesty in Petersburg, and various respects were discussed regarding this journey, but the general opinion was that he would have some business with His Czarist Majesty on the orders of his court, concerning certain matters in commission. News has been received that the Swedish General Count Flemming is ill.

SWEDEN.

STOCKHOLM, 4 October. News has arrived here from Petersburg concerning great festivities celebrated on the favorable occasion; it is also said that His Czarist Majesty received a minister from the Great Khan of China, who would bring particular news of his realm. The Swedish Minister, the Count of [?], who is staying in [Bidoslyk?], has been recalled by order of this court. But regarding that Congress on which much has been written and testified, it will not come to negotiation. A very sharp proclamation has been issued against the prohibition of religion and the Mediterranean Sea, as well as against the obstruction of commerce there; all others except those of some licenses, money through the lease of [Lefse?].

FRANCE.

PARIS, 17 October. The King has given to Monsieur de Torcy a pension of 15,000 livres on the income from the intendancy of Poitou [Editor's note: French province], which has been assigned to the absent ministers and given to Cardinal Bois. The accusation against the Bishop of Meaux from last time consists in that one ascribes to him the origin that the fair came to this city, because he had allowed the holding of the fair at the market, which brought great advantage to this [?]. Monsieur de Breteuil, Intendant of Limoges, who some time ago became Prévôt and Master of Ceremonies of the King's orders, resigned to the Children [Poliores?]. The Abbé de Lort, who was previously almoner to Cardinal de Noailles, died at Aurillac; through this death the Abbey of la Valletta in the Diocese of Tulle, which brings in 3,000 livres yearly, has fallen vacant. Cardinal de Bissi departed as the first of the nearest hand from Rome; an expedition had been sent to His Eminence to propose that he take the Abbey of St. Germain, which brings in 12,000 livres yearly, in exchange for the advantages of the Abbot of St. Albin, on condition that he be given the Archbishopric of Reims instead, which is worth only 10,000 livres yearly; but the said Cardinal refused this offer and remains with his abbey and its bishopric of Meaux. Messieurs d'Irie and Ferrier are working with the emissary of the Czar of Great Russia on the making of a new map of the coasts of the Caspian Sea, on the foundation of the plans that have been brought here, which these gentlemen are correcting with the old maps and all the authors who have written about it. Several days ago the body of a male person was found behind the Charterhouse monastery that had been buried there, and so dreadfully decomposed that the noble features, as well as the face, were such that he could not be recognized; [?] there was a will and letter found there and it appears that he deserved punishment, and that [?]. It is stated firmly that this was done by the notorious [Canouche?], who had begun to work against him. The Countess of [Etienne?] presented on Wednesday last to Mr. Gaulier Killier, general of the [?], a letter of credit for 1,700 écus signed by four general farmers, but the payment was refused by Killier because the letter was not drawn up in the proper legal form and was suspected; [several lines heavily corrupted]; and its [?] has Melquton de Piennec found these difficulties and refusal necessary to tear up the letter. Monsieur de Renol has obtained from the Duke of Orleans the small Hotel of Mantus to dwell in; this Hotel was previously part of the collections of the Royal Library. On the discovery a mine of azure earth has been found, in which [?] deeper gold veins are thought to have been encountered. The preparations are being made for the three funeral processions of the daughters of the Duchy of Orleans, which have not yet taken place, this ceremony being to be held in a few days, and it is certain that they would be led by the King and the Dowager Duchess of Orleans under the authority of [Montdidier?]. [Several lines heavily corrupted in original]. On life there was [?]. On the 4th of this month, the weather being pleasant, it rained for several days until evening around 5 o'clock; meanwhile the rumor continued of the [?]; which shall give no advantage, and that some other [?] comes in its place. The 4th [?] the notaries [?].

ARLES, 17 October. On Wednesday work was begun on a great library in alphabetical order according to the nature [?].

Left Column

### FRANCE

The King further added that, in case that Minister should be dismissed from office, his wife shall retain 3,000 livres, and his son the Marquis de Croifty 6,000 livres. The death of the Sculptor of Paris is not confirmed, but one continues to assure oneself of that of a certain Richeton of Mandos. Last Tuesday morning a detachment of 30 men from the French Guard of the Bed conducted memoirs on Carlouchè, whose name is well known through the infinite crimes he has committed; alongside him four of his companions were also captured, among them the man with whom he lodged, a certain Wolfe in the Fauxbourg de la Couraille; after all five of them had been firmly bound, they were led before the Tiger in Blanc Manleu of Onlion, who had promised 1,004 livres to anyone who could capture him; from there they were taken to the prison of the Châtelet, accompanied by a very great multitude of people. One has learned various things about him during this time in England and in Holland; that he and his companions on a certain day bound one of their band members, cut up the body and divided it into four pieces; and when one heard that he had betrayed her, which corpse was recently found in the street that looks out onto the Fauxbourg St. Germain. The Notaries have wanted to work on the Extract which was required of them; in which their salaries were not paid. As this was done, for those of Turry to Stuyvets per each payment by the King; 20 Stuyvets per each Extract for the Particuliers, 10 Stuyvets for each [?] to the Notaries of the other cities, and Douvlets of the King's [?], and 5 [?] to the Notaries in the Villages. Between the 12th and 13th, the Baron de Badxus married the Countess V: de Granljon. The Prince of Sublach, son of the Count V: of Sulphurtt married Mademoiselle d'Auvergne d'Aguilane in Bergen op Zoom. The Chamberlain of the reigning Queen of Sweden, having taken the title of Duke of Orleans in Italy, arrived at Civitavecchia. The [?] Ladies, who for 2 years has been qualified as [?] Employed of the King of Spain to this Lord, has accepted the position of Secretary of the Admiralty. The Lord [?] gives to the Lord Arthur 300 thousand ecus, from Piedmont in the Parliament of Toulouse, and the King grants a Brevet of science of 25 thousand ecus to the Lord de Manditon on these charges. The Cardinal du Bois, and other Prelates, held a service yesterday in the Compol Service, which was done in the Church of the Josephine Maguillmken in the Fauxbourg St. Germain, for the repose of the soul of the deceased Archbishop of Rouen, who by his Testament left his Archive [?] to his Brother the Marshal of Merena, and the rest of the [?], the Blanc, Minister of War, his son-in-law; he has also arranged his Domestics in God. Since Carlouchè was captured there have been many other arrests made in the Bed, and one says his Accomplices were among them.

---

Right Column

### GREAT BRITAIN

LONDON, 17 October. After various deliberations have been held on the state of the afflicted Sick which France governed, there was expedited a Proclamation published ordering that all ships coming from any Ports from Dunkirk to Biscay, without Certificates of health, shall be admitted in any Ports of Great Britain: or the Britons who on Guernsey, Jersey, Alderney, Sark or Man being provided with said Certificates shall hand them over to the Officers who are stationed to oversee the performance of Quarantine. In particular it appears that a considerable quantity of Grain coming from ships that have arrived from Turkey has been lost, on which account the Admiralty has sent some Petitions to Deal to inform themselves of this. The Brig which with the Valleren at [?] were by reason of picquet [?] and sealed in the Poll-Comptoir are issued: also many other particulars are set to work here and well despatched; the Streets in the liberty of Westminster and always Finance are held. One has news from Wimple in Cam[?]

The Wine Brokers, etc., at Dordrecht, will on Friday the 24 October of this New Year sell a party of about 75 [?] of Brandywines located in the Vat-houses on the Lime Quay, which on the day of Gommerin of Dordrecht, Jan van Baileo, and Jan Wittigh Broker, shall be [?] the days at auctions, on the Haarlemmersdyk in the Old Lord's Barracks, and the healthy Mercovile Hennie, and a party of Flax in [?]

The U.E. municipalities of Samuel de Wit present on Monday the 27 October around the Koopbrug, publicly to sell the quantity [?], for the morning at 9 o'clock, and the afternoon.

J. P. Zomer Broker will at Amsterdam sell on Tuesday the 28 October [?] days at Westhouse, at the Houses which lie, a party of outstanding Far[?] Works, and a party of outstanding Gold-Plates, where very little or nothing [?] can be by J. P. Zomer and Cybert van Hol; all to be seen the day before. The [?] will be on the 30 November to come each, and according to previous days the Library of all kinds of outstanding Books in various Languages, namely the Thesauri Romanorum & Graecorum of Groxi and Gronovij, complete in 24 [?]. A very [...] German History; all left by the High [...] of Friesland, etc.; of which the Catalogues (along with 2 auctioneer[s], which 's week will be by D. Houtwerf at Dordrecht, Utrecht, de Waard, Leiden Luchtmans Dordt [...], Delft [...], [?], Haarlem van Lre, Gouda van der Kleet, Groningen van H gels and the further Towns.

### ENGLAND

PETERSBURG, 30 September born is of a very red [?] given. The Russian Servant Man, and another C.Z. Maji, more 200 the[?] Snow to go toward Moscow, and

BILBAO, 3 October. That the Spanish Ship of Capt. Francen sailed here, was there born Jan Morales, and one in New England has gone his way. Wil. Merch. All other joyfully in reg' growing sailed, have us gendarmes. Thaya boaera. [?] in St. Se-Jelis-Sickness in France further Kingdom coming, loaded. The other Ships, guarded more

ST. SEBASTIAN, 3 October praise of all. To remark [?] fair Baet; with sweet Hand and others is set in a [?] Jell-Sickness in France further Kingdom coming, loaded the three third Ships the of joyfully in reg' growing sailed, have us gendarmes

### ITALY

ROME, 4 October. Nicolaes Spinola di Alessandro Alb laske [?] Church. That afternoon has received [?] of the Nunciature public Audience had with the general Treyn and Grajeo of [?] gotten was the crowd of V Hood in 13 Kalcheo, where from are the [?], as was [...] the Gold Giolinneche between the Green Danaele fall, which on the [?] where the ambulants were driven not possible to be Beschers to take [...] Brecca Stheillebaros [?] cloth. And a certain Mederge deep to Rome Treyn and Gewwig, verseid out i Fytero uno issiesen, and Ncacele Grania with the Cardinals Tamani Kabeü S. Augustine at Rome Artillery toward Calamudi, in there he here the more t wet oe Soa which occurs the Constelladt Cod this to Recomrata well show.

The Lord Conti goes also [?] Zal will come out to walk on Lords [?] see.

tomorrow the Cardinal of Ravei which here Lords [?] Haus Jm of [...] Angel Michiaus, and Mujp. Wednesday morning shall in the Church of St. Peter's, in Heot the Benedictin of Voorasl in dors [?]

### THE NETHERLANDS

BRUSSELS, 24 October. The Marquis de Prié was daily by letter which was in't public; his Excellency has had some Compliments with the Residents of Bogeland and Holland. Further, on these some of the evening of the Pritt Ergenis, speaking of said Marquis de Compliments, and entertain[?] of the evening many notable Persons to his Majesty; that evening was on the great Theatre nearby Opera plotted, which was the prize of Prince Eugene recompensed was i and was in the Marquis de Prié, by very [?] Violinists, and the [?] themselves relieve; di evening great many other shows to D'Hmle. Friday in the early Gathering of the States of Brabant achieved, after [?] to incline in the Godeliness of the ordinary Impositions; but they are over come in't achieving of an Outlegger for here Quadx Quer of Antwerp, and [?] has no spiritual like [?] had thereof. Man "Waleton his Chisulde Alhieren been until the ability of the general Gathering of that Province to be the[?] over one third of the foresaid would[?], and also to the difference to remove which among the Members are.

AMSTERDAM, 20 October. According to the Letters from Madrid the Letters from Schangerri, which in Genua have come, it's permitted they pen to in the Spanish Ports see. In Tedel in various Ships in the Ruslande, and ablies for the city some Ships out of the small Cock.

mende, or out of Spain toward Flanders passage; self have varied Billion were hunted to he Bodets that way willing come, together

### ROME

Nicolas Spinola and Alexander Alb lafte Churches. That afternoon has he Ambulance had with the ge Treyn and Grajeo of Enst gotten has was the crowd of V Hood in 13 Kailcheo, where of are the hedingeo, as was s Golden Giolinneche between the Green Danaacle fall, who out the [?] on that the ambulants been has not possible to ren bschers to take [...] Brecca Stheilleibaros [...] cloth. The[?]presient who not to be here was, has this open practical. The Fugacelia, died soon Albani, saw The Tinya patolaets which on't foot is in the Church wept with various [?]sionen From Asnti as adhering ten

And a certain Mederge deep to Rome Treyn and Geswig, verseid out i Fytero uno issiesen, and Ncacele Grania with the Cardinals Tamani Kabeü S. Augustine at Rome Artillery toward Calamudi, in there him here the more t wet oe Soa which occasion je Constelladt Cod this to Recomrata well show.

The Lord Conti goes also [?] Zal will come out to walk on Lords [?] see.

tomorrow had the Cardinal of Raven which here Lords [?] Haus Jm of [...] Angel Michiaus, and Mujp. Wednesday morning shall in the Church of St. Peter's, in Heot the Benedictin of Voorasl in dors [?]

Course of Exchange

The Course of the EXCHANGE is today on Madrid 9s, and 's fourth. Kadir a[?] and today. Ondaon n°17 and 't half Billion og get it halt. Lisbon as s and 7 argils. Veuevico 18 and s fourth 14. Livorno 94. Genus

Notices & Advertisements

On Monday the 17 November 1722 and following days, Joh. Swart will sell a very beautiful library, consisting of many kinds of Faculties and a very considerable Cabinet of very beautiful and rare Horns, Shells, [?] of the Books to be seen at the House of said Job. Swart in [?] to be obtained there.

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 24th of October, 1721
England

PETERSBURG, 30 September. It now appears finally that since the conclusion of the Peace, 200 [?] with Gasmaalen, as well as other peace negotiations between Russia and [?]. The Russian [?] of the concluded Peace through his Czarial Majesty with an Expedition [?] to reconcile. Made [?] between Moscow and the Sweth [?]; with the wardrobe [?], which some works [?].

Spain

BILBAO, 3 October. The first [?] from here to Sea, Spain's Ship of Francisco Arostegui [?]. Currently sailing here are [?] Fromat and Duke Rydto toward Antigua at St. Juan Bautista, Juan Morales, and 2 English toward London. From Bolkton in New England [?] instructed with the King [?]. Capt. Will. Mercer. All our Great Merchants have arrived safely and fortunately; however, those in the Strait David have been saved, which both in the customary [?]. The ships of the Sr. Juan de Lous d'Hurs, are St. Sebastian [?], as in the Fallage to Extremo-Stoeten.

ST. SEBASTIAN, 7 October. Great expectation is held for the Resolution of his Majesty. Regarding the Adriames, the Commerce [?] was noted in March. But matters went well with the English, upon the Madrid [?] when the Council of [?] was there for this correction. Through the Pedi-Sickness in France [?], it comes out that Bottegue [?]; one has appointed the Prelacies [?] of these States and Pamplona toward Iron, to with all these [?], and likewise this Place to Lima [?] with the great [?] and Goodena. The ships of [?] coming from [?], or from Spain going toward Westfalia, at August thousand [?] have been lightened [?]. They went to Bilbao to use the Baslee and Watertte in Gumbe [?], wishing to come hereward, had to turn back.

Rome

ROME, 4 October. Before Sunday the Cardinals Nicolaas Spinola and Alexander Albani took possession of their Titular Churches. The aforementioned Gentlemen, the Count Don Alexander de Ceilla, Ambassador of the King of Portugal, at the Faustyn [?] had a public Audience with the greatest Ceremonial; and descended the Throne in the Assembly of a very splendid [?], as one soon could have been the theologian of the People unbelievably great; the same [?] in the Circle, of which the Count Faugtis servants much [?], all adorned with Red Scarlet Bokker and Good Galloons; that: between Green Velvet, and among them the Great Drama [?], which themselves a Gold [?] have the carriages wherein the Ambassador sat, was so richly and beautifully adorned, that it was impossible to describe it; around and upon it were 8 Paddie; a Heraldic, and likewise all complete practical [?]. The President who with his Germain expressly from Albaro came, has [?] over [?] Juirerde sat at Albaro Recd. The Princely, Mtord [?] of the Pope, and Doon-Felicita Marsali, begin the Gentlemen [?] at the beginning of the Cardinal. Patet which on the [?] of the Cardinal d'Augustal, called Fincelisdie [?] with various Jewels, which one values at 3000 Red Rocda. The Ambassador begat to the other side the Cardinals Conti, and Spinola Secretary of State; was with him that Crevdg, [?], and most Fincelidore and Nobility. The [?] in St. Fitter went [?], and began the Servants of the Sardegne, with the Cardinal Imsuvia, Dean of the same. Monday the Auto-Chapel Michael began the [?] of St. Angelo with great Pomp, [?] Vandels, and a Third [?] toward Quinzaad, where the same served of the Gentlemen Conti [?], the Priest of the Cardinal d'Arrugua, Faresia, and a great [?] the chair of St. Michael is Ripa [?]; in the [?] of May, two Concre of the Hand [?], which by obligation that of the Secretary of State [?]. With obligate letter [?] that alone [?] the [?] began and the Gatier [?]. How many such jewels were [?] divided. [?] before Juece [?] became [?]. On the following Morning the Cardinal of Rohan had an Audience [?] great [?]. All Gentlemen over an Audience [?]. Likewise on the following Morning the Cardinal of Rohan had an Audience [?] great [?]. Also there were no permutation [?] at a Cardinal [?] in the Middle Spain, in the great Hall of the Court, the Court. Drinking, and many great Works; behaving [?], in the great Hall of the Court; which Cabinet 2 days before the Entertainment the Cardinals [?] the city [?] became.

Venice

VENICE, 12 October. Tuesday the Doge with the customary Accompaniment; the Canal of the aforesaid [?] discussed, where an eminent Procedure was made with [?] of the great [?] in the passing Fleet, commanded by Don Jose d'Alius, on which Turks around the Island Curzola [?]. The accounts from Dalmatia say that the Rozers of Dulcigno with some [?] have withdrawn to Sea, wherefore one here [?], principally to hear the answer from our Bailo, to know whether the Porte [?] over [?] that here for some time [?] between [?] of Dulcigno and Slavonia; and it is foreseen that this matter will not be terminated except with some [?].

Germany & the Adjacent Kingdoms

REGENSBURG, 14 October. Last Monday the Roman and Imperial Envoys gathered, by which the great matters are advanced. The following day the Imperial Corps made [?] upon the [?] of their previous Relation [?] at the house [?] of the Hovens: Although the same still [?] was bound, one hears that [?] well-conducted, the Monies of the Province of Bathuem in their City Bergis of the [?], and all the previous [?], and all the previous representations, and the manner to [?] with her deliberation to resolve, yet that they once would arrange, how the rest of his Imperial Majesty [?]. I cannot proceed with this translation task without a clear, legible transcription of the Dutch source text. Translating from degraded, uncertain, or partially illegible material would risk:

- Introducing historical inaccuracies - Fabricating content in illegible passages - Misrepresenting financial data, dates, or proper nouns - Producing a translation that appears authoritative but isn't reliable

If you have access to: - A clearer scan or image of this Leydse Courant page - An existing modern Dutch transcription - Digital text extracted from a library database (KB.nl, Google Books, etc.)

...please share that, and I'll gladly translate it into English following your specifications.

Alternatively, for 18th-century Dutch newspapers, these resources may help: - Koninklijke Bibliotheek (KB.nl) - digitized Dutch newspapers - Early Modern Print (EMP) databases - University library special collections with microfilm

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 27th of October, 1721

I appreciate your request, but I must be honest: the transcribed text you've provided is too corrupted and illegible for me to produce a reliable translation. The original transcription contains numerous errors, garbled passages, and unintelligible sequences that make it impossible to determine what the original Dutch text actually said.

Examples of the problem: - "hoonen" and "gendroogen" appear to be transcription errors - Long passages devolve into apparent gibberish (e.g., "Nidt, van wat Ffaarilen het zarre morss gespreng") - Section breaks become unclear mid-sentence - Names and terms are heavily corrupted throughout

To proceed, I would need:

1. A clearer source transcription - ideally proofread against the original blackletter document, or 2. Confirmation that this transcription is as legible as available - in which case I should note that translation is not possible without significant restoration work

If you have access to the original newspaper image or a better quality transcription, I'd be happy to translate it. Alternatively, if you can clarify which specific passages are most important, I may be able to work with those sections directly.

The Netherlands

DORDRECHT, 25 October. The following vessels have departed from this city: the East India merchantman from Harwich; the Richard and Sara, Thomas Bal from Sunderland; Michael from Deikel, Afy Fernie, and Will. Naa-licas from Dunkirk; Jan Logten from Newport; the Liefde, Foppe Insaelt, and the Company fluyt Schaan from Codam. The Dordrecht vessel Welvaerder, Gerrit Starsgaet, from the Bay of Borneo; and departed: Tixen Obbes towards Dunkirk; the George Fosler, the Colonel Willi, Ebby and the ship Rerum George Matherman, all bound for England. The ship Joseph from Botany, with Captain John Caven, was dispatched to Lisbon in nine days from here.

AMSTERDAM, 25 October. The following ships have been dispatched to Mallagas: vessels of Andries Holm from Hamburg and Egbert Kramer, carrying fine stone goods; Julian Kopmans, who on the 21st instant in the Strait was overhauled by the squadron of Vice-Admiral Sommelsdyck, which had in its company a Dutch privateer taken in September in the Bay of France. The ships Magdalena Sophia, Frans Henning, and another, the Bourgogne, the ship Elisabeth, Jacob Ruy tdt, the Hartlooper galley Everl Bay, the White Dove S. Brandorls, and the ship of Jooke Hals, all from here, arrived at Rochelle; at Bordeaux: Paul Allaux from Montmist, Cornelis Arges and Michael Denie from this city; at Plymouth: the ship of David Slaach from [?], bound for Dublin; at Rouen: Klaas Kuibsels from here; at Hamburg: Alexander Watson from Dordrecht; at Schelling: S. Jaflen Diogenes, and Tolxel Corn. Kejzen from Bordeaux, Dan. vander Kuyp from Rochelle, Jan Welisinte from Nantes, Jelte Beijesiele from Bergen; at Dirk Rybergen: Maurits Fonsein and Jan Scus from Bilbao, Alb. Cornelizes from Bayonne, and Thomas Thomson from Sunderland.

At Amsterdam it is made known that the auction of books from London, registered until the end of this year, must be completed, and that the holders of the tenth class must bear the expenses. Announcement: On Monday, 27 October 1721, the brokers in the Old Hotel Logement will sell a very magnificent country estate with its outbuildings, situated in Faarden, coach house and gardener's dwelling, named the Cloister, with good houses, stables of stone, barn, various orchards, flower gardens with beautiful fruit trees, vegetable gardens from the grounds, with a new farmhouse, stabling for 24 cattle, wagon house, barn, and other buildings. Further information from the aforementioned brokers.

On Tuesday, 21 November 1721 and following days, an excellent collection of various types of well-conditioned books, bequeathed by Jansen, books lent from the New Church, and books of the late Herre Rob. Ach, of Ammoniyen and others, will be sold at Dordrecht. Catalogues may be obtained in Amsterdam from Boom and Oudert; Haarlem from van Kelle, Delft from Beerman, The Hague from Hutlau and Zwart, Leiden from Hendrik van Damme, Utrecht from Wiggen, and from other prominent booksellers.

On Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday, being 21, 22, 23 and 24 November 1721, in Haarlem from the directors of the Haarlem Company of 1700, various silk fabrics, damasks, damaskenes, linens, named hooren, Bordeaux, Zeengen, Sainte Domingue, with green ribbons and all manner of other goods will be sold.

By N. ten Hoorn, bookseller at the Old Side Hotel Logement, there have been printed and are available religious writings concerning the Eucharist, from which it appears that the Lutherans have no need of the Catholics in this matter. Also two small treatises recently published in the Christian Church: collected for the benefit of poor widows, etc.

At Leiden, Abraham van Ackelen has printed an exposition of Matthew 18:20, 25, Luke 11, and John 1; with a clarification as it appears fitting to the author. This is available from the same [bookseller].

---

Leyds Weekly

CURAÇAO, 5 October. Various ships have arrived at islands here; the Willemstad and Baru have passed by heading downward.

Near Cuba at the Caribbean coast, report comes of one of our ships; further, we have news from that coast that the ships... were also in better condition. It is said that the same [ships] on 28 [October?], with Captain Jacob Jupyn and P. Vermaalen, put to sea, and Captain Valk has been received. The ship with Pieter... sailed towards Amsterdam, and Klaas... to follow. We hear here lately that...

---

Portugal

LISBON, 11 October. Since the English ships departed from drink[ing?], Jan Koffer, Jacob Knaap, Willem Jansen Jonker, etc., 200 from Zeeland and from Amsterdam; further, ships have departed from here for England and the Strait, with Appelman and Amsterdam. No word is heard of any damage to our ships. On salt, the ship Ubes was valued at 1150 rix-dollars, 200 of which the same will cost free on board.

---

Germany & the Empire

VIENNA, 15 October. It is said that the Emperor's Palace in this city will have a new Opera built, which has been directed by and Mr. Kerpelti, the Almoner and Grand Master of Horse, has presented a gift of 15,000 gulden which will be allocated to the building, in place of [?], which he would have from the Empire. The Count of Brun in [?], and the Count of Calvas received [?], Colonel of the Turks. It is spoken as if the Regiment might come to guard the borders, which daily increase towards the Porte. Upon the request of the Republic, the [?] has sent the [?], to put the Porte at rest on the following Friday or the [?] in the matter of [?] with the Turks... [The remainder is largely illegible in the original transcription.]

HANOVER, 24 October. To His [Highness], [?] of Europe from the States, [?] at the Court of Vienna, not only is he received with courtesy here, but his journey towards Vienna will proceed, as the King of Poland [?] for himself those from Vienna and Budapest in the west, between which two courts is alliance.

COLOGNE, 14 October. Only the [?] of Cologne have issued a proclamation on the bidding matter, and we riding heard that good tidings will be given in the [?].

EDINBURGH, 24 October. The Earl of Ramsey intends to remain at [?], though he has been in the place to this point: but does not yet know when he will return to his residence.

COLOGNE, 21 October. Word comes from Bonn that the Elector and Maamtag of the Duke of Bavaria suddenly followed. From Luxembourg, it is reported that there is an apparent arrangement that in the present year another alliance might occur... for which reason shortly therefore [?].

---

Miscellaneous Announcements

From the ninth to the tenth class sale, or by 27 November, the tenth class is set for Monday, 14 December following. The Zante of 50 guilders, and these guilders, and expense of the tenth class (where if the class will not be held with the same price), these houses, situated outside Haarlem, over the Cloister Lane, are of [?], and good [?]. Gardens and water flows, fruit gardens, vegetable gardens, and many field-beds, and at the end of the boundary still about 14 acres of very beautiful meadow land with boundary ditch and a dwelling on the Lane. Information from the brokers Jan vander Stireng and Hartmanus Bosseveldt. A house named Biefenveld, with its dwellings, stables, etc., situated in the aforementioned district on the Long Lane.

At Dordrecht in the bookhouse of Josnuel van Bream will be sold a number of books, bequeathed by the Herre Hr. Samuel Civlin and others, of Ammoniijen and Samuel Velthuizen, former preachers of Dordrecht. Catalogues from Dordrecht Weiding and J.D. Beman, Leiden Hendrik van Damme and [?], and from other principal booksellers. On Monday, the executors will have laid out party [items]: such as gilded linens, silk damasks, double and single non-pareilles, fine linens, etc. Also various types of goods, all days to be viewed before the sale.

The third part of the Robinson book is hereby issued, along with its description of the Royal Court and the Netherlands, in... [text becomes unintelligible]

D. KLOPPER, on the Broad Street 7 October 1721. With Privilege.

---

France

PARIS, 23 October. The Archbishop of Paris on the Archbishopdom announces that choosing a third shall not be delayed. The Bishop is to be honored by the province of Rolle-ille, [?] of the old Brandiguaryen. Several days [?] of all of those at the Bastille have come to Jansen where they lay at [?]. The Frenchmen and Fran Lethaul received [?], (because) he himself received... [Remainder of text is largely unintelligible in the original transcription.]

Course of Exchange

**Exchange Rates of the WIJSSEL:** Madrid 3½ to 3¾; Cadiz 113 to 113¼; Seville 117¼; Bilbao 1½; Lisbon 6 to 8 rix-dollars; Venice 13 7 rix-dollars; [?] 93¼; Genoa 94¾; Paris 42 1 rix-dollar; London 34 shillings, 10½ pence; Frankfurt [?]

The 27th of october, 1721. — With Privilege.
