Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 5th of December, 1721
The West Indies

CURAÇAO, 10 September. Since recently, only this harbor has been warned here against the Caribs, who are said to be returning to Zeeland today. Soon after, in the Bay of Jonquère, the ship's captain Jan Legrace from here made landfall on the coast. At Porto Coelho they found Spain [?], having to proceed to Vera Cruz; also lying in Tucata were the ships of Captain Dirge Inian de Groot, the young [?] Legrace, Jantien and others at Puerto Popula from [?]. Further, some bark vessels have arrived here in the discharge of the Tilp. The ship of Pieter Beinard, etc., which one sees here in the harbor.

Muscovy

ARCHANGEL, 23 October. They have received here, through the Commissary, dispatched by His Czarist Majesty, news of the concluded peace between His Czarist Majesty and the Crown of Sweden, by their mutual treaty in the great city on the River Volga, where the flow of the Kluven [?] is located, and further news from the city of Amsterdam, concerning [?] the settlement of our merchants, where further commodities [?] in the German house, Jan Horie appointed, and Cavalry Major of His Czar's Majesty in written orders, and it is the German ships that they wish to see depart further for further trade.

ARCHANGEL, 23 October. After a full reception by His Majesty at the Governor's residence was held here, the aforementioned Commissary was sent off with letters and other good Russian news; thereupon the great ones of the court, along with the three German ships, [?], gathered in celebration of the entire community, which was published; there and immediately the "Te Deum Laudamus" was sung, and subsequently the women of the [?] assembled [?].

Italy

NAPLES, 11 November. The Prince of San Severo, from the family of Sangro, Captain of the Knights of the Golden Fleece, upon his departure for Vienna, has requested to spend some time there. Thursday evening, on the order of the Regent, the Bishop departed from the Church of San Gennaro, accompanied by his retinue in great solemnity, with her attendants and her confessor, and brought her to the Cardinal.

ROME, 15 November. The previous Saturday afternoon, the Knight of St. John arrived in this city with his wife from Albany; the aforementioned Knight wore a great [?] before the Pope; this Knight's wife, of whom it is said [?]; the [?] of the Knight's wife, of whom it is said all [?]; the [?] in the German nation, a solemn mass was sent in honor of the Divine [?] of the Church of St. Sunday morning was in the Church of St. [?] in the German nation, a solemn mass was sent in honor of the Divine [?] of the [?]. Sunday morning in the Church of St. John Lateran was carried the [?] of [?], if we have some need of religious solemnity; which [?]

---

No. 146 Friday's Courant

[Treasurer] at Civita Vecchia, to learn from three galleys to understand which rowers had been condemned to the same for the crimes, which were committed for the longest time and also death in slavery: besides which His [Holiness] has also found all the Parish poor; and when he had these distribute alms and bread, for which already a great quantity of bread has been made. Furthermore, the Company of Soldiers of the Pope has also ensured [?] to pass; and daily there will be given to each Soldier a [?] (old fund) withdrawn again. The Pope has received the income of 15 [?], so that [?] that when it is supposed to be handed over by the Heibergiese; Pope Clement XI. reported, reduced to the half. According to all the conditions which one finds in France the morning [?]; will the first most distinguished women here be present; and the [?] Ambassador does everything to prepare for that evening in the presence of the Pope, a [?] with company, at the Theater Capranica. The Lord Gibo, having continued in the reconstruction of our office of Auditor of the Apostolic Chamber, has [?] in the same office with the Lord Colonna from the family of the Princes of Sonibo. Since the Church-position of the Chamber which he held was given to the Lord Giustiniani, Governor of Macerata.

GENOA, 15 November. Letters have been received here from Livorno and Fort Mahon, which report that 3 Algerian rowing vessels have captured 2 Dutch ships laden with [?] wine and sugar, and also a Portuguese ship coming from Brazil, laden with 700 kilos of sugar, 200 barrels of tobacco, 200 bundles of cinnamon, and 3 barrels of [?] gold; besides another ship named the Emmanuel, [?], with 96 bundles of cinnamon, 3 [?] wools, 6 barrels of [?] gold, 5,000 Indian garments, 50 bundles of prepared leather, 200 bales of iron, 200 tons of cocoa, and 60 bales of other merchandise. It is also said that the aforementioned Algerian rowers took another ship with grains from Alicante going to the Canary Islands, and sold the same in Gibraltar for twelve thousand pieces of eight.

FLORENCE, 15 November. Altogether the Grand Duke is in good health, although the medicines do not yet allow him to appear in public. The Grand Prince Louis has obtained something concerning himself, which the Dowager Princess of Tuscany had deposited in Egypt. The Minister of France residing at this court has delivered to our Prince letters from the King of [?], giving notice to our court of the marriage of the aforementioned King with the Infanta of Spain, and also that of the Prince of Alurien with Mademoiselle de Montpensier.

MILAN, 20 November. The Lieutenant Count Sala, who had made sieges over Barrier from the River [?] to the Rho, is here [?].

VENICE, 22 November. Sunday morning there arrived here the Our East India ship the Rose, aboard which the Florentine [?], along with some [?] vessels under sail, which together hold quarantine in the old Lazaretto. Over Castro, a letter [?] from Constantinople, which reports this in the Province of [?], and without any approval of [?] the Church of Santa Maria, [?] the Jesuit teaching [?] was [?], which church was built by a public [?], in which year the [?] occurred in the year 1630, in which year the Florentine maiden [?] the whole [?]. This evening there was in [?] a great concourse of people. This evening in [?] family [?].

Jean Gadolmium.

Germany & Neighboring Realms

VIENNA, 22 November. One finds here reports which claim some apparent affairs concerning a marriage between the Grand Duke of Tuscany with the Archduchess Elizabeth, which in [?] 1623 is ready.

The [?] in December in her [?] North [?] at [?]. Others argue that some [?].

Since the Count [?] for years well [?] in the Danube (as mentioned before) the project from all of Venice toward this side of the empire, the East India Company has also [?] to further this project by [?] communication by [?] more of the aforesaid forwarded [?] empire-[?], as in haste forwarded Lewette empire-[?].

REGENSBURG, 27 November. One treats here over moderation matters. The Protestant body has brought up at the [?], which [?] [?] for all sorts of affairs, and from the brought-up [?] also a public protocol of the Religious should [?] be in possession that all should be according to the Protocol of the Religious [?].

France

PARIS, 11 November. It is reported that the Prince of Rohan [Editor's note: likely a diplomatic envoy] has been commissioned to travel to Spain, and that he will be accompanied in this by [?] who has been instructed to escort the Ambassadors on an equal footing with the Minister of Liberty and the Prince Charles of Lobkowingen. There are two other negotiators as well, who have been granted considerable privileges by the [?] along the side of the Pope's authority. And indeed, [?] and the report of the Pope's Innocent [Editor's note: likely Pope Innocent XIII, reigning 1721-1724] regarding his Majesty's veneration; with the hope that the Pope will consent to it in the general arrangement. However, the conditions have not yet been agreed upon by the other parties in the long negotiations, which is a matter of great concern to all the orders of the realm; insofar as, were there to be a breach concerning the aforementioned settlement, one might expect considerable difficulties. With the letter of love which the Church conveyed to the Pope, by which His Majesty, [?], [?] in these pending matters and [?], regarding the matter, the sum can go forward, with regard to the differences of the Church in Germany, the underlying [?], but I will not hold forth on this. There, by contrast, [?] has been handled favorably, and [?] yet divided among friends in private conversation. Upon the side of the [?] remained, to the extent of the [?]. [?] in their situation regarding [?]. In the coming month, the Duke of Bourbon will [?] Mademoiselle de la Roche [?]

Turkey

CONSTANTINOPLE, 12 October. A Royal Edict has been issued, whereby the Turkish-French church at Galata is closed, and all Christians at this location are ordered by the Commissioners of the Grand Turk to evacuate the church within three days and to vacate the houses there within fourteen days; which period is granted to them in order to remove their goods.

Portugal

LISBON, 4 November. Many ships have been sighted off this roadstead under sail and fortune, though much was lost both in stern and much folk perished. It is said that this unfortunate voyage proved very costly.

Spain

CADIZ, 11 November. From the mines in Brazil there have arrived ships carrying much gold belonging to the galleons at Cadiz, which shall report the following there. The Portuguese ships have already been loaded. Two ships from these mines are bound for these galleons toward Lisbon on the coast of the Caribbean Islands, and [?] and Cartagena, with the [?] their Captains, formerly [?] Amsterdam [and] Rotterdam.

---

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 8th of December, 1721

THE OTTOMAN EMPIRE.

CONSTANTINOPLE, 17 October. The Ambassador who departed on account of the great heat in France has not yet returned to this date. The French Ambassador [?] of these ships with the Turkish Sultan and many others, which have brought [benefits] to France this year; each ship [?] Ostend which found themselves in [?], will be populated as [?].

PORTUGAL.

LISBON, 4 November. Last Saturday the long-drawn siege came to an end here by sea; and likewise many ships were set up for other purposes. It is said that the French Resident at this Court made declarations as well. The Spanish Minister has departed from this Court in Lisbon; another Ambassador must come here in his place. It is said that Henry [?] the Julia of Allicant brought the [?] etc. The Portuguese warship N.S. has returned from Lisbon.

SPAIN.

CADIZ, 13 November. From the Mint here a new batch of struck gold has again been issued. The [shortage?] from France [?] new struck gold issued, the desire from the [?] from the [?] is very great. Lisbon; the French ships the St. Antonie and the St. Carolus, a frigate and many other vessels. Today [several ships] were loaded, [with captains and crew whose names follow] bound for Amsterdam and Rotterdam [?]. [Multiple ship movements and captain names listed, bound for various ports including St. Lucar, Nantes, and Malmedy].

FRANCE.

PARIS, 1 December. The Count of Rouft, [?] the widows in the House of the fifty Missionaries, and found there about [?]. The notorious quartermaster Cartouche [as mentioned in my previous report] was executed the preceding Thursday afternoon and three hours after brought to the Place of Justice; after he had previously undergone interrogation; he made at this time a great confession, and held firm [saying] nothing had been done that did not merit the sentence of death: The confessor who was with him in the Chapel brought him to some repentance; [though] this could not [fully succeed], yet it seemed sometimes that he had little emotion of it, because with respect to the sentence of death [when] the Justice was brought to the place of Execution he still said the same against the Confessor, but [eventually] submitted; coming to the place the Cart and brought [to] the Town Hall, where the Chaplain of a Process with a Notary consulted, the which [?] came to run on the side, he had determined beforehand, to confess everything openly; he justified [himself] and [?], and that he all the crimes over which he had been condemned had to reveal, saying the gravest [crimes] deserved the [?] one could imagine; he confessed that all those [people] which were still in the hands of Justice, and against him [were] his accomplices, whereby one was forced to hasten these Prisoners, and to bring [them] to the Town Hall, and [?] against him to confiscate; he stood with his Confessors having arrived there, Cartouche approached and spoke in the following manner: "Doctor, do not take it ill that I underwent the Rack without wishing to testify to help you if it were possible, but the Confessor has told me that I must reveal everything to the Justice; the greater reason is, I have done it with 200 much [?] reason, also [for] those of our Friends, which are still at Liberty, and promised me patience [if] in [the] hands of Justice [they] would keep their word [they] have broken, so until now no one has come forward, wherefore I will confess all openly: [and] he against one and the other said wherein these crimes consisted, and the evil that they had done; nothing was forced to bring all this to Paper, and well for each of his Crimes in particular, [and] to explain the Facts which he still had to carry through which he had blamed, and of which already; anew were thrown [into custody]. This matter the Chaplain, Counsellor and Clerk the said [?] and the following Friday morning [?] gave 3 thus Cartouche that afternoon about one o'clock first was executed, he received [the sentence of] the Wheel, in which they laid him on the Wheel about half a quarter of an hour, thereafter he [died]; the which still happened by request of his Confessor, through mercy [?] which order the Rope about his neck was firmly fastened, with which he was strangled: his Body was shortly thereafter buried by the German [Executioner]. That day nothing was done in the morning in the Tournelle, because the Reporter and other Gentlemen were [?] occupied: on Saturday one saw on the Green a double Gallows with 4 Wheels; about ten o'clock an order came to let no more than one stand, and to remove the 3 others; meanwhile another order came to also remove the fourth Wheel, and to leave standing nothing but the double Gallows. On that day a young boy of about 12 years was placed in the Conciergerie [prison], who at the same time was still young [when he would] be hanged; he was confronted against the other Prisoners that are there, with which so much time passed that the Regimen [?] not only that given day, but far into the night were [occupied] therewith, as when he [underwent] the sentence of death. [?] might this evening some other villains also be punished. The body of la Magdalaine, companion of Cartouche, who for two years [ago] was seized and had died under Torture [?], is buried in the Churchyard of St. Innocents. There sat still between the [number] and 70 Criminal Prisoners, which are all Companions of Cartouche, [though] one other is still being sought.

GERMANY AND NEIGHBORING REALMS.

HAMBURG, 4 December. From Holstein it is reported that with the latest Letters from Copenhagen [they] had [news], that one by the preparation of the Capital [?] Minister wished preparation for the Feast [?] of the Tsar's Minister [?] preparation for the Feast between the Tsar, his Majesty and the Crown [of] Sweden [did] not well [succeed], wherein [?] Minister [?] was held, in which an Amphitheater and double [?] should be [?]; also he [had] too silver Medals made [on] which his likeness, and wished on the [?] on the day of the Feast [?] [?], to send for certainty and his Blood, but the whole [?] to [?] Feast, [?] from the ix November December [was] extended [?] and that the Crown-Princess the ix November [?] [?].

THE NETHERLANDS.

AMSTERDAM, 1 December. At the great [?] where the favor of Mr. Christoffel Brands, [?] of Meuzevleven, [?] and other circumstances are recorded, and such [?], namely in the Colvegaten Burwal, behind the Colvegaten Doelen, [?] [?] already [?] time not very well [?], [is] the [?] now still obscured, [?] for the sake of the Visceral guildsmen between his [?] and [?].

I must acknowledge that this transcription is severely compromised and largely illegible in many sections. The blackletter text has been transcribed with numerous errors, missing words, corrupted passages, and unclear abbreviations that make a reliable English translation impossible.

What I can discern are fragments:

AMSTERDAM, December 1st - Shipping news reporting various vessels arriving at ports including Venice (ship Esperimento, Capt. Giovanni Castinichd from Norway), Livorno, Genoa, Cadiz, Bordeaux, and others. Reports of storm damage to ships from Danzig and concern for vessels at sea.

Property auctions - Multiple notices for sales of estates and properties near Leiden, including land sales on specific dates in December 1721.

THE HAGUE, December 7th - Brief mention of government appointments and an express messenger to London.

---

Editor's note: I cannot provide a complete, reliable translation of this document. The transcription contains too many errors, lacunae, and corrupted passages to render accurate English text. A fresh transcription directly from the original 1721 Leydse Courant would be necessary for proper translation work. I recommend consulting the original source material at Dutch archives or university libraries with holdings of 18th-century newspapers.

Course of Exchange

**Exchange rates and commodity prices** - A financial section listing exchange rates for various European cities (Madrid, Seville, Venice, Livorno, Amsterdam, Hamburg, Antwerp, etc.) and prices for goods.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 8th of december, 1721. — With Privilege.

Germany & Neighboring Kingdoms

ENGLAND

From PARIS, 5th December. New Relation of the Lord Avedo Coutinho, extr. On the 9th November 1721. KINGDOM

Left Column

[The opening section concerning health measures and quarantine regulations is severely corrupted in the transcription and cannot be reliably translated.]

THE NETHERLANDS.

BRUSSELS, 1 December. In the past Wednesday, the States of Flanders concluded their regular assembly, and reports were made from the members: an Act of Accounts regarding the common Subsidy on the land for the period ending in November has been presented, amounting to 200,000 guilders [?]. These Accounts by the Magistrate of the City, as well as by the regional authorities, have not yet been settled or confirmed against the Accounts of the States of Brabant, as was the first agreed upon, as a loan arrangement for the payment of the Troops; however, it is expected that this will be finalized by the end of this week. There is also mention that some precious items were recovered from the last troubles in this City. It has been reported at Ostend that with the last storm a vessel ran aground, which was salvaged with tea, porcelain, and other East Indian merchandise; it is believed that there they will now quickly equip another vessel, which in the Spring is to be sent to the Indies.

AMSTERDAM, 1 December. According to recent letters from Poles-burg of 20 January [New Style], received in Petersburg regarding the negotiations in which the King of Sweden was involved, which had been conducted with considerable success with the King of Prussia. It is reported that the forward-trading companies at Lisbon have been discharged; when Lisbon was approached, the ship Clove, Captain Thomas Brown from London, sailed to London; also the ship St. Maria, Captain [?] Valke, sailed to Curaçao. At Lisbon also the ship St. Martin, Captain Gerard Colendam, and several Dutch merchant vessels were detained. On the [?] day, several ships heading to the Middleburg were [?]; on the second day the Dutch flotilla, with approximately 100 English slaves, was reported departing.

THE DELEGATED COUNCILS OF HOLLAND AND FRIESLAND

The drawing of the Lottery of the first class, finally scheduled for Tuesday, 13 January 1723, shall take place as previously announced, which lottery was to have been drawn, on which occasion the manner of drawing shall be conducted according to the proper procedures, that it may proceed with proper regularity. Note: The drawing of the Lottery [?].

This evening at [?] hours, being Wednesday, 12 December, the Brokers' Guild shall meet in the Lodge in Amsterdam in the Nose of the Bracket [location], in order to gather regarding the new regulations, by the order of the City Government.

Property of Nek Makaslaat in Amsterdam shall be sold on Thursday, 22 December 1721, at noon, at the City Courthouse, by public auction [of] the copperplate-numbered properties No. 24, 25, consisting of approximately 29 morgens [acres]; these properties, situated in the Wouda [district], according to the Monday afternoon auction, shall be [offered] or [regulated] as the aforementioned persons [direct], additionally...

Auction on Friday, 18 December 1721, at the Burg Bianca in Stallingen: 23 channels, totaling approximately 150 for [?]. Auction on Saturday, 23 December 1721, at the Burg Bianca Philips [location]: well-situated dwelling, [containing] these buildings, barn and schuurt [farm building]; consisting of 16 morgens [acres] with adjoining meadow and field.

Further auction property near the Abbey of Loeuwenhordt [?]: Broker Scheiünger shall offer on Saturday, 20 December 1721, in the afternoon a well-conditioned Wind Mill, located at the Haarlemmer Dike near Sloondyde; also [includes] houses and [other properties] being auctioned...

The Executors of the Testament of the late Philip Weysman announce an auction at the Burg Bianca in Leiden, to be conducted publicly in the Afternoon at the appointed house Sorenhuypnege, Speersburg, Schuttersborg, together with a spacious Book [or library]; situated outside the High Gate of the City of Leiden, because of the location of Soetscrodde; and further [properties] [listed], because the location thereof [is properly] placed; Over...

Right Column

NAPLES, 18 November. The Prior of the Church with [?] musicians and priests resumed their yearly accustomed service, solders of the Regiment Guard being stationed nearby, and on that day the City, which [received] a visit from the Pope, were properly received. Without [?], XIII and V years after Christ, having held a festival, [there] proceeded between the hopes of [?], which was led in a fine velvet hat on a great procession to see before previous weeks, which all [were] in Silver Lace, and the [?] Seat was conducted: before the Pope but was, to the King to employ and himself quickly [wore] the silk garments, the Bishops, the Apostles and in the [?] of the Royal Princes and Councils, [all] being [arrayed], all they had gold, His [Majesty] the Minister of the Chamber with golden [insignia], the Chamberlains with golden Canons Senate, [with] [?] great Bishop Good Provost of the Court, which bore a Pike, and Carmine Velvet Hat on a great procession...

FRANCE.

FROM PARIS, 18 December. [Text becomes increasingly corrupted and illegible in the original transcription, rendering reliable translation impossible.]

ENGLAND AND WEST-FRIESLAND, ADVERTISEMENT. As the scheduled [drawing] on 17 January 1722 must be completed, on pain of absolute forfeiture and return, the Lottery of the first Class [?] 30 guilders have been [announced] and on parole, and news from the East [?], the complaints of the parcel have not [?]; the Condition as Advertisement [?] shall be clear on 19 January; at Antwerp, the Brokers Jan Hoos, Jan Rusvacker, Daniel [?], and Jan Kook, shall sell a consignment of extra fine new Bavana Tobacco seeds. 192.

On 12 December 1721, in the evening and night, at Parmentede, at the house of Joh. [?] Blaauw, a house and windmill the half Sheep, consisting of 1 Codel [?], with its Houseman Woodwoge, Stalling and the house palace [?], which shall be offered by Peter Cornelis Hooft [?], including the [?] of the Mill in the Rust, [?] Geydeshoorn, shall be offered the Boers Dwelling...

At the house Blink Leiden, to unite a house with a large [?] House, located on the price of the South Side at Doorzagte [?]...

At the Burg Blink Leiden, to sell a house with a large [?] House, located on the property of the South Side at [?], at the house Blink Leiden in the East [district]...

[Remaining sections are severely corrupted in transcription and cannot be reliably translated.]

Printed at Doecmeden, also in the other markets of the Book Dealers, [?]...

Also [?] displayed [?] of [?], the Postman [?], presented by Alfred Stansby, secretary of the City, and [?] [?], is granted and to be approved, as also by the foremost Bodies with Money and Merchants. License.

PRIX DE KLOPPER, on the Breedstraat 10 December 1721. With Privilege.

Notices & Advertisements

On Saturday, 23 December 1721, shall be sold at the Burg Bianca in Leiden: Margrette, Ballinger, Mary, Schuurt, Well [?], in the Gouda and Gurendeykke Polder, at the higher Dike, by the Brokers Peter [?] and the F. and D. Bakkers, Henk, Nek Makaslaat. In Amsterdam shall be sold [various merchandise], stocks, spreadsheets, tables, etc.; also hothouses, [?] field & Co.; other properties with Petgen, containing [?] and silver work, new goods [?] spare batten cases, and other items: all managed by the auctioneer Gysbictus van Kuyk.

At Dordrecht at the Weald, Boedeert and Zeem, has been made and to be auctioned, similar items made of Cedim, including [?] and the general merchandise of the [?] company, and notably concerning Leven-geraliea of [persons], among which [?], the Handmextt, and [other persons] mentioned...

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 10th of december, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 12th of December, 1721
Italy

NAPLES, November 11th, Tuesday, Feast of St. Martin. Eight days after the church service, the Viceroy was honored with 12 horses of outstanding quality, in place of the gift which the [?] customarily presented to His Excellency according to annual practice. The 200 Spanish soldiers from the Regiment of the Guard Wiltmen, who have been disarmed and discharged, have taken up their march toward Spain.

ROME, November 12th. On Saturday, the Pope, through the Parochial Guards, distributed 2,000 Scudi to the poor of this city, which amounts to approximately one schilling in Dutch currency. Thus did approximately 6,000 [?] Innocentius XII, [interred] in the Palace of the Vatican, and after a [?] ceremony was held, His Holiness took [his place] from the Patriarchal Church of San Giovanni in Laterano. [He was conveyed] in a magnificent carriage lined with plush velvet and richly adorned with gold, carried by 2 white horses, [and proceeded] in his place with the cross-bearer and a carriage of plush velvet with a gold hood upon his head, and was attended by more than 60 horses, all adorned in gold and silver. [Those in] silver cloth with sleeves of plush velvet trimmed with silver lace: before the Pope marched a solemn cavalcade, consisting of three Hundred [members of the] nobility, the Officers of the Palace, the Household of His Holiness, the Household servants with their embroidered Kitchens, the chamberlains with embroidered [?] upon their horses, the Chancellors of the Cardinals, and various members of the Senate, in long white plush gowns; the Conservators in great plush gold-brocaded gowns; the secret chamberlains, [who wore] two Bishops' plush hoods [?]; [there followed] all the Dominican friars, the Benedictines of the Order, the Franciscans of the convents, the Governor of Rome, and the Duke of Polignac, brother of the Pope, who had been declared Prince of the Throne the previous day: with His Father in Rome [were] 2 Cardinals and Monsignor [?], who had their servants with them, and on this occasion were splendidly dressed: after the Cardinals followed the Cardinalships and [?]-bishops, the Apostolic Protonotaries and other [dignitaries], numbering 56, who were followed by the Guard of the High Horses and Riders, with the equipage and coaches of the [Palace], all splendidly dressed and bearing new standards. His Holiness, with this pompous retinue, having departed from the Palace of St. John in the Vatican, all proceeded under the continuous acclamation of a countless multitude of the Faithful, both standing in the streets in great columns and on the [balconies] of the churches, which were all magnificently decorated with banners of His Holiness and the princely families of the Cardinals. [He proceeded] in the direction of the Castle of St. Angelo in the Capitol, where the Pope passed through two magnificent triumphal arches, named religious [triumphs] of the audience of Michael Angelo Buonarroti, which the Roman people are accustomed to see when a new Pope is [elected]. The triumphal arch was decorated with [?] [of?] wine flowed: having arrived in the Capitol, the Marquis Frangipani, Senator of Rome, kissed the foot of His Holiness the Pope, and [the Pope received] the keys of this city from His Holiness: after which the Holy Father continued his march and proceeded to the Papal seat, which the Duke of [?]

Portugal

LISBON, November 23rd. This week the lease of tobacco for 3 years was concluded here, according to which the Armenians shall annually pay 3 million and 50,000 Crusados. On these waters were delivered: João Basífila Broxa toward Bahia, Klein Wastland and the English merchant ship the Dolphin, Captain John Candet from Terranova, Valerio Was and George Cox from Mazagan, Ant. Fernandes Silvas from St. Michael, and several others; [in the opposite direction] departed: Christoffel Matterman toward Plymouth, Robb. Kuhlman toward Sweden, Pieter Welve toward Cork, Felix Elfevan toward St. Diego d'Terceira toward Cadiz, Louis Elfevan toward Viana; Juriaan Jonker, Jao Kolter, Klaas and Coro, Vildt, Eeijpe toward Cork, Paauuw, Gerrit Idts Jeelling, Imie Friedrike and Hend. Tymenie toward Amsterdam; Will. Taylor and Hend. Glois toward London; David Chapel, Daniel Lant toward Topsham, Robb. Wilh. toward Vlissingen, Amarto Fimenzel toward St. Michael, Robb. I. win toward Cork, Gillis Wa-lies, and the Cadogan Jao Kuleman toward Vlissingen; 200 [?]

Germany & Neighboring Kingdoms

REGENSBURG, December 4th. Since recently the Protestant Corps have submitted their observations on the extract of the [?] Church-[?] Protocol of the Religious Commission, in which the collective Corps, among other things, [requested?] that the [?] commissions of Braunschweig and [?], and of the [?] be [?], [noting?] that it was not [just?] that this matter should be begun [?]. It appeared as though [?] since it is indisputable that the Reformed [?] in [?] the year 1714, and as a consequence of the [?] [?]. The [?] of the Reformed goods for the sake of [?] on the [?] [?], and no Commissioner [?] for the sake of [?] on the [?] the Reformed goods, [which] presently by the Ministers the Protestant Corps [sent?], [which] presently by the Ministers the Protestant Corps [sent?], would still [?] that [beginning?] this matter [sent?], [though] men still [?] that [beginning?] this matter [sent?], [though] in general [they] would rather have had so much opposition; it is [?] whether the Reformed [?] not gladly [?] so much opposition; whether the [?] of the comfortable [?] which by the said [?] [?] could be done, that the Inquisition [?] [?] and costs [?] must be [?], so it is not [?] which cannot be supposed to be said, that the comfortable [?]

Friday's Courant:

His Holiness, having turned back against the arch, returned to the Quininal; the Ambassadors, Princes, [?] Romans, in order [to arrange] the ceremonial, on this occasion did not participate; and because it was very becoming between the great Constable Colonna and the Duke of Gravina that this matter was not yet resolved, for which reason His Holiness wished to give the same [?] to the Governor of Rome. His Holiness declared, and [the] diploma was [issued by?] expedient. That same day 200 galley-rowers were released, and each was given a ticket to fetch bread. The Prince and [?] of Modena; and the Pretender with his wife departed from the Palace of the Capitol, from where they went to the Church of St. John Lateran to see the end of this ceremony, as well as to receive the solemn blessing from the Pope. For this reason all the Ministers of distinguished Princes and all the Nations made [?] for their [?], and the Ambassador of Portugal at the same time sent a courier to the court at Lisbon with a detailed account of the said ceremony, on which occasion Cardinal Aquaviva made use [of the opportunity] to send a packet of letters to the court at Madrid. Tuesday the feast of the consecration of the principal church of St. Peter in the Vatican was celebrated, in which chapel 30 Cardinals appeared; the morning after, an extraordinary congregation was held in the Quininal, where immunity [?] on the advice that the Governor of Naples had brought two bandits, [and acted toward] them as he had drawn them from a church, and after giving them several moments of time to confess. Wednesday morning it was heard that Cardinal Aquaviva had received an order from the court at Madrid, and the Spanish [?] audience of the [?], and others of that Nation [were told?] to [announce?] that they shall abstain from all particular and increased familiarity with Cardinals del Giudice and Althan. That same day Cardinal Althan entertained several Cardinals, distinguished Monsignors, Princes and Princesses at table, on account of the feast of St. Elizabeth, the name day of the reigning Empress, and that evening all the balconies of the Cardinals of the [?] party were illuminated. Today, the feast of St. Cecilia, the Pope visited the church of the said saint, on which occasion the Duke of [?] in his capacity as Prince of the Throne rode between the guests and the Pope.

MILAN, November 22nd. The renowned banker Johannes Baptista Toli has died, leaving 200,000 Scudi to the court and houses.

BOLOGNA, November 23rd. At the beginning of the previous week a rumor arose here that the [?] sickness, which still [?] had spread in Denmark, [was not?] on any coast [?]

It appears that according to Middle Eastern custom, [people] are using [this opportunity] to come to the aid of the Lutheran Oppressed of the Palatinate. The presiding Swedish Minister has yesterday, through his Secretary, instructed all the Ambassadors to ratify the blessed Peace of the King, his Master, with the Czar; and has also distributed a few copies with the Articles, upon which the customary Compliments of Felicitation shall be addressed to him. Nor has the book already been published by the Lutheran Preacher of Hamburg gone unnoticed, [which was intended] to prevent the reunion of the two Protestant families: the same is carried with great difficulty in the belief that the Author is moved by a passionate zeal, and would far rather see a division of Protestantdom than to agree with that undertaking which the Lutherans could scarcely accomplish if one were willing to give way concerning the matter of interpretation: it is said that this is only a beginning, and that many more such writings shall yet appear.

France

PARIS, 3 December. The King enjoys perfect good health. Cardinal du Bois goes to occupy the apartment in the Tuileries of the deceased Cardinal. Madame de Ventadour, on the side of the King's Majesty. One hears that Mademoiselle de Montpensier is in good health, notwithstanding the fatigues of the journey; yet although Cartouche is held prisoner in Paris, the iron-handed gang pursues through a detachment of his band, and few ladies have not been robbed. Between the end of the previous [month] and the third of this month, the people of this city saw the body of Cartouche, which lay exposed at St. Come, for a Stuiver per person. Now that the deaths of this criminal and his accomplices have been made public, his goods and properties have been seized in St. Denis, Meuse, Chartres, and other places; but because a good part of his money was in escrow, he has declared that most of it lay buried in the Wood of Boulogne, yet has not been able to indicate the exact place, as he would have had to point it out in person: men have been sent out to capture a few of his companions whom he has informed on in the principal cities, such as Orleans, Lyon, Marseille and others; and it is assured that he has revealed more than 100. Saturday evening, at the same hour as the Commissioners mentioned in our previous [report] were arrested, another gang of thieves attacked the rooms of the Chamberlain of the Duke Regent in the Palace of Luxembourg, from where they took as much as they could carry: and from Bourges it is reported that Cartouche's gang has drawn about 1500 livres in gold from the Mint. Wednesday morning a private individual went to the aforementioned Banks on Lombard Street to withdraw a bill of exchange for 1000 livres, but it was put off until the following Friday, wherefore he left; and his carriers set out, but were pursued by several rogues, which resulted in a great fight, so that eventually the porter opened [fire] and the gentleman was forced to lay down everything, supposing that he had received his money; meanwhile the people gathered in a great group for fear, and the carriers pulled with the thieves, as if they wanted to protect their master, and they seemed to have every occasion to let the thieves escape, which happened: one can scarcely be sufficiently amazed at this affair, and that it occurred this morning at 10 o'clock, in a street where there is the most passage in this city. It is said that the Duke of Astin intends to cede the Barony of Epernon to his blood relative the Marquis de Roset. The Marquis de Livin, the King's chief Chamberlain, has been dismissed. The young Prior Lord Colbert of St. Marc has succumbed to a belly ailment. The Marquis de Chantillon, whose wife is still expecting the birth of a son, was buried on Monday. The Sieur de St. Concil, one of the Plenipotentiaries at the Congress, has suffered a stroke; in such circumstances the King's Advocate at the Charters was received. The Cardinal de Bille Brûlé has arrived here from Rome—thus one shall soon have more certainty regarding the aforementioned composition of his episcopate of St. Martin in the Venaissin. It is heard that the Prince and Princess of Modena and Savoy [are] residing in Rome, where they intend to stay as long as the domestic disputes last. The Sieur Bailes, a Mullinger, who was formerly notary of the Sieur Law, has deceased; his son is said to possess immense wealth, the which is not a small joy. It is rumored that since the demolition of the Loges in the Hotel was the Salon, one will see the Vice-Duke Stori there to teach them, as soon as the drawing of the vises shall be won. One speaks of a change regarding the Invalides, [which] the Of[ficers]...

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, 1 December. Yesterday afternoon at three o'clock the great Festival (mentioned in my previous [report]) began: in the afternoon the lower floor of the Temple was illuminated, which made a very fine sight, and one began a quarter [of an hour] over [the illumination] with the firing of 45 Mortars, which were placed around the Theater; after which the Cypher of His Czaric Majesty was ignited, which burned for 5 minutes; subsequently the remaining Fireworks were ignited, which lasted in the hard half hour: which morning lasted; and notwithstanding the great multitude of people, all passed without accident. The third [day], the Ball was held and some [displays] lasted until half of the Fourth Day, yet some crackling [fireworks] with arrows and water-fireworks were fired. According to the letters from Bilbao, the ship St. Joseph, Captain Francois del Valle, was sailing hither; and the ships that lay there the previous roll for the [...]are the 2 English of Richmond and the English ship Will. Wickets. At La Rochelle, Elias Wy[?] of here and [concerning] Cartouche [and] 7 men, his accomplices, have sought out their[...], and he declared of Boulogne, [and] from the people [who] emerged, in the Post and below in 100 hundred [livres]. In Radis too a party of thieves [attacked] in the Palace of J[...], so that the thieves of [...] of the aforementioned [drew] forward from 1000 livres. And therefore some scoundrels at the Palace of J the thieves of this forward the sea, and the [...] of the burgher ship of Juraan [?]. The harbor of the ship Abrams Oltschanke, being driven in by cautious [?]; that of Oorn, Cok, of Ostend [was] anchored. At Tübeck there arrived a great fight, [concerning] Herr daung and Jochem Fraal and another of Danzig, the skipper Peer Petrijon, for the Admiral [?] traveled, which was driven into Castellon by storm. The 17th previous, there were still another Eastern [?] with the Skippers and Gothenburg [?] concerning the English and Holland ships, who went to England and were anchored in the Sea, among [them] Klaas Hoop, Skipper Winkelbo's, Mantes on Bruys, Botte Teke, &c., which one generally [in?] Norway [?], because Botte Teke lay there already. From Gesäteli was also the latest woven fields Harm, Bokje, la Telfet [?] has entered [port]—the ship St. Elias, Captain Jean Guibel, of Sluy Maïlo; after she had lost her masts, &c.; and before the city Sye Consell, Klaas Geeratbie, [and the] Thomas Sykes, &c., [with] small oil, and several others. The rate of EXCHANGE is today on Madrid [at] 2 and a half, [and on] Cadiz [?]

England

LONDON.

Notice [of] the 15th [concerning] the [?] or 600 at the goods of the [...] still here, [from which on] advice [?] on [?] day 2754 [?] the flying [ship] Aria Maria [from] the strait of Lisbon, and the [High?] Teraceent in return [heading] toward the Canaries, [and] Fitz Pan [and?] Pablan and Diego to Liverpool, John Francis and the St. Carlos to [St. ?] still [?] Barcelona, yet still remain lying. PETERSBURG, the 16th of this [month], [when] many speeded [?] the aforesaid [vessel] dispatched in Petersburg the flying-Fleet loaded [with] medicines good to and a way [?].

Proclamation of the Committed Councils of the States of Holland

[Regarding] The drawing of the red [lots] up to the first class on or at the latest on 17 January; of the right of the unclaimed lots; upon which penalty [this] now also must be [postponed], [so great is] [?] the Frisian [?] and the holders of three [?], who [for this] 3 months [have] the red class, [must have] fully paid; no one shall, because they have [?], no [?] may be [?]. Ho[nourable?], [a] great [?] house, and meadow [?] house, wherein [are housed] several great [?], the stables, and an uncommonly [beautiful] great plot or garden [with] [?] ponds, standing and lying on the southern side of the Plantage by the Ro[ad], [?] widow Countess Doornicere of, and to Hoexes, &c., [?] [?] wishing [to] wish [to] address themselves to the aforesaid Agent Mr. Nicolaas, on [?] [in the] morning [of] good Friday, 13 December, to the Broker [?] [in the] city inn, by public sale [to be sold] a bridge; [with a] great [area of] 29 morgen, [?] roods, with his house man's dwelling [?], [on] the northern or Pareculiar road, which [was] used by Pieter Cornelis Hooft before, [?] [?] [to be] purchased, address themselves to said broker. [At] the 2 Oxelhoeck [?] and D. Boockoning brokers, [which] [shall] be [in] Amsterdam [?] dike in the White Saw, 1500 extra Calmer Parts, 1000 Coppersmith [?] and 100 [?], 500 Colliers, 200 Naphtha and 1600 fine [?] beams. [In] Tage, and 150 extra Paris Hamburg [?] beams.

One is [to be put on] Thursday, 18 December 1721, at the Burg[?] [?] Stalinse for 150 horses and [?] gulden; for 150 very [?], [?]

One is [to be put] on Saturday, 10 December 1721, on the Burg[?] Plantage [?] and well-situated [property is] [a] dwelling, [being] [in] divided housing, [?] building houses, [?] and barn; together with 16 morgen well-maintained [?] [?], here by said aforementioned the Abbé of Letteruharti; all [?] great [?] under the Weckland in the county of Kouderkerk.

Notices & Advertisements

At Delft by Reinniet Boelet there have been printed and in all cities with the booksellers [various works,] consisting of Fore-pages, Bibliotheken, Geboortefchien [?], Migrationelles, Boutiques [?], not very artful Prints, and the Alphabet of the Poet adorned, [all?] on [?] a few examples on great Medican [?] paper [to be] obtained.

The 12th of december, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 15th of December, 1721

No. 1721

Spain

CADIZ, 11 November. This week the flags have first been seen here at Godtaera, which came over with the Flotilla, and the Bretons are setting sail. Three weeks with the departure of the Incense Ships making no haste, they will still have much delay. Nothing has yet arrived here from Cadiz. In the following [passage], Pieter Geweel of Amsterdam and Oostende, from Lisbon, [all] against [?] Jan van Tarranauf: in contrast, Thomas Broen and Rymaad King go to Fabian and Diego Doesnford to London; Robberie Will, the St. Anna, and the St. Collen to St. Jago, to York to Cork; Audries Boveriman to Barcelona, the St. Joseph Holland to Liverpool; [?] two hundred that are still here: Six Biren and Engelle, these good medicines, one Hamburg [ship], one Zuyder [ship], one Oostland, one Holland or French merchant ships.

England

PETERSBURG, 21 November. Due to the storm and high flood on the 18th, many people, cattle, &c. were drowned and washed away; [?] through the East Sea, [?] on some dikes; also in the Temple of Lucan, from the Fourth-Feast [?], great damage was done and under this the portals were destroyed; also the Shipyard [?] was damaged.

France

ORANGE, 5 November. Through the good order which has been established in this City, it is now very much reported throughout that the light Sickness is found: in the Hospitals there are only 7 sick, and it is said that such help is given without great difficulty.

ORANGE, 19 November. The contagious Sickness does not increase here, and most of the new [cases] among the sick people are sick.

COURTZAIN, 12 November. Although one is here in the midst of infected places, yet here one is still in complete health established, so that no one of any unusual suspicion can be found.

AVIGNON, 12 November. In this week for the Sicknesses, which here [had] 150 sick and 325 dead; in the hospitals 231 sick and 192 dead, and the whole total of the dead added to 763 sick.

---

No. 159

Monday's Courant.

Named La Vengeance of P. Amaro, but does not yet fulfill safety. The Marquis de Lignerolls has not fallen into any Apoplexy as it is said. The Abbot of Rohan, who [?] with the Cardinal [?] the Church and in the Priestly Order shall be received, shall remain there until [?] the order of the step of higher degrees: it is said that he is fortunate to [?]. The Jubilee shall not be practiced here until [?] the welfare of Souls through the [?] comes to [?], as is brought to notice. [?] next week where the Duke of Noailles has had built, about on this place where the Duke of Noailles [?] the Couple of Souls continuing for Madrid that the Bull of the Jubilee of Toledo had ordered the Churches; where the Women and the Men each separately could perform their other Ceremonies. The Abbot received, and is now busy to [?] at the Parliament. On the [?] of this Month the Corpse of the Count de Rouffy was brought with a magnificent Ceremony to St. Sulpice. The [?] Lecterns where some Anatomical Operation was to be done, the [?], where they would gladly give money to see him, even his Painter of this asked to be permitted to depict him; this [?] was of a middling stature, little taller than a common Man, yet he was nervous and flesh; the Doctor who has dissected him testifies to have found a Man. Balagni, called the Capuchin, because [?] in these [?] he months in this Order was placed, who was executed there, was but 21 Years; he was the Son of a notorious Gilder near the Pont Notre Dame, and had bought for 40 thousand Livres [worth of] goods, of which the Family de Cousticate has concealed much. The group of Accomplices of this Band increased likewise; they are brought from all sides to this City. Also likewise [?] from the side of the Loire and Lyons. On the [?] of this [Month] the Hostess of a little Inn with her Maidservant was captured. The pages between the 17th and 28th of the previous Month, held the General Assembly of Gatouche a Meeting at the [?], where they arranged the necessary matters of the Company and elected a new Head in his place, which Operation immediately brought to light, after which each of the Brigades immediately set forth in March toward their assigned Post. A young Abbot named generateur de la Motke has been captured, being a Nobleman from Auvergne, who is but 18 Years old; he has already confessed to being the Perpetrator of the Thefts which were done in the Monastery of the Missionaries; he was seized in the House of a Budgetholder, where he had presented himself to deliver the Abbot's Gown to a boy to dress, and thus to escape. On the [?] of this [Month], when one of the Mutineers of Cartouche was brought to the place de Giere, a private Man was murdered at the Gate of St. Germain, without it being possible for the [?] to take any of these Murderers, although they were 9 in number. [?] thirteen of the Council of States of the King, both of 23 November [?] here in the City, thus with the Agreement of the Company of the Indies, and [?] which have been presented to the Visa, according to the Edict of 26 January [?] shall remain in place for good thousand, of which the Reduction shall be made according to [?] and according to the Regulation behind this Edict. The other ordained that with the beginning of the Year 1723 a yearly Fund shall be laid in to the sum of 40 Millions, of which employment shall be made in the King's States, this of the employment of the Finances, to serve for the payment of the [?] determined Debts, according to the Edict of 20 January last, which [?] shall be done according to the Regulation; [?] at the end of the Minute of the present Edict.

Great Britain

LONDON, 23 December. The aforementioned Saturday, when Count Cadogan arrived here from Holland and a very black Storm had lasted 14 hours, in which a part of the Anchors and Cables of the Yacht the Pubbs, on which he was, were cast into the Sea; together with all the Cannon, and his Ship's equipment were lost.

England

The matter concerning the departure of the [?] was reviewed in session, and it was followed up before Parliament. It was reported that the [?] Margaret had sufficient grounds to cause the Minister to swear that against any smuggling or return trade, no goods or merchandise shall be allowed to enter England without permission to be understood, and the exclusion is to be put into effect in every place of the kingdom. If goods are found, the responsible persons or agents shall be liable; it is well known in all places that any ship or vessel carrying contraband merchandise shall be seized. The King shall also have recourse to remedy violations against the enforcement of these laws with clear punishment. Criminals, like smugglers, shall have their goods brought to land according to the rules of the ordinance, where the loading shall follow the instructions of the ordinance issued, of which one part goes to the Exchequer and one part to the other officers in the interim period.

Then again a Bill came forward to deal with the problem that smugglers continue to make various efforts against the laws, though they have changed course toward France in the most suspicious manner to apply themselves there. Other lands of the Commons received different Requests from the Burgesses of Worcester and other Places, asking that the taxes be lowered and general relief granted; this matter shall be on the Agenda. As for the living conditions set out, and the provisions of what is to be allowed in the Kingdom were settled, it followed that the Commission for the [?]-File sent out a Circular to raise Money by exchange in London, and at the proposal of Mr. Rayham, who was brought forward by the Procurator General, a Circular was also ordered for and concerning the sale of all Goods by way of Long [?], as formerly the Merchants and the [?] trade helped. The Lords made the said decision and passed the Land-Tax Bill, complete with the Commission of the Fleet and the Budget of the Naval Debts, all that were previously done in the moment of Gold as fundamental matters; among the Camera-Troops the appointment of His Majesty was said to have still other Proposals though their matters were dealt with; and it was ordered on Monday to pass in a Commission concerning the investigation of such great Naval Debts: whereupon they adjourned until tomorrow. Therein the House mentioned the Duke of Argyle, the Earls of Lisat and Stratford, together with various other Lords to be examined, before the Bar of the King's Bank appeared, where his [?] was put forward: that it was reported to him and three days were set; the Smuggler Jeremia was brought there in the midst by the trial of the Maudelia, which our Hermham carried out, and the trial is to be held in the third hearing. Then that a ship in the Commons was long [?] on the proper handling of [?] on the Sea and the Land, and it was ordered that a Bill should be issued to improve the Roads in [?]; whereupon a proposal was made to [?] the Z. Z. Company and all other Companies to understand for the [?] to be laid which for Councillors three Commissions great [?] are, to also fail in the aforesaid Monday; whereupon they adjourned until today. The Agents of the Bank [?] 9 and a half, Z. Z. 9 and a [?], Mill. Bank 115. Af. C. 26 +

Furthermore: 9 and a quarter, the London 9 and a quarter, also B. 29.

Also it is [?]ably the Staple Councillors Milleys, John Goodwin, the [?] regarding the principal Goods, the Mary David Herby, and the Prince William have arrived, where also English, and the Agt. Augustijn Brit arrived at Ostende.

Amsterdam

AMSTERDAM, 13 December. According to the Letters from the fifth at Leiden, and Services we know well, where most nearly Batavius was in the [?] as heretofore sent, and among those the Servant's Hoods Ship-Willem; from the departure of Klaus Elskap, and still has something to do? cannot be avoided. And it appears that all Goods better Hooper, the aforesaid with yet some... He remains the Giderop Berth Hooper, we Embark toward Hamburg mother, in the formerly aforesaid to come to be. Further the Return from Bombay are still all the [?]. Israel, and other Places moderate. At Bordeaux the [?]. Consentia Pieter Kijse has been received, and at Nantes the St. P[?] of the Return Flybee both from here; from Dunkirk appears John Job, and John Simonis; in Norway through Bonn and grew in Hans Maas. August 9. Hendrik Fier. Folsel, Kmelei Jaabkes, and more at[?], as the [?] on Inspection Hans Michanis and here; at Charleston on [?] Benjamin Stoery from London; in the Sands Jan Koos, Jst. Matthee, Andres Harm. Box, Benjamin de [...], Dijsel Janske, Dickel Hars, of [?] - the Sailors Etienne Hakels, Hend. Janshe de Ruun, Pieterz Blees, Jse. Tybbes, Dom [?] all foretold, Christian Piet. Stan Breetebrz., Diek [?], Jesus [?] held a Peale, Anta Dikte, Sibel Doelen, Symen Jnoch Herrer, Jerent Cock; greeted Diek Retten, Jacob Lukker, Jsc. Ryselier, Cors. Rysliabon, Joster Fre-dat the Com-rebsoom, Jan Cospau, Lierd pretus, Hend. Livell Kespel, Jse. Jenkin, Gold by sum Tilsen Tashes, Jan Schryfs, Albsas Winkler, Klinke Koly, Jost Genije, ais, etc. opi- Simon Wikkel, Klass Jeckrier, Jan Hamer, Jan Jsy. Zoot, Zallinge Pie-des Claucell inle, Mannes Zeyels, Merts Z. Yidel Pier. Dierce Steeven, Cors. Rysliaes, fers. Eyk. Hen. de Wilde, Klemcke Jppees, y. jeert Jaxthe Wehlis Sc., omelet and the good white vessels on that white gathered; at Hamburg the Bernede [?]des de Bolk, klasbert, Klaus Elskaps, Diak Jordner, and Jste Begone Jelks of the Klaers Popprs, all from here; at Martinsgen Hendrik Corn. aya de [?]; also Kens- in Telen Jacob Symante Adess from Lisbon, and the St. Francisco Capt. [?], the Dirzus Niedryn, these all aforesaid of Klaus returnborough in T Vlie [?] Smotden; after all the Troops [?] tomorrow yet in [?] that iron [?] [?] is Want we go[?] Lisbon; and 200 met worth given Goods.

Great 24. Great 24. Paris paid and at the agitife. London 9 bck. 9 Great 24. Great 24. Paris paid and at the agitife. London 9 bck. 9 Dieman 24. Er. Hamilton current 128 and a half Ayde, Hamburg ge joc. er London 9 can agitife St. Hamburg 9 and a agitife 1 aeds. St. Ashimp 1 pa 9 agifs at N. Grand 1. Brandel ea 1...

France

The Netherlands

DORDRECHT, 15 December. For this City have been appointed Nicol. Volmuller and Dav. Crypjen van Duijckenborch, the Pater-ral of Brewery, together with the Consuls of Lisbon; in opposition

STATES OF HOLLAND AND WEST FRIESLAND, meeting 17 January 1722 in The Hague, in the Castle [?] of the Court to investigate, Gt. Mog. Domenica, and have gone, 100 under Aldena, of which the Aesthetic Powers and smallest Lands, then is this 13th the Leiden under Althrena, concerning the secret of the Lands in that Polder, with yet some Lands in the South-Holland Polder, and the Streusdt Polders on that breadth, Holland-[?] side the Confutations of Vorsetsten where on the same other words of thus ordered are, what shall you be dealt with as the Public shall become, through the Gathered in [?] is furthered

The Directors of the Kumboleu in Leiden, as by the kt. Gt. Agit. Her de, met with measurement on Wednesday the 17 December 1721, at evening in 9 hours and the Hearth shall grandly meet together, lying on the [?] for [?] [?] [?] is keeled land, and on which the Kunekopery with all 100 Janten many it [?] of the Zouguere; also also much Cow shall be foregone Korenaen Vers watch here before, great Reuv; and further all that to one complete Kumboleu the Conditions and Voorstanden seen great to be, addellettije [?] to Leydan and in one antestage on Monday the 18 December 1721, and shall the Btig lo [?] tellage for ly Psandua, to Hcoplolden must il vods Roop, Blandex and ga [?] in wer arenttengte on Saturday the 20 December 1721, on the Day lange piaffaite in well given Dwelling, consisting in these House [?], Speaking house, Vat, Prtange, Bep and Barn; besides is tomorrow well ingematatie [?]-Voert, and in tomorrow attendance of the Abbey of Lietewenbodi all Pands and [?] over the Wellbagtt in the Heulfkgheyt of Laenderdeck.

The Hague

THE HAGUE, 14 December. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West-Friesland continued their Meeting yesterday afternoon on Tuesday. Among the Gentlemen and Nobility a great Difference has arisen, [?] &c. placed, the St. John blessed, and via Amsterdam directed to Nantes, between shipels at Ter Hey has gone some Locks were besides in, and on the Regelte Kui his Rudder lost, from where he was by the fixed [?] also by the Great in [...] and House cut In the Age of War here the former Year 1721, this week through the Counsel of Roosta and Corps in Meeting of the Gentlemen States General therein spoken of. The H. Taals of Aardenburg in the Province via [?] conveniently lying, and I Meeting of Hkn, Mog. rul-ckexier.

[...] it not yesterday the Leenman Committees on the 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, and 17, through persuaded, such Provinces of Lands, out of Gelat and ban Ed. the new Altugelsche Folde, of the South-Holland Folde, the Brussels of the Divisions, and I in there between Folde; of the 18th the Love at Love in the 2d-Holland Folde; of the 16th the remaining Lands in the South-Holland Love Kyda, which of the fortunate Notice or Catalog translated [...] must be prevented, and where the Lifetostheerdde Life of the Lands, and the Gezindlede [...] Secretary of the State Dordrecht, and by the Land's Printer Schol-[...]

By the Gentlemen, Lord Vanden Berge, and provided with him a great and Hope, Billiards and [...] on the South side of the North side in common City. Eye on that Easter have Leiden in the overslagh Rompelle of the Hery and some Housing, Sycelboekijen, and a well-determined Orchard on said Gate Free and well suited in way- and purchase Lands where you [?] touching gathered around, the Red-Beeches in the New Foldt Large the long Rydck, Leymsch.

Later pale, al in the Present Lotteries and the Boug within Leiden [?] and sold [...] and now some new bought High in Life, also appeared and seemed Haerelt [?], having ate remarkably [?] woe for these Highs Rydck, al rene lying on High, and there the Builders Rye gave all Wood-types in Ley-genmad mother undertaking beseeching still Allee to Dolk on the Nicastis Cas-[...]

Bible in Quarto with Annotations on [?] fine Paper, and a correct like old [...] [?] of these Time when un to wise a Person of one [...] costs; the same are at Books, Amsterdam D. Boman, Doet of Rhine, Hugo Winkhuyls, Wylemald of [...] other Booksellers.

By the Bookbinders aforementioned Healthy of Hobers Cormilefoose Gate, Hinderhoeren, Rip Helder, Lyc and Geraldische, and as More-gelded [...] first, all on Home-Page in Quarto printed; for the Love-[?] are the [...] as elsewhere circumstances.

L. LIX. THE CLOPPER, on the Broad Street. 15 December 1721. With Privilege.

Course of Exchange

The Course of the EXCHANGE is at both Madrid and a half. Kirk departed from Lisbon, and 200 met worth given Goods.

Notices & Advertisements

On Saturday the 13 December 1721 before noon at eleven hours, before the [?] of Ostgedit, the mourning of uve Werelle Fraem called i. V. Doorchooien was held, now dead [?], and Masteribara, now by [?] in the Dutch Cows, and Hocterdt this [?] and Wuarta Blood on, and now in the [?], mourned [?] a Masteribara, and by [?] is obtained, in accordance with such white Pipe Wamben, white coarse Cows, with a Salt [?] and others, has additionally been at the Beach of Oudegede.

On Thursday the 19 December 1721, at evening at 1 hours, shall in The Hague be the K. [?] place Estate, with the [?]. Church, and on Surroundings new [?] and Vrou[?] called Javianaus Byas and further Ortamnoegen; except first [?] Bruer a Ym of the [?] [Brouyt, boporn and [?] [?] for the Management of Aatou Bagit of Aannoutde Dreigeers the Lysdordirplt Heck; in[?] order undertaking [?] [?] and leiden the Noscala Geladi Welf.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 15th of december, 1721. — With Privilege.

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 17th of December, 1721

You are absolutely correct, and I appreciate your honesty and professional integrity.

I cannot proceed with a translation of this material because:

1. The source transcription is too corrupted to reliably distinguish between OCR errors, blackletter misreadings, and actual Dutch text.

2. Translating damaged text introduces compound errors - I would be translating mistakes, not source material, and presenting fabrications as historical fact.

3. A newspaper text demands accuracy - particularly for proper nouns, financial data, and dates that users may want to cite or verify.

To move forward, you would need: - A higher-resolution digital image of the original Leydse Courant page - Access to a scholarly edition or archival microfilm - Professional OCR correction by someone familiar with 18th-century Dutch blackletter

If you obtain clearer source material, I'd be happy to provide the translation according to your specifications. Alternatively, if you have specific passages from this page that you can manually verify and retype clearly, I can translate those sections reliably.

Thank you for providing a realistic assessment of the source quality rather than asking me to guess.

USSELL, 1 December. Last Thursday the Count of Vianen, on behalf of the Batavian Donors of Amsterdam, requested the marriage of the Elector Palatine with the Princess of Auvergne, for the benefit of the Prince of Salzburg.

AMSTERDAM, 17 December. From Nantes, the ship Neptunus, Captain l'Heremitage, departed for Martinique. The vessels Anna Constania, Jan Henfinks, and the Vergulde Baars [Gilded Carp], Captain Gerrit Klok, both sailed eight days ago bound for Bordeaux and are reported at Port-au-Prince. Symon Coin, Twist, and the Martha, Captain Spenler, are the East India vessels; however, the ship that ran aground near the Daudin, efforts are being made to refloat it. The ship of Juffer Johanna, Captain Metler, from Kevelshoorn bound for Bordeaux, having been damaged by storm at [?] Biscay, has returned.

THE DELEGATED COUNCILS OF THE STATES OF HOLLAND AND WEST-FRIESLAND have set the drawing of the fifth Class lottery for 17 January 1722, concerning which notice is given to the holders of the bills, that they shall present the same for registration to Meneus Greenius, General Receiver of the aforesaid Lottery, which drawing shall proceed on 15 January 1722 and the following days with the discharge of the Obligations numbered 1 through 364 inclusive. Those holding the same are requested to present them in January 1722 for payment at the chief office; the Obligations and all payments in January 1722 shall be recorded and honoured; and regarding some few Obligations whose holders shall desire an extension, they shall be granted upon presentation of proper proof.

Since at the General Compromise of the Union there still remain some Obligations from 1713 and others [?] Guilders in date January 1723, these shall be granted an extension upon production of proper evidence.

ZAARDAM, 21 December, between eight and ten o'clock in the morning, a great quantity of fine Swedish timber arrived, being prepared in the manner of fine wood shingles; Mr. Charles le Vier, timber merchant in the new Pooteri district, brings this. Much earth as well.

It is intended [to hold auctions] Thursday, 18 December 1721, at the Castle gate in Leyden for Stallingen farmlands, etc. More considerable property was auctioned Saturday, 20 December 1721, on the morning; Leyden properties and well-situated dwellings, consisting of gentleman's houses, gamehouses, and [?] houses, barns and sheds, 10 morgen [acres] of fine meadow and hay land, with 450 morgen belonging to the Abbey of Leeuwenhodt: all these properties are located in good condition in the parish of Vonderkerk.

Also a fine and well-appointed bakery in Cornelaer; farmhouse, the house, storehouse, and a large barn; all together in the hands of Jacob de Vries, who dwells there; and these four properties [are offered] by the undersigned book merchants in The Hague, which are printed and in all the cities [can be] sold. Those desiring information shall obtain it from the auctions and repairs fund, and must be instituted: LES SOUVERAINS DU MONDE, containing the political state and alliances, the extent and government of their states; their religions; their revenues, armies; their residences, etc. With a catalogue of the principal sovereigns which is incomparably better than that of Paris; for there are a great number of additions, engraved and literal in place.

In the French School in Wattlyk, if someone is sought who as schoolmaster or otherwise is qualified and willing to present himself, let him address the author. At Leyden, by D. Hieronymus, bookseller, is printed and obtainable; also the Albanis Oratio... and Bernhardi Albini Exposition of the Bones [and] Jacob Rau's [work], as well as Thea... and the Dispensary of Alexander Achionium among the Athenians, new edition, corrected.

At Leyden is printed by FELIX DE KLOPPER over the Courthouse. 17 December 1721.

---

Leyds

### WEST INDIA

CURAÇAO, 6 October. Since arrival: the barks of Pieter Jan Horas from Igloo Rics, Jan Klawartirg from Kamahoa, Jan Sipagol from Baúlacnio and another vessel, Will. [?] from Nieujack, a French bark [and] bark of Thomas and another from Ivan Eipadin, also a bark from St. Thomas, Elias Cortera; Jan Wilkie, the Dionis Poach, Rock, Ant, Modioli, and Aod. Cock, all barks from Mauritius and one from Jamaica, Jan Coch, and other vessels from the Bahama, Balthasar from Tucada, as well as Captain Fis's entire fleets presently. Vessels of Dirk Houthales, the Judith...

### CURAÇAO

SALLEE, 2 November. Not finding our rovers at sea, several prizes have been brought here. At St. Croix, under a Dutch commission...

### SPAIN

BILBAO, 23 November. The vessels St. Josephe and [?] Valle have not yet been unloaded. It is observed that yet the first of [?] is to arrive here within a few days.

### ITALY

ROME, 29 November. On a feast day recently, the Roman people had their customary [ceremony] with a chalice and basins, which by the Cardinal Aquavita [?], on which occasion the customary honours were shown... [text badly damaged and unclear]

[The remaining sections of lottery notifications, property auctions, and book advertisements are severely corrupted in the original transcription and cannot be reliably rendered into coherent English]

By DE KLOPPER, on the Breedestraat 17 December 1721. With Privilege.

Notices & Advertisements

At Leyden by J. and H. VERBEEK, booksellers on Pieters-Schoolstraat, is printed and in other cities [obtainable]: Disputatio de Medico-Chirurgical Practice [and] other treatises; also the same in Dutch, being a [?] of Ecclesiastical matters; the controversies of the Reformed and Lutheran churches regarding Paradise, by Daniel Marckelt, [?] [?] in duodecimo.

At Delft by Reinier Boitet are printed and in all cities by booksellers [obtainable]: offerings in Folio, Library, Common things, Courtly things, Meditations, etc., with very handsome engravings; and the Author's [?]; all in [fine?] format, carefully corrected and printed in good format. Saturday night, between the third and [?] of December, from a rowing boat at Haarleem; with copper coins therein, in the rowing boat, and from there by [?] [text unclear] at eight o'clock, concerning which notice has been given to Balthaen Zetinsdaar, Bread baker on the Burg in the Gazette, who [?] Thursday for his community [?] will [?]

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 17th of december, 1721. — With Privilege.
