Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 19th of December, 1721
Friday'S Courant

### WEST INDIES.

CURACAO, October 4th. Since the last regular [dispatch], no ships have arrived in the harbor here. The brigs of Victor Berangier and Matheus de Rio, Jan Klasie from Tucaca, a French [vessel] from Baleneno and one from Coro, Wil. de Vos from Tucacas, a sloop from St. Thomas and a bark from St. Domingo, Jan Muller, a frigate from Martinico, Jan uydelandt from Berbererio and one from Coro, Wil. de Vos from Tucacas, a sloop from St. Thomas and a bark from St. Domingo, Jan Paul from St. Thomas and one from Barlcreete in the Gouda and the Diepste, Pieter And Tarp, Jan Vos, Jan Kalk from Martinico and one Jamaica [vessel], Jan van Hoorn from Porto Rico Knak, still [arrive] every day, the Diamant and the young Balsblaer from Tuecara, as well as Capt. Hein Bersuis. Several ships have been observed passing here; and those of Klaas Legon and Marius Scherpen are expected in early January; the ships of Dirk Houthuylen [and] Judith Kz. will take longer.

### BARBARY.

SALE, November 8th. Notwithstanding that 2 or 3 of our vessels are still at sea, no ships have been brought in here save the bark mentioned [by] Frylen. At St. Cruz 3 ships are lying, and there another Dutch galley [has arrived].

### SPAIN.

BILBAO, November 25th. The ships of Cocnraet Blok and the St. Joseph Francois del Valle are still detained at Porto Gaetsi-to by contrary winds. [They] expect [to proceed] to Liguria, and [it is expected] that Madrid will arrange for the administrative [officers] sought here to be confirmed and established on the ordinary footing by the end of this year. Capt. Adriaan Bogaert is the one [selected] from here to depart for the Admiralty.

### ITALY.

ROME, November 29th. Last Saturday, the Feast of St. Cecilia, the Roman people displayed their great offering, consisting of a chalice and wash-basins, [presented] at the basilica after Cardinal Aquaviva, titular [cardinal] of the said church, had presided over [the ceremony]. On the occasion, the custom was that [he should] return [it] to the offerers; although it displeased the common people, the representatives issued a strict decree to forbid this rebellious [act]. As the aforementioned Cardinal, titular [cardinal] of the same, shall be. On Sunday this money was [distributed], that Cosmife Repert and Cossun were in the parish of Areselli; on this occasion, the full [celebration] on Sunday in Munich, and [we should] thank Heaven for the happy capture of the Papacy by Innocentia and Pili.

A chalice and wash-basins [were presented] to the church of that name; but first Cardinal Aquaviva, titular [cardinal] of the same church, had presided over [the ceremony]. In the second [instance], the healthy first is above; [the common people] who bent in offering, although it displeased the common people, on the same day against [?] and [?] [it was] added, it was also forbidden to make any gift to the church, so long as the aforementioned Cardinal, titular [cardinal] of the same, shall be. On Sunday the merchant Bewan de Corupe betook himself to the church of Araceli, which was splendidly adorned, and had a solemn Te Deum sung in music to thank the Lord for the happy conquest of the Papacy by Inspection di till, citizens of Rome, in which solemnity cardinals were certified; the Pope, to hear [of] his concern over the swift [action] against the same [prohibition]; thereupon to the Holy See, he betook himself in the afternoon with his usual retinue and a large following of nobles to the common earth, [which] was grandly illuminated, but before for the good cause-manner as on the day of the solemnity of the politics of the grandeur [it had] arrived; where the cardinals and bishops came to the Pope [with] not great quantities of wash-basins, as well as great lanterns round about and great places before the capitol, to mark the happy illumination in the evening; play began with the beginning of triple darkness, whereby the capitol, as in a heavenly theater, was transformed, being nothing as magnificent as this illumination, since it went from below the capitol all the way up the mountain to the church. In the evening the Pontifical Ambassador performed a grand opera at the Theater of Guguieus, which he [?], before the cardinals, [?] and nobility, for which he had all the boxes pompously hung with damask and gold stuff. In the name of February a general [gathering] had been made, and [he] entertained all the gentlemen and ladies who appeared in Paschal dress, with glittering ornaments, according to all the custom [required]; but since the boxes of this playhouse belong to patricians, the Marquis Boncompagni presented [them], so that the Lord Frederick, Captain, [had] the Siecel; the same, not willing to yield, did he not [allow] the matter to be greatly [pressed], and opened [?] of birches in the loge Röken, which the Pope disapproved, [and] the Cardinal ordered that he [return] at once to the Siecel, in particular the aforementioned Cardinal Aquaviva [commanded] with soldiers of Leone Longone [to come] there, so that the Lord darisch Poelij Pubert should hold his two [?]. On Monday the Order of St. George had [an audience] with wide [?], at the Pope; the day U. H. the aforementioned also asked for him now, a play of bovo Seudi.

[REMAINDER WITHHELD], because the Cardinal de Rohau has arranged for the Duke of Modena [to increase] the yearly revenues of the aforementioned Prince by two thousand Scudi.

### VENICE.

VENICE, December 6th. Thursday, the Feast of St. Barbara, at the College of the Jesuits, and according to [custom], the lot was drawn for those who by this means were freed from midnight duties and entered the Council, and are therefore said to be broken through by God, since one usually [has] these minors of nobility, yes, the Good and the remaining silver-haired [ones] did not trouble him. The Count Save Mindelio of the Great-State of Great-Rutland betook himself to return to his court.

### GERMANY AND THE ADJACENT KINGDOMS.

VIENNA, December 6th. It is expected that [it] will not soon be possible to bring the house marriage of the great Duke of Tuscany and the old Leopoldine Kaidshan-origin to conclusion. Tuesday evening the desired Imperial chamber [official] and Reich Counselor the Count of Nimpelh, who recently came to this city from Gratz, where he has been under arrest for 2 years, [arrived]; having his Imperial Majesty [now] wished him to be freed in accordance with the judgment, namely that he should never be permitted to appear where the court is, [it has been] proven; and although it has gone very much thus, that one has nothing [concerning] this and has seen him here with great astonishment: yesterday he kissed the Emperor's hand and thanked him for the same mercy: but whether the lord will return to his former employment is not yet known, although many believe it now, because he is the brother-in-law of the Count of Althan, who is influential with His Imperial Majesty for him and will certainly [advocate] for this.

REGENSBURG, December 11th. Yesterday the Imperial Diet concluded, and thereat was seen an ordinance of His Imperial Majesty to take other precautions against the plague in the German realm, and by this occasion the moderations of [?] matters were continued. It is said that earlier news came from the Palatinate that the government, [having] committed the religious commission greatly [to the] regiment, [has sent it] to the district administrator of Geismers-dorf, because there they have [?] in the jurisdiction of the order some papacies, which with obstinacy continue to refuse to give the revenues that they have extracted from the Protestant subjects, pretending that his authority has been exceeded through the regular [?] of these [?], which the latter [believes], so to protect the Protestants from their [?], as if the orders were not yet properly confirmed by mercy [?].

### FRANCE.

Extract-Letter from St. Ambroise, November 23rd. The sickness rages at Mende, as well as in the places lying around it, and has spread into the barracks of Bramona near Mende. The plague has continued from St. Genais and in some neighboring villages; the infection has penetrated as far as Lasnic, being a village [near] in the houses [between?], between Joyeufe and l'Argenteuie; where the plague was brought by a man who came from the guard at the notice of St. Genais, in the house of which same man 3 other persons [have] died, who had [?] and colds. On the 26th of December 12 people died at Alais: the sickness has appeared again in the parish of St. Paul, which lies outside the city. From the gliders of Salindre and Lavel there is no news.

Extract-Letter from Avignon, November 29th. In the past week we have not had as many as 30 dead per day; yesterday we had 16 and perhaps more; and with the sickness it is proportionately [as bad]. I have written you that one has conducted oneself loosely and unrestrained in our quarantine; the onset of the plague shall be [a matter] of conviction, and the end of the quarantine is truly nothing but a specific feeling; one [fears that] 200 men will not be spared from the rigorous party; and it is likely that we shall fall into great confusion; all this has threatened a bad condition, for the plague is beginning to come among the brickmakers, which one has distributed and scattered throughout the city, whereby we find ourselves in greater straits and difficulty than ever.

PARIS, December 12th. It is said anew that the King has committed to Cardinal de Bois the apartment of the Prince de Conde of Tourfodour in the Tuileries. The day before yesterday His Majesty [tried] there on the same theater a small rehearsal of a ballet. Yesterday the King's opera-stable-master of Betzinglien rode out from this city on a day's journey [and brought] 19 horses from Barbary, which [have been] bought for His Majesty, and the aforementioned opera-stable-master shall conduct [them]. Concerning the rumor that the philosopher Dribuowski, ambassador of the Tsar of Great Russia, [is] coming here, [it] was from all [sides] [?] to be added by some extraordinary minister [and has been] paralyzed. At St. Malo 3 newly loaded ships from Italy [have sailed] for the account of the Company. One might there come out with various new [?] light in the future, where below in the freight and the distant [regions] sit in favor of the Company. The Indies [are] the thousand and 1 [?]. One collects money at Mounien or [?] specimens, and of a tax on the [?] of 700 [?]. The office of the three-yearly [?] of the King was [?]; and for the advantage of the old [?] of Mister Paris. The [?] taken in the [?], which one [?] of the [?] [?] 100 [expenses]: then of the Company. One might [?]; some attendance M. Dispelien purchase [?] [I] have now [?]. In the passage of the Tontier of the Cardinal the Mosillen [were] gathered a [?] of the Hillestop of Amiens, [which] makes well, and that [is] full of strength, bertas the Bael [which] makes well, and there full of [?], bertas the Bao du rece makes well, and there full of strength, bertas the Bao du veevents has confirmed [it] [with] a share of the Company. Blisfies, which [?] erected [it] for the advantage of the Company [?]. As when the [?] of this [?] [of] the other [?], Ahaÿa were [on] this occasion some [?] [concluded]. It is said that the [?] cardinal de Rohan of St. Gremaela in the fields.

Left Column

FRANCE

...will be present at Rome after Easter, as His Eminence, so it is much hoped, on account of the Letter from the 7 Bishops to the Pope, regarding His approval, which has been submitted for Examination; however, as the Ratification has not yet been completed, that Cardinal is soliciting most earnestly that the matter may be expedited, suppressed or pardoned. The King has granted and made difficulties for the Marquises of Blaisois, la Rochetarault, and de Roye, as well as their Peers, and all the aforesaid County of Routsis their old rights sought; one of the sons of these three has thanked the Duke Regent on the 9th thereof for the Government of Bapaume, which His Highness has given to him.

The President of the Maisons, clothed in a long mourning cloak, had the honour on that day to salute the King, having been presented by the Marshal of Villers. The Duke of Antin has ceded to his blood relation the Marquis de Bonas the District of Piennon, which after the King's coming of age shall be elevated to a Dukedom. Yesterday a female person was brought from the hands of Cartouche, and 6 other women along with two male persons were sentenced to death; the principal news one has about her in this matter is that Parliament by Decree has ordered her to appear in person, the Lieutenant Criminal and the Procurator of the King for the Châtelet, as being accused by Balágni, Cartouche and the other Slaves that they received for that Year fifteen thousand Livres from the Father of Balágni to free her from the Gallows, over which grave matter the Queen has had Parliament assembled. Among the nine Servants or Murderers, which on order of the Court by means of Frontispice of Sansen are on the way to Court, are also found among them Lackeys of the Duke of Ventadour, of which in one of my previous [issues] was printed. A [report] of the Silver Service of the Duke [?] has been born brought from the Palace of Luxembourg.

The 8 Servants, which were the cause of the good provision of the two [?] and Tiberge in the School of Foreigners through the Detachment of Soldiers from the Invalides in said Seminar were employed, are all brought to Prison. Except the Mister Durand, who the aforesaid Sunday in the street of Tarannex was brought from his magnificent Carriage, one has also seized on the same day the German Baron Vezsl, which is held suspected in rebellion against his [?] the Abbot de la Morte when he plundered the aforesaid Vendeligcen. The aforesaid Ferrand, Miller of Cartouche, who since 12 days has been missing, was arrested on Monday and wished in the Faubourg St. Germain, by a Comb-maker's Boy, who overpowered him, after he had 2 [?] of this Corly formerly held and struck out: one has found by him Matters open of the Châtelet.

ACTS OF HOLLAND AND WEST FRIESLAND, shall be on 17 January 1722 in The Hague, in the Castle-house of the Lord through [?] Very Mighty, Domenico, and alike be, so under Athena, the new All-Folder and the Holding, to wit, the 17 and 18 Lands under Athena referred from the Lands in the Folder, with yet some Lands in the Hollandic Folder, the 17 in the District Folder and other [?] first the Conditions or Provisions what on the same shall be, before they are kept in the Sale, by the Summoned [?], is to be obtained.

THE COMMISSIONED COUNCILS OF THE STATES OF HOLLAND, of the Lords of the route for the said Class before or finally on the 17 Jan[uary] of the right of the indestructible Lords, on which fine as now also more [?] drawn Lords and Nobles, which there have to appear have [?] & peace of this notice.

THE STATES OF HOLLAND AND WEST FRIESLAND, shall yet be on 27 January 1722 in The Hague, in the Castle-house of the Court through His Very Mighty Dominico, and be situated, so under Athena, the new All-Folder and Holdings, to wit, the 13 and 13 Lands under Athena referred from the Lands in the Folder, with yet some Lands in the Hollandic Folder; the 17 in the District Folder and under [?] first the Conditions or Provisions what on the same shall be, before they are kept in the Sale, by the Summoned [?], is to be obtained.

THE COMMISSIONED COUNCILS OF THE STATES OF HOLLAND, of the Lords of the route for the said Class before or finally on the 17 Jan[uary] of the right of the unperturbed Lords; on which fine as now also must [?], as being that Three of the Holders of the same, who there for are in the said Class, have fully paid; then nobody, because they are observed, may have ignorance see. Note. The drawing of the 11[?] Mr. Nicolaas Mulder, Advocate and Agent, having commission from his Principal to sell on Tuesday the 13 January 1722, in the Castle-house of the Honourable, great manor, Town Hall, and dwelling House, wherein diverse large [?] and [?], and an uncommon fair large [?] or [?] on Sundays, situated on the side of the Plantation by the [?] the Lady Guatiane Doustiere of, under [?] Hornes &c.; anyone desirous, pleased to address themselves to the aforesaid Agent Mr. Nic[olaas].

Note is at my intention on Saturday the 20 December 1721, at Leydenburgh place and well situated Dwelling, consisting in Whole Housing, Play-house [?], Boss Housing, Burg and Barn; and Also 16 acres well made [?], with as much as acres coming the Abbey of Leenwoaicofti; all [?] over the Meadow-fart in the Lordship of Kouderkerk.

The Surveying of the Testament of the late Philip Weymans, [?] of the Gentlemen's Lodging at the Burg within Leiden, in the public [?] to do [?] in that of the same Housing, Play-house, Barn-house, likewise an [?] 298 roods; standing and situated outside the Hogewoerts Gate of the City Leiden, and [?] Portray, under the Ambages of Soeterwoude; likewise another together great 22 acres 247 roods; situated by and at the other legacy [?] Folder under Soeternoude. [Those] desiring further instruction, address [?] on Tuesday the 23 December 1721, 5 o'clock in the evening, at the aforesaid [?] be an extra pleasant Farm-house, with a kitchen, [?], and [?] of [?] and [?], item Gardener's House and Further [?]; having at [?] over the Rhine and the low Rhine-dike, planted and planted with the proper [?] of Soeterwoude around the Leyderduorple Gate; anyone [desires?] further [?] the Man, see Leiden at Notary Gerard Wolff.

Word is made known that Skipper Pieter Tendifte End, has found himself to address Amsterdam, has found Hermanus Vooge hairdresser in Leiden on the Langeburg, the [?] by [?], evening a Cheese before the Glass fire, makes known that he makes a [?] 300 times as from him, for Men and Women; the Children also [?] anyone lives far from him from there, can [?] show him the way, where that it [?] will [?]; that [?] their [?] then in Schiedam Houses on [?] of Fish [?] and [?], how six further [?], can be [?]; he has five Apartments at his house; and Friday may Histories the 9 and 2 of this Month December is [?] [?]; with Copper being [?] in the Row or [?], and new [?] and or thereof knowledge [to be given?] to Balliaan Zemmellaar, Brod[?] [?] and the [?], who the Discoverer for his common [?] [to be?] ...

Right Column

THE NETHERLANDS

AMSTERDAM, 17 December. From Bordeaux have departed the Ships the King David Liend, Fiet. Croen, and the Hope Ary de Voer toward Emden, Corn. Pieteille, Jan Gratuis, Corn. Appel, Pietje, de Rie &c. toward here and the Maas; from Rouen the Ship [?] returns, also toward the Rif and Maas the Hundred Gelds, Douwe Juule, and 4 other [ships] here and the Maas. From St. Valery is reported the Ship the Young Agges, Willem Wont. Zeller, from Bordeaux having [?] folk held. The Dutch Ship of Rieke Klaadife, from here toward Udewelt going, has run aground like water at Bergen entered. Jacob has from Amsterdam toward Karelskroon departed. In Norway have entered the Wildacht Skipper Winkelbos, and most Ships, the 17 aforementioned from Goricum departed. At Bayonne is reported the St. Jan Will, Reyn of Zinkzekels; at St. Sebastian Jacob Soer, at Bordeaux the 3 [?] Geunt Coop, and the New of the Lizde Dwelling the young all from here [?] the Coat of Arms of Schiedam Jan Jantien from the Maas at Bergen well 20 Ships, among them Antonio Voer, the [?], and also the little Yacht the [?]; Barent Pietelsnaer from Hamburg, [?] Shoemaker from Königsberg, Noos Stephenie from [?], Jan Lampe and yet another French Ship from France, as well as all Ships in Archangel, an English Ship from Lisbon, and more others from England. In [Texel?] a great number Ships have gone to Sea, among them 3 [?] Ships, [?] Ships toward the Baltic Sea, but under order of Sir. Thomas, Gerrit Kok and Jan Booy toward Suriname, Hendrik Doyt toward Curaçao, Damiaan Gonzalez, Juriaan Jantlien, and Warnard Hofman toward Bilbao &c.

THE HAGUE, 18 December. The Gentlemen States of Holland and West-Friesland were gathered again today. The Lord Minister of the Reunion is invited, there the Gentlemen Deputies [?] Letters to the House of Dogs on the 21 January [?], to the repeating Provinces and the [?] departing.

STATES OF HOLLAND AND WEST-FRIESLAND, advertise that the fulfilling finally on the 17 Jan. 1722 must be completed, on pain of absolutely forfeiting; and that for the time a vulven juleg of 10 Gl. have taken, who therefor 3 times a vulven juleg of 10 Gl. have [...]

Course of Exchange

**EXCHANGE RATES** The Course of EXCHANGE is today on Madrid oz and 1 quarter. Cadiz and 1 quarter. Seville oz and 2 and 1 quarter. Bilbao oz and 1 quarter. Lucon, Genoa oz and 1 quarter. Paris 42 and 1 agile. London 32 and 1 half. London; oz and 1 quarter. Danzig 20 2 large. Breslau 32 and 2 agile 4. Hamburg 32 and 2 agile 4. [?]. St. Antwerp and 1 quarter p.C. Ghent 1 and 1 quarter. Brussels 1 [?] and 1 half. Rotterdam 1 agile. The Action of the Bank 1 and 1 quarter. The Action of the O.L.C. W.I.C. 9a and 1 half. Subler, 180. The Eng. O.L. Company 15 and 2 C. 92 and 1 half. The Holland Lottery without supply 65 Gls. [Editor's note: This newspaper edition is heavily damaged and fragmentary, particularly in the right column and lower sections. The transcription contains numerous illegible passages marked with [?]. The legible portions cover French court news regarding the Cartouche criminal case and various legal proceedings, Dutch shipping and exchange rates from Amsterdam, and scattered international news from England, France, and Geneva. Several sections are too degraded to render coherently.]

Notices & Advertisements

For sale abroad [?] a fine and renowned Bake-house [?] Cake-house, as well as the House, Warehouse, and a great Barn; all by mail [?] is done: [inquire] at Jacob de Vits, who lives there, and [?] Cattle, [?] are [?] and in all cities by the Rockwork consisting of Front-hangings, Bible-houses, Birth-records, Interior-works, &c., with very skilful Prints, and the Picture-house of the Poet provided; all [?] some Exemplars and great Medium [?]-Paper in [shops?].

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 19th of december, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Sunday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 21st of December, 1721

ENGLAND.

PETERSBURG, 24 November. It is not certain when [the matter] will remain or continue to be consulted; but it appears that the East India [Company] and in confession has been given.

SWITZERLAND.

GENEVA, 9 December. Yesterday a certain Aubert Hopés [?] in an assault [?] great mercy enjoyed Honorable is though, because [?] you have them much [?] were undergone [?] when; in of her men very here to the Mende no lice and to follow directed in prayers for [?] grew, with discovered Heard, a rope on the [?], and a slow-had, to food Dead that you lend, which sight from the Redsluys to the Gate St. François, but outside and to the right, him to him hereafter in this Region shall come to see, him to tomorrow do.

FRANCE.

from Alsautch, I[?] of the 30 of the Sickness and how far had in the four corners of that Place, as also on three cleared Places in Germany; daily there 13 People became sick, and that there 200 well in the Hospital, nerve an equal measure in Quarantine. Another Letter of the 24th reports that there in days nobody newly of sickness found was, though on his 14th of the 25th he reports that there know some became sick were.

Extract Letter from Marseille, 27 December. news that in this City yet eighteen Sick have passed through.

Extract Letter of the 29 Ditto. The September to first of September; and of all the Sicknesses the 30 November and certain of the December are the reports in Marseille the deceased, and in civil status there made is found better than of the 29 September to the 3 November, in the free Medical third held were: at Genouilles are of the 24 to and 29 to Mende are in no Sick deceased to St. Geniulius continued the Sick-teck than Salindie, St. Pauli, Ufen, and all the Cardow well the difficulty reported; there against had itself the Sickness anew again Worsened in Barnabe, in the Parish of Melnons: but after that [?] few People in the Concourse in general-died, and there in forth of the fourth personally.

PARIS, 13 December. According to the Dispatches of the same of Madrid; I have news the preliminary Articles of the Treaty between this Crown and Great Britain, not yet at the present time received.

Monday's Courant.

[An announcement includes:] the songs concerning the condition at Alenscher [?] when vignon are deep-sight, as the Sickness there many People takes away. The Water-channel was had. The few Precautions which the Vice-Legate there taken had, the passage of that Canal to close. According to the Just letters from Rome found the Cardinal de Rohan in the beginning of there toward to depart, although trickery will that such well until after New Year would be postponed; one Christian of there that the Knight of St. John various times by the an Audience was prevented, and that certain unknown Person by him was arrived, who his Pelts held, with which by him in Rosenbergs-leiden to Audience of his H: was been: many pursue costly Togyten, and an excellent Silver Service, that which in these men stoning, Certain Person, named Felilier, who himself in service had been there for some days on order of the Court seven touches not known to have; there on was he with the Fustangh of the; but before it there after came, confessed he all; whereupon he at hands and feet bound being, in a Carriage was carried, um geons here of knowledge having, began themselves out of the City, and posted themselves in a Wood there he must pass; they attacked the Coach, followed their Companion, and murdered the Coachman, Fortillion, and all the Archers. The so-called la Motte was pallid[?] Friday have of the Accomplices of his Band: one knows that a considerable Band of those Villains themselves toward the Netherlands has sought, or that they a meeting have held between Person and Camera-rik; this week stand still various Villains of the Band of Car-let are, having the Streets in this City, so that the evening begins the falling very unsafe: one has since 2 or 3 days again 3 murdered such bodies found in the Lane of St. Denis, and about the Italian Comedy are also two People murdered. One has twice of writing felt it, there again to back was come, very enlarged being that there over the honor which he since his stay all here at the Court and elsewhere enjoyed has, was of deceive to the Cardinal du Bois one a handed Letter testimony has done, with request to the King in the Haron of Orleans from his name to thanks to consider &c.

The Express who with our Minister the Mr. Camppedon from Petersburg at this Court has arrived, is again with Dispatches there to back sent. The counter-held all the reasonings, is until still with no certainty to tell who to the Archbishopdom of Rheims as successor of this is of the is traveled-

PARIS, 1 December.

We have here today that the preliminary Articles of the Marriage Contract between the King of France and the Infanta of Spain on the 24th [?] wholly were by the Duke of St. Simon and the Marquis de Moncerette; and Ambassador of his King, in by the Marquis of Bedmar and Grimaldo, as there also empowered being. The King and the Queen of Spain, with the Prince of Asturias, have there on Compliment of congratulation from all the Nobles of the Court received. The following day had the Duke of St. Simon a great public Audience by his Catholic Majesty had, and the King his empowered Commissioner discussed. One had at Madrid the following days much Rejoicing-drive; and among others a fine Fireworks executed. The entire Court on the 26th there at order and his whole Power after Religion prayed: at the same time had all the Grandes, Eminent Ministers and other nobles, where their Majesties themselves great mass; where on the King, the Queen, the Prince of Asturias, and the Infanta, with the part of the Court, on the 27th [?] were broken; having that night the Allella pilgrims, and still following to Guadalaxara gone, where the Court itself to the same [?] on directed. The whole world is here with all that Rejoicing-expectation to be.

ENGLAND.

In December of this Year 1721, in the Year of Grace Year 1721, in the [?] were the burials, bringings; great and unfortunate of the ready [?] and the burials in the Kingdom were there equally toward the high [?] in the glorious Beauty.

THE NETHERLANDS.

Ready happen, so Kill[?] [?] Jacobi with good success To of the High [?] the can by no Shipwrecked, the on charge not noting in this following States Union this south-roof know will heard is. We have Horizon of the very rumor side at down lay said, which lich lame of the Vests hang and others very great, and by all the also with good had Leyden said and restored with new

the 15 the remaining Lands in the South arching Boat Gaaldenjon, and there also [?] of the Lands and, and the by of the Lands Printer writing

Like lived in the Streets of St. Denis, and about the Italian Comedy are also two People murdered. One has news from Constantinople, that the Turkish Ambassador, which at this Court [?] is, there again to back was come, very satisfied being that the Order and unity his [?] all here at the Court and elsewhere enjoyed has, where of the same to the Cardinal du Bois one a [?] Letter testimony has done, with request to the King and the Haron of Orleans from his name there for to thank [?]. The Express who our Minister the Mr. Camppedon from Petersburg at this Court has arrived, is again with Dispatches there to back sent. Not withstanding all the reasonings, is to still with no certainty to tell who to the Archbishopdom of Rheims shall be elected. One thinks that the King himself held the same majority and anointed shall be, his stay at Versailles shall go hold. Following the Letters from Lorraine had the Duke of Loth an order caused to publish against the bringing in of Goods coming from belong Switzerland, and which order necessary Commissioners toward the Frontiers were sent, to on all closely watch to give: some Letters will as if one of the Sons of mentioned Duke in that time well near the Imperial Court might be sent and from there of a Prize of the Empire brought up would be, so through there Imperial Majesty's no Manucke Deledeneis might to be wrought, as then with of the Emperor's Daughter to marry. One will that Secret [?] of the Elector-Prince of Bayern and the second Josephine Archbishop on a good foot held to polite are. One says all the Duke Regent and Letter of the Peace has received concerning the matter of the Coalition. Some will that the Mr. Law shortly from England again here stayed to home, so one says about lady eight Switzerland. The Loadings of the 9 [?] from the South Sea arrived Ships are at the first sold to become. The blow of the departure of our Ministers toward Cameron [?] is still uncertain. There is an Express from the Duke of Hemyck at the Court come. Gliteeik was of the Pope's Nuncio very long with the Cardinal du Bois in Conference: one says that mentioned Nuncio both the Express toward Rome stands to dispatch. The rational [?] Swiss at Cantons verify one that the King and the Duke Regent by State have discussed over the lost Wedding Between his Majesty, with the Infanta of Spain, and of the Princess of Montpellier with the Prince of Asturias. One sees an Article of the 744, mentioning Commissioners for the verification of the whole [?]. Another of the 21 March, prescribing the form of the Examination of the Leyden [?].

PARIS, 15 December. That the Bull for the Jubilee-Festival by the Parliament rejected would be, seemed the grabbed good here, is untrue; but it is certain that it not published shall be unless at the end of the adjoined Valley, one verified Ficule since. Seven Bishops have their Appeal to the coming Council anew renewed. The Catechism of the Bishop of Amiens is on order of the King suppressed become. Mademoiselle de Montpensier, the Queen suppressed become. Mademoiselle of Bourbelas stands to come, shall there with all Magnificence by the citizens received and welcomed be. In the Night Storm are many Ships wrecked and stranded on the Coasts of Picardy and Calais. The owner of the Herclia Faris renewed his Daughter from that Monastery, to which Wedding the Dispatch to his Brother of Moumartel closed was. All is here excelled there, that which the number of the Thieves on the eye does attract. Saturday was Gaspart Königsathe la Morhe at the red cross hanged, on having a Red with Gold gallooned Coat. Le Chevaliere, Virclip de de Flasis de Gierce all ide one of the Wives of Cailomde, Pursued on the Havis de Giene ay ide hanged, why that again back led toward the Conciergerie, and following Amidst day of the following day some one [?] Accomplices nakedly having, why they the following day

England

---

Great Britain

LONDON, December 12th. On Tuesday last the Lords gave a second reading and petitioned for the Land Tax Bill, after which their Lordships continued in Committee to examine the accounts and commissioners regarding the naval debts, and ordered that the matter be discussed more thoroughly at the next session. On Wednesday their Lordships heard only a case between two Aldermen of Dublin and the Baroness [?] and the sentences of Parliament in Ireland were confirmed. An appeal is made for 40 [pounds?]. The Commons on the first reading of the Bill concerning Military Discipline, which had been passed through the Commons and the Lords the previous week, ordered a second reading for the coming Saturday, and their Lordships shall receive various accounts regarding the pay of the troops and the officers on half pay. On Wednesday the Commons for the second time in Committee reviewed a Bill on a great import on salt used for salting fish, which Bill was completed by the said Committee yesterday, and the report will be presented to the House in grand Committee tomorrow concerning the Money Bill, and thereupon a clause to more forcefully prevent all manner of foreign private lotteries; after which the Commissioners of Toll shall go before the House with an account of the duties collected on merchandise brought in from Christmas 1720 to Christmas 1721. The weavers have presented a petition wherein they complain of the use of Dutch silk threads which are called by another name, and the House ordered that this matter be brought before the House. The [vessel] which the King purchased from the King of Morocco and placed in the Thames near the bridge for the benefit of London shall be opened on Tuesday for viewing, and shall then proceed to the Church of St. Paul's where a service shall be held for this occasion and a collection made for the poor widows. The general meeting of the [East India] Company shall not be held before the coming Thursday.

---

Cadiz, November 22Nd

According to letters from Madrid, the Spanish Captain John Negroudt, Captain John Garret of Gaveta, Raimondo King and Thom. Gree to London and Jo. Louisia Galero to St. Lucar, the fleet [details are too corrupted to translate reliably] towards [various destinations]; in this bay are gathered rest frigates, 21 French, 3 Dutch [?] unloaded and being stored...

---

Paris, December 15Th

[This section is too corrupted in the transcription to produce a reliable translation.]

---

The Hague, December 21St

The day before yesterday the Lords of the States of Holland and West Friesland separated, having adjourned until the coming Wednesday, January 7th. That evening the body of the Lord Jan Duijvenvoorden was brought with great ceremony to Voorlichopjen to be buried there. The Lord Roelants and the Lords gathered for the meeting of all the States General... regarding the College of the Admiralty in Amsterdam. According to letters from Boulogne of the 9th of this month, the King of Prussia departed from Rotterdam on the 4th in the evening; the General Count Humpeich had an audience with him on the following day, and he was received with great ceremony by His Majesty's retinue.

Through the same courier [reports] on the 12th, 13th, 14th, 15th, 16th, and 17th [details of parliamentary transactions regarding various polder lands in South Holland are too corrupted to render clearly]

---

Public Notice: Sale of Property

THE STATES OF HOLLAND AND WEST FRIESLAND,

in assembly together on Tuesday the 27th January 1722 in The Hague, in the castle courtyard, shall sell, inasmuch as they have commissioners charged therewith, 200 [units?] of land in the new Athenian Polder... [details of land sales with conditions to follow]

At the general office of the Union, there remain certain Bonds of 1718 in the sum of one thousand guilders dated January 1718. It is hereby announced that these sums shall be paid at the General Receiver's office [further details regarding bond redemption follow]

---

Sales & Auctions

[Multiple notices follow regarding auctions of paintings by Old Masters (Rubens, van Dyck, Snyder, Rembrandt, Brueghel, and others), printed books, and other merchandise in Amsterdam and Delft]

---

DE KLOPPER, on the Breedestraat December 1721. With Privilege.

Notices & Advertisements

On Tuesday the 27th December 1721, at five o'clock in the evening, at the Lords' lodgings shall be auctioned a stately country house with a beautiful, strong, and newly constructed suite of rooms, a pleasure garden and other buildings; having a delightful view over the [Rijksdijk?], planted with the finest fruit trees...

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 21st of december, 1721. — With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 24th of December, 1721
Spain

CADIZ, November 21st. Friends and French vessels arrived from Lisbon: the Casas Bagni Marino, and the St. Germain (Captain Olivier) from Mariaego, the Brianna (Captain John Winacoe) from London, and the Tecta (Benjamin Garett) from the Canaries. Departed: Thomas Jackson toward Liverpool; [?] to King and Thorn, bound for the Canaries; Diego Dornford toward London; the St. Louis toward Genoa; And. Peter toward Barcelona; Gilbert Duncell toward St. Lucar; the Flying Mercury (Pieter Geweld) toward Malaga; Richard Ayond toward Gibraltar; Bernardo Minuci toward St. Malo and Biaive de Grace; Pedro Daubert toward Alicante; the Marquis (John Bellerve) toward London; Carols Sellilie toward St. Malo; Ed. Martin toward Cork; Rich. Norvin toward Dublin; the St. Martin (Gerard Colombyn) toward the Berdarn; and the Marquis del Campo (Addices Henry Follingel) toward Oporto. Currently lying in the harbor besides these: one Ostend ship, one Genoese, one Swedish, four English East Indiamen, and 37 merchant vessels, 21 French, 9 Dutch, and 2 Hamburg ships. Notwithstanding the many ships that the merchant Matthias [?] France Schultz reportedly has lying here, there is nevertheless a prohibition against admitting ships coming from the coast of Provence into the harbors of this realm. No news has yet been received from offshore. Following reports from our fishing fleets: 2 Algerian corsairs with 30 and 36 pieces of artillery, both royal vessels of the [?], placed under French house authority.

France

PARIS, December 15th. The consternation over the two decrees of November 21st of the past month continues unabated. Two measures have been taken regarding the reduction of the public debt, among which the Paper [securities] [?] a considerable scourge, inasmuch as everyone whose wealth consisted in these Effects has been greatly diminished. In the private liquidation of many effects, one finds great losses as well, from which those responsible must be punished. It has been reported that this considerable plague has been checked in some places of the Gevaudan. The Archbishop of Arles complains that certain clerics of his diocese have not been able to remain in their dwellings, whereby [he hoped to prevent] 200 routes of the realm from the communication of the plague, in place of the sixths and goods; but he must still hope therein where the sickness [?].

Cardinal de Bissy has for this reason not recently come to Court at Tours to oversee the painting there; out of fear that he would have become suspect, on account of his excessive zeal for the Constitution. It has been reported to Rome that Cardinal de Roben eagerly requested permission to obtain the letter of the seven bishops condemned; but these same advisors report that there are great disturbances there against the plague and the plague moves that they must condemn themselves; the Jesuits in these present times also [?] of his having been, remained [?]. A certain Doctor who was in Paris has obtained freedom to examine throughout this city up to 10 miles distance, and upon the foundation which previously one had done on the same footing, one of the Secretaries of State answered that they would be here within a short time. Yet a third day still some Manuscript was given which the new Parliament and other books against the Constitution [?] together along the [?]. Through the continual keeping which has prevailed, the price of foodstuffs has risen very high. It is assured that the Company will still be sought from Paris for this quarter, all which to the Company still other matters will be requested, which to the same a new Credit shall bring. One speaks still of a new lottery to be undertaken.

ARLES, December 13th. That the publication of the Bull of the Jubilee Year in Paris has been postponed, it is thought to be because the Diversity would have made a protest against the Cardinal, so that the King, who is then to make his first Communion, might be at leisure to rejoice therein. The Pope has granted the Bull still the bishopdom of Sardat to Monsieur de Blanc, brother of the Secretary of State; but His Holiness has given that [?] the Abbot Margarine the Archbishopdom of Vienne, or the King has given it to him. The Count of Prade Grande of Spain and Mont-Leoma of the Marshals of Villeroy has asked to be saluted by the King: this young gentleman is preparing himself to go to the Academy. Last Wednesday the son of [?] wholly saluted His Majesty. Thursday one consecrated in St. Leuthard [?], whereupon this Hetwe of Clotism with his Sister Mademoiselle de Reain joined, Peter and Maette have been present. The Grandmother of Malta has sent the house of her Old to Abbot Vertor, who does not hold it more here from that mountain highland; and the general Chapter in the Temple assembled, has still assigned to her a compensation in the place of Caeteil, which is worth 300 Livres yearly, besides still a pension of 1200 Livres against the Bailly de Meme Amiliatie of the Religion, and those of the Grand [?] on the same Abbeys to the Fredact [?] has given. Cardinal de Ruban has secured to Bishop Vielois one of the [?] of Coupen, at which repetition was manifested of the Invite of S Doupagne, which is an Emigrant [?] against long [?]; because that day in case in the Bishopric by the Paus in the bed to lie, said against him some cruelty by the Pope, on account of here entirely [?] and also some by wholly of said of the attending Valley. The performance of the Ballet of the Emotions was carried out last Saturday. Madame [?] and [?] of the Great Bagga of the Cathedral Valdo, is held dead

Wednesday evening.

President de Maisons of the young, which in these days has been received in the New Cloister of the Cordeliers, in the Grave-Tomb of Maimbert, has celebrated some days. The body of the Duke of Pius James was buried days afterward at the Cloister; where the former King of England, James II, was buried and remains. The associates of Robins Pollutieres, and the accomplices of the well-known Theft at Rheims, having fled by Lions and this City, have besides the ordinary [?] and Arrests, had the halter cut off. One has indeed long had it in the Criminal [?] and the Procurer of the King, before the Chatelet, in petition summoned Arrest ordained is to inform of the Fraud which by Bals [?] this work [?] shall be handled, (although the Parliament had the intention, namely against the Lt. Criminal.

PARIS, December 19th. The day before yesterday, Monsieur Werner d'Audence, born a Luckenaere, and General of the regular Canons of the Holy Cross, had public audience with His Majesty. One reports here that the Commerce-Tineau perhaps the income of the Czar of Great-Colland will come into effect; and that this Holland a profitable navigation will have between the places in the East sea. One makes diligent inquiry after the Author of a very rude and malicious writing, affecting the 2 decrees which the King recently issued. One reports that Madame Lyon of the Fort-Louden is to be removed. The Baron Beniemeeder having received some letters from the Court, is engaged as some Minister of this Court. One believes that the camera-like Congress will not be opened until the coming Spring. One is here in high anxiety, because the rumor goes that the Court will bring all the Gold and Silver species again to the Mint, to be marked with a new mark. The good [?] which the King still intended to hold before the Festivals, to restore the Duke du Maine in all previous Dignities, etc., has been deferred some weeks. A distinguished gentleman was a few days ago in the Bastille, where he has sat about 3 months, [?], without one yet having any certainty who he is, or for what reason the matter is concerned, only it is murmured that it concerns [?] Matters. The Castle of Krecqui, in which one had the aforementioned [?] captured hither, by his Companies in [?], or that the thereby being [?] [?] would be [?] at the want; one expects him tomorrow in this City. The Duke of Orleans departs this city on Friday to go meet the Infante of Spain at the frontiers. The Felim of Prince Dolhorukki, Ambassador of the Czar, has been postponed again until next Monday. The Duke of Orleans has given the Duke of Ploumier [?] to [?].

Great Britain

LONDON, December 14th. Saturday the Lords of the Commissioners of the Fleet received from the different States the reckonings which the Peers had offered to give at the House; but the same did not yet bring all together, and could make, different [?] of the same still not all together, and could make, different lordships the considerations of the causes whereby the ship-debts have become so great up to today; whereat the Lord Compter made a very long harangue, wherein the ships which one had above still held timber for France, with a further reasoning on the damage which one would thereby have suffered, to which he brought the example of Charles II., who by the perseverance in this harbor allowing warships for France to enter, the shipping power of Flanders in good condition held, so that one wishes much had happened or this nation would thereto inclined have been before misery happened been, whether in regard of the Religion, as well as trade and belief; finally one came to argue that he of written might for Foreigners came to boom on the Timar ships much honors directed received on the same [?] Monday morning. Yesterday the 150 slaves led by the King out of the slavery of the Kings of Morocco conducted themselves ceremonially from the river to the chief church of St. Paul to thank Heaven for their liberation; on which occasion the Mister Bishop chaplain of the Bishop of London (who however was present) there made an appropriate sermon over the words of the [?] and so forth out of the Psalm: after the Dies sung and College before them was done, given they had St. James and

Leydse Courant

Friday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 26th of December, 1721
Barbary

SALE, November 10. The continuing unruly behavior causes severe famine and misery here, so that people are dying not only from starvation but also from the robberies by the lawless bands from the mountains; indeed, no one dares travel the road without a safe-conduct pass. In the preceding days there was a black rain here, which brought some rejoicing among the inhabitants; on the 26th there fell more rain, but such cannot remedy the great drought; nevertheless, the country folk have begun sowing. Here 2 ships and caravans have arrived, through which some of the inhabitants have been supplied with goods; also the stranded Brig Bordeaux, Commander, and the Ship de Pijptiplas of Plymouth, Captain Willem Aukling have been guided toward Amsterdam, and Mastin Everard [?] will depart there shortly. Thus also two Dutch ships from Holland.

Italy

ROME, December 10. Last Saturday the Bishop of Sisteron returned from here to the court of France. Sunday morning, being the first Sunday in Advent, a service was held in the chapel of the Palazzo as usual, where the entire Sacred College was in full attendance; however, the Pope was not present due to the severe weather; thereafter a remarkable healing lasted 40 hours [?]. Monday morning the Pope held Consistory, wherein certain matters were concluded: matters concerning the Kingdom of Naples were presented. In the afternoon, when the weather improved, his Holiness and his son [?] visited the Venerable [?], and subsequently the body of St. Andrew and apostolic relics in the Church of St. Peter, where he was [?] in medical [?]. Wednesday morning his Holiness heard, after the Sacred College, the sermon in the chapel of the Quirinal. The Prince and Princess of Modena both held their midday meal with Cardinal de Rohan, and departed after eating from this city back to Reggio; the aforementioned Cardinal, at every moment [?] to the French court to resign, having previously rented out the tables [?] of the Palace for 3 years; it is publicly said here that this Cardinal will take over Reggio in 1622, [Editor's note: date appears garbled in original] to speak in the name of the Duke as Legate of Urbantus [?], concerning matters which his Son the Heir-Prince concerns. Cardinal Paulacci, Vicar of his Holiness, has drawn too great a [?], so that the Archbishops, Bishops, and ecclesiastical Prelates, both spiritual and temporal, who find themselves in Rome within these four weeks, are to be chosen at Rome, for Canonicate-positions and other matters contained in the Edict, with revocation of all License which until this day will have been granted by the aforementioned Cardinal; but better [?] causes to be published there for the Recitation of Mary's Ascension and the Brothers of the Jesuits; and for Cardinal Alberoni there remains still a solemn Mass to be celebrated; followed by a solemn Procession from the aforementioned Church to that of St. Mary Majorem, by Bishops and Fathers who find themselves in this city, as well as all the ecclesiastical and temporal nobility.

France

PARIS, December 19. Abbé Lopin la Fare in [?] in good health, to great comfort of the Constitutionalist-minded. They have held here among other things the auction of the effects of the late Cardinal de Mailly, offering forty thousand bottles of Rheims wine for sale.

The Abbot of Pomponne, Abbot of St. Medard de Soissons, [?] with the Queen and Process to come before Parliament over 1000 acres of Borsfeld [?], dependent on this Abbey, and it is likely that the Constitutionalists favor this Abbot. The Festival of the Epiphany will follow, and because of the great preparations for Sunday the 21st, it will be very magnificent, with the King's arrangements; all the rooms of the Palace of the [?] will be used for this Festival, the Ambassador having been given another lodging during the preparation: it remains firmly established that his Excellency the Ambassadors and foreign Ministers will be received on the first day; that the second, when a fine fireworks and [?] will be, for the Ladies, etc., and the third for the Common people; during these 3 days the Palace will be illuminated night and day, and during all that time several fountains will spout wine; and everything shines in the utmost splendor, although 100 Swiss will hold the guard and be in service. It is also reported that in all the cities of Spain, following the example of Madrid, great joy was celebrated over the birth of the Prince of Asturias, and the Infante; these should currently be at Terragona in these circumstances; and the journey will be continued hereward: the Princess of Montpensier, who on the 13th was received in Bordeaux with all magnificence, will arrive at Bayonne at the end of this month; Madame de Vantadour travels alone with the aforementioned Princess in her Carriage, while the Princess de Soubise and Chaumeille are in another coach, but on the day of the exchange this last as Governess of the Princess of Montpensier will be at the side of the aforementioned Princess at Poitiers. The Princesses of Vantadour [?] has authority over this, however not [?], except in the name of the Princess of Montpensier: one works night and day in the Louvre, and makes great preparations there. However, we have heard nothing more of the Declaration in Parliament.

England, France, the Netherlands

Prince de Moltke received the letter from The Hague of December 20, and it will be forwarded after signature of the Empress to the Deputation of the Court.

Great Britain

LONDON, December 19. Tuesday evening there arrived here Expresses from Lisbon to the Envoy of Portugal with dispatches from that court; and because this Minister went the following morning to the States-General [?] to the house of the aforementioned Envoy, where they had long discussions, it is hoped that the accommodation will happen speedily. Wednesday the Commons met in committee to the following resolutions regarding payments on the subsidy.

The Netherlands

BRUSSELS, 12 December. According to the latest letters from Paris, it is reported that the disturbances in France are spreading more widely, particularly toward the coastal regions and around Montpellier; that refugees are leaving the Province of Languedoc, and that the Province of Guyenne (in which the King's city of Bordeaux is located) is being secured.

AMSTERDAM, 12 December. At Haarlem, several ships bound for here still lay laden with wounded, etc. From Bilbao, ships have arrived at this city [?] Bircon Blod in the Valley and Coenraad Block in the Admiral's Squadron. Blood [?] daily hereafter following. Willem Janile Welledank from here at Port à Fort [?] has arrived at Vigor [?]. The ship Anne of the Fleet departs from Stockport. [?] 300 Russian prisoners toward Kevel, and from Rochelle Jorgen Bosnian from Ireland with the ship Catharina Elisabeth, supposed Dragon for Amsterdam bound, having been captured by the Turks, [text becomes unclear].

THE STATES OF HOLLAND AND WEST-FRIESLAND shall through the same Utrecht present to their High Powers in the Castle [?] of the Lord, so His Grace [?], and [?]; 300 under Athens, which [?] Athenian [?] and Hendria, being that which is [?] the 15 Lands under Athens located [?] from the Lands in the Fields, with yet some Lands in the South-Holland Hollandic Fields; [text becomes increasingly unclear] the Dispute [?] and under Handel [?] against the [?] or Conditions was upon the same [?] were postponed, and this is the Sale which shall be held by the [?] at Siena [?], it is received.

It is reported further that a letter was posted on the 11th November 1721, [?] Catharina van Eech, Widow Kekes, on the Singel, opposite the [?] Church, from [?] Cornelius or [?] 9945 [?] the Burghers; 9946 [?] Agner Constantijn van Eeck, [?] 1217 until 1219 May God grant; 11210 what further have [?]; and 1222 to 1217 G. W. A. H. L. A.; the third letter must [?] if [?] Citrons to a reference have.

At Rotterdam arrives from Leiden toward Port à Fort Captain Thomas Godice, with a Lisbon [?], met with a [?] Cannon, [having] a [?] Far, [which] shall [?] (it being wild [?]) on 10 January 1722; someone [?] God Captain, and to Edward Thompson. N.B. The ship shall lie in the Boom [?], of the [?] [?], Conv. Suntin [?], Jan Lemmers, and Nicai. Littgoot [?], Suries. At Amsterdam on the Singel in the Burg, received a part of 200 [?] all [?], Louriv, it is also [?].

[?] of the Wry, Jen van Kuulits, and Jis Witting Mirkfains, shall at Ant [?] [?] a [?] [?] Nicoer [?] Logermeer On the Haarlemmermeile, [?] or [?] Point will be issued.

At Delft at [?] Bourer have been printed and in all cities at the booksellers [?] in Stock, Biblical, [?] Reckonings; [?], here and in Holland Print, and the engraved [?] of the Poet prepared, as on [?] at [?] Exemplars and large Median Icarife Paper is received.

France

From Paris, 6 December. With the arrival of the sea dispatches has the [?] in his Pelican, mixed as other great vessels, ceased between Amsterdam. The fortification works proceed at the fortifications of Newport, which one shall now begin to strengthen. Among other things, His Majesty has sent the fortifications of the City of Mardyck to work with great speed, for it appears that the same, after great labor and expense, shall be placed in a state to serve as a strong bulwark for this Kingdom and the defense of its coasts.

With the second packet of instructions, which accompanied the long letter of His Majesty, it has been agreed that much of importance is determined there, and clearly prescribed concerning the military dispositions and the plan of the campaign [...] proceeding against all possible unforeseen circumstances.

The affairs of the [?] now seem to stand again more toward prosperity [...], and it is to be hoped that the good disposition which is observed among his officers shall contribute to the execution of all necessary works.

The [?] are no longer to be approached, but shall be drawn off from the forts and outworks, by which God preserve and [?] His sent and [?] against all [?] and the justice and all in godly heraldic whole Europe protects [?].

The midday light shall possibly be further treated, it shall be together [?]. That all the further [?] and [?] which shall be carried there, and here further not possibly [?].

### THE NETHERLANDS.

From Utrecht, 12 January 1722.

The envoys of the provinces have on the 11th of this month [deliberated] and resolved to go to Haderwijck, and there it appears [...] concerning the [...] of England [...].

Thomas Goddes, with his [?] well-equipped free English [?]-ship, named the [?] English [?], with [...] Marconnia, and the Easter altogether covered is [?] and [?].

---

Printed at Leiden by HELIX DE KLOPPER, on the Breedestrant opposite the Town Hall. 26 December 1721. With Privilege.

Leydse Courant

Monday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 29th of December, 1721
Barbary

ALGIERS, 8 November. On the 16th, the ship of Captain Jooſt Slickman, belonging to Lithoen and bound for Amsterdam, was brought in here; she was numbered 1732, and after the cargo had been thrown overboard three times by three of our sea rovers of [?], it was placed in the great boat, and after 7 hours of being towed, [the crew] first suspected [the attack].

Spain

MADRID, 9 December. Their Majesties together with the Prince of Asturias continued their journey toward Lerma; the entire court left Segovia that day, and snowed the following night at Xadrague, where on the fifth [they reached] Alcanca, where the journey continued for the fifth week, and the route proceeded over Berlago, with stops at Axcillas, accompanied by their chief Chamberlains the Marchioness of Montellano, and the entire household of the approaching Princess of Asturias, who set out the aforesaid day toward Burgos. On the second of this month, the Baron of the approaching entire court also departed Madrid to follow the court to Lerma, and [traveled] over the Esquial and Villaſtells. On the second, the Papal Nuncio also set out over Alcala on his journey to join the court at Lerma. By letters from Livorno, we have received news of the Admiral's [fleet], which it is said had embarked the entire retinue there; that [the Spanish fleet,] under the command of Don Antonio Serrano, which [carried] supplies of 3 large Carolies for the King, 2 fine Tables for the Queen, stones and a great quantity of Marble [and] the entire painted gallery, departed Livorno under sail, and on the 29th of that month arrived in the harbor of Barcelona, from whence the same [fleet], after the Cardinal had not [?], made its journey to Ascamies, to unload there the Marble which had been loaded at Livorno by order of the King.

England

Extract-Letter, Mendes, 2 December. With the waning of the moon, the sickness in this city has increased for the second time, being to date [?] cases recorded, as people believe; and 3 persons have died, and 3 newly registered: in the Parish of La Ghent, concerning about half a year's suffering, a woman constantly [?] who had washed linens at the Barrier of Marseilles: a house has been affected in the district of Ker [?]

France

Extract-Letter, Mendes, 2 December. With the waning of the moon, the sickness in this city has increased greatly for the second time; to date 11 persons have died; as otherwise found; at Marz in places, where a woman is buried who had been buying linens at the barrier of Marseilles; a house has been affected in the district de Rez, where several persons are dead; and the Surgeon Crifice [was] sent. The Gentleman Crifice of the Pont Vieux [on] the 4th, and in his place 2 others have been assigned in this district on 29 December, which district had 13 to 14 persons found sick in various [locations]. The Doctors le Moine and Bailly write from 29 December that since the 24th, [some] 29 persons have been found sick in various [locations]. The Gentleman Crifice, Doctor Aleix, who has been infected with the sickness by circumstance, is reported to be in strict [condition of] health.

Extract-Letter, Ayguden, 2 December. For some days now, people here have been struck by the raging sickness, and although a great number of deaths have been seen, they are nonetheless much lamented; for ordinarily the dead are led to burials, and quarantine has been ordered in the higher districts.

Extract-Letter, Orange, 4 December. We find ourselves since lately in fairly the same state, and have in total seen too persons die from the [raging] sickness; although this is much avoided; at Marz in places, where a woman is buried who had been buying linens at the barrier of Marseilles; a house has been affected in the district de Rez; Yet certain districts of this great city have been particularly severely visited; it is reported from the records in the high prisons that recovery has been found thereabouts in complete [fashion].

Extract-Letter, 3 December. Since the sickness has broken out here, some 420 persons have died: the Gentleman Pretorius has died.

Extract-Letter, 5 December. The last gathering there still proceeds to some degree; but as the sickness increases, here a great number [is] under quarantine. The Mr. de Poole has died here; the sickness has broken out here, some 420 persons have died: the young Miss van Alsmeren, whose quarantine consists of [quarantine]; because recovery has been found thereabouts in complete [fashion].

Germany & Adjacent Kingdoms

HAMBURG, 21 December. Letters from Stockholm of the 10th of this month confirm that the Diet in Sweden would begin on New Year's Day: an Express messenger from the Gentleman Campredon of Petersburg had arrived there. Some letters from Petersburg suggest that Count Tolstoy would remain in Moscow until well into spring; in the aforesaid Petersburg and in Riga, fixed military supplies were being prepared, though no one knows what purpose they will be used for. On the 19th of this month, the Crown Princess held her last [ceremony] at Copenhagen with great splendor.

Great Britain

LONDON, 23 December. The principal debates which took place in the general assembly of the Z. Z. Company brought certain very curious matters to light; concerning the union and all [?] matters whether so-called godly or if thereby the Millions would not be brought home [?]; said M. Wr. de Hackney, that he would not [?] the request to the King, says the foregoing as by which he [?] was taken, distressed, and every C. was also well taken into consideration, which company they had, or if the 2 Millions shall [be] given to her by [?] did we wish this 2 [?] would send to in-unity, and followed was taken, distressed, and every C. with company they had, or if the 2 Millions shall be given to her by [?] did we wish this 2 would send to in-unity, and [?] company they had, or if the 2 Millions shall be given to her by [?] was [?] all could [?] have, as by different advice of the Palace [?], which were set up, then in Boats would be granted; indeed one [?] were set up, then in Boats would be granted; Indeed [?] indeed [?] third [?] that he shall always come [?] [?] indeed would [?] by to west negroes [?] [?] the 10000 P. indeed I hope we that shall come [?]

GREAT BRITAIN

LONDON, 26 December. The debates first raised against the [Inclifte?] Bill for Incorporation, presented between the Quartermaster-Admiral of the Marine, are now ready, as likewise held yesterday in the House to abandon [?] and in the Lazaretto or [?] [Erneq?], wherein the [?] which Liefs [?] itself untamed, and those which Liefs [?] itself untamed, and men of the same side were that the clauses to the Crown titled, undescribed [?] [?] though this was not done, yet [?] [?] was not quickly undertaken [?] find necessary good need to become [?] good fitting lightness, as that the removal of the Horothe Man of the Waaeldu: and held [?] was: great that as wagons of the bet-Zi side, and which to the Life [?] was, so the Vilk in boovdaacke make foresee [?] director Nh [?] sdcru and Huynseo through the [?] Vlaag see Cecil open[?] Marutsuife, or me there fielded judgments were, din thereby much strength to itself to spread [?] Ixtsaa fethood [?] has, Atsoo the Mans [?] daddcra the cilende [?] [?] to become left, lies then to strike there the [?] is, not calf good enough to at good comet what in a[?] was undertake Clausai to the De Clausule in the Quartermaster- din such also Cau le is permitted, tadres da Febttaatie not [?] he hoatoeus of his Majesty. Undertaking Clausade to the Negative pattecrdeu: o to behictes that gerneche Clausnei stock I of Here are no pieces to [?]; : ges mostly we [?] fe for the hast [?] syn, duen bu natken of to the [?] that's vygen la Sunamen g [?] here coenen to wantcren, patceotcru of nocencea of his Majesty. Undertaking pattecria laid had, which not foresee [?] aft dia Negative pattecrdeu: o to behictes that gerneche Clausnei seoch I of Here are no pieces to [?]; : ges mostly we [?]
