Leydse Courant

Wednesday Edition
Printed at Leyden, the 31st of December, 1721
Spain

CADIZ, December 2nd. The silver and fruits have now been distributed to all the shareholders; however some broken spices are still expected from the fleet. The ship de Liefde, coming from Amsterdam to here, which was delivered four days before The Hague, has not been permitted to enter to come to the harbor houses. This ship will have to undergo quarantine, and the Dutch and English ships coming from the North Sea should take this plague precaution carefully. The Governor of Cadiz has been presented with [a petition] that comes from here to depart with the Spanish ships, which according to orders will make the voyage to India in March or April, where they must arrive by then. Given that no fleet will depart from here for the year 1725, the Adios will not depart and will continue until further order; it is said the Argoan ships will not depart before February 1723 from here. In this bay there are still 200 great ships anchored, including 1 [ship named] Liefde, [with captains] Richard Tucket to Topham's, Mar. Guilielmo to St. Lucas, Henrico Aiguet to Bordeaux, Niel Malbood to Alcantara, Pieter Ardenby to Hamburg, Thomas Bland to Dublin, Andi. Forer to Nantes, Louis de V. to St. Malo and Barle de Grace, Robert van de Belde, David Negado to Conan, Will. Childers to Malaga, and Francisco Marchand to Pasia. Still 300 [ships]; in this bay there yet lie 3 warships and 23 English merchant [ships] and French, one Hamburg, one East German, one Swedish, and one Genoese ship.

Germany & the Adjacent Kingdoms

REGENSBURG, December 23rd. Nonetheless no Imperial Diet has been held. The Committee of Ministers reports further that the Commissioner's Decree of His Imperial Majesty concerning the matters of the Regency, and as it comes to the Imperial Diet, will be communicated: some [representatives] from the Emperor turn themselves therein to the Committee regarding his matters, to go to the Hague's Financial Assembly, also not without [some] complaint: meanwhile the Protestant Corps is very attentive to learn from this whether the Vienna Court the conduct of [?] N.D. from [?] as if tolerated; one hears in good faith that [?] 120.

Great Britain

LONDON, December 24th. The debates which in the House of Lords regarding the Insolve Bill have taken place, or rather the clauses which one found difficult to adjust the quarantine [?] was what one may have given to Leyden which [?].

LONDON, December 26th. The debates which in the House of Lords against the Infamous Bill for the Quarantine-Act were to the removing of the power which one had [?] to Leyden which one [?], is the one Henry Huyen [?] and into the Lazarettos or old Hospitals to go, a line or lines drawn around a confined city or village before which line or lines no one would be permitted to venture, except with permission of [?] or officer appointed thereto: the principal persons of this place were all the [?] clauses made Claudius to the Crown counted, incredibly firm, and this remains without assumption against each other in the lands to go, and even must from the War fields and Horses previous be found heard higher [?] was, says that whenever the Magazine good alike and the [?] and then the line of the Lazarettos to go, where in rightfully had [?] the director Kirkgolf one of the [?] or appointed thereto were Deep to the end [?] had Found were thus the [?] of the Dwarf with one not to forbid [?] well in hands of the hurried Man who Waerells: and affair Lord who before the Claudius on the line of the Services [?] was, says that all whereto the come the same to guard: one did likewise that through [?] were not answerable that of this Magre a break made would be to lightening of [?] People's Freedom, as befits propriety; and if the [?] [?] well in hands of the hurried Man who Waerells: and affair Lord who before the Claudius on the line of the Services [?] was, says that all whereto the come the same to guard: one did likewise that through [?] were not answerable that of this Magre a break made would be to lightening of [?] People's Freedom, as befits propriety; and if the [?] [?] well in hands of the hurried Man who Waerells: and affair Lord who before the Claudius the Cruyte-alike going was made with the making of lines around this city [?]; [?] was impossible, does all the Cross advantage of this state [?].

France

PARIS, December 24th. Last Wednesday evening the King heard the first Vespers sung in music in the chapel of the Palais des Tuileries, where the Coordinator of Carcassonne performed the service. The following day, being Christmas, the Militia affixed themselves in music, which was celebrated pontifically by the same prelate; in the afternoon His Majesty heard the sermon of the Pater [?], and subsequently the Vespers. The Count of Prada, here come from Portugal, as was mentioned in the previous [report], finds himself here, and mentioned in the previous [report], finds himself with Villeroy; this young Count fled from the aforesaid kingdom; where he has been imprisoned for 3 years, such that they [?] of Father who is the first nobleman of the King's chamber, his age and [?] a company [?] to handle that book; the cause of that imprisonment could not [be] lifted; from Lisbon, while in his carriage, moved as when he [?] this the state did not meet, in place which relay in hands to leave before he ride, as the King had commanded all the [?]. Yesterday the Duke and Duchess of Orleans, [?] with the Henry of Charrot, went to hear Mass in their parish church of St. [?]. Various merchants in this city and in Rouen have [...]

France

PARIS, 22 December. Early in the morning, in the German neighborhood, in the house where Mr. de la Moufe, Member of the French Academy, resides, a delivery took place. On Friday, at the home of Mr. Durand, one of the Advocates of the Council, a first clerk in a certain profession, lodging in the lying-in house, was delivered of a child. This lying-in woman is recovering very well. On Saturday, she was moved, along with the child, after it had previously been considered whether one might not wait a few days and allow a chambermaid to attend to all four [children?], or whether one was obliged to provide one to each of the children. It had been thought that one [priest] would suffice for all persons, which is why he had preached only once; however, they [the children] lasted but two days. Some days ago, a young Procurator before the Court undertook to forbid Mr. de la Moufe from seeking this matter, because Nerva was married there to two women, which in this manner was permitted. The men present—when he was publicly present at the blessing of the Place—he married in secret with the chambermaid serving there, by whom he has had children. Mr. du Plessis had denied him his Charter, though he urged him to marry, whereby he fell into poverty and some good opportunity was created for him. However, his wife being unwilling to abandon [him], from [a] bad [situation?], Mala and Fort obtained his fortune without having... he had... the courage to bring the matter to his wife too far, so that she gave him freedom to stem a second [time?], which was wealthy. Thus he [?] and were relatives of all her property, to preserve the secret, as she also promised for this reason, that she saw that her... and Joachim Wits's governing son was. Because she began some persecutions, Willem Will and [?] to make, whereby the man was forced to flee with Martin Klym. The Marquis de Torty de la Tour, for this reason [?] here; at Plymouth are the lying fields of the Guard, and which was married with M. [?] London John Mi Gamilcille de Gamfchen, at Aigueille deceased, being a landsman, John M, count of the much-praised Valeurtibteau. The Marquis de Saville [?] burg; Roelof Kh [?] general in service died, and also these days Mr. Derpeutice of the troops, Thomas Teleimat of the Troops. They report the Duke of Bourbon changed [?], Robert [?] Master of the King's [?], at the request of the Duke of Vil-Fort, in Teifel [?] leroy, and the Princess de Vantsadour, [who] has promised a considerable Gift for the City Jan to make to the [?] Celuse to pay the money that was invested for his Amph [?]. Madame Lies is here in great motion over the State... [?]... and is divided from her Husband's debts; she has had the Creditors submit [?] with regard to... and [?]... Declaration of what is needed. Mr. de La Cour, for [?]... and [?]... their Sperdics of Frestalue, some days ago on Order of the [?]; [?] Mr. Chief of the Police, is sufficient in management, because he [?] per Cent. [?] a young Heinje has seduced [?]. One is advised by the Instruction of the exploitation of followed Compilations of Canouche; the Schepen [?] of the O. given is with good will inclined to pursue... [?]... following the Commander, and Knights of the Order of St. Lazare, [who] held their general Chapter in the Cloister of the aforesaid Carmelites, in the presence of the Duke... and Captain... their Grandmother, in which occasion the Prior of the Don Novas, the aforesaid Carmelite, Knight Chaplain of that Order, a Dutchman by... of the Tertia in for the Marquise de Boully [?], a matter [which] occurred, which thus much [stirred?] in this City [and] thus happened: one of the [?]... of said Marquise, the Conseil de Sia who... rode hunting in the [?] service, appeared some days ago and [?] of Or. at midnight, under pretense of fierce Love, in her Head-Bedroom, took hold, and this she by her wild [?], where this Lady was not very pleased... but finally not with a restless behavior against him... [?]

Meanwhile, the Commanders and Knights of the Order of St. Lazare have held their general Chapter in the Cloister of the aforesaid Carmelites, in the presence of the Duke of Chartres, their Grandfather, in which occasion the Prior Don Noyan, the aforesaid Carmelite, Knight Chaplain of that Order, performed a Service. These days a matter happened to the Marquise de Bouley, which stirred much rumor in this City and opened a deep contradiction. A young woman of the said Marquise, who for 10 years had been in her Service, appeared some days ago at midnight, under pretext of fierce Love, in her Bedroom, seeking to sleep with her, where this Lady was not long willing; but finally with a restless behavior against him [which] gives to understand [?] the said day; [?]... by mercier fulfilled [?]... [?]... worthy [?], as when a broader between and a made [?] while Madam... has been able... Loringe very much; whereupon he stood in the Wardrobe Martin Courier [?]... a Lande Laarven, must on advancing or at latest on 17 January 1721 must a full [?], on pain of absolute [?]... on which piece as still also must be required [?]... the [?] in the first Class good [?] of this [?]... for once a free flight of the oil, have good, and shall the niemand, for this authority here of, so through the Condition as Advertisers... [?]... all... the [?] Class on the 12 January [following?] this Evening of the [?] hours, being Wednesday the 21 December 1721, shall the Makelars and Hend. Liragest at Amsterdam sell on the Cingel the Burg, 102 packs of Hides, [?] Hides, 30 Wool and [?], 21 packs of Masthair, and 12 Carris Casual. Mr. Jacob [?] Ctitioa, Secretary of Soeterwoude, as by Schout and Schepenen [Burgomasters and Aldermen] of the Brodd and Gordenem, Jan Daniel van Cingetsiusck, is of office on Monday the High... [?]... in the Directorial the Fonten; [?] the Regie of Soeterwoude at that place... [?]..., bedding in the same [?]... the westmost... [?]... as well with a separate Garden and Garden house attached, and these [same] hanging Landynen, together great... 20 mergen [acres] lying on the Delfde Four under the Authority of Soeterwoude before, named 't HUYST te Rusige Leeroeng, [which] being bound by the States of Holland and West Friesland in this [?]... and thus the right obtained [they] have... [and?] to speak; without in the future that any consent of [it] is needed [from] the States of Holland and West Friesland in the [date] 27 July 1719, thereof [being?] granted. 'Willem Iosepet ie Roeland van Leuven Makedam, shall [be able?] Middeldam sell in the Brekke Ground, a party of 1a Bailieu Zyde, containing in 7 Taay C.; 2 ordinary [?] one Bailur Milaaidie Orgenegle. The Biddersks Hoffadl Wyugaardeo, is to let against May 1722, lying... aged to h [?] land, [?]... Stables, Coach house and Plantations, great... 8 mergen [?] [?] Bailju at Voorschooten.

At Leiden, printed by Jan Goorl vander As and is followed [by] Sigchemi Havercampi Para, Disquisitions on the Coins of Alexander the Great: [?] Questions [?] to the Teuris pistis, with Figures Enels, A. [?] also the Signpost of the Enumdivden: following the manner of biography of articles and journals of the Childly Youth, by Jaepus de Hiscuw, published within Leiden, 8: [?] Huben in the other Street in the RosksKovpers [?] in the [?]... that further during Exemplars to obtain of Amnias Heidesipel van To ganuus Tapo, of each one... Police to Mad. M. A. Babbier, 11 with ties and [?] Method 3.

At Delft, printed by Renier Boites and in all Cities by the Booksellers... to obtain [?] Fore-songs, Biblical [texts], Birth-songs, Memorials, Biblical [texts], Ten, with first burst of the Prime and the Libeldel of the Poet brought, all on [?] also some Exemplars on large Medical [?] Writing-Paper to obtain.

The Netherlands

AMSTERDAM, 29 December. The Princess of Montpensier finds herself still at Bordeaux, and preparations were made there to receive that Princess in the most solemn manner. The [?] of Nerva guarded [?] Dutch Ships, which are called the Fifty Ships, as well as the Skipper Tieert Tietjcs, Jan Pieterste, Symen Annes, Terbke Brouwer, and Rynder Tierks, without that anything was first reported from there: but of the wrecked East Indian Ship the Rasdhuys of Enkhuyleu, the Companies [?] some Goods were booked. It is said here that [?] those named and St. Mank Breudt and the Hollandise Shipyard would be [?], but of one of the last would the People be reported; also around the Rift of Schaogen wrecked the Ship the Will. John Hunter, from Darsztrig to London [was intended], but the People and Goods were salvaged. At Lisbon various Ships have been [?]... among them Robert Fagus of Svilhten, in the Zond Willem Abryns and Jochem Wiernelburg of Calmer; at Bordeaux the Friendship Willem Will and the Adriaen Gerrit de Boer, at Nantes the Miss Sulanu-ra, Marien Kiyn, and at St. Marten the [?] Sisters Gerrit Syn, all here; at Plymouth Capt. Nic. del Barco of Bilbao; on the River of London John Michiel, John Willing, John Hillers, Hend Wjnnts, Ira Dykman, John Msalon, John Foulon, and Jcronsimua Bouien of Hamburg; Roelof Klaasien of here; Richard Perkins, Robb. Klark, John Henton, Thomas Filips &c. of Rotterdam; John Chesman of Amstel, Robert Smith of Stockholm, and Thomas Wigram of Four [?]... various Ships [have come] in, as well as [?]... l'Vlie or before the City Jan Adr. de Boer of Stockholm, Symen Ham &c.

The Hague

'S GRAVENHAGE, 30 December. Yesterday morning, Mr. Pype, Minister of the King of Sweden, was in conversation with some Gentlemen of the Government; likewise the General Major de Bross, Minister of the King of Poland, Elector of Saxony. The Baron Ulner, Minister of the Elector of the Palatinate, will be seen in the short term without return; as well as the Keur-Meurliten Minister the Baron de Isonge. This morning, Mr. Gobern van Slingeland before the King in State delivered the letter as Receiver of the City of the Lands of Overmale, and further [?], forced under the same Company. P.S. It is reported that the Spanish Minister the Marquis de Montieone, and the Holding of the last Conference with Her Ho. Mog. Deputized, has dispatched an Express in all diligence to Madrid.

The last [notification], that the Drawing of the Lotteries from the 15th to the 17th Class on the following terms [shall?] be fallen [expired] by the 16th of the mentioned Lotteries. [?] in the first Class [?] have drawn, as [they?] sent the Place to the Ho. O., and [despite?] the red Class [?]... [?]... as Advertisements are [?]... no ignorance can [?] thereof assume.

[12 January 1721], the Brokers Joh. Staars, Cora, van den Heuvel, Jaa Lemtin the Burg, shall sell [?] packs of Morescrie Jogien, 218 Bercebuyden, 450 Masela and the Kettu Casassule Talk; all to be seen of that day.

chout and Schepenen [Burgomasters and Aldermen] of the same Office set as Schepfier [?] in [?] Place of [?] on Monday the 12 January 1721, in the evening at [?] hours, of [?] of Soeterwoude, in [the] open [air?] to [do?] auction and sell, a [?]... sen Farmhouse, Barn and [?], [together?] with Orchard with its [?] attached, and a Lane running to the [?] fields with 20 mergen [acres] lying outside the [?] of the City Leiden on [?]... made House at [?]; being the [?], 20 mergen Land [?] from Holland and West-Friesland, being conveyed to a [?] dike [?] to thereby dispose in the morning... the same lie..., the... out of... [is?] needed... nothing [in excess?] of the... of [?] of... thereof [is?] produced.

[?] at Amsterdam, on the Spiegelgreft in the Witte Zon, makes known that through lost [?]... some other [?]... to remove, wherein something happened would: having [?]... of my [?] and Elisabeth, which [have been?] blessed all losses of Venereal Sickness and Achilles-[?], and this [?]... Course of Exchange

**EXCHANGE RATES [Couva van de WISSEL]:** Madrid 95 and ¼; Cadiz 116 and ¼; Seville 116 and ¼; Bilbao 92 and ½; Lisbon 23 and ¼; Venice 85; Livorno 93; Genoa 94 and ¼; Peru [?] 83 and ¼ and 7 [?]; London 34 Sch. 8 and ½; Leipzig cash 98 and ¼ [?] [?]; St. Dantsig 293 and ½ à 94 [?]; Bremen 8 and ¼; St. Hamburg 32 and [?]; St. Antwerp 7 and ¼ per Cent.; Ghent [?] and ¼; Brussels [?] and ¼; Zealand 7 [?]; Rotterdam [?]. **Bank Agio** 5 and [?]; [?] [?]. **Stocks:** O.I.C. 386, W.I.C. 94, SubGef. 870 à 75. English D.I.C. 240, Bank 123, Z.Z. 93 and ½. Dutch Loxen [Loans] in the [?] class 29 [?] the [?].

Notices & Advertisements

Mr. Thomas Angelnome, Surgeon, Operator and Breaksmeelier at Amsterdam, on the S [?]... in the esteemed Philosophical Mercurius, the which his operation first without [?] 500 [?]... of the same undiscovered [?]... free Women and young Children was used... [?]... Asbrabueck, brought through the same use, on an act and singular [?]... [?]... or malignant also is, without that anyone [?]... frequently or often Patients could by the fore[mentioned]... Medicines help themselves, provided they follow... in this journey... treatment and my daily oversight and own treatment... until [?]... the Chamber street... [?]... if therein according to his... [?]... of that [?]... bush... [?]... still... willing we (the Patients) soon answer, but to full satisfaction... by accord... through the aforesaid Medicine... the ungentle Sickness and Accidents, [?] much [too?] much to unsettle here; avoided... [?]... [It?] is also not helped, unless they have no Venereal Sickness.

Printed at Leyden by Felix de Klopper, on the Breedestraat, opposite the City Hall.
The 31st of december, 1721. — With Privilege.
